matthew:18:33.csb: Shouldn’t you also have had mercy on your fellow servant, as I had mercy on you?’
matthew:18:33.nrsv: Should you not have had mercy on your fellow slave, as I had mercy on you?'

matthew:18:34.niv: In anger his master turned him over to the jailers to be tortured, until he should pay back all he owed.
matthew:18:34.csb: And because he was angry, his master handed him over to the jailers to be tortured until he could pay everything that was owed.
matthew:18:34.nrsv: And in anger his lord handed him over to be tortured until he would pay his entire debt.

matthew:18:35.niv: "This is how my heavenly Father will treat each of you unless you forgive your brother from your heart."
matthew:18:35.csb: So also my heavenly Father will do to you unless every one of you forgives his brother or sister from your heart.”
matthew:18:35.nrsv: So my heavenly Father will also do to every one of you, if you do not forgive your brother or sister from your heart."

matthew:19:1.niv: When Jesus had finished saying these things, he left Galilee and went into the region of Judea to the other side of the Jordan.
matthew:19:1.csb: When Jesus had finished saying these things, he departed from Galilee and went to the region of Judea across the Jordan.
matthew:19:1.nrsv: When Jesus had finished saying these things, he left Galilee and went to the region of Judea beyond the Jordan.

matthew:19:2.niv: Large crowds followed him, and he healed them there.
matthew:19:2.csb: Large crowds followed him, and he healed them there.
matthew:19:2.nrsv: Large crowds followed him, and he cured them there.

matthew:19:3.niv: Some Pharisees came to him to test him. They asked, "Is it lawful for a man to divorce his wife for any and every reason?"
matthew:19:3.csb: Some Pharisees approached him to test him. They asked, “Is it lawful for a man to divorce his wife on any grounds?”
matthew:19:3.nrsv: Some Pharisees came to him, and to test him they asked, "Is it lawful for a man to divorce his wife for any cause?"

matthew:19:4.niv: "Haven't you read," he replied, "that at the beginning the Creator 'made them male and female,'
matthew:19:4.csb: “Haven’t you read,” he replied, “that he who created them in the beginning made them male and female,”
matthew:19:4.nrsv: He answered, "Have you not read that the one who made them at the beginning 'made them male and female,'

matthew:19:5.niv: and said, 'For this reason a man will leave his father and mother and be united to his wife, and the two will become one flesh'?
matthew:19:5.csb: and he also said, “For this reason a man will leave his father and mother and be joined to his wife, and the two will become one flesh?
matthew:19:5.nrsv: and said, 'For this reason a man shall leave his father and mother and be joined to his wife, and the two shall become one flesh'?

matthew:19:6.niv: So they are no longer two, but one. Therefore what God has joined together, let man not separate."
matthew:19:6.csb: So they are no longer two, but one flesh. Therefore, what God has joined together, let no one separate.”
matthew:19:6.nrsv: So they are no longer two, but one flesh. Therefore what God has joined together, let no one separate."

matthew:19:7.niv: "Why then," they asked, "did Moses command that a man give his wife a certificate of divorce and send her away?"
matthew:19:7.csb: “Why then,” they asked him, “did Moses command us to give divorce papers and to send her away?”
matthew:19:7.nrsv: They said to him, "Why then did Moses command us to give a certificate of dismissal and to divorce her?"

matthew:19:8.niv: Jesus replied, "Moses permitted you to divorce your wives because your hearts were hard. But it was not this way from the beginning.
matthew:19:8.csb: He told them, “Moses permitted you to divorce your wives because of the hardness of your hearts, but it was not like that from the beginning.
matthew:19:8.nrsv: He said to them, "It was because you were so hard-hearted that Moses allowed you to divorce your wives, but from the beginning it was not so.

matthew:19:9.niv: I tell you that anyone who divorces his wife, except for marital unfaithfulness, and marries another woman commits adultery."
matthew:19:9.csb: I tell you, whoever divorces his wife, except for sexual immorality, and marries another commits adultery.”
matthew:19:9.nrsv: And I say to you, whoever divorces his wife, except for unchastity, and marries another commits adultery."

matthew:19:10.niv: The disciples said to him, "If this is the situation between a husband and wife, it is better not to marry."
matthew:19:10.csb: His disciples said to him, “If the relationship of a man with his wife is like this, it’s better not to marry.”
matthew:19:10.nrsv: His disciples said to him, "If such is the case of a man with his wife, it is better not to marry."

matthew:19:11.niv: Jesus replied, "Not everyone can accept this word, but only those to whom it has been given.
matthew:19:11.csb: He responded, “Not everyone can accept this saying, but only those to whom it has been given.
matthew:19:11.nrsv: But he said to them, "Not everyone can accept this teaching, but only those to whom it is given.

matthew:19:12.niv: For some are eunuchs because they were born that way; others were made that way by men; and others have renounced marriage because of the kingdom of heaven. The one who can accept this should accept it."
matthew:19:12.csb: For there are eunuchs who were born that way from their mother’s womb, there are eunuchs who were made by men, and there are eunuchs who have made themselves that way because of the kingdom of heaven. The one who is able to accept it should accept it.”
matthew:19:12.nrsv: For there are eunuchs who have been so from birth, and there are eunuchs who have been made eunuchs by others, and there are eunuchs who have made themselves eunuchs for the sake of the kingdom of heaven. Let anyone accept this who can."

matthew:19:13.niv: Then little children were brought to Jesus for him to place his hands on them and pray for them. But the disciples rebuked those who brought them.
matthew:19:13.csb: Then children were brought to Jesus for him to place his hands on them and pray, but the disciples rebuked them.
matthew:19:13.nrsv: Then little children were being brought to him in order that he might lay his hands on them and pray. The disciples spoke sternly to those who brought them;

matthew:19:14.niv: Jesus said, "Let the little children come to me, and do not hinder them, for the kingdom of heaven belongs to such as these."
matthew:19:14.csb: Jesus said, “Leave the children alone, and don’t try to keep them from coming to me, because the kingdom of heaven belongs to such as these.”
matthew:19:14.nrsv: but Jesus said, "Let the little children come to me, and do not stop them; for it is to such as these that the kingdom of heaven belongs."

matthew:19:15.niv: When he had placed his hands on them, he went on from there.
matthew:19:15.csb: After placing his hands on them, he went on from there.
matthew:19:15.nrsv: And he laid his hands on them and went on his way.

matthew:19:16.niv: Now a man came up to Jesus and asked, "Teacher, what good thing must I do to get eternal life?"
matthew:19:16.csb: Just then someone came up and asked him, “Teacher, what good must I do to have eternal life?”
matthew:19:16.nrsv: Then someone came to him and said, "Teacher, what good deed must I do to have eternal life?"

matthew:19:17.niv: "Why do you ask me about what is good?" Jesus replied. "There is only One who is good. If you want to enter life, obey the commandments."
matthew:19:17.csb: “Why do you ask me about what is good?” he said to him. “There is only one who is good. If you want to enter into life, keep the commandments.”
matthew:19:17.nrsv: And he said to him, "Why do you ask me about what is good? There is only one who is good. If you wish to enter into life, keep the commandments."

matthew:19:18.csb: “Which ones?” he asked him. Jesus answered: Do not murder; do not commit adultery; do not steal; do not bear false witness;
matthew:19:18.nrsv: He said to him, "Which ones?" And Jesus said, "You shall not murder; You shall not commit adultery; You shall not steal; You shall not bear false witness;

matthew:19:19.niv: Jesus replied, "'Do not murder, do not commit adultery, do not steal, do not give false testimony, honor your father and mother,' and 'love your neighbor as yourself.'"
matthew:19:19.csb: honor your father and your mother; and love your neighbor as yourself.
matthew:19:19.nrsv: Honor your father and mother; also, You shall love your neighbor as yourself."

matthew:19:20.niv: "All these I have kept," the young man said. "What do I still lack?"
matthew:19:20.csb: “I have kept all these,” the young man told him. “What do I still lack?”
matthew:19:20.nrsv: The young man said to him, "I have kept all these; what do I still lack?"

matthew:19:21.niv: Jesus answered, "If you want to be perfect, go, sell your possessions and give to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven. Then come, follow me."
matthew:19:21.csb: “If you want to be perfect,” Jesus said to him, “go, sell your belongings and give to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven. Then come, follow me.”
matthew:19:21.nrsv: Jesus said to him, "If you wish to be perfect, go, sell your possessions, and give the money to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven; then come, follow me."

matthew:19:22.niv: When the young man heard this, he went away sad, because he had great wealth.
matthew:19:22.csb: When the young man heard that, he went away grieving, because he had many possessions.
matthew:19:22.nrsv: When the young man heard this word, he went away grieving, for he had many possessions.

matthew:19:23.niv: Then Jesus said to his disciples, "I tell you the truth, it is hard for a rich man to enter the kingdom of heaven.
matthew:19:23.csb: Jesus said to his disciples, “Truly I tell you, it will be hard for a rich person to enter the kingdom of heaven.
matthew:19:23.nrsv: Then Jesus said to his disciples, "Truly I tell you, it will be hard for a rich person to enter the kingdom of heaven.

matthew:19:24.niv: Again I tell you, it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich man to enter the kingdom of God."
matthew:19:24.csb: Again I tell you, it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter the kingdom of God.”
matthew:19:24.nrsv: Again I tell you, it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for someone who is rich to enter the kingdom of God."

matthew:19:25.niv: When the disciples heard this, they were greatly astonished and asked, "Who then can be saved?"
matthew:19:25.csb: When the disciples heard this, they were utterly astonished and asked, “Then who can be saved?”
matthew:19:25.nrsv: When the disciples heard this, they were greatly astounded and said, "Then who can be saved?"

matthew:19:26.niv: Jesus looked at them and said, "With man this is impossible, but with God all things are possible."
matthew:19:26.csb: Jesus looked at them and said, “With man this is impossible, but with God all things are possible.”
matthew:19:26.nrsv: But Jesus looked at them and said, "For mortals it is impossible, but for God all things are possible."

matthew:19:27.niv: Peter answered him, "We have left everything to follow you! What then will there be for us?"
matthew:19:27.csb: Then Peter responded to him, “See, we have left everything and followed you. So what will there be for us?”
matthew:19:27.nrsv: Then Peter said in reply, "Look, we have left everything and followed you. What then will we have?"

matthew:19:28.niv: Jesus said to them, "I tell you the truth, at the renewal of all things, when the Son of Man sits on his glorious throne, you who have followed me will also sit on twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
matthew:19:28.csb: Jesus said to them, “Truly I tell you, in the renewal of all things, when the Son of Man sits on his glorious throne, you who have followed me will also sit on twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
matthew:19:28.nrsv: Jesus said to them, "Truly I tell you, at the renewal of all things, when the Son of Man is seated on the throne of his glory, you who have followed me will also sit on twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.

matthew:19:29.niv: And everyone who has left houses or brothers or sisters or father or mother or children or fields for my sake will receive a hundred times as much and will inherit eternal life.
matthew:19:29.csb: And everyone who has left houses or brothers or sisters or father or mother or children or fields because of my name will receive a hundred times more and will inherit eternal life.
matthew:19:29.nrsv: And everyone who has left houses or brothers or sisters or father or mother or children or fields, for my name's sake, will receive a hundredfold, and will inherit eternal life.

matthew:19:30.niv: But many who are first will be last, and many who are last will be first.
matthew:19:30.csb: But many who are first will be last, and the last first.
matthew:19:30.nrsv: But many who are first will be last, and the last will be first.

matthew:20:1.niv: "For the kingdom of heaven is like a landowner who went out early in the morning to hire men to work in his vineyard.
matthew:20:1.csb: “For the kingdom of heaven is like a landowner who went out early in the morning to hire workers for his vineyard.
matthew:20:1.nrsv: "For the kingdom of heaven is like a landowner who went out early in the morning to hire laborers for his vineyard.

matthew:20:2.niv: He agreed to pay them a denarius for the day and sent them into his vineyard.
matthew:20:2.csb: After agreeing with the workers on one denarius, he sent them into his vineyard for the day.
matthew:20:2.nrsv: After agreeing with the laborers for the usual daily wage, he sent them into his vineyard.

matthew:20:3.niv: "About the third hour he went out and saw others standing in the marketplace doing nothing.
matthew:20:3.csb: When he went out about nine in the morning, he saw others standing in the marketplace doing nothing.
matthew:20:3.nrsv: When he went out about nine o'clock, he saw others standing idle in the marketplace;

matthew:20:4.niv: He told them, 'You also go and work in my vineyard, and I will pay you whatever is right.'
matthew:20:4.csb: He said to them, ‘You also go into my vineyard, and I’ll give you whatever is right.’ So off they went.
matthew:20:4.nrsv: and he said to them, 'You also go into the vineyard, and I will pay you whatever is right.' So they went.

matthew:20:5.csb: About noon and about three, he went out again and did the same thing.
matthew:20:5.nrsv: When he went out again about noon and about three o'clock, he did the same.

matthew:20:6.niv: "He went out again about the sixth hour and the ninth hour and did the same thing. About the eleventh hour he went out and found still others standing around. He asked them, 'Why have you been standing here all day long doing nothing?'
matthew:20:6.csb: Then about five he went and found others standing around and said to them, ‘Why have you been standing here all day doing nothing?’
matthew:20:6.nrsv: And about five o'clock he went out and found others standing around; and he said to them, 'Why are you standing here idle all day?'

matthew:20:7.niv: "'Because no one has hired us,' they answered. "He said to them, 'You also go and work in my vineyard.'
matthew:20:7.csb: “‘Because no one hired us,’ they said to him. “‘You also go into my vineyard,’ he told them.
matthew:20:7.nrsv: They said to him, 'Because no one has hired us.' He said to them, 'You also go into the vineyard.'

matthew:20:8.niv: "When evening came, the owner of the vineyard said to his foreman, 'Call the workers and pay them their wages, beginning with the last ones hired and going on to the first.'
matthew:20:8.csb: When evening came, the owner of the vineyard told his foreman, ‘Call the workers and give them their pay, starting with the last and ending with the first.’
matthew:20:8.nrsv: When evening came, the owner of the vineyard said to his manager, 'Call the laborers and give them their pay, beginning with the last and then going to the first.'

matthew:20:9.niv: "The workers who were hired about the eleventh hour came and each received a denarius.
matthew:20:9.csb: “When those who were hired about five came, they each received one denarius.
matthew:20:9.nrsv: When those hired about five o'clock came, each of them received the usual daily wage.

matthew:20:10.niv: So when those came who were hired first, they expected to receive more. But each one of them also received a denarius.
matthew:20:10.csb: So when the first ones came, they assumed they would get more, but they also received a denarius each.
matthew:20:10.nrsv: Now when the first came, they thought they would receive more; but each of them also received the usual daily wage.

matthew:20:11.niv: When they received it, they began to grumble against the landowner.
matthew:20:11.csb: When they received it, they began to complain to the landowner:
matthew:20:11.nrsv: And when they received it, they grumbled against the landowner,

matthew:20:12.niv: 'These men who were hired last worked only one hour,' they said, 'and you have made them equal to us who have borne the burden of the work and the heat of the day.'
matthew:20:12.csb: ‘These last men put in one hour, and you made them equal to us who bore the burden of the day’s work and the burning heat.’
matthew:20:12.nrsv: saying, 'These last worked only one hour, and you have made them equal to us who have borne the burden of the day and the scorching heat.'

matthew:20:13.niv: "But he answered one of them, 'Friend, I am not being unfair to you. Didn't you agree to work for a denarius?
matthew:20:13.csb: “He replied to one of them, ‘Friend, I’m doing you no wrong. Didn’t you agree with me on a denarius?
matthew:20:13.nrsv: But he replied to one of them, 'Friend, I am doing you no wrong; did you not agree with me for the usual daily wage?

matthew:20:14.niv: Take your pay and go. I want to give the man who was hired last the same as I gave you.
matthew:20:14.csb: Take what’s yours and go. I want to give this last man the same as I gave you.
matthew:20:14.nrsv: Take what belongs to you and go; I choose to give to this last the same as I give to you.

matthew:20:15.niv: Don't I have the right to do what I want with my own money? Or are you envious because I am generous?'
matthew:20:15.csb: Don’t I have the right to do what I want with what is mine? Are you jealous because I’m generous?’
matthew:20:15.nrsv: Am I not allowed to do what I choose with what belongs to me? Or are you envious because I am generous?'

matthew:20:16.niv: "So the last will be first, and the first will be last."
matthew:20:16.csb: “So the last will be first, and the first last.”
matthew:20:16.nrsv: So the last will be first, and the first will be last."

matthew:20:17.niv: Now as Jesus was going up to Jerusalem, he took the twelve disciples aside and said to them,
matthew:20:17.csb: While going up to Jerusalem, Jesus took the twelve disciples aside privately and said to them on the way,
matthew:20:17.nrsv: While Jesus was going up to Jerusalem, he took the twelve disciples aside by themselves, and said to them on the way,

matthew:20:18.niv: "We are going up to Jerusalem, and the Son of Man will be betrayed to the chief priests and the teachers of the law. They will condemn him to death
matthew:20:18.csb: “See, we are going up to Jerusalem. The Son of Man will be handed over to the chief priests and scribes, and they will condemn him to death.
matthew:20:18.nrsv: "See, we are going up to Jerusalem, and the Son of Man will be handed over to the chief priests and scribes, and they will condemn him to death;

matthew:20:19.niv: and will turn him over to the Gentiles to be mocked and flogged and crucified. On the third day he will be raised to life!"
matthew:20:19.csb: They will hand him over to the Gentiles to be mocked, flogged, and crucified, and on the third day he will be raised.”
matthew:20:19.nrsv: then they will hand him over to the Gentiles to be mocked and flogged and crucified; and on the third day he will be raised."

matthew:20:20.niv: Then the mother of Zebedee's sons came to Jesus with her sons and, kneeling down, asked a favor of him.
matthew:20:20.csb: Then the mother of Zebedee’s sons approached him with her sons. She knelt down to ask him for something.
matthew:20:20.nrsv: Then the mother of the sons of Zebedee came to him with her sons, and kneeling before him, she asked a favor of him.

matthew:20:21.niv: "What is it you want?" he asked. She said, "Grant that one of these two sons of mine may sit at your right and the other at your left in your kingdom."
matthew:20:21.csb: “What do you want?” he asked her. “Promise,” she said to him, “that these two sons of mine may sit, one on your right and the other on your left, in your kingdom.”
matthew:20:21.nrsv: And he said to her, "What do you want?" She said to him, "Declare that these two sons of mine will sit, one at your right hand and one at your left, in your kingdom."

matthew:20:22.niv: "You don't know what you are asking," Jesus said to them. "Can you drink the cup I am going to drink?We can," they answered.
matthew:20:22.csb: Jesus answered, “You don’t know what you’re asking. Are you able to drink the cup that I am about to drink?” “We are able,” they said to him.
matthew:20:22.nrsv: But Jesus answered, "You do not know what you are asking. Are you able to drink the cup that I am about to drink?" They said to him, "We are able."

matthew:20:23.niv: Jesus said to them, "You will indeed drink from my cup, but to sit at my right or left is not for me to grant. These places belong to those for whom they have been prepared by my Father."
matthew:20:23.csb: He told them, “You will indeed drink my cup, but to sit at my right and left is not mine to give; instead, it is for those for whom it has been prepared by my Father.”
matthew:20:23.nrsv: He said to them, "You will indeed drink my cup, but to sit at my right hand and at my left, this is not mine to grant, but it is for those for whom it has been prepared by my Father."

matthew:20:24.niv: When the ten heard about this, they were indignant with the two brothers.
matthew:20:24.csb: When the ten disciples heard this, they became indignant with the two brothers.
matthew:20:24.nrsv: When the ten heard it, they were angry with the two brothers.

matthew:20:25.niv: Jesus called them together and said, "You know that the rulers of the Gentiles lord it over them, and their high officials exercise authority over them.
matthew:20:25.csb: Jesus called them over and said, “You know that the rulers of the Gentiles lord it over them, and those in high positions act as tyrants over them.
matthew:20:25.nrsv: But Jesus called them to him and said, "You know that the rulers of the Gentiles lord it over them, and their great ones are tyrants over them.

matthew:20:26.niv: Not so with you. Instead, whoever wants to become great among you must be your servant,
matthew:20:26.csb: It must not be like that among you. On the contrary, whoever wants to become great among you must be your servant,
matthew:20:26.nrsv: It will not be so among you; but whoever wishes to be great among you must be your servant,

matthew:20:27.niv: and whoever wants to be first must be your slave--
matthew:20:27.csb: and whoever wants to be first among you must be your slave;
matthew:20:27.nrsv: and whoever wishes to be first among you must be your slave;

matthew:20:28.niv: just as the Son of Man did not come to be served, but to serve, and to give his life as a ransom for many."
matthew:20:28.csb: just as the Son of Man did not come to be served, but to serve, and to give his life as a ransom for many.”
matthew:20:28.nrsv: just as the Son of Man came not to be served but to serve, and to give his life a ransom for many."

matthew:20:29.niv: As Jesus and his disciples were leaving Jericho, a large crowd followed him.
matthew:20:29.csb: As they were leaving Jericho, a large crowd followed him.
matthew:20:29.nrsv: As they were leaving Jericho, a large crowd followed him.

matthew:20:30.niv: Two blind men were sitting by the roadside, and when they heard that Jesus was going by, they shouted, "Lord, Son of David, have mercy on us!"
matthew:20:30.csb: There were two blind men sitting by the road. When they heard that Jesus was passing by, they cried out, “Lord, have mercy on us, Son of David!”
matthew:20:30.nrsv: There were two blind men sitting by the roadside. When they heard that Jesus was passing by, they shouted, "Lord, have mercy on us, Son of David!"

matthew:20:31.niv: The crowd rebuked them and told them to be quiet, but they shouted all the louder, "Lord, Son of David, have mercy on us!"
matthew:20:31.csb: The crowd demanded that they keep quiet, but they cried out all the more, “Lord, have mercy on us, Son of David!”
matthew:20:31.nrsv: The crowd sternly ordered them to be quiet; but they shouted even more loudly, "Have mercy on us, Lord, Son of David!"

matthew:20:32.niv: Jesus stopped and called them. "What do you want me to do for you?" he asked.
matthew:20:32.csb: Jesus stopped, called them, and said, “What do you want me to do for you?”
matthew:20:32.nrsv: Jesus stood still and called them, saying, "What do you want me to do for you?"

matthew:20:33.csb: “Lord,” they said to him, “open our eyes.”
matthew:20:33.nrsv: They said to him, "Lord, let our eyes be opened."

matthew:20:34.niv: Jesus had compassion on them and touched their eyes. Immediately they received their sight and followed him.
matthew:20:34.csb: Moved with compassion, Jesus touched their eyes. Immediately they could see, and they followed him.
matthew:20:34.nrsv: Moved with compassion, Jesus touched their eyes. Immediately they regained their sight and followed him.

matthew:21:1.niv: As they approached Jerusalem and came to Bethphage on the Mount of Olives, Jesus sent two disciples,
matthew:21:1.csb: When they approached Jerusalem and came to Bethphage at the Mount of Olives, Jesus then sent two disciples,
matthew:21:1.nrsv: When they had come near Jerusalem and had reached Bethphage, at the Mount of Olives, Jesus sent two disciples,

matthew:21:2.niv: saying to them, "Go to the village ahead of you, and at once you will find a donkey tied there, with her colt by her. Untie them and bring them to me.
matthew:21:2.csb: telling them, “Go into the village ahead of you. At once you will find a donkey tied there with her foal. Untie them and bring them to me.
matthew:21:2.nrsv: saying to them, "Go into the village ahead of you, and immediately you will find a donkey tied, and a colt with her; untie them and bring them to me.

matthew:21:3.niv: If anyone says anything to you, tell him that the Lord needs them, and he will send them right away."
matthew:21:3.csb: If anyone says anything to you, say that the Lord needs them, and he will send them at once.”
matthew:21:3.nrsv: If anyone says anything to you, just say this, 'The Lord needs them.' And he will send them immediately. "

matthew:21:4.niv: This took place to fulfill what was spoken through the prophet:
matthew:21:4.csb: This took place so that what was spoken through the prophet might be fulfilled:
matthew:21:4.nrsv: This took place to fulfill what had been spoken through the prophet, saying,

matthew:21:5.niv: "Say to the Daughter of Zion, 'See, your king comes to you, gentle and riding on a donkey, on a colt, the foal of a donkey.'"
matthew:21:5.csb: Tell Daughter Zion, “See, your King is coming to you, gentle, and mounted on a donkey, and on a colt, the foal of a donkey.”
matthew:21:5.nrsv: "Tell the daughter of Zion, Look, your king is coming to you, humble, and mounted on a donkey, and on a colt, the foal of a donkey."

matthew:21:6.niv: The disciples went and did as Jesus had instructed them.
matthew:21:6.csb: The disciples went and did just as Jesus directed them.
matthew:21:6.nrsv: The disciples went and did as Jesus had directed them;

matthew:21:7.niv: They brought the donkey and the colt, placed their cloaks on them, and Jesus sat on them.
matthew:21:7.csb: They brought the donkey and its foal; then they laid their clothes on them, and he sat on them.
matthew:21:7.nrsv: they brought the donkey and the colt, and put their cloaks on them, and he sat on them.

matthew:21:8.niv: A very large crowd spread their cloaks on the road, while others cut branches from the trees and spread them on the road.
matthew:21:8.csb: A very large crowd spread their clothes on the road; others were cutting branches from the trees and spreading them on the road.
matthew:21:8.nrsv: A very large crowd spread their cloaks on the road, and others cut branches from the trees and spread them on the road.

matthew:21:9.niv: The crowds that went ahead of him and those that followed shouted, "Hosanna to the Son of David!Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord!Hosanna in the highest!"
matthew:21:9.csb: Then the crowds who went ahead of him and those who followed shouted: Hosanna to the Son of David! Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord! Hosanna in the highest heaven!
matthew:21:9.nrsv: The crowds that went ahead of him and that followed were shouting, "Hosanna to the Son of David! Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord! Hosanna in the highest heaven!"

matthew:21:10.niv: When Jesus entered Jerusalem, the whole city was stirred and asked, "Who is this?"
matthew:21:10.csb: When he entered Jerusalem, the whole city was in an uproar, saying, “Who is this?”
matthew:21:10.nrsv: When he entered Jerusalem, the whole city was in turmoil, asking, "Who is this?"

matthew:21:11.niv: The crowds answered, "This is Jesus, the prophet from Nazareth in Galilee."
matthew:21:11.csb: The crowds were saying, “This is the prophet Jesus from Nazareth in Galilee.”
matthew:21:11.nrsv: The crowds were saying, "This is the prophet Jesus from Nazareth in Galilee."

matthew:21:12.niv: Jesus entered the temple area and drove out all who were buying and selling there. He overturned the tables of the money changers and the benches of those selling doves.
matthew:21:12.csb: Jesus went into the temple and threw out all those buying and selling. He overturned the tables of the money changers and the chairs of those selling doves.
matthew:21:12.nrsv: Then Jesus entered the temple and drove out all who were selling and buying in the temple, and he overturned the tables of the money changers and the seats of those who sold doves.

matthew:21:13.niv: "It is written," he said to them, "'My house will be called a house of prayer,' but you are making it a 'den of robbers.'"
matthew:21:13.csb: He said to them, “It is written, my house will be called a house of prayer, but you are making it a den of thieves!”
matthew:21:13.nrsv: He said to them, "It is written, 'My house shall be called a house of prayer'; but you are making it a den of robbers."

matthew:21:14.niv: The blind and the lame came to him at the temple, and he healed them.
matthew:21:14.csb: The blind and the lame came to him in the temple, and he healed them.
matthew:21:14.nrsv: The blind and the lame came to him in the temple, and he cured them.

matthew:21:15.niv: But when the chief priests and the teachers of the law saw the wonderful things he did and the children shouting in the temple area, "Hosanna to the Son of David," they were indignant.
matthew:21:15.csb: When the chief priests and the scribes saw the wonders that he did and the children shouting in the temple,” Hosanna to the Son of David!” they were indignant
matthew:21:15.nrsv: But when the chief priests and the scribes saw the amazing things that he did, and heard the children crying out in the temple, "Hosanna to the Son of David," they became angry

matthew:21:16.niv: "Do you hear what these children are saying?" they asked him. "Yes," replied Jesus, "have you never read, "'From the lips of children and infants you have ordained praise'?"
matthew:21:16.csb: and said to him, “Do you hear what these children are saying?” Jesus replied, “Yes, have you never read: You have prepared praise from the mouths of infants and nursing babies?”
matthew:21:16.nrsv: and said to him, "Do you hear what these are saying?" Jesus said to them, "Yes; have you never read, 'Out of the mouths of infants and nursing babies you have prepared praise for yourself'?"

matthew:21:17.niv: And he left them and went out of the city to Bethany, where he spent the night.
matthew:21:17.csb: Then he left them, went out of the city to Bethany, and spent the night there.
matthew:21:17.nrsv: He left them, went out of the city to Bethany, and spent the night there.

matthew:21:18.niv: Early in the morning, as he was on his way back to the city, he was hungry.
matthew:21:18.csb: Early in the morning, as he was returning to the city, he was hungry.
matthew:21:18.nrsv: In the morning, when he returned to the city, he was hungry.

matthew:21:19.niv: Seeing a fig tree by the road, he went up to it but found nothing on it except leaves. Then he said to it, "May you never bear fruit again!" Immediately the tree withered.
matthew:21:19.csb: Seeing a lone fig tree by the road, he went up to it and found nothing on it except leaves. And he said to it, “May no fruit ever come from you again!” At once the fig tree withered.
matthew:21:19.nrsv: And seeing a fig tree by the side of the road, he went to it and found nothing at all on it but leaves. Then he said to it, "May no fruit ever come from you again!" And the fig tree withered at once.

matthew:21:20.niv: When the disciples saw this, they were amazed. "How did the fig tree wither so quickly?" they asked.
matthew:21:20.csb: When the disciples saw it, they were amazed and said, “How did the fig tree wither so quickly?”
matthew:21:20.nrsv: When the disciples saw it, they were amazed, saying, "How did the fig tree wither at once?"

matthew:21:21.niv: Jesus replied, "I tell you the truth, if you have faith and do not doubt, not only can you do what was done to the fig tree, but also you can say to this mountain, 'Go, throw yourself into the sea,' and it will be done.
matthew:21:21.csb: Jesus answered them, “Truly I tell you, if you have faith and do not doubt, you will not only do what was done to the fig tree, but even if you tell this mountain, ‘Be lifted up and thrown into the sea,’ it will be done.
matthew:21:21.nrsv: Jesus answered them, "Truly I tell you, if you have faith and do not doubt, not only will you do what has been done to the fig tree, but even if you say to this mountain, 'Be lifted up and thrown into the sea,' it will be done.

matthew:21:22.niv: If you believe, you will receive whatever you ask for in prayer."
matthew:21:22.csb: And if you believe, you will receive whatever you ask for in prayer.”
matthew:21:22.nrsv: Whatever you ask for in prayer with faith, you will receive."

matthew:21:23.niv: Jesus entered the temple courts, and, while he was teaching, the chief priests and the elders of the people came to him. "By what authority are you doing these things?" they asked. "And who gave you this authority?"
matthew:21:23.csb: When he entered the temple, the chief priests and the elders of the people came to him as he was teaching and said, “By what authority are you doing these things? Who gave you this authority?”
matthew:21:23.nrsv: When he entered the temple, the chief priests and the elders of the people came to him as he was teaching, and said, "By what authority are you doing these things, and who gave you this authority?"

matthew:21:24.niv: Jesus replied, "I will also ask you one question. If you answer me, I will tell you by what authority I am doing these things.
matthew:21:24.csb: Jesus answered them, “I will also ask you one question, and if you answer it for me, then I will tell you by what authority I do these things.
matthew:21:24.nrsv: Jesus said to them, "I will also ask you one question; if you tell me the answer, then I will also tell you by what authority I do these things.

matthew:21:25.niv: John's baptism--where did it come from? Was it from heaven, or from men?"
matthew:21:25.csb: Did John’s baptism come from heaven, or was it of human origin?” They discussed it among themselves, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ he will say to us, ‘Then why didn’t you believe him? ’
matthew:21:25.nrsv: Did the baptism of John come from heaven, or was it of human origin?" And they argued with one another, "If we say, 'From heaven,' he will say to us, 'Why then did you not believe him?'

matthew:21:26.niv: They discussed it among themselves and said, "If we say, 'From heaven,' he will ask, 'Then why didn't you believe him?' But if we say, 'From men'--we are afraid of the people, for they all hold that John was a prophet."
matthew:21:26.csb: But if we say, ‘Of human origin,’ we’re afraid of the crowd, because everyone considers John to be a prophet.”
matthew:21:26.nrsv: But if we say, 'Of human origin,' we are afraid of the crowd; for all regard John as a prophet."

matthew:21:27.niv: So they answered Jesus, "We don't know." Then he said, "Neither will I tell you by what authority I am doing these things.
matthew:21:27.csb: So they answered Jesus, “We don’t know.” And he said to them, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I do these things.
matthew:21:27.nrsv: So they answered Jesus, "We do not know." And he said to them, "Neither will I tell you by what authority I am doing these things.

matthew:21:28.niv: "What do you think? There was a man who had two sons. He went to the first and said, 'Son, go and work today in the vineyard.'
matthew:21:28.csb: “What do you think? A man had two sons. He went to the first and said, ‘My son, go work in the vineyard today.’
matthew:21:28.nrsv: "What do you think? A man had two sons; he went to the first and said, 'Son, go and work in the vineyard today.'

matthew:21:29.niv: "'I will not,' he answered, but later he changed his mind and went.
matthew:21:29.csb: “He answered, ‘I don’t want to’ but later he changed his mind and went.
matthew:21:29.nrsv: He answered, 'I will not'; but later he changed his mind and went.

matthew:21:30.niv: "Then the father went to the other son and said the same thing. He answered, 'I will, sir,' but he did not go.
matthew:21:30.csb: Then the man went to the other and said the same thing. ‘I will, sir,’ he answered, but he didn’t go.
matthew:21:30.nrsv: The father went to the second and said the same; and he answered, 'I go, sir'; but he did not go.

matthew:21:31.niv: "Which of the two did what his father wanted?The first," they answered.
matthew:21:31.csb: Which of the two did his father’s will?” They said, “The first.” Jesus said to them, “Truly I tell you, tax collectors and prostitutes are entering the kingdom of God before you.
matthew:21:31.nrsv: Which of the two did the will of his father?" They said, "The first." Jesus said to them, "Truly I tell you, the tax collectors and the prostitutes are going into the kingdom of God ahead of you.

matthew:21:32.niv: Jesus said to them, "I tell you the truth, the tax collectors and the prostitutes are entering the kingdom of God ahead of you. For John came to you to show you the way of righteousness, and you did not believe him, but the tax collectors and the prostitutes did. And even after you saw this, you did not repent and believe him.
matthew:21:32.csb: For John came to you in the way of righteousness, and you didn’t believe him. Tax collectors and prostitutes did believe him; but you, when you saw it, didn’t even change your minds then and believe him.
matthew:21:32.nrsv: For John came to you in the way of righteousness and you did not believe him, but the tax collectors and the prostitutes believed him; and even after you saw it, you did not change your minds and believe him.

matthew:21:33.niv: "Listen to another parable: There was a landowner who planted a vineyard. He put a wall around it, dug a winepress in it and built a watchtower. Then he rented the vineyard to some farmers and went away on a journey.
matthew:21:33.csb: “Listen to another parable: There was a landowner, who planted a vineyard, put a fence around it, dug a winepress in it, and built a watchtower. He leased it to tenant farmers and went away.
matthew:21:33.nrsv: "Listen to another parable. There was a landowner who planted a vineyard, put a fence around it, dug a wine press in it, and built a watchtower. Then he leased it to tenants and went to another country.

matthew:21:34.niv: When the harvest time approached, he sent his servants to the tenants to collect his fruit.
matthew:21:34.csb: When the time came to harvest fruit, he sent his servants to the farmers to collect his fruit.
matthew:21:34.nrsv: When the harvest time had come, he sent his slaves to the tenants to collect his produce.

matthew:21:35.niv: "The tenants seized his servants; they beat one, killed another, and stoned a third.
matthew:21:35.csb: The farmers took his servants, beat one, killed another, and stoned a third.
matthew:21:35.nrsv: But the tenants seized his slaves and beat one, killed another, and stoned another.

matthew:21:36.niv: Then he sent other servants to them, more than the first time, and the tenants treated them the same way.
matthew:21:36.csb: Again, he sent other servants, more than the first group, and they did the same to them.
matthew:21:36.nrsv: Again he sent other slaves, more than the first; and they treated them in the same way.

matthew:21:37.niv: Last of all, he sent his son to them. 'They will respect my son,' he said.
matthew:21:37.csb: Finally, he sent his son to them. ‘They will respect my son,’ he said.
matthew:21:37.nrsv: Finally he sent his son to them, saying, 'They will respect my son.'

matthew:21:38.niv: "But when the tenants saw the son, they said to each other, 'This is the heir. Come, let's kill him and take his inheritance.'
matthew:21:38.csb: “But when the tenant farmers saw the son, they said to each other, ‘This is the heir. Come, let’s kill him and take his inheritance.’
matthew:21:38.nrsv: But when the tenants saw the son, they said to themselves, 'This is the heir; come, let us kill him and get his inheritance.'

matthew:21:39.niv: So they took him and threw him out of the vineyard and killed him.
matthew:21:39.csb: So they seized him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
matthew:21:39.nrsv: So they seized him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.

matthew:21:40.niv: "Therefore, when the owner of the vineyard comes, what will he do to those tenants?"
matthew:21:40.csb: Therefore, when the owner of the vineyard comes, what will he do to those farmers?”
matthew:21:40.nrsv: Now when the owner of the vineyard comes, what will he do to those tenants?"

matthew:21:41.niv: "He will bring those wretches to a wretched end," they replied, "and he will rent the vineyard to other tenants, who will give him his share of the crop at harvest time."
matthew:21:41.csb: “He will completely destroy those terrible men,” they told him, “and lease his vineyard to other farmers who will give him his fruit at the harvest.”
matthew:21:41.nrsv: They said to him, "He will put those wretches to a miserable death, and lease the vineyard to other tenants who will give him the produce at the harvest time."

matthew:21:42.niv: Jesus said to them, "Have you never read in the Scriptures: "'The stone the builders rejected has become the capstone; the Lord has done this, and it is marvelous in our eyes'?
matthew:21:42.csb: Jesus said to them, “Have you never read in the Scriptures: The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone. This is what the Lord has done and it is wonderful in our eyes?
matthew:21:42.nrsv: Jesus said to them, "Have you never read in the scriptures: 'The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone; this was the Lord's doing, and it is amazing in our eyes'?

matthew:21:43.niv: "Therefore I tell you that the kingdom of God will be taken away from you and given to a people who will produce its fruit.
matthew:21:43.csb: Therefore I tell you, the kingdom of God will be taken away from you and given to a people producing its fruit.
matthew:21:43.nrsv: Therefore I tell you, the kingdom of God will be taken away from you and given to a people that produces the fruits of the kingdom.

matthew:21:44.niv: He who falls on this stone will be broken to pieces, but he on whom it falls will be crushed."
matthew:21:44.csb: Whoever falls on this stone will be broken to pieces; but on whomever it falls, it will shatter him.”
matthew:21:44.nrsv: The one who falls on this stone will be broken to pieces; and it will crush anyone on whom it falls."

matthew:21:45.niv: When the chief priests and the Pharisees heard Jesus' parables, they knew he was talking about them.
matthew:21:45.csb: When the chief priests and the Pharisees heard his parables, they knew he was speaking about them.
matthew:21:45.nrsv: When the chief priests and the Pharisees heard his parables, they realized that he was speaking about them.

matthew:21:46.niv: They looked for a way to arrest him, but they were afraid of the crowd because the people held that he was a prophet.
matthew:21:46.csb: Although they were looking for a way to arrest him, they feared the crowds, because the people regarded him as a prophet.
matthew:21:46.nrsv: They wanted to arrest him, but they feared the crowds, because they regarded him as a prophet.

matthew:22:1.niv: Jesus spoke to them again in parables, saying:
matthew:22:1.csb: Once more Jesus spoke to them in parables:
matthew:22:1.nrsv: Once more Jesus spoke to them in parables, saying:

matthew:22:2.niv: "The kingdom of heaven is like a king who prepared a wedding banquet for his son.
matthew:22:2.csb: “The kingdom of heaven is like a king who gave a wedding banquet for his son.
matthew:22:2.nrsv: "The kingdom of heaven may be compared to a king who gave a wedding banquet for his son.

matthew:22:3.niv: He sent his servants to those who had been invited to the banquet to tell them to come, but they refused to come.
matthew:22:3.csb: He sent his servants to summon those invited to the banquet, but they didn’t want to come.
matthew:22:3.nrsv: He sent his slaves to call those who had been invited to the wedding banquet, but they would not come.

matthew:22:4.niv: "Then he sent some more servants and said, 'Tell those who have been invited that I have prepared my dinner: My oxen and fattened cattle have been butchered, and everything is ready. Come to the wedding banquet.'
matthew:22:4.csb: Again, he sent out other servants and said, ‘Tell those who are invited: See, I’ve prepared my dinner; my oxen and fattened cattle have been slaughtered, and everything is ready. Come to the wedding banquet.’
matthew:22:4.nrsv: Again he sent other slaves, saying, 'Tell those who have been invited: Look, I have prepared my dinner, my oxen and my fat calves have been slaughtered, and everything is ready; come to the wedding banquet.'

matthew:22:5.niv: "But they paid no attention and went off--one to his field, another to his business.
matthew:22:5.csb: “But they paid no attention and went away, one to his own farm, another to his business,
matthew:22:5.nrsv: But they made light of it and went away, one to his farm, another to his business,

matthew:22:6.niv: The rest seized his servants, mistreated them and killed them.
matthew:22:6.csb: while the rest seized his servants, mistreated them, and killed them.
matthew:22:6.nrsv: while the rest seized his slaves, mistreated them, and killed them.

matthew:22:7.niv: The king was enraged. He sent his army and destroyed those murderers and burned their city.
matthew:22:7.csb: The king was enraged, and he sent out his troops, killed those murderers, and burned down their city.
matthew:22:7.nrsv: The king was enraged. He sent his troops, destroyed those murderers, and burned their city.

matthew:22:8.niv: "Then he said to his servants, 'The wedding banquet is ready, but those I invited did not deserve to come.
matthew:22:8.csb: “Then he told his servants, ‘The banquet is ready, but those who were invited were not worthy.
matthew:22:8.nrsv: Then he said to his slaves, 'The wedding is ready, but those invited were not worthy.

matthew:22:9.niv: Go to the street corners and invite to the banquet anyone you find.'
matthew:22:9.csb: Go then to where the roads exit the city and invite everyone you find to the banquet.’
matthew:22:9.nrsv: Go therefore into the main streets, and invite everyone you find to the wedding banquet.'

matthew:22:10.niv: So the servants went out into the streets and gathered all the people they could find, both good and bad, and the wedding hall was filled with guests.
matthew:22:10.csb: So those servants went out on the roads and gathered everyone they found, both evil and good. The wedding banquet was filled with guests.
matthew:22:10.nrsv: Those slaves went out into the streets and gathered all whom they found, both good and bad; so the wedding hall was filled with guests.

matthew:22:11.niv: "But when the king came in to see the guests, he noticed a man there who was not wearing wedding clothes.
matthew:22:11.csb: When the king came in to see the guests, he saw a man there who was not dressed for a wedding.
matthew:22:11.nrsv: "But when the king came in to see the guests, he noticed a man there who was not wearing a wedding robe,

matthew:22:12.niv: 'Friend,' he asked, 'how did you get in here without wedding clothes?' The man was speechless.
matthew:22:12.csb: So he said to him, ‘Friend, how did you get in here without wedding clothes?’ The man was speechless.
matthew:22:12.nrsv: and he said to him, 'Friend, how did you get in here without a wedding robe?' And he was speechless.

matthew:22:13.niv: "Then the king told the attendants, 'Tie him hand and foot, and throw him outside, into the darkness, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.'
matthew:22:13.csb: “Then the king told the attendants, ‘Tie him up hand and foot, and throw him into the outer darkness, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.’
matthew:22:13.nrsv: Then the king said to the attendants, 'Bind him hand and foot, and throw him into the outer darkness, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.'

matthew:22:14.niv: "For many are invited, but few are chosen."
matthew:22:14.csb: “For many are invited, but few are chosen.”
matthew:22:14.nrsv: For many are called, but few are chosen."

matthew:22:15.niv: Then the Pharisees went out and laid plans to trap him in his words.
matthew:22:15.csb: Then the Pharisees went and plotted how to trap him by what he said.
matthew:22:15.nrsv: Then the Pharisees went and plotted to entrap him in what he said.

matthew:22:16.niv: They sent their disciples to him along with the Herodians. "Teacher," they said, "we know you are a man of integrity and that you teach the way of God in accordance with the truth. You aren't swayed by men, because you pay no attention to who they are.
matthew:22:16.csb: So they sent their disciples to him, along with the Herodians. “Teacher,” they said, “we know that you are truthful and teach truthfully the way of God. You don’t care what anyone thinks nor do you show partiality.
matthew:22:16.nrsv: So they sent their disciples to him, along with the Herodians, saying, "Teacher, we know that you are sincere, and teach the way of God in accordance with truth, and show deference to no one; for you do not regard people with partiality.

matthew:22:17.niv: Tell us then, what is your opinion? Is it right to pay taxes to Caesar or not?"
matthew:22:17.csb: Tell us, then, what you think. Is it lawful to pay taxes to Caesar or not?”
matthew:22:17.nrsv: Tell us, then, what you think. Is it lawful to pay taxes to the emperor, or not?"

matthew:22:18.niv: But Jesus, knowing their evil intent, said, "You hypocrites, why are you trying to trap me?
matthew:22:18.csb: Perceiving their malicious intent, Jesus said, “Why are you testing me, hypocrites?
matthew:22:18.nrsv: But Jesus, aware of their malice, said, "Why are you putting me to the test, you hypocrites?

matthew:22:19.niv: Show me the coin used for paying the tax." They brought him a denarius,
matthew:22:19.csb: Show me the coin used for the tax.” They brought him a denarius.
matthew:22:19.nrsv: Show me the coin used for the tax." And they brought him a denarius.

matthew:22:20.niv: and he asked them, "Whose portrait is this? And whose inscription?"
matthew:22:20.csb: “Whose image and inscription is this?” he asked them.
matthew:22:20.nrsv: Then he said to them, "Whose head is this, and whose title?"

matthew:22:21.niv: "Caesar's," they replied. Then he said to them, "Give to Caesar what is Caesar's, and to God what is God's."
matthew:22:21.csb: “Caesar’s,” they said to him. Then he said to them, “Give, then, to Caesar the things that are Caesar’s, and to God the things that are God’s.”
matthew:22:21.nrsv: They answered, "The emperor's." Then he said to them, "Give therefore to the emperor the things that are the emperor's, and to God the things that are God's."

matthew:22:22.niv: When they heard this, they were amazed. So they left him and went away.
matthew:22:22.csb: When they heard this, they were amazed. So they left him and went away.
matthew:22:22.nrsv: When they heard this, they were amazed; and they left him and went away.

matthew:22:23.niv: That same day the Sadducees, who say there is no resurrection, came to him with a question.
matthew:22:23.csb: That same day some Sadducees, who say there is no resurrection, came up to him and questioned him:
matthew:22:23.nrsv: The same day some Sadducees came to him, saying there is no resurrection; and they asked him a question, saying,

matthew:22:24.niv: "Teacher," they said, "Moses told us that if a man dies without having children, his brother must marry the widow and have children for him.
matthew:22:24.csb: “Teacher, Moses said, if a man dies, having no children, his brother is to marry his wife and raise up offspring for his brother.
matthew:22:24.nrsv: "Teacher, Moses said, 'If a man dies childless, his brother shall marry the widow, and raise up children for his brother.'

matthew:22:25.niv: Now there were seven brothers among us. The first one married and died, and since he had no children, he left his wife to his brother.
matthew:22:25.csb: Now there were seven brothers among us. The first got married and died. Having no offspring, he left his wife to his brother.
matthew:22:25.nrsv: Now there were seven brothers among us; the first married, and died childless, leaving the widow to his brother.

matthew:22:26.niv: The same thing happened to the second and third brother, right on down to the seventh.
matthew:22:26.csb: The same thing happened to the second also, and the third, and so on to all seven.
matthew:22:26.nrsv: The second did the same, so also the third, down to the seventh.

matthew:22:27.csb: Last of all, the woman died.
matthew:22:27.nrsv: Last of all, the woman herself died.

matthew:22:28.niv: Now then, at the resurrection, whose wife will she be of the seven, since all of them were married to her?"
matthew:22:28.csb: In the resurrection, then, whose wife will she be of the seven? For they all had married her.”
matthew:22:28.nrsv: In the resurrection, then, whose wife of the seven will she be? For all of them had married her."

matthew:22:29.niv: Jesus replied, "You are in error because you do not know the Scriptures or the power of God.
matthew:22:29.csb: Jesus answered them, “You are mistaken, because you don’t know the Scriptures or the power of God.
matthew:22:29.nrsv: Jesus answered them, "You are wrong, because you know neither the scriptures nor the power of God.

matthew:22:30.niv: At the resurrection people will neither marry nor be given in marriage; they will be like the angels in heaven.
matthew:22:30.csb: For in the resurrection they neither marry nor are given in marriage but are like angels in heaven.
matthew:22:30.nrsv: For in the resurrection they neither marry nor are given in marriage, but are like angels in heaven.

matthew:22:31.niv: But about the resurrection of the dead--have you not read what God said to you,
matthew:22:31.csb: Now concerning the resurrection of the dead, haven’t you read what was spoken to you by God:
matthew:22:31.nrsv: And as for the resurrection of the dead, have you not read what was said to you by God,

matthew:22:32.niv: 'I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob'? He is not the God of the dead but of the living."
matthew:22:32.csb: I am the God of Abraham and the God of Isaac and the God of Jacob? He is not the God of the dead, but of the living.”
matthew:22:32.nrsv: 'I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob'? He is God not of the dead, but of the living."

matthew:22:33.niv: When the crowds heard this, they were astonished at his teaching.
matthew:22:33.csb: And when the crowds heard this, they were astonished at his teaching.
matthew:22:33.nrsv: And when the crowd heard it, they were astounded at his teaching.

matthew:22:34.niv: Hearing that Jesus had silenced the Sadducees, the Pharisees got together.
matthew:22:34.csb: When the Pharisees heard that he had silenced the Sadducees, they came together.
matthew:22:34.nrsv: When the Pharisees heard that he had silenced the Sadducees, they gathered together,

matthew:22:35.niv: One of them, an expert in the law, tested him with this question:
matthew:22:35.csb: And one of them, an expert in the law, asked a question to test him:
matthew:22:35.nrsv: and one of them, a lawyer, asked him a question to test him.

matthew:22:36.niv: "Teacher, which is the greatest commandment in the Law?"
matthew:22:36.csb: “Teacher, which command in the law is the greatest?”
matthew:22:36.nrsv: "Teacher, which commandment in the law is the greatest?"

matthew:22:37.niv: Jesus replied: "'Love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your mind.'
matthew:22:37.csb: He said to him, “Love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your soul, and with all your mind.
matthew:22:37.nrsv: He said to him, "'You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, and with all your soul, and with all your mind.'

matthew:22:38.niv: This is the first and greatest commandment.
matthew:22:38.csb: This is the greatest and most important command.
matthew:22:38.nrsv: This is the greatest and first commandment.

matthew:22:39.niv: And the second is like it: 'Love your neighbor as yourself.'
matthew:22:39.csb: The second is like it: Love your neighbor as yourself.
matthew:22:39.nrsv: And a second is like it: 'You shall love your neighbor as yourself.'

matthew:22:40.niv: All the Law and the Prophets hang on these two commandments."
matthew:22:40.csb: All the Law and the Prophets depend on these two commands.”
matthew:22:40.nrsv: On these two commandments hang all the law and the prophets."

matthew:22:41.niv: While the Pharisees were gathered together, Jesus asked them,
matthew:22:41.csb: While the Pharisees were together, Jesus questioned them,
matthew:22:41.nrsv: Now while the Pharisees were gathered together, Jesus asked them this question:

matthew:22:42.niv: "What do you think about the Christ? Whose son is he?The son of David," they replied.
matthew:22:42.csb: “What do you think about the Messiah? Whose son is he?” They replied, “David’s.”
matthew:22:42.nrsv: "What do you think of the Messiah? Whose son is he?" They said to him, "The son of David."

matthew:22:43.niv: He said to them, "How is it then that David, speaking by the Spirit, calls him 'Lord'? For he says,
matthew:22:43.csb: He asked them, “How is it then that David, inspired by the Spirit, calls him ‘Lord’
matthew:22:43.nrsv: He said to them, "How is it then that David by the Spirit calls him Lord, saying,

matthew:22:44.niv: "'The Lord said to my Lord: "Sit at my right hand until I put your enemies under your feet."'
matthew:22:44.csb: The Lord declared to my Lord, ‘Sit at my right hand until I put your enemies under your feet’?
matthew:22:44.nrsv: 'The Lord said to my Lord, "Sit at my right hand, until I put your enemies under your feet" '?

matthew:22:45.niv: If then David calls him 'Lord,' how can he be his son?"
matthew:22:45.csb: “If David calls him ‘Lord,’ how then can he be his son?”
matthew:22:45.nrsv: If David thus calls him Lord, how can he be his son?"

matthew:22:46.niv: No one could say a word in reply, and from that day on no one dared to ask him any more questions.
matthew:22:46.csb: No one was able to answer him at all, and from that day no one dared to question him anymore.
matthew:22:46.nrsv: No one was able to give him an answer, nor from that day did anyone dare to ask him any more questions.

matthew:23:1.niv: Then Jesus said to the crowds and to his disciples:
matthew:23:1.csb: Then Jesus spoke to the crowds and to his disciples:
matthew:23:1.nrsv: Then Jesus said to the crowds and to his disciples,

matthew:23:2.niv: "The teachers of the law and the Pharisees sit in Moses' seat.
matthew:23:2.csb: “The scribes and the Pharisees are seated in the chair of Moses.
matthew:23:2.nrsv: "The scribes and the Pharisees sit on Moses' seat;

matthew:23:3.niv: So you must obey them and do everything they tell you. But do not do what they do, for they do not practice what they preach.
matthew:23:3.csb: Therefore do whatever they tell you, and observe it. But don’t do what they do, because they don’t practice what they teach.
matthew:23:3.nrsv: therefore, do whatever they teach you and follow it; but do not do as they do, for they do not practice what they teach.

matthew:23:4.niv: They tie up heavy loads and put them on men's shoulders, but they themselves are not willing to lift a finger to move them.
matthew:23:4.csb: They tie up heavy loads that are hard to carry and put them on people’s shoulders, but they themselves aren’t willing to lift a finger to move them.
matthew:23:4.nrsv: They tie up heavy burdens, hard to bear, and lay them on the shoulders of others; but they themselves are unwilling to lift a finger to move them.

matthew:23:5.niv: "Everything they do is done for men to see: They make their phylacteries wide and the tassels on their garments long;
matthew:23:5.csb: They do everything to be seen by others: They enlarge their phylacteries and lengthen their tassels.
matthew:23:5.nrsv: They do all their deeds to be seen by others; for they make their phylacteries broad and their fringes long.

matthew:23:6.niv: they love the place of honor at banquets and the most important seats in the synagogues;
matthew:23:6.csb: They love the place of honor at banquets, the front seats in the synagogues,
matthew:23:6.nrsv: They love to have the place of honor at banquets and the best seats in the synagogues,

matthew:23:7.niv: they love to be greeted in the marketplaces and to have men call them 'Rabbi.'
matthew:23:7.csb: greetings in the marketplaces, and to be called ‘Rabbi’ by people.
matthew:23:7.nrsv: and to be greeted with respect in the marketplaces, and to have people call them rabbi.

matthew:23:8.niv: "But you are not to be called 'Rabbi,' for you have only one Master and you are all brothers.
matthew:23:8.csb: “But you are not to be called ‘Rabbi,’ because you have one Teacher, and you are all brothers and sisters.
matthew:23:8.nrsv: But you are not to be called rabbi, for you have one teacher, and you are all students.

matthew:23:9.niv: And do not call anyone on earth 'father,' for you have one Father, and he is in heaven.
matthew:23:9.csb: Do not call anyone on earth your father, because you have one Father, who is in heaven.
matthew:23:9.nrsv: And call no one your father on earth, for you have one Father-- the one in heaven.

matthew:23:10.niv: Nor are you to be called 'teacher,' for you have one Teacher, the Christ.
matthew:23:10.csb: You are not to be called instructors either, because you have one Instructor, the Messiah.
matthew:23:10.nrsv: Nor are you to be called instructors, for you have one instructor, the Messiah.

matthew:23:11.niv: The greatest among you will be your servant.
matthew:23:11.csb: The greatest among you will be your servant.
matthew:23:11.nrsv: The greatest among you will be your servant.

matthew:23:12.niv: For whoever exalts himself will be humbled, and whoever humbles himself will be exalted.
matthew:23:12.csb: Whoever exalts himself will be humbled, and whoever humbles himself will be exalted.
matthew:23:12.nrsv: All who exalt themselves will be humbled, and all who humble themselves will be exalted.

matthew:23:13.niv: "Woe to you, teachers of the law and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You shut the kingdom of heaven in men's faces. You yourselves do not enter, nor will you let those enter who are trying to.
matthew:23:13.csb: “Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! You shut the door of the kingdom of heaven in people’s faces. For you don’t go in, and you don’t allow those entering to go in.
matthew:23:13.nrsv: "But woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you lock people out of the kingdom of heaven. For you do not go in yourselves, and when others are going in, you stop them.

matthew:23:15.niv: "Woe to you, teachers of the law and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You travel over land and sea to win a single convert, and when he becomes one, you make him twice as much a son of hell as you are.
matthew:23:15.csb: “Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! You travel over land and sea to make one convert, and when he becomes one, you make him twice as fit for hell as you are!
matthew:23:15.nrsv: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you cross sea and land to make a single convert, and you make the new convert twice as much a child of hell as yourselves.

matthew:23:16.niv: "Woe to you, blind guides! You say, 'If anyone swears by the temple, it means nothing; but if anyone swears by the gold of the temple, he is bound by his oath.'
matthew:23:16.csb: “Woe to you, blind guides, who say, ‘Whoever takes an oath by the temple, it means nothing. But whoever takes an oath by the gold of the temple is bound by his oath.’
matthew:23:16.nrsv: "Woe to you, blind guides, who say, 'Whoever swears by the sanctuary is bound by nothing, but whoever swears by the gold of the sanctuary is bound by the oath.'

matthew:23:17.niv: You blind fools! Which is greater: the gold, or the temple that makes the gold sacred?
matthew:23:17.csb: Blind fools! For which is greater, the gold or the temple that sanctified the gold?
matthew:23:17.nrsv: You blind fools! For which is greater, the gold or the sanctuary that has made the gold sacred?

matthew:23:18.niv: You also say, 'If anyone swears by the altar, it means nothing; but if anyone swears by the gift on it, he is bound by his oath.'
matthew:23:18.csb: Also, ‘Whoever takes an oath by the altar, it means nothing; but whoever takes an oath by the gift that is on it is bound by his oath.’
matthew:23:18.nrsv: And you say, 'Whoever swears by the altar is bound by nothing, but whoever swears by the gift that is on the altar is bound by the oath.'

matthew:23:19.niv: You blind men! Which is greater: the gift, or the altar that makes the gift sacred?
matthew:23:19.csb: Blind people! For which is greater, the gift or the altar that sanctifies the gift?
matthew:23:19.nrsv: How blind you are! For which is greater, the gift or the altar that makes the gift sacred?

matthew:23:20.niv: Therefore, he who swears by the altar swears by it and by everything on it.
matthew:23:20.csb: Therefore, the one who takes an oath by the altar takes an oath by it and by everything on it.
matthew:23:20.nrsv: So whoever swears by the altar, swears by it and by everything on it;

matthew:23:21.niv: And he who swears by the temple swears by it and by the one who dwells in it.
matthew:23:21.csb: The one who takes an oath by the temple takes an oath by it and by him who dwells in it.
matthew:23:21.nrsv: and whoever swears by the sanctuary, swears by it and by the one who dwells in it;

matthew:23:22.niv: And he who swears by heaven swears by God's throne and by the one who sits on it.
matthew:23:22.csb: And the one who takes an oath by heaven takes an oath by God’s throne and by him who sits on it.
matthew:23:22.nrsv: and whoever swears by heaven, swears by the throne of God and by the one who is seated upon it.

matthew:23:23.niv: "Woe to you, teachers of the law and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You give a tenth of your spices--mint, dill and cummin. But you have neglected the more important matters of the law--justice, mercy and faithfulness. You should have practiced the latter, without neglecting the former.
matthew:23:23.csb: “Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! You pay a tenth of mint, dill, and cumin, and yet you have neglected the more important matters of the law — justice, mercy, and faithfulness. These things should have been done without neglecting the others.
matthew:23:23.nrsv: "Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you tithe mint, dill, and cummin, and have neglected the weightier matters of the law: justice and mercy and faith. It is these you ought to have practiced without neglecting the others.

matthew:23:24.niv: You blind guides! You strain out a gnat but swallow a camel.
matthew:23:24.csb: Blind guides! You strain out a gnat, but gulp down a camel!
matthew:23:24.nrsv: You blind guides! You strain out a gnat but swallow a camel!

matthew:23:25.niv: "Woe to you, teachers of the law and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You clean the outside of the cup and dish, but inside they are full of greed and self-indulgence.
matthew:23:25.csb: “Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! You clean the outside of the cup and dish, but inside they are full of greed and self-indulgence.
matthew:23:25.nrsv: "Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you clean the outside of the cup and of the plate, but inside they are full of greed and self-indulgence.

matthew:23:26.niv: Blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of the cup and dish, and then the outside also will be clean.
matthew:23:26.csb: Blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of the cup, so that the outside of it may also become clean.
matthew:23:26.nrsv: You blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of the cup, so that the outside also may become clean.

matthew:23:27.niv: "Woe to you, teachers of the law and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You are like whitewashed tombs, which look beautiful on the outside but on the inside are full of dead men's bones and everything unclean.
matthew:23:27.csb: “Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! You are like whitewashed tombs, which appear beautiful on the outside, but inside are full of the bones of the dead and every kind of impurity.
matthew:23:27.nrsv: "Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you are like whitewashed tombs, which on the outside look beautiful, but inside they are full of the bones of the dead and of all kinds of filth.

matthew:23:28.niv: In the same way, on the outside you appear to people as righteous but on the inside you are full of hypocrisy and wickedness.
matthew:23:28.csb: In the same way, on the outside you seem righteous to people, but inside you are full of hypocrisy and lawlessness.
matthew:23:28.nrsv: So you also on the outside look righteous to others, but inside you are full of hypocrisy and lawlessness.

matthew:23:29.niv: "Woe to you, teachers of the law and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You build tombs for the prophets and decorate the graves of the righteous.
matthew:23:29.csb: “Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! You build the tombs of the prophets and decorate the graves of the righteous,
matthew:23:29.nrsv: "Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you build the tombs of the prophets and decorate the graves of the righteous,

matthew:23:30.niv: And you say, 'If we had lived in the days of our forefathers, we would not have taken part with them in shedding the blood of the prophets.'
matthew:23:30.csb: and you say, ‘If we had lived in the days of our ancestors, we wouldn’t have taken part with them in shedding the prophets’ blood.’
matthew:23:30.nrsv: and you say, 'If we had lived in the days of our ancestors, we would not have taken part with them in shedding the blood of the prophets.'

matthew:23:31.niv: So you testify against yourselves that you are the descendants of those who murdered the prophets.
matthew:23:31.csb: So you testify against yourselves that you are descendants of those who murdered the prophets.
matthew:23:31.nrsv: Thus you testify against yourselves that you are descendants of those who murdered the prophets.

matthew:23:32.niv: Fill up, then, the measure of the sin of your forefathers!
matthew:23:32.csb: Fill up, then, the measure of your ancestors’ sins!
matthew:23:32.nrsv: Fill up, then, the measure of your ancestors.

matthew:23:33.niv: "You snakes! You brood of vipers! How will you escape being condemned to hell?
matthew:23:33.csb: “Snakes! Brood of vipers! How can you escape being condemned to hell?
matthew:23:33.nrsv: You snakes, you brood of vipers! How can you escape being sentenced to hell?

matthew:23:34.niv: Therefore I am sending you prophets and wise men and teachers. Some of them you will kill and crucify; others you will flog in your synagogues and pursue from town to town.
matthew:23:34.csb: This is why I am sending you prophets, sages, and scribes. Some of them you will kill and crucify, and some of them you will flog in your synagogues and pursue from town to town.
matthew:23:34.nrsv: Therefore I send you prophets, sages, and scribes, some of whom you will kill and crucify, and some you will flog in your synagogues and pursue from town to town,

matthew:23:35.niv: And so upon you will come all the righteous blood that has been shed on earth, from the blood of righteous Abel to the blood of Zechariah son of Berekiah, whom you murdered between the temple and the altar.
matthew:23:35.csb: So all the righteous blood shed on the earth will be charged to you, from the blood of righteous Abel to the blood of Zechariah, son of Berechiah, whom you murdered between the sanctuary and the altar.
matthew:23:35.nrsv: so that upon you may come all the righteous blood shed on earth, from the blood of righteous Abel to the blood of Zechariah son of Barachiah, whom you murdered between the sanctuary and the altar.

matthew:23:36.niv: I tell you the truth, all this will come upon this generation.
matthew:23:36.csb: Truly I tell you, all these things will come on this generation.
matthew:23:36.nrsv: Truly I tell you, all this will come upon this generation.

matthew:23:37.niv: "O Jerusalem, Jerusalem, you who kill the prophets and stone those sent to you, how often I have longed to gather your children together, as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings, but you were not willing.
matthew:23:37.csb: “Jerusalem, Jerusalem, who kills the prophets and stones those who are sent to her. How often I wanted to gather your children together, as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings, but you were not willing!
matthew:23:37.nrsv: "Jerusalem, Jerusalem, the city that kills the prophets and stones those who are sent to it! How often have I desired to gather your children together as a hen gathers her brood under her wings, and you were not willing!

matthew:23:38.niv: Look, your house is left to you desolate.
matthew:23:38.csb: See, your house is left to you desolate.
matthew:23:38.nrsv: See, your house is left to you, desolate.

matthew:23:39.niv: For I tell you, you will not see me again until you say, 'Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord.'"
matthew:23:39.csb: For I tell you, you will not see me again until you say,’ Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord ’!”
matthew:23:39.nrsv: For I tell you, you will not see me again until you say, 'Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord.'"

matthew:24:1.niv: Jesus left the temple and was walking away when his disciples came up to him to call his attention to its buildings.
matthew:24:1.csb: As Jesus left and was going out of the temple, his disciples came up and called his attention to its buildings.
matthew:24:1.nrsv: As Jesus came out of the temple and was going away, his disciples came to point out to him the buildings of the temple.

matthew:24:2.niv: "Do you see all these things?" he asked. "I tell you the truth, not one stone here will be left on another; every one will be thrown down."
matthew:24:2.csb: He replied to them, “Do you see all these things? Truly I tell you, not one stone will be left here on another that will not be thrown down.”
matthew:24:2.nrsv: Then he asked them, "You see all these, do you not? Truly I tell you, not one stone will be left here upon another; all will be thrown down."

matthew:24:3.niv: As Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives, the disciples came to him privately. "Tell us," they said, "when will this happen, and what will be the sign of your coming and of the end of the age?"
matthew:24:3.csb: While he was sitting on the Mount of Olives, the disciples approached him privately and said, “Tell us, when will these things happen? And what is the sign of your coming and of the end of the age?”
matthew:24:3.nrsv: When he was sitting on the Mount of Olives, the disciples came to him privately, saying, "Tell us, when will this be, and what will be the sign of your coming and of the end of the age?"

matthew:24:4.niv: Jesus answered: "Watch out that no one deceives you.
matthew:24:4.csb: Jesus replied to them: “Watch out that no one deceives you.
matthew:24:4.nrsv: Jesus answered them, "Beware that no one leads you astray.

matthew:24:5.niv: For many will come in my name, claiming, 'I am the Christ, 'and will deceive many.
matthew:24:5.csb: For many will come in my name, saying, ‘I am the Messiah,’ and they will deceive many.
matthew:24:5.nrsv: For many will come in my name, saying, 'I am the Messiah!' and they will lead many astray.

matthew:24:6.niv: You will hear of wars and rumors of wars, but see to it that you are not alarmed. Such things must happen, but the end is still to come.
matthew:24:6.csb: You are going to hear of wars and rumors of wars. See that you are not alarmed, because these things must take place, but the end is not yet.
matthew:24:6.nrsv: And you will hear of wars and rumors of wars; see that you are not alarmed; for this must take place, but the end is not yet.

matthew:24:7.niv: Nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom. There will be famines and earthquakes in various places.
matthew:24:7.csb: For nation will rise up against nation, and kingdom against kingdom. There will be famines and earthquakes in various places.
matthew:24:7.nrsv: For nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom, and there will be famines and earthquakes in various places:

matthew:24:8.niv: All these are the beginning of birth pains.
matthew:24:8.csb: All these events are the beginning of labor pains.
matthew:24:8.nrsv: all this is but the beginning of the birth pangs.

matthew:24:9.niv: "Then you will be handed over to be persecuted and put to death, and you will be hated by all nations because of me.
matthew:24:9.csb: “Then they will hand you over to be persecuted, and they will kill you. You will be hated by all nations because of my name.
matthew:24:9.nrsv: "Then they will hand you over to be tortured and will put you to death, and you will be hated by all nations because of my name.

matthew:24:10.niv: At that time many will turn away from the faith and will betray and hate each other,
matthew:24:10.csb: Then many will fall away, betray one another, and hate one another.
