matthew:27:51.niv: At that moment the curtain of the temple was torn in two from top to bottom. The earth shook and the rocks split.
matthew:27:51.csb: Suddenly, the curtain of the sanctuary was torn in two from top to bottom, the earth quaked, and the rocks were split.
matthew:27:51.nrsv: At that moment the curtain of the temple was torn in two, from top to bottom. The earth shook, and the rocks were split.

matthew:27:52.niv: The tombs broke open and the bodies of many holy people who had died were raised to life.
matthew:27:52.csb: The tombs were also opened and many bodies of the saints who had fallen asleep were raised.
matthew:27:52.nrsv: The tombs also were opened, and many bodies of the saints who had fallen asleep were raised.

matthew:27:53.niv: They came out of the tombs, and after Jesus' resurrection they went into the holy city and appeared to many people.
matthew:27:53.csb: And they came out of the tombs after his resurrection, entered the holy city, and appeared to many.
matthew:27:53.nrsv: After his resurrection they came out of the tombs and entered the holy city and appeared to many.

matthew:27:54.niv: When the centurion and those with him who were guarding Jesus saw the earthquake and all that had happened, they were terrified, and exclaimed, "Surely he was the Son of God!"
matthew:27:54.csb: When the centurion and those with him, who were keeping watch over Jesus, saw the earthquake and the things that had happened, they were terrified and said, “Truly this man was the Son of God!”
matthew:27:54.nrsv: Now when the centurion and those with him, who were keeping watch over Jesus, saw the earthquake and what took place, they were terrified and said, "Truly this man was God's Son!"

matthew:27:55.niv: Many women were there, watching from a distance. They had followed Jesus from Galilee to care for his needs.
matthew:27:55.csb: Many women who had followed Jesus from Galilee and looked after him were there, watching from a distance.
matthew:27:55.nrsv: Many women were also there, looking on from a distance; they had followed Jesus from Galilee and had provided for him.

matthew:27:56.niv: Among them were Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James and Joses, and the mother of Zebedee's sons.
matthew:27:56.csb: Among them were Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James and Joseph, and the mother of Zebedee’s sons.
matthew:27:56.nrsv: Among them were Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James and Joseph, and the mother of the sons of Zebedee.

matthew:27:57.niv: As evening approached, there came a rich man from Arimathea, named Joseph, who had himself become a disciple of Jesus.
matthew:27:57.csb: When it was evening, a rich man from Arimathea named Joseph came, who himself had also become a disciple of Jesus.
matthew:27:57.nrsv: When it was evening, there came a rich man from Arimathea, named Joseph, who was also a disciple of Jesus.

matthew:27:58.niv: Going to Pilate, he asked for Jesus' body, and Pilate ordered that it be given to him.
matthew:27:58.csb: He approached Pilate and asked for Jesus’s body. Then Pilate ordered that it be released.
matthew:27:58.nrsv: He went to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus; then Pilate ordered it to be given to him.

matthew:27:59.niv: Joseph took the body, wrapped it in a clean linen cloth,
matthew:27:59.csb: So Joseph took the body, wrapped it in clean, fine linen,
matthew:27:59.nrsv: So Joseph took the body and wrapped it in a clean linen cloth

matthew:27:60.niv: and placed it in his own new tomb that he had cut out of the rock. He rolled a big stone in front of the entrance to the tomb and went away.
matthew:27:60.csb: and placed it in his new tomb, which he had cut into the rock. He left after rolling a great stone against the entrance of the tomb.
matthew:27:60.nrsv: and laid it in his own new tomb, which he had hewn in the rock. He then rolled a great stone to the door of the tomb and went away.

matthew:27:61.niv: Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were sitting there opposite the tomb.
matthew:27:61.csb: Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were seated there, facing the tomb.
matthew:27:61.nrsv: Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were there, sitting opposite the tomb.

matthew:27:62.niv: The next day, the one after Preparation Day, the chief priests and the Pharisees went to Pilate.
matthew:27:62.csb: The next day, which followed the preparation day, the chief priests and the Pharisees gathered before Pilate
matthew:27:62.nrsv: The next day, that is, after the day of Preparation, the chief priests and the Pharisees gathered before Pilate

matthew:27:63.niv: "Sir," they said, "we remember that while he was still alive that deceiver said, 'After three days I will rise again.'
matthew:27:63.csb: and said, “Sir, we remember that while this deceiver was still alive he said, ‘After three days I will rise again.’
matthew:27:63.nrsv: and said, "Sir, we remember what that impostor said while he was still alive, 'After three days I will rise again.'

matthew:27:64.niv: So give the order for the tomb to be made secure until the third day. Otherwise, his disciples may come and steal the body and tell the people that he has been raised from the dead. This last deception will be worse than the first."
matthew:27:64.csb: So give orders that the tomb be made secure until the third day. Otherwise, his disciples may come, steal him, and tell the people, ‘He has been raised from the dead,’ and the last deception will be worse than the first.”
matthew:27:64.nrsv: Therefore command the tomb to be made secure until the third day; otherwise his disciples may go and steal him away, and tell the people, 'He has been raised from the dead,' and the last deception would be worse than the first."

matthew:27:65.niv: "Take a guard," Pilate answered. "Go, make the tomb as secure as you know how."
matthew:27:65.csb: “You have a guard of soldiers,” Pilate told them. “Go and make it as secure as you know how.”
matthew:27:65.nrsv: Pilate said to them, "You have a guard of soldiers; go, make it as secure as you can."

matthew:27:66.niv: So they went and made the tomb secure by putting a seal on the stone and posting the guard.
matthew:27:66.csb: They went and secured the tomb by setting a seal on the stone and placing the guard.
matthew:27:66.nrsv: So they went with the guard and made the tomb secure by sealing the stone.

matthew:28:1.niv: After the Sabbath, at dawn on the first day of the week, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary went to look at the tomb.
matthew:28:1.csb: After the Sabbath, as the first day of the week was dawning, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary went to view the tomb.
matthew:28:1.nrsv: After the sabbath, as the first day of the week was dawning, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary went to see the tomb.

matthew:28:2.niv: There was a violent earthquake, for an angel of the Lord came down from heaven and, going to the tomb, rolled back the stone and sat on it.
matthew:28:2.csb: There was a violent earthquake, because an angel of the Lord descended from heaven and approached the tomb. He rolled back the stone and was sitting on it.
matthew:28:2.nrsv: And suddenly there was a great earthquake; for an angel of the Lord, descending from heaven, came and rolled back the stone and sat on it.

matthew:28:3.niv: His appearance was like lightning, and his clothes were white as snow.
matthew:28:3.csb: His appearance was like lightning, and his clothing was as white as snow.
matthew:28:3.nrsv: His appearance was like lightning, and his clothing white as snow.

matthew:28:4.niv: The guards were so afraid of him that they shook and became like dead men.
matthew:28:4.csb: The guards were so shaken by fear of him that they became like dead men.
matthew:28:4.nrsv: For fear of him the guards shook and became like dead men.

matthew:28:5.niv: The angel said to the women, "Do not be afraid, for I know that you are looking for Jesus, who was crucified.
matthew:28:5.csb: The angel told the women, “Don’t be afraid, because I know you are looking for Jesus who was crucified.
matthew:28:5.nrsv: But the angel said to the women, "Do not be afraid; I know that you are looking for Jesus who was crucified.

matthew:28:6.niv: He is not here; he has risen, just as he said. Come and see the place where he lay.
matthew:28:6.csb: He is not here. For he has risen, just as he said. Come and see the place where he lay.
matthew:28:6.nrsv: He is not here; for he has been raised, as he said. Come, see the place where he lay.

matthew:28:7.niv: Then go quickly and tell his disciples: 'He has risen from the dead and is going ahead of you into Galilee. There you will see him.' Now I have told you."
matthew:28:7.csb: Then go quickly and tell his disciples, ‘He has risen from the dead and indeed he is going ahead of you to Galilee; you will see him there.’ Listen, I have told you.”
matthew:28:7.nrsv: Then go quickly and tell his disciples, 'He has been raised from the dead, and indeed he is going ahead of you to Galilee; there you will see him.' This is my message for you."

matthew:28:8.niv: So the women hurried away from the tomb, afraid yet filled with joy, and ran to tell his disciples.
matthew:28:8.csb: So, departing quickly from the tomb with fear and great joy, they ran to tell his disciples the news.
matthew:28:8.nrsv: So they left the tomb quickly with fear and great joy, and ran to tell his disciples.

matthew:28:9.niv: Suddenly Jesus met them. "Greetings," he said. They came to him, clasped his feet and worshiped him.
matthew:28:9.csb: Just then Jesus met them and said, “Greetings!” They came up, took hold of his feet, and worshiped him.
matthew:28:9.nrsv: Suddenly Jesus met them and said, "Greetings!" And they came to him, took hold of his feet, and worshiped him.

matthew:28:10.niv: Then Jesus said to them, "Do not be afraid. Go and tell my brothers to go to Galilee; there they will see me."
matthew:28:10.csb: Then Jesus told them, “Do not be afraid. Go and tell my brothers to leave for Galilee, and they will see me there.”
matthew:28:10.nrsv: Then Jesus said to them, "Do not be afraid; go and tell my brothers to go to Galilee; there they will see me."

matthew:28:11.niv: While the women were on their way, some of the guards went into the city and reported to the chief priests everything that had happened.
matthew:28:11.csb: As they were on their way, some of the guards came into the city and reported to the chief priests everything that had happened.
matthew:28:11.nrsv: While they were going, some of the guard went into the city and told the chief priests everything that had happened.

matthew:28:12.niv: When the chief priests had met with the elders and devised a plan, they gave the soldiers a large sum of money,
matthew:28:12.csb: After the priests had assembled with the elders and agreed on a plan, they gave the soldiers a large sum of money
matthew:28:12.nrsv: After the priests had assembled with the elders, they devised a plan to give a large sum of money to the soldiers,

matthew:28:13.niv: telling them, "You are to say, 'His disciples came during the night and stole him away while we were asleep.'
matthew:28:13.csb: and told them, “Say this, ‘His disciples came during the night and stole him while we were sleeping.’
matthew:28:13.nrsv: telling them, "You must say, 'His disciples came by night and stole him away while we were asleep.'

matthew:28:14.niv: If this report gets to the governor, we will satisfy him and keep you out of trouble."
matthew:28:14.csb: If this reaches the governor’s ears, we will deal with him and keep you out of trouble.”
matthew:28:14.nrsv: If this comes to the governor's ears, we will satisfy him and keep you out of trouble."

matthew:28:15.niv: So the soldiers took the money and did as they were instructed. And this story has been widely circulated among the Jews to this very day.
matthew:28:15.csb: They took the money and did as they were instructed, and this story has been spread among Jewish people to this day.
matthew:28:15.nrsv: So they took the money and did as they were directed. And this story is still told among the Jews to this day.

matthew:28:16.niv: Then the eleven disciples went to Galilee, to the mountain where Jesus had told them to go.
matthew:28:16.csb: The eleven disciples traveled to Galilee, to the mountain where Jesus had directed them.
matthew:28:16.nrsv: Now the eleven disciples went to Galilee, to the mountain to which Jesus had directed them.

matthew:28:17.niv: When they saw him, they worshiped him; but some doubted.
matthew:28:17.csb: When they saw him, they worshiped, but some doubted.
matthew:28:17.nrsv: When they saw him, they worshiped him; but some doubted.

matthew:28:18.niv: Then Jesus came to them and said, "All authority in heaven and on earth has been given to me.
matthew:28:18.csb: Jesus came near and said to them, “All authority has been given to me in heaven and on earth.
matthew:28:18.nrsv: And Jesus came and said to them, "All authority in heaven and on earth has been given to me.

matthew:28:19.niv: Therefore go and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit,
matthew:28:19.csb: Go, therefore, and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit,
matthew:28:19.nrsv: Go therefore and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit,

matthew:28:20.niv: and teaching them to obey everything I have commanded you. And surely I am with you always, to the very end of the age."
matthew:28:20.csb: teaching them to observe everything I have commanded you. And remember, I am with you always, to the end of the age.”
matthew:28:20.nrsv: and teaching them to obey everything that I have commanded you. And remember, I am with you always, to the end of the age."

mark:1:1.niv: The beginning of the gospel about Jesus Christ, the Son of God.
mark:1:1.csb: The beginning of the gospel of Jesus Christ, the Son of God.
mark:1:1.nrsv: The beginning of the good news of Jesus Christ, the Son of God.

mark:1:2.niv: It is written in Isaiah the prophet: "I will send my messenger ahead of you, who will prepare your way"--
mark:1:2.csb: As it is written in Isaiah the prophet: See, I am sending my messenger ahead of you; he will prepare your way.
mark:1:2.nrsv: As it is written in the prophet Isaiah, "See, I am sending my messenger ahead of you, who will prepare your way;

mark:1:3.niv: "a voice of one calling in the desert, 'Prepare the way for the Lord, make straight paths for him.'"
mark:1:3.csb: A voice of one crying out in the wilderness: Prepare the way for the Lord; make his paths straight!
mark:1:3.nrsv: the voice of one crying out in the wilderness: 'Prepare the way of the Lord, make his paths straight,'"

mark:1:4.niv: And so John came, baptizing in the desert region and preaching a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins.
mark:1:4.csb: John came baptizing in the wilderness and proclaiming a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins.
mark:1:4.nrsv: John the baptizer appeared in the wilderness, proclaiming a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins.

mark:1:5.niv: The whole Judean countryside and all the people of Jerusalem went out to him. Confessing their sins, they were baptized by him in the Jordan River.
mark:1:5.csb: The whole Judean countryside and all the people of Jerusalem were going out to him, and they were baptized by him in the Jordan River, confessing their sins.
mark:1:5.nrsv: And people from the whole Judean countryside and all the people of Jerusalem were going out to him, and were baptized by him in the river Jordan, confessing their sins.

mark:1:6.niv: John wore clothing made of camel's hair, with a leather belt around his waist, and he ate locusts and wild honey.
mark:1:6.csb: John wore a camel-hair garment with a leather belt around his waist and ate locusts and wild honey.
mark:1:6.nrsv: Now John was clothed with camel's hair, with a leather belt around his waist, and he ate locusts and wild honey.

mark:1:7.niv: And this was his message: "After me will come one more powerful than I, the thongs of whose sandals I am not worthy to stoop down and untie.
mark:1:7.csb: He proclaimed, “One who is more powerful than I am is coming after me. I am not worthy to stoop down and untie the strap of his sandals.
mark:1:7.nrsv: He proclaimed, "The one who is more powerful than I is coming after me; I am not worthy to stoop down and untie the thong of his sandals.

mark:1:8.niv: I baptize you with water, but he will baptize you with the Holy Spirit."
mark:1:8.csb: I baptize you with water, but he will baptize you with the Holy Spirit.”
mark:1:8.nrsv: I have baptized you with water; but he will baptize you with the Holy Spirit."

mark:1:9.niv: At that time Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee and was baptized by John in the Jordan.
mark:1:9.csb: In those days Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee and was baptized in the Jordan by John.
mark:1:9.nrsv: In those days Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee and was baptized by John in the Jordan.

mark:1:10.niv: As Jesus was coming up out of the water, he saw heaven being torn open and the Spirit descending on him like a dove.
mark:1:10.csb: As soon as he came up out of the water, he saw the heavens being torn open and the Spirit descending on him like a dove.
mark:1:10.nrsv: And just as he was coming up out of the water, he saw the heavens torn apart and the Spirit descending like a dove on him.

mark:1:11.niv: And a voice came from heaven: "You are my Son, whom I love; with you I am well pleased."
mark:1:11.csb: And a voice came from heaven: “You are my beloved Son; with you I am well-pleased.”
mark:1:11.nrsv: And a voice came from heaven, "You are my Son, the Beloved; with you I am well pleased."

mark:1:12.niv: At once the Spirit sent him out into the desert,
mark:1:12.csb: Immediately the Spirit drove him into the wilderness.
mark:1:12.nrsv: And the Spirit immediately drove him out into the wilderness.

mark:1:13.niv: and he was in the desert forty days, being tempted by Satan. He was with the wild animals, and angels attended him.
mark:1:13.csb: He was in the wilderness forty days, being tempted by Satan. He was with the wild animals, and the angels were serving him.
mark:1:13.nrsv: He was in the wilderness forty days, tempted by Satan; and he was with the wild beasts; and the angels waited on him.

mark:1:14.niv: After John was put in prison, Jesus went into Galilee, proclaiming the good news of God.
mark:1:14.csb: After John was arrested, Jesus went to Galilee, proclaiming the good news of God:
mark:1:14.nrsv: Now after John was arrested, Jesus came to Galilee, proclaiming the good news of God,

mark:1:15.niv: "The time has come," he said. "The kingdom of God is near. Repent and believe the good news!"
mark:1:15.csb: “The time is fulfilled, and the kingdom of God has come near. Repent and believe the good news!”
mark:1:15.nrsv: and saying, "The time is fulfilled, and the kingdom of God has come near; repent, and believe in the good news."

mark:1:16.niv: As Jesus walked beside the Sea of Galilee, he saw Simon and his brother Andrew casting a net into the lake, for they were fishermen.
mark:1:16.csb: As he passed alongside the Sea of Galilee, he saw Simon and Andrew, Simon’s brother, casting a net into the sea — for they were fishermen.
mark:1:16.nrsv: As Jesus passed along the Sea of Galilee, he saw Simon and his brother Andrew casting a net into the sea-- for they were fishermen.

mark:1:17.niv: "Come, follow me," Jesus said, "and I will make you fishers of men."
mark:1:17.csb: “Follow me,” Jesus told them, “and I will make you fish for people.”
mark:1:17.nrsv: And Jesus said to them, "Follow me and I will make you fish for people."

mark:1:18.niv: At once they left their nets and followed him.
mark:1:18.csb: Immediately they left their nets and followed him.
mark:1:18.nrsv: And immediately they left their nets and followed him.

mark:1:19.niv: When he had gone a little farther, he saw James son of Zebedee and his brother John in a boat, preparing their nets.
mark:1:19.csb: Going on a little farther, he saw James the son of Zebedee and his brother John in a boat putting their nets in order.
mark:1:19.nrsv: As he went a little farther, he saw James son of Zebedee and his brother John, who were in their boat mending the nets.

mark:1:20.niv: Without delay he called them, and they left their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired men and followed him.
mark:1:20.csb: Immediately he called them, and they left their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired men and followed him.
mark:1:20.nrsv: Immediately he called them; and they left their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired men, and followed him.

mark:1:21.niv: They went to Capernaum, and when the Sabbath came, Jesus went into the synagogue and began to teach.
mark:1:21.csb: They went into Capernaum, and right away he entered the synagogue on the Sabbath and began to teach.
mark:1:21.nrsv: They went to Capernaum; and when the sabbath came, he entered the synagogue and taught.

mark:1:22.niv: The people were amazed at his teaching, because he taught them as one who had authority, not as the teachers of the law.
mark:1:22.csb: They were astonished at his teaching because he was teaching them as one who had authority, and not like the scribes.
mark:1:22.nrsv: They were astounded at his teaching, for he taught them as one having authority, and not as the scribes.

mark:1:23.niv: Just then a man in their synagogue who was possessed by an evil spirit cried out,
mark:1:23.csb: Just then a man with an unclean spirit was in their synagogue. He cried out,
mark:1:23.nrsv: Just then there was in their synagogue a man with an unclean spirit,

mark:1:24.niv: "What do you want with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are--the Holy One of God!"
mark:1:24.csb: “What do you have to do with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are — the Holy One of God!”
mark:1:24.nrsv: and he cried out, "What have you to do with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are, the Holy One of God."

mark:1:25.niv: "Be quiet!" said Jesus sternly. "Come out of him!"
mark:1:25.csb: Jesus rebuked him saying, “Be silent, and come out of him!”
mark:1:25.nrsv: But Jesus rebuked him, saying, "Be silent, and come out of him!"

mark:1:26.niv: The evil spirit shook the man violently and came out of him with a shriek.
mark:1:26.csb: And the unclean spirit threw him into convulsions, shouted with a loud voice, and came out of him.
mark:1:26.nrsv: And the unclean spirit, convulsing him and crying with a loud voice, came out of him.

mark:1:27.niv: The people were all so amazed that they asked each other, "What is this? A new teaching--and with authority! He even gives orders to evil spirits and they obey him."
mark:1:27.csb: They were all amazed, and so they began to ask each other: “What is this? A new teaching with authority! He commands even the unclean spirits, and they obey him.”
mark:1:27.nrsv: They were all amazed, and they kept on asking one another, "What is this? A new teaching-- with authority! He commands even the unclean spirits, and they obey him."

mark:1:28.niv: News about him spread quickly over the whole region of Galilee.
mark:1:28.csb: At once the news about him spread throughout the entire vicinity of Galilee.
mark:1:28.nrsv: At once his fame began to spread throughout the surrounding region of Galilee.

mark:1:29.niv: As soon as they left the synagogue, they went with James and John to the home of Simon and Andrew.
mark:1:29.csb: As soon as they left the synagogue, they went into Simon and Andrew’s house with James and John.
mark:1:29.nrsv: As soon as they left the synagogue, they entered the house of Simon and Andrew, with James and John.

mark:1:30.niv: Simon's mother-in-law was in bed with a fever, and they told Jesus about her.
mark:1:30.csb: Simon’s mother-in-law was lying in bed with a fever, and they told him about her at once.
mark:1:30.nrsv: Now Simon's mother-in-law was in bed with a fever, and they told him about her at once.

mark:1:31.niv: So he went to her, took her hand and helped her up. The fever left her and she began to wait on them.
mark:1:31.csb: So he went to her, took her by the hand, and raised her up. The fever left her, and she began to serve them.
mark:1:31.nrsv: He came and took her by the hand and lifted her up. Then the fever left her, and she began to serve them.

mark:1:32.niv: That evening after sunset the people brought to Jesus all the sick and demon-possessed.
mark:1:32.csb: When evening came, after the sun had set, they brought to him all those who were sick and demon-possessed.
mark:1:32.nrsv: That evening, at sundown, they brought to him all who were sick or possessed with demons.

mark:1:33.niv: The whole town gathered at the door,
mark:1:33.csb: The whole town was assembled at the door,
mark:1:33.nrsv: And the whole city was gathered around the door.

mark:1:34.niv: and Jesus healed many who had various diseases. He also drove out many demons, but he would not let the demons speak because they knew who he was.
mark:1:34.csb: and he healed many who were sick with various diseases and drove out many demons. And he would not permit the demons to speak, because they knew him.
mark:1:34.nrsv: And he cured many who were sick with various diseases, and cast out many demons; and he would not permit the demons to speak, because they knew him.

mark:1:35.niv: Very early in the morning, while it was still dark, Jesus got up, left the house and went off to a solitary place, where he prayed.
mark:1:35.csb: Very early in the morning, while it was still dark, he got up, went out, and made his way to a deserted place; and there he was praying.
mark:1:35.nrsv: In the morning, while it was still very dark, he got up and went out to a deserted place, and there he prayed.

mark:1:36.niv: Simon and his companions went to look for him,
mark:1:36.csb: Simon and his companions searched for him,
mark:1:36.nrsv: And Simon and his companions hunted for him.

mark:1:37.niv: and when they found him, they exclaimed: "Everyone is looking for you!"
mark:1:37.csb: and when they found him they said, “Everyone is looking for you.”
mark:1:37.nrsv: When they found him, they said to him, "Everyone is searching for you."

mark:1:38.niv: Jesus replied, "Let us go somewhere else--to the nearby villages--so I can preach there also. That is why I have come."
mark:1:38.csb: And he said to them, “Let’s go on to the neighboring villages so that I may preach there too. This is why I have come.”
mark:1:38.nrsv: He answered, "Let us go on to the neighboring towns, so that I may proclaim the message there also; for that is what I came out to do."

mark:1:39.niv: So he traveled throughout Galilee, preaching in their synagogues and driving out demons.
mark:1:39.csb: He went into all of Galilee, preaching in their synagogues and driving out demons.
mark:1:39.nrsv: And he went throughout Galilee, proclaiming the message in their synagogues and casting out demons.

mark:1:40.niv: A man with leprosy came to him and begged him on his knees, "If you are willing, you can make me clean."
mark:1:40.csb: Then a man with leprosy came to him and, on his knees, begged him: “If you are willing, you can make me clean.”
mark:1:40.nrsv: A leper came to him begging him, and kneeling he said to him, "If you choose, you can make me clean."

mark:1:41.niv: Filled with compassion, Jesus reached out his hand and touched the man. "I am willing," he said. "Be clean!"
mark:1:41.csb: Moved with compassion, Jesus reached out his hand and touched him. “I am willing,” he told him. “Be made clean.”
mark:1:41.nrsv: Moved with pity, Jesus stretched out his hand and touched him, and said to him, "I do choose. Be made clean!"

mark:1:42.niv: Immediately the leprosy left him and he was cured.
mark:1:42.csb: Immediately the leprosy left him, and he was made clean.
mark:1:42.nrsv: Immediately the leprosy left him, and he was made clean.

mark:1:43.niv: Jesus sent him away at once with a strong warning:
mark:1:43.csb: Then he sternly warned him and sent him away at once,
mark:1:43.nrsv: After sternly warning him he sent him away at once,

mark:1:44.niv: "See that you don't tell this to anyone. But go, show yourself to the priest and offer the sacrifices that Moses commanded for your cleansing, as a testimony to them."
mark:1:44.csb: telling him, “See that you say nothing to anyone; but go and show yourself to the priest, and offer what Moses commanded for your cleansing, as a testimony to them.”
mark:1:44.nrsv: saying to him, "See that you say nothing to anyone; but go, show yourself to the priest, and offer for your cleansing what Moses commanded, as a testimony to them."

mark:1:45.niv: Instead he went out and began to talk freely, spreading the news. As a result, Jesus could no longer enter a town openly but stayed outside in lonely places. Yet the people still came to him from everywhere.
mark:1:45.csb: Yet he went out and began to proclaim it widely and to spread the news, with the result that Jesus could no longer enter a town openly. But he was out in deserted places, and they came to him from everywhere.
mark:1:45.nrsv: But he went out and began to proclaim it freely, and to spread the word, so that Jesus could no longer go into a town openly, but stayed out in the country; and people came to him from every quarter.

mark:2:1.niv: A few days later, when Jesus again entered Capernaum, the people heard that he had come home.
mark:2:1.csb: When he entered Capernaum again after some days, it was reported that he was at home.
mark:2:1.nrsv: When he returned to Capernaum after some days, it was reported that he was at home.

mark:2:2.niv: So many gathered that there was no room left, not even outside the door, and he preached the word to them.
mark:2:2.csb: So many people gathered together that there was no more room, not even in the doorway, and he was speaking the word to them.
mark:2:2.nrsv: So many gathered around that there was no longer room for them, not even in front of the door; and he was speaking the word to them.

mark:2:3.niv: Some men came, bringing to him a paralytic, carried by four of them.
mark:2:3.csb: They came to him bringing a paralytic, carried by four of them.
mark:2:3.nrsv: Then some people came, bringing to him a paralyzed man, carried by four of them.

mark:2:4.niv: Since they could not get him to Jesus because of the crowd, they made an opening in the roof above Jesus and, after digging through it, lowered the mat the paralyzed man was lying on.
mark:2:4.csb: Since they were not able to bring him to Jesus because of the crowd, they removed the roof above him, and after digging through it, they lowered the mat on which the paralytic was lying.
mark:2:4.nrsv: And when they could not bring him to Jesus because of the crowd, they removed the roof above him; and after having dug through it, they let down the mat on which the paralytic lay.

mark:2:5.niv: When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralytic, "Son, your sins are forgiven."
mark:2:5.csb: Seeing their faith, Jesus told the paralytic, “Son, your sins are forgiven.”
mark:2:5.nrsv: When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralytic, "Son, your sins are forgiven."

mark:2:6.niv: Now some teachers of the law were sitting there, thinking to themselves,
mark:2:6.csb: But some of the scribes were sitting there, questioning in their hearts:
mark:2:6.nrsv: Now some of the scribes were sitting there, questioning in their hearts,

mark:2:7.niv: "Why does this fellow talk like that? He's blaspheming! Who can forgive sins but God alone?"
mark:2:7.csb: “Why does he speak like this? He’s blaspheming! Who can forgive sins but God alone?”
mark:2:7.nrsv: "Why does this fellow speak in this way? It is blasphemy! Who can forgive sins but God alone?"

mark:2:8.niv: Immediately Jesus knew in his spirit that this was what they were thinking in their hearts, and he said to them, "Why are you thinking these things?
mark:2:8.csb: Right away Jesus perceived in his spirit that they were thinking like this within themselves and said to them, “Why are you thinking these things in your hearts?
mark:2:8.nrsv: At once Jesus perceived in his spirit that they were discussing these questions among themselves; and he said to them, "Why do you raise such questions in your hearts?

mark:2:9.niv: Which is easier: to say to the paralytic, 'Your sins are forgiven,' or to say, 'Get up, take your mat and walk'?
mark:2:9.csb: Which is easier: to say to the paralytic, ‘Your sins are forgiven,’ or to say, ‘Get up, take your mat, and walk’?
mark:2:9.nrsv: Which is easier, to say to the paralytic, 'Your sins are forgiven,' or to say, 'Stand up and take your mat and walk'?

mark:2:10.niv: But that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins...." He said to the paralytic,
mark:2:10.csb: But so that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins” — he told the paralytic —
mark:2:10.nrsv: But so that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins"-- he said to the paralytic--

mark:2:11.niv: "I tell you, get up, take your mat and go home."
mark:2:11.csb: “I tell you: get up, take your mat, and go home.”
mark:2:11.nrsv: "I say to you, stand up, take your mat and go to your home."

mark:2:12.niv: He got up, took his mat and walked out in full view of them all. This amazed everyone and they praised God, saying, "We have never seen anything like this!"
mark:2:12.csb: Immediately he got up, took the mat, and went out in front of everyone. As a result, they were all astounded and gave glory to God, saying, “We have never seen anything like this!”
mark:2:12.nrsv: And he stood up, and immediately took the mat and went out before all of them; so that they were all amazed and glorified God, saying, "We have never seen anything like this!"

mark:2:13.niv: Once again Jesus went out beside the lake. A large crowd came to him, and he began to teach them.
mark:2:13.csb: Jesus went out again beside the sea. The whole crowd was coming to him, and he was teaching them.
mark:2:13.nrsv: Jesus went out again beside the sea; the whole crowd gathered around him, and he taught them.

mark:2:14.niv: As he walked along, he saw Levi son of Alphaeus sitting at the tax collector's booth. "Follow me," Jesus told him, and Levi got up and followed him.
mark:2:14.csb: Then, passing by, he saw Levi the son of Alphaeus sitting at the toll booth, and he said to him, “Follow me,” and he got up and followed him.
mark:2:14.nrsv: As he was walking along, he saw Levi son of Alphaeus sitting at the tax booth, and he said to him, "Follow me." And he got up and followed him.

mark:2:15.niv: While Jesus was having dinner at Levi's house, many tax collectors and "sinners" were eating with him and his disciples, for there were many who followed him.
mark:2:15.csb: While he was reclining at the table in Levi’s house, many tax collectors and sinners were eating with Jesus and his disciples, for there were many who were following him.
mark:2:15.nrsv: And as he sat at dinner in Levi's house, many tax collectors and sinners were also sitting with Jesus and his disciples-- for there were many who followed him.

mark:2:16.niv: When the teachers of the law who were Pharisees saw him eating with the "sinners" and tax collectors, they asked his disciples: "Why does he eat with tax collectors and 'sinners'?"
mark:2:16.csb: When the scribes who were Pharisees saw that he was eating with sinners and tax collectors, they asked his disciples, “Why does he eat with tax collectors and sinners?”
mark:2:16.nrsv: When the scribes of the Pharisees saw that he was eating with sinners and tax collectors, they said to his disciples, "Why does he eat with tax collectors and sinners?"

mark:2:17.niv: On hearing this, Jesus said to them, "It is not the healthy who need a doctor, but the sick. I have not come to call the righteous, but sinners."
mark:2:17.csb: When Jesus heard this, he told them, “It is not those who are well who need a doctor, but those who are sick. I didn’t come to call the righteous, but sinners.”
mark:2:17.nrsv: When Jesus heard this, he said to them, "Those who are well have no need of a physician, but those who are sick; I have come to call not the righteous but sinners."

mark:2:18.niv: Now John's disciples and the Pharisees were fasting. Some people came and asked Jesus, "How is it that John's disciples and the disciples of the Pharisees are fasting, but yours are not?"
mark:2:18.csb: Now John’s disciples and the Pharisees were fasting. People came and asked him, “Why do John’s disciples and the Pharisees’ disciples fast, but your disciples do not fast?”
mark:2:18.nrsv: Now John's disciples and the Pharisees were fasting; and people came and said to him, "Why do John's disciples and the disciples of the Pharisees fast, but your disciples do not fast?"

mark:2:19.niv: Jesus answered, "How can the guests of the bridegroom fast while he is with them? They cannot, so long as they have him with them.
mark:2:19.csb: Jesus said to them, “The wedding guests cannot fast while the groom is with them, can they? As long as they have the groom with them, they cannot fast.
mark:2:19.nrsv: Jesus said to them, "The wedding guests cannot fast while the bridegroom is with them, can they? As long as they have the bridegroom with them, they cannot fast.

mark:2:20.niv: But the time will come when the bridegroom will be taken from them, and on that day they will fast.
mark:2:20.csb: But the time will come when the groom will be taken away from them, and then they will fast on that day.
mark:2:20.nrsv: The days will come when the bridegroom is taken away from them, and then they will fast on that day.

mark:2:21.niv: "No one sews a patch of unshrunk cloth on an old garment. If he does, the new piece will pull away from the old, making the tear worse.
mark:2:21.csb: No one sews a patch of unshrunk cloth on an old garment. Otherwise, the new patch pulls away from the old cloth, and a worse tear is made.
mark:2:21.nrsv: "No one sews a piece of unshrunk cloth on an old cloak; otherwise, the patch pulls away from it, the new from the old, and a worse tear is made.

mark:2:22.niv: And no one pours new wine into old wineskins. If he does, the wine will burst the skins, and both the wine and the wineskins will be ruined. No, he pours new wine into new wineskins."
mark:2:22.csb: And no one puts new wine into old wineskins. Otherwise, the wine will burst the skins, and the wine is lost as well as the skins. No, new wine is put into fresh wineskins.”
mark:2:22.nrsv: And no one puts new wine into old wineskins; otherwise, the wine will burst the skins, and the wine is lost, and so are the skins; but one puts new wine into fresh wineskins."

mark:2:23.niv: One Sabbath Jesus was going through the grainfields, and as his disciples walked along, they began to pick some heads of grain.
mark:2:23.csb: On the Sabbath he was going through the grainfields, and his disciples began to make their way, picking some heads of grain.
mark:2:23.nrsv: One sabbath he was going through the grainfields; and as they made their way his disciples began to pluck heads of grain.

mark:2:24.niv: The Pharisees said to him, "Look, why are they doing what is unlawful on the Sabbath?"
mark:2:24.csb: The Pharisees said to him, “Look, why are they doing what is not lawful on the Sabbath?”
mark:2:24.nrsv: The Pharisees said to him, "Look, why are they doing what is not lawful on the sabbath?"

mark:2:25.niv: He answered, "Have you never read what David did when he and his companions were hungry and in need?
mark:2:25.csb: He said to them, “Have you never read what David and those who were with him did when he was in need and hungry —
mark:2:25.nrsv: And he said to them, "Have you never read what David did when he and his companions were hungry and in need of food?

mark:2:26.niv: In the days of Abiathar the high priest, he entered the house of God and ate the consecrated bread, which is lawful only for priests to eat. And he also gave some to his companions."
mark:2:26.csb: how he entered the house of God in the time of Abiathar the high priest and ate the bread of the Presence — which is not lawful for anyone to eat except the priests — and also gave some to his companions?”
mark:2:26.nrsv: He entered the house of God, when Abiathar was high priest, and ate the bread of the Presence, which it is not lawful for any but the priests to eat, and he gave some to his companions."

mark:2:27.niv: Then he said to them, "The Sabbath was made for man, not man for the Sabbath.
mark:2:27.csb: Then he told them, “The Sabbath was made for man and not man for the Sabbath.
mark:2:27.nrsv: Then he said to them, "The sabbath was made for humankind, and not humankind for the sabbath;

mark:2:28.niv: So the Son of Man is Lord even of the Sabbath."
mark:2:28.csb: So then, the Son of Man is Lord even of the Sabbath.”
mark:2:28.nrsv: so the Son of Man is lord even of the sabbath."

mark:3:1.niv: Another time he went into the synagogue, and a man with a shriveled hand was there.
mark:3:1.csb: Jesus entered the synagogue again, and a man was there who had a shriveled hand.
mark:3:1.nrsv: Again he entered the synagogue, and a man was there who had a withered hand.

mark:3:2.niv: Some of them were looking for a reason to accuse Jesus, so they watched him closely to see if he would heal him on the Sabbath.
mark:3:2.csb: In order to accuse him, they were watching him closely to see whether he would heal him on the Sabbath.
mark:3:2.nrsv: They watched him to see whether he would cure him on the sabbath, so that they might accuse him.

mark:3:3.niv: Jesus said to the man with the shriveled hand, "Stand up in front of everyone."
mark:3:3.csb: He told the man with the shriveled hand, “Stand before us.”
mark:3:3.nrsv: And he said to the man who had the withered hand, "Come forward."

mark:3:4.niv: Then Jesus asked them, "Which is lawful on the Sabbath: to do good or to do evil, to save life or to kill?" But they remained silent.
mark:3:4.csb: Then he said to them, “Is it lawful to do good on the Sabbath or to do evil, to save life or to kill?” But they were silent.
mark:3:4.nrsv: Then he said to them, "Is it lawful to do good or to do harm on the sabbath, to save life or to kill?" But they were silent.

mark:3:5.niv: He looked around at them in anger and, deeply distressed at their stubborn hearts, said to the man, "Stretch out your hand." He stretched it out, and his hand was completely restored.
mark:3:5.csb: After looking around at them with anger, he was grieved at the hardness of their hearts and told the man, “Stretch out your hand.” So he stretched it out, and his hand was restored.
mark:3:5.nrsv: He looked around at them with anger; he was grieved at their hardness of heart and said to the man, "Stretch out your hand." He stretched it out, and his hand was restored.

mark:3:6.niv: Then the Pharisees went out and began to plot with the Herodians how they might kill Jesus.
mark:3:6.csb: Immediately the Pharisees went out and started plotting with the Herodians against him, how they might kill him.
mark:3:6.nrsv: The Pharisees went out and immediately conspired with the Herodians against him, how to destroy him.

mark:3:7.niv: Jesus withdrew with his disciples to the lake, and a large crowd from Galilee followed.
mark:3:7.csb: Jesus departed with his disciples to the sea, and a large crowd followed from Galilee, and a large crowd followed from Judea,
mark:3:7.nrsv: Jesus departed with his disciples to the sea, and a great multitude from Galilee followed him;

mark:3:8.niv: When they heard all he was doing, many people came to him from Judea, Jerusalem, Idumea, and the regions across the Jordan and around Tyre and Sidon.
mark:3:8.csb: Jerusalem, Idumea, beyond the Jordan, and around Tyre and Sidon. The large crowd came to him because they heard about everything he was doing.
mark:3:8.nrsv: hearing all that he was doing, they came to him in great numbers from Judea, Jerusalem, Idumea, beyond the Jordan, and the region around Tyre and Sidon.

mark:3:9.niv: Because of the crowd he told his disciples to have a small boat ready for him, to keep the people from crowding him.
mark:3:9.csb: Then he told his disciples to have a small boat ready for him, so that the crowd wouldn’t crush him.
mark:3:9.nrsv: He told his disciples to have a boat ready for him because of the crowd, so that they would not crush him;

mark:3:10.niv: For he had healed many, so that those with diseases were pushing forward to touch him.
mark:3:10.csb: Since he had healed many, all who had diseases were pressing toward him to touch him.
mark:3:10.nrsv: for he had cured many, so that all who had diseases pressed upon him to touch him.

mark:3:11.niv: Whenever the evil spirits saw him, they fell down before him and cried out, "You are the Son of God."
mark:3:11.csb: Whenever the unclean spirits saw him, they fell down before him and cried out, “You are the Son of God!”
mark:3:11.nrsv: Whenever the unclean spirits saw him, they fell down before him and shouted, "You are the Son of God!"

mark:3:12.niv: But he gave them strict orders not to tell who he was.
mark:3:12.csb: And he would strongly warn them not to make him known.
mark:3:12.nrsv: But he sternly ordered them not to make him known.

mark:3:13.niv: Jesus went up on a mountainside and called to him those he wanted, and they came to him.
mark:3:13.csb: Jesus went up the mountain and summoned those he wanted, and they came to him.
mark:3:13.nrsv: He went up the mountain and called to him those whom he wanted, and they came to him.

mark:3:14.niv: He appointed twelve--designating them apostles--that they might be with him and that he might send them out to preach
mark:3:14.csb: He appointed twelve, whom he also named apostles, to be with him, to send them out to preach,
mark:3:14.nrsv: And he appointed twelve, whom he also named apostles, to be with him, and to be sent out to proclaim the message,

mark:3:15.niv: and to have authority to drive out demons.
mark:3:15.csb: and to have authority to drive out demons.
mark:3:15.nrsv: and to have authority to cast out demons.

mark:3:16.niv: These are the twelve he appointed: Simon (to whom he gave the name Peter);
mark:3:16.csb: He appointed the Twelve: To Simon, he gave the name Peter;
mark:3:16.nrsv: So he appointed the twelve: Simon (to whom he gave the name Peter);

mark:3:17.niv: James son of Zebedee and his brother John (to them he gave the name Boanerges, which means Sons of Thunder);
mark:3:17.csb: and to James the son of Zebedee, and to his brother John, he gave the name “Boanerges” (that is, “Sons of Thunder” );
mark:3:17.nrsv: James son of Zebedee and John the brother of James (to whom he gave the name Boanerges, that is, Sons of Thunder);

mark:3:18.niv: Andrew, Philip, Bartholomew, Matthew, Thomas, James son of Alphaeus, Thaddaeus, Simon the Zealot
mark:3:18.csb: Andrew; Philip and Bartholomew; Matthew and Thomas; James the son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus; Simon the Zealot,
mark:3:18.nrsv: and Andrew, and Philip, and Bartholomew, and Matthew, and Thomas, and James son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus, and Simon the Cananaean,

mark:3:19.niv: and Judas Iscariot, who betrayed him.
mark:3:19.csb: and Judas Iscariot, who also betrayed him.
mark:3:19.nrsv: and Judas Iscariot, who betrayed him. Then he went home;

mark:3:20.niv: Then Jesus entered a house, and again a crowd gathered, so that he and his disciples were not even able to eat.
mark:3:20.csb: Jesus entered a house, and the crowd gathered again so that they were not even able to eat.
mark:3:20.nrsv: and the crowd came together again, so that they could not even eat.

mark:3:21.niv: When his family heard about this, they went to take charge of him, for they said, "He is out of his mind."
mark:3:21.csb: When his family heard this, they set out to restrain him, because they said, “He’s out of his mind.”
mark:3:21.nrsv: When his family heard it, they went out to restrain him, for people were saying, "He has gone out of his mind."

mark:3:22.niv: And the teachers of the law who came down from Jerusalem said, "He is possessed by Beelzebub! By the prince of demons he is driving out demons."
mark:3:22.csb: The scribes who had come down from Jerusalem said, “He is possessed by Beelzebul,” and, “He drives out demons by the ruler of the demons.”
mark:3:22.nrsv: And the scribes who came down from Jerusalem said, "He has Beelzebul, and by the ruler of the demons he casts out demons."

mark:3:23.niv: So Jesus called them and spoke to them in parables: "How can Satan drive out Satan?
mark:3:23.csb: So he summoned them and spoke to them in parables: “How can Satan drive out Satan?
mark:3:23.nrsv: And he called them to him, and spoke to them in parables, "How can Satan cast out Satan?

mark:3:24.niv: If a kingdom is divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.
mark:3:24.csb: If a kingdom is divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.
mark:3:24.nrsv: If a kingdom is divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.

mark:3:25.niv: If a house is divided against itself, that house cannot stand.
mark:3:25.csb: If a house is divided against itself, that house cannot stand.
mark:3:25.nrsv: And if a house is divided against itself, that house will not be able to stand.

mark:3:26.niv: And if Satan opposes himself and is divided, he cannot stand; his end has come.
mark:3:26.csb: And if Satan opposes himself and is divided, he cannot stand but is finished.
mark:3:26.nrsv: And if Satan has risen up against himself and is divided, he cannot stand, but his end has come.

mark:3:27.niv: In fact, no one can enter a strong man's house and carry off his possessions unless he first ties up the strong man. Then he can rob his house.
mark:3:27.csb: But no one can enter a strong man’s house and plunder his possessions unless he first ties up the strong man. Then he can plunder his house.
mark:3:27.nrsv: But no one can enter a strong man's house and plunder his property without first tying up the strong man; then indeed the house can be plundered.

mark:3:28.niv: I tell you the truth, all the sins and blasphemies of men will be forgiven them.
mark:3:28.csb: “Truly I tell you, people will be forgiven for all sins and whatever blasphemies they utter.
mark:3:28.nrsv: "Truly I tell you, people will be forgiven for their sins and whatever blasphemies they utter;

mark:3:29.niv: But whoever blasphemes against the Holy Spirit will never be forgiven; he is guilty of an eternal sin."
mark:3:29.csb: But whoever blasphemes against the Holy Spirit never has forgiveness, but is guilty of an eternal sin” —
mark:3:29.nrsv: but whoever blasphemes against the Holy Spirit can never have forgiveness, but is guilty of an eternal sin"--

mark:3:30.niv: He said this because they were saying, "He has an evil spirit."
mark:3:30.csb: because they were saying, “He has an unclean spirit.”
mark:3:30.nrsv: for they had said, "He has an unclean spirit."

mark:3:31.niv: Then Jesus' mother and brothers arrived. Standing outside, they sent someone in to call him.
mark:3:31.csb: His mother and his brothers came, and standing outside, they sent word to him and called him.
mark:3:31.nrsv: Then his mother and his brothers came; and standing outside, they sent to him and called him.

mark:3:32.niv: A crowd was sitting around him, and they told him, "Your mother and brothers are outside looking for you."
mark:3:32.csb: A crowd was sitting around him and told him, “Look, your mother, your brothers, and your sisters are outside asking for you.”
mark:3:32.nrsv: A crowd was sitting around him; and they said to him, "Your mother and your brothers and sisters are outside, asking for you."

mark:3:33.niv: "Who are my mother and my brothers?" he asked.
mark:3:33.csb: He replied to them, “Who are my mother and my brothers?”
mark:3:33.nrsv: And he replied, "Who are my mother and my brothers?"

mark:3:34.niv: Then he looked at those seated in a circle around him and said, "Here are my mother and my brothers!
mark:3:34.csb: Looking at those sitting in a circle around him, he said, “Here are my mother and my brothers!
mark:3:34.nrsv: And looking at those who sat around him, he said, "Here are my mother and my brothers!

mark:3:35.niv: Whoever does God's will is my brother and sister and mother."
mark:3:35.csb: Whoever does the will of God is my brother and sister and mother.”
mark:3:35.nrsv: Whoever does the will of God is my brother and sister and mother."

mark:4:1.niv: Again Jesus began to teach by the lake. The crowd that gathered around him was so large that he got into a boat and sat in it out on the lake, while all the people were along the shore at the water's edge.
mark:4:1.csb: Again he began to teach by the sea, and a very large crowd gathered around him. So he got into a boat on the sea and sat down, while the whole crowd was by the sea on the shore.
mark:4:1.nrsv: Again he began to teach beside the sea. Such a very large crowd gathered around him that he got into a boat on the sea and sat there, while the whole crowd was beside the sea on the land.

mark:4:2.niv: He taught them many things by parables, and in his teaching said:
mark:4:2.csb: He taught them many things in parables, and in his teaching he said to them:
mark:4:2.nrsv: He began to teach them many things in parables, and in his teaching he said to them:

mark:4:3.niv: "Listen! A farmer went out to sow his seed.
mark:4:3.csb: “Listen! Consider the sower who went out to sow.
mark:4:3.nrsv: "Listen! A sower went out to sow.

mark:4:4.niv: As he was scattering the seed, some fell along the path, and the birds came and ate it up.
mark:4:4.csb: As he sowed, some seed fell along the path, and the birds came and devoured it.
mark:4:4.nrsv: And as he sowed, some seed fell on the path, and the birds came and ate it up.

mark:4:5.niv: Some fell on rocky places, where it did not have much soil. It sprang up quickly, because the soil was shallow.
mark:4:5.csb: Other seed fell on rocky ground where it didn’t have much soil, and it grew up quickly, since the soil wasn’t deep.
mark:4:5.nrsv: Other seed fell on rocky ground, where it did not have much soil, and it sprang up quickly, since it had no depth of soil.

mark:4:6.niv: But when the sun came up, the plants were scorched, and they withered because they had no root.
mark:4:6.csb: When the sun came up, it was scorched, and since it had no root, it withered away.
mark:4:6.nrsv: And when the sun rose, it was scorched; and since it had no root, it withered away.

mark:4:7.niv: Other seed fell among thorns, which grew up and choked the plants, so that they did not bear grain.
mark:4:7.csb: Other seed fell among thorns, and the thorns came up and choked it, and it didn’t produce fruit.
mark:4:7.nrsv: Other seed fell among thorns, and the thorns grew up and choked it, and it yielded no grain.

mark:4:8.niv: Still other seed fell on good soil. It came up, grew and produced a crop, multiplying thirty, sixty, or even a hundred times."
mark:4:8.csb: Still other seed fell on good ground and it grew up, producing fruit that increased thirty, sixty, and a hundred times.”
mark:4:8.nrsv: Other seed fell into good soil and brought forth grain, growing up and increasing and yielding thirty and sixty and a hundredfold."

mark:4:9.niv: Then Jesus said, "He who has ears to hear, let him hear."
mark:4:9.csb: Then he said, “Let anyone who has ears to hear listen.”
mark:4:9.nrsv: And he said, "Let anyone with ears to hear listen!"

mark:4:10.niv: When he was alone, the Twelve and the others around him asked him about the parables.
mark:4:10.csb: When he was alone, those around him with the Twelve, asked him about the parables.
mark:4:10.nrsv: When he was alone, those who were around him along with the twelve asked him about the parables.

mark:4:11.niv: He told them, "The secret of the kingdom of God has been given to you. But to those on the outside everything is said in parables
mark:4:11.csb: He answered them, “The secret of the kingdom of God has been given to you, but to those outside, everything comes in parables
mark:4:11.nrsv: And he said to them, "To you has been given the secret of the kingdom of God, but for those outside, everything comes in parables;

mark:4:12.niv: so that, "'they may be ever seeing but never perceiving, and ever hearing but never understanding; otherwise they might turn and be forgiven!'"
mark:4:12.csb: so that they may indeed look, and yet not perceive; they may indeed listen, and yet not understand; otherwise, they might turn back and be forgiven.”
mark:4:12.nrsv: in order that 'they may indeed look, but not perceive, and may indeed listen, but not understand; so that they may not turn again and be forgiven.'"

mark:4:13.niv: Then Jesus said to them, "Don't you understand this parable? How then will you understand any parable?
mark:4:13.csb: Then he said to them: “Don’t you understand this parable? How then will you understand all of the parables?
mark:4:13.nrsv: And he said to them, "Do you not understand this parable? Then how will you understand all the parables?

mark:4:15.niv: Some people are like seed along the path, where the word is sown. As soon as they hear it, Satan comes and takes away the word that was sown in them.
mark:4:15.csb: Some are like the word sown on the path. When they hear, immediately Satan comes and takes away the word sown in them.
mark:4:15.nrsv: These are the ones on the path where the word is sown: when they hear, Satan immediately comes and takes away the word that is sown in them.

mark:4:16.niv: Others, like seed sown on rocky places, hear the word and at once receive it with joy.
mark:4:16.csb: And others are like seed sown on rocky ground. When they hear the word, immediately they receive it with joy.
mark:4:16.nrsv: And these are the ones sown on rocky ground: when they hear the word, they immediately receive it with joy.

mark:4:17.niv: But since they have no root, they last only a short time. When trouble or persecution comes because of the word, they quickly fall away.
mark:4:17.csb: But they have no root; they are short-lived. When distress or persecution comes because of the word, they immediately fall away.
mark:4:17.nrsv: But they have no root, and endure only for a while; then, when trouble or persecution arises on account of the word, immediately they fall away.

mark:4:18.niv: Still others, like seed sown among thorns, hear the word;
mark:4:18.csb: Others are like seed sown among thorns; these are the ones who hear the word,
mark:4:18.nrsv: And others are those sown among the thorns: these are the ones who hear the word,

mark:4:19.niv: but the worries of this life, the deceitfulness of wealth and the desires for other things come in and choke the word, making it unfruitful.
mark:4:19.csb: but the worries of this age, the deceitfulness of wealth, and the desires for other things enter in and choke the word, and it becomes unfruitful.
mark:4:19.nrsv: but the cares of the world, and the lure of wealth, and the desire for other things come in and choke the word, and it yields nothing.

mark:4:20.niv: Others, like seed sown on good soil, hear the word, accept it, and produce a crop--thirty, sixty or even a hundred times what was sown."
mark:4:20.csb: And those like seed sown on good ground hear the word, welcome it, and produce fruit thirty, sixty, and a hundred times what was sown.”
mark:4:20.nrsv: And these are the ones sown on the good soil: they hear the word and accept it and bear fruit, thirty and sixty and a hundredfold."

mark:4:21.niv: He said to them, "Do you bring in a lamp to put it under a bowl or a bed? Instead, don't you put it on its stand?
mark:4:21.csb: He also said to them, “Is a lamp brought in to be put under a basket or under a bed? Isn’t it to be put on a lampstand?
mark:4:21.nrsv: He said to them, "Is a lamp brought in to be put under the bushel basket, or under the bed, and not on the lampstand?

mark:4:22.niv: For whatever is hidden is meant to be disclosed, and whatever is concealed is meant to be brought out into the open.
mark:4:22.csb: For there is nothing hidden that will not be revealed, and nothing concealed that will not be brought to light.
mark:4:22.nrsv: For there is nothing hidden, except to be disclosed; nor is anything secret, except to come to light.

mark:4:23.niv: If anyone has ears to hear, let him hear."
mark:4:23.csb: If anyone has ears to hear, let him listen.”
mark:4:23.nrsv: Let anyone with ears to hear listen!"

mark:4:24.niv: "Consider carefully what you hear," he continued. "With the measure you use, it will be measured to you--and even more.
mark:4:24.csb: And he said to them, “Pay attention to what you hear. By the measure you use, it will be measured to you — and more will be added to you.
mark:4:24.nrsv: And he said to them, "Pay attention to what you hear; the measure you give will be the measure you get, and still more will be given you.

mark:4:25.niv: Whoever has will be given more; whoever does not have, even what he has will be taken from him."
mark:4:25.csb: For whoever has, more will be given to him, and whoever does not have, even what he has will be taken away from him.”
mark:4:25.nrsv: For to those who have, more will be given; and from those who have nothing, even what they have will be taken away."

mark:4:26.niv: He also said, "This is what the kingdom of God is like. A man scatters seed on the ground.
mark:4:26.csb: “The kingdom of God is like this,” he said. “A man scatters seed on the ground.
mark:4:26.nrsv: He also said, "The kingdom of God is as if someone would scatter seed on the ground,

mark:4:27.niv: Night and day, whether he sleeps or gets up, the seed sprouts and grows, though he does not know how.
mark:4:27.csb: He sleeps and rises night and day; the seed sprouts and grows, although he doesn’t know how.
mark:4:27.nrsv: and would sleep and rise night and day, and the seed would sprout and grow, he does not know how.

mark:4:28.niv: All by itself the soil produces grain--first the stalk, then the head, then the full kernel in the head.
mark:4:28.csb: The soil produces a crop by itself — first the blade, then the head, and then the full grain on the head.
mark:4:28.nrsv: The earth produces of itself, first the stalk, then the head, then the full grain in the head.

mark:4:29.niv: As soon as the grain is ripe, he puts the sickle to it, because the harvest has come."
mark:4:29.csb: As soon as the crop is ready, he sends for the sickle, because the harvest has come.”
mark:4:29.nrsv: But when the grain is ripe, at once he goes in with his sickle, because the harvest has come."

mark:4:30.niv: Again he said, "What shall we say the kingdom of God is like, or what parable shall we use to describe it?
mark:4:30.csb: And he said, “With what can we compare the kingdom of God, or what parable can we use to describe it?
mark:4:30.nrsv: He also said, "With what can we compare the kingdom of God, or what parable will we use for it?

mark:4:31.niv: It is like a mustard seed, which is the smallest seed you plant in the ground.
mark:4:31.csb: It’s like a mustard seed that, when sown upon the soil, is the smallest of all the seeds on the ground.
mark:4:31.nrsv: It is like a mustard seed, which, when sown upon the ground, is the smallest of all the seeds on earth;

mark:4:32.niv: Yet when planted, it grows and becomes the largest of all garden plants, with such big branches that the birds of the air can perch in its shade."
mark:4:32.csb: And when sown, it comes up and grows taller than all the garden plants, and produces large branches, so that the birds of the sky can nest in its shade.”
mark:4:32.nrsv: yet when it is sown it grows up and becomes the greatest of all shrubs, and puts forth large branches, so that the birds of the air can make nests in its shade."

mark:4:33.niv: With many similar parables Jesus spoke the word to them, as much as they could understand.
mark:4:33.csb: He was speaking the word to them with many parables like these, as they were able to understand.
mark:4:33.nrsv: With many such parables he spoke the word to them, as they were able to hear it;

mark:4:34.niv: He did not say anything to them without using a parable. But when he was alone with his own disciples, he explained everything.
mark:4:34.csb: He did not speak to them without a parable. Privately, however, he explained everything to his own disciples.
mark:4:34.nrsv: he did not speak to them except in parables, but he explained everything in private to his disciples.

mark:4:35.niv: That day when evening came, he said to his disciples, "Let us go over to the other side."
mark:4:35.csb: On that day, when evening had come, he told them, “Let’s cross over to the other side of the sea.”
mark:4:35.nrsv: On that day, when evening had come, he said to them, "Let us go across to the other side."

mark:4:36.niv: Leaving the crowd behind, they took him along, just as he was, in the boat. There were also other boats with him.
mark:4:36.csb: So they left the crowd and took him along since he was in the boat. And other boats were with him.
mark:4:36.nrsv: And leaving the crowd behind, they took him with them in the boat, just as he was. Other boats were with him.

mark:4:37.niv: A furious squall came up, and the waves broke over the boat, so that it was nearly swamped.
mark:4:37.csb: A great windstorm arose, and the waves were breaking over the boat, so that the boat was already being swamped.
mark:4:37.nrsv: A great windstorm arose, and the waves beat into the boat, so that the boat was already being swamped.

mark:4:38.niv: Jesus was in the stern, sleeping on a cushion. The disciples woke him and said to him, "Teacher, don't you care if we drown?"
mark:4:38.csb: He was in the stern, sleeping on the cushion. So they woke him up and said to him, “Teacher! Don’t you care that we’re going to die?”
mark:4:38.nrsv: But he was in the stern, asleep on the cushion; and they woke him up and said to him, "Teacher, do you not care that we are perishing?"

mark:4:39.niv: He got up, rebuked the wind and said to the waves, "Quiet! Be still!" Then the wind died down and it was completely calm.
mark:4:39.csb: He got up, rebuked the wind, and said to the sea, “Silence! Be still!” The wind ceased, and there was a great calm.
mark:4:39.nrsv: He woke up and rebuked the wind, and said to the sea, "Peace! Be still!" Then the wind ceased, and there was a dead calm.

mark:4:40.niv: He said to his disciples, "Why are you so afraid? Do you still have no faith?"
mark:4:40.csb: Then he said to them, “Why are you afraid? Do you still have no faith?”
mark:4:40.nrsv: He said to them, "Why are you afraid? Have you still no faith?"

mark:4:41.niv: They were terrified and asked each other, "Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him!"
mark:4:41.csb: And they were terrified and asked one another, “Who then is this? Even the wind and the sea obey him!”
mark:4:41.nrsv: And they were filled with great awe and said to one another, "Who then is this, that even the wind and the sea obey him?"

mark:5:1.niv: They went across the lake to the region of the Gerasenes.
mark:5:1.csb: They came to the other side of the sea, to the region of the Gerasenes.
mark:5:1.nrsv: They came to the other side of the sea, to the country of the Gerasenes.

mark:5:2.niv: When Jesus got out of the boat, a man with an evil spirit came from the tombs to meet him.
mark:5:2.csb: As soon as he got out of the boat, a man with an unclean spirit came out of the tombs and met him.
mark:5:2.nrsv: And when he had stepped out of the boat, immediately a man out of the tombs with an unclean spirit met him.

mark:5:3.niv: This man lived in the tombs, and no one could bind him any more, not even with a chain.
mark:5:3.csb: He lived in the tombs, and no one was able to restrain him anymore — not even with a chain —
mark:5:3.nrsv: He lived among the tombs; and no one could restrain him any more, even with a chain;

mark:5:4.niv: For he had often been chained hand and foot, but he tore the chains apart and broke the irons on his feet. No one was strong enough to subdue him.
mark:5:4.csb: because he often had been bound with shackles and chains, but had torn the chains apart and smashed the shackles. No one was strong enough to subdue him.
mark:5:4.nrsv: for he had often been restrained with shackles and chains, but the chains he wrenched apart, and the shackles he broke in pieces; and no one had the strength to subdue him.

mark:5:5.niv: Night and day among the tombs and in the hills he would cry out and cut himself with stones.
mark:5:5.csb: Night and day among the tombs and on the mountains, he was always crying out and cutting himself with stones.
mark:5:5.nrsv: Night and day among the tombs and on the mountains he was always howling and bruising himself with stones.

mark:5:6.niv: When he saw Jesus from a distance, he ran and fell on his knees in front of him.
mark:5:6.csb: When he saw Jesus from a distance, he ran and knelt down before him.
mark:5:6.nrsv: When he saw Jesus from a distance, he ran and bowed down before him;

mark:5:7.niv: He shouted at the top of his voice, "What do you want with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? Swear to God that you won't torture me!"
mark:5:7.csb: And he cried out with a loud voice, “What do you have to do with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? I beg you before God, don’t torment me!”
mark:5:7.nrsv: and he shouted at the top of his voice, "What have you to do with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? I adjure you by God, do not torment me."

mark:5:8.niv: For Jesus had said to him, "Come out of this man, you evil spirit!"
mark:5:8.csb: For he had told him, “Come out of the man, you unclean spirit!”
mark:5:8.nrsv: For he had said to him, "Come out of the man, you unclean spirit!"

mark:5:9.niv: Then Jesus asked him, "What is your name?"
mark:5:9.csb: “What is your name?” he asked him. “My name is Legion,” he answered him, “because we are many.”
mark:5:9.nrsv: Then Jesus asked him, "What is your name?" He replied, "My name is Legion; for we are many."

mark:5:10.niv: "My name is Legion," he replied, "for we are many." And he begged Jesus again and again not to send them out of the area.
mark:5:10.csb: And he begged him earnestly not to send them out of the region.
mark:5:10.nrsv: He begged him earnestly not to send them out of the country.

mark:5:11.niv: A large herd of pigs was feeding on the nearby hillside.
mark:5:11.csb: A large herd of pigs was there, feeding on the hillside.
mark:5:11.nrsv: Now there on the hillside a great herd of swine was feeding;

mark:5:12.niv: The demons begged Jesus, "Send us among the pigs; allow us to go into them."
mark:5:12.csb: The demons begged him, “Send us to the pigs, so that we may enter them.”
mark:5:12.nrsv: and the unclean spirits begged him, "Send us into the swine; let us enter them."

mark:5:13.niv: He gave them permission, and the evil spirits came out and went into the pigs. The herd, about two thousand in number, rushed down the steep bank into the lake and were drowned.
mark:5:13.csb: So he gave them permission, and the unclean spirits came out and entered the pigs. The herd of about two thousand rushed down the steep bank into the sea and drowned there.
mark:5:13.nrsv: So he gave them permission. And the unclean spirits came out and entered the swine; and the herd, numbering about two thousand, rushed down the steep bank into the sea, and were drowned in the sea.

mark:5:14.niv: Those tending the pigs ran off and reported this in the town and countryside, and the people went out to see what had happened.
mark:5:14.csb: The men who tended them ran off and reported it in the town and the countryside, and people went to see what had happened.
mark:5:14.nrsv: The swineherds ran off and told it in the city and in the country. Then people came to see what it was that had happened.

mark:5:15.niv: When they came to Jesus, they saw the man who had been possessed by the legion of demons, sitting there, dressed and in his right mind; and they were afraid.
mark:5:15.csb: They came to Jesus and saw the man who had been demon-possessed, sitting there, dressed and in his right mind; and they were afraid.
mark:5:15.nrsv: They came to Jesus and saw the demoniac sitting there, clothed and in his right mind, the very man who had had the legion; and they were afraid.

mark:5:16.niv: Those who had seen it told the people what had happened to the demon-possessed man--and told about the pigs as well.
mark:5:16.csb: Those who had seen it described to them what had happened to the demon-possessed man and told about the pigs.
mark:5:16.nrsv: Those who had seen what had happened to the demoniac and to the swine reported it.

mark:5:17.niv: Then the people began to plead with Jesus to leave their region.
mark:5:17.csb: Then they began to beg him to leave their region.
mark:5:17.nrsv: Then they began to beg Jesus to leave their neighborhood.

mark:5:18.niv: As Jesus was getting into the boat, the man who had been demon-possessed begged to go with him.
mark:5:18.csb: As he was getting into the boat, the man who had been demon-possessed begged him earnestly that he might remain with him.
mark:5:18.nrsv: As he was getting into the boat, the man who had been possessed by demons begged him that he might be with him.

mark:5:19.niv: Jesus did not let him, but said, "Go home to your family and tell them how much the Lord has done for you, and how he has had mercy on you."
mark:5:19.csb: Jesus did not let him but told him, “Go home to your own people, and report to them how much the Lord has done for you and how he has had mercy on you.”
mark:5:19.nrsv: But Jesus refused, and said to him, "Go home to your friends, and tell them how much the Lord has done for you, and what mercy he has shown you."

mark:5:20.niv: So the man went away and began to tell in the Decapolis how much Jesus had done for him. And all the people were amazed.
mark:5:20.csb: So he went out and began to proclaim in the Decapolis how much Jesus had done for him, and they were all amazed.
mark:5:20.nrsv: And he went away and began to proclaim in the Decapolis how much Jesus had done for him; and everyone was amazed.

mark:5:21.niv: When Jesus had again crossed over by boat to the other side of the lake, a large crowd gathered around him while he was by the lake.
mark:5:21.csb: When Jesus had crossed over again by boat to the other side, a large crowd gathered around him while he was by the sea.
mark:5:21.nrsv: When Jesus had crossed again in the boat to the other side, a great crowd gathered around him; and he was by the sea.

mark:5:22.niv: Then one of the synagogue rulers, named Jairus, came there. Seeing Jesus, he fell at his feet
mark:5:22.csb: One of the synagogue leaders, named Jairus, came, and when he saw Jesus, he fell at his feet
mark:5:22.nrsv: Then one of the leaders of the synagogue named Jairus came and, when he saw him, fell at his feet

mark:5:23.niv: and pleaded earnestly with him, "My little daughter is dying. Please come and put your hands on her so that she will be healed and live."
mark:5:23.csb: and begged him earnestly, “My little daughter is dying. Come and lay your hands on her so that she can get well and live.”
mark:5:23.nrsv: and begged him repeatedly, "My little daughter is at the point of death. Come and lay your hands on her, so that she may be made well, and live."

mark:5:24.csb: So Jesus went with him, and a large crowd was following and pressing against him.
mark:5:24.nrsv: So he went with him. And a large crowd followed him and pressed in on him.

mark:5:25.niv: A large crowd followed and pressed around him. And a woman was there who had been subject to bleeding for twelve years.
mark:5:25.csb: Now a woman suffering from bleeding for twelve years
mark:5:25.nrsv: Now there was a woman who had been suffering from hemorrhages for twelve years.

mark:5:26.niv: She had suffered a great deal under the care of many doctors and had spent all she had, yet instead of getting better she grew worse.
mark:5:26.csb: had endured much under many doctors. She had spent everything she had and was not helped at all. On the contrary, she became worse.
mark:5:26.nrsv: She had endured much under many physicians, and had spent all that she had; and she was no better, but rather grew worse.

mark:5:27.niv: When she heard about Jesus, she came up behind him in the crowd and touched his cloak,
mark:5:27.csb: Having heard about Jesus, she came up behind him in the crowd and touched his clothing.
mark:5:27.nrsv: She had heard about Jesus, and came up behind him in the crowd and touched his cloak,

mark:5:28.niv: because she thought, "If I just touch his clothes, I will be healed."
mark:5:28.csb: For she said, “If I just touch his clothes, I’ll be made well.”
mark:5:28.nrsv: for she said, "If I but touch his clothes, I will be made well."

mark:5:29.niv: Immediately her bleeding stopped and she felt in her body that she was freed from her suffering.
mark:5:29.csb: Instantly her flow of blood ceased, and she sensed in her body that she was healed of her affliction.
mark:5:29.nrsv: Immediately her hemorrhage stopped; and she felt in her body that she was healed of her disease.

mark:5:30.niv: At once Jesus realized that power had gone out from him. He turned around in the crowd and asked, "Who touched my clothes?"
mark:5:30.csb: At once Jesus realized in himself that power had gone out from him. He turned around in the crowd and said, “Who touched my clothes?”
mark:5:30.nrsv: Immediately aware that power had gone forth from him, Jesus turned about in the crowd and said, "Who touched my clothes?"

mark:5:31.niv: "You see the people crowding against you," his disciples answered, "and yet you can ask, 'Who touched me?'"
mark:5:31.csb: His disciples said to him, “You see the crowd pressing against you, and yet you say, ‘Who touched me?’ ”
mark:5:31.nrsv: And his disciples said to him, "You see the crowd pressing in on you; how can you say, 'Who touched me?'"

mark:5:32.niv: But Jesus kept looking around to see who had done it.
mark:5:32.csb: But he was looking around to see who had done this.
mark:5:32.nrsv: He looked all around to see who had done it.

mark:5:33.niv: Then the woman, knowing what had happened to her, came and fell at his feet and, trembling with fear, told him the whole truth.
mark:5:33.csb: The woman, with fear and trembling, knowing what had happened to her, came and fell down before him, and told him the whole truth.
mark:5:33.nrsv: But the woman, knowing what had happened to her, came in fear and trembling, fell down before him, and told him the whole truth.

mark:5:34.niv: He said to her, "Daughter, your faith has healed you. Go in peace and be freed from your suffering."
mark:5:34.csb: “Daughter,” he said to her, “your faith has saved you. Go in peace and be healed from your affliction.”
mark:5:34.nrsv: He said to her, "Daughter, your faith has made you well; go in peace, and be healed of your disease."

mark:5:35.niv: While Jesus was still speaking, some men came from the house of Jairus, the synagogue ruler. "Your daughter is dead," they said. "Why bother the teacher any more?"
mark:5:35.csb: While he was still speaking, people came from the synagogue leader’s house and said, “Your daughter is dead. Why bother the teacher anymore?”
mark:5:35.nrsv: While he was still speaking, some people came from the leader's house to say, "Your daughter is dead. Why trouble the teacher any further?"

mark:5:36.niv: Ignoring what they said, Jesus told the synagogue ruler, "Don't be afraid; just believe."
