mark:5:36.csb: When Jesus overheard what was said, he told the synagogue leader, “Don’t be afraid. Only believe.”
mark:5:36.nrsv: But overhearing what they said, Jesus said to the leader of the synagogue, "Do not fear, only believe."

mark:5:37.niv: He did not let anyone follow him except Peter, James and John the brother of James.
mark:5:37.csb: He did not let anyone accompany him except Peter, James, and John, James’s brother.
mark:5:37.nrsv: He allowed no one to follow him except Peter, James, and John, the brother of James.

mark:5:38.niv: When they came to the home of the synagogue ruler, Jesus saw a commotion, with people crying and wailing loudly.
mark:5:38.csb: They came to the leader’s house, and he saw a commotion — people weeping and wailing loudly.
mark:5:38.nrsv: When they came to the house of the leader of the synagogue, he saw a commotion, people weeping and wailing loudly.

mark:5:39.niv: He went in and said to them, "Why all this commotion and wailing? The child is not dead but asleep."
mark:5:39.csb: He went in and said to them, “Why are you making a commotion and weeping? The child is not dead but asleep.”
mark:5:39.nrsv: When he had entered, he said to them, "Why do you make a commotion and weep? The child is not dead but sleeping."

mark:5:40.csb: They laughed at him, but he put them all outside. He took the child’s father, mother, and those who were with him, and entered the place where the child was.
mark:5:40.nrsv: And they laughed at him. Then he put them all outside, and took the child's father and mother and those who were with him, and went in where the child was.

mark:5:41.niv: After he put them all out, he took the child's father and mother and the disciples who were with him, and went in where the child was. He took her by the hand and said to her, "Talitha koum!" (which means, "Little girl, I say to you, get up!" ).
mark:5:41.csb: Then he took the child by the hand and said to her, “Talitha koum” (which is translated, “Little girl, I say to you, get up” ).
mark:5:41.nrsv: He took her by the hand and said to her, "Talitha cum," which means, "Little girl, get up!"

mark:5:42.niv: Immediately the girl stood up and walked around (she was twelve years old). At this they were completely astonished.
mark:5:42.csb: Immediately the girl got up and began to walk. (She was twelve years old.) At this they were utterly astounded.
mark:5:42.nrsv: And immediately the girl got up and began to walk about (she was twelve years of age). At this they were overcome with amazement.

mark:5:43.niv: He gave strict orders not to let anyone know about this, and told them to give her something to eat.
mark:5:43.csb: Then he gave them strict orders that no one should know about this and told them to give her something to eat.
mark:5:43.nrsv: He strictly ordered them that no one should know this, and told them to give her something to eat.

mark:6:1.niv: Jesus left there and went to his hometown, accompanied by his disciples.
mark:6:1.csb: He left there and came to his hometown, and his disciples followed him.
mark:6:1.nrsv: He left that place and came to his hometown, and his disciples followed him.

mark:6:2.niv: When the Sabbath came, he began to teach in the synagogue, and many who heard him were amazed.
mark:6:2.csb: When the Sabbath came, he began to teach in the synagogue, and many who heard him were astonished. “Where did this man get these things?” they said. “What is this wisdom that has been given to him, and how are these miracles performed by his hands?
mark:6:2.nrsv: On the sabbath he began to teach in the synagogue, and many who heard him were astounded. They said, "Where did this man get all this? What is this wisdom that has been given to him? What deeds of power are being done by his hands!

mark:6:3.niv: "Where did this man get these things?" they asked. "What's this wisdom that has been given him, that he even does miracles! Isn't this the carpenter? Isn't this Mary's son and the brother of James, Joseph, Judas and Simon? Aren't his sisters here with us?" And they took offense at him.
mark:6:3.csb: Isn’t this the carpenter, the son of Mary, and the brother of James, Joses, Judas, and Simon? And aren’t his sisters here with us?” So they were offended by him.
mark:6:3.nrsv: Is not this the carpenter, the son of Mary and brother of James and Joses and Judas and Simon, and are not his sisters here with us?" And they took offense at him.

mark:6:4.niv: Jesus said to them, "Only in his hometown, among his relatives and in his own house is a prophet without honor."
mark:6:4.csb: Jesus said to them, “A prophet is not without honor except in his hometown, among his relatives, and in his household.”
mark:6:4.nrsv: Then Jesus said to them, "Prophets are not without honor, except in their hometown, and among their own kin, and in their own house."

mark:6:5.niv: He could not do any miracles there, except lay his hands on a few sick people and heal them.
mark:6:5.csb: He was not able to do a miracle there, except that he laid his hands on a few sick people and healed them.
mark:6:5.nrsv: And he could do no deed of power there, except that he laid his hands on a few sick people and cured them.

mark:6:6.niv: And he was amazed at their lack of faith.
mark:6:6.csb: And he was amazed at their unbelief. He was going around the villages teaching.
mark:6:6.nrsv: And he was amazed at their unbelief. Then he went about among the villages teaching.

mark:6:7.niv: Then Jesus went around teaching from village to village. Calling the Twelve to him, he sent them out two by two and gave them authority over evil spirits.
mark:6:7.csb: He summoned the Twelve and began to send them out in pairs and gave them authority over unclean spirits.
mark:6:7.nrsv: He called the twelve and began to send them out two by two, and gave them authority over the unclean spirits.

mark:6:8.niv: These were his instructions: "Take nothing for the journey except a staff--no bread, no bag, no money in your belts.
mark:6:8.csb: He instructed them to take nothing for the road except a staff — no bread, no traveling bag, no money in their belts,
mark:6:8.nrsv: He ordered them to take nothing for their journey except a staff; no bread, no bag, no money in their belts;

mark:6:9.niv: Wear sandals but not an extra tunic.
mark:6:9.csb: but to wear sandals and not put on an extra shirt.
mark:6:9.nrsv: but to wear sandals and not to put on two tunics.

mark:6:10.niv: Whenever you enter a house, stay there until you leave that town.
mark:6:10.csb: He said to them, “Whenever you enter a house, stay there until you leave that place.
mark:6:10.nrsv: He said to them, "Wherever you enter a house, stay there until you leave the place.

mark:6:11.niv: And if any place will not welcome you or listen to you, shake the dust off your feet when you leave, as a testimony against them."
mark:6:11.csb: If any place does not welcome you or listen to you, when you leave there, shake the dust off your feet as a testimony against them.”
mark:6:11.nrsv: If any place will not welcome you and they refuse to hear you, as you leave, shake off the dust that is on your feet as a testimony against them."

mark:6:12.niv: They went out and preached that people should repent.
mark:6:12.csb: So they went out and preached that people should repent.
mark:6:12.nrsv: So they went out and proclaimed that all should repent.

mark:6:13.niv: They drove out many demons and anointed many sick people with oil and healed them.
mark:6:13.csb: They drove out many demons, anointed many sick people with oil and healed them.
mark:6:13.nrsv: They cast out many demons, and anointed with oil many who were sick and cured them.

mark:6:14.niv: King Herod heard about this, for Jesus' name had become well known. Some were saying, "John the Baptist has been raised from the dead, and that is why miraculous powers are at work in him."
mark:6:14.csb: King Herod heard about it, because Jesus’s name had become well known. Some said, “John the Baptist has been raised from the dead, and that’s why miraculous powers are at work in him.”
mark:6:14.nrsv: King Herod heard of it, for Jesus' name had become known. Some were saying, "John the baptizer has been raised from the dead; and for this reason these powers are at work in him."

mark:6:15.niv: Others said, "He is Elijah." And still others claimed, "He is a prophet, like one of the prophets of long ago."
mark:6:15.csb: But others said, “He’s Elijah.” Still others said, “He’s a prophet, like one of the prophets from long ago.”
mark:6:15.nrsv: But others said, "It is Elijah." And others said, "It is a prophet, like one of the prophets of old."

mark:6:16.niv: But when Herod heard this, he said, "John, the man I beheaded, has been raised from the dead!"
mark:6:16.csb: When Herod heard of it, he said, “John, the one I beheaded, has been raised!”
mark:6:16.nrsv: But when Herod heard of it, he said, "John, whom I beheaded, has been raised."

mark:6:17.niv: For Herod himself had given orders to have John arrested, and he had him bound and put in prison. He did this because of Herodias, his brother Philip's wife, whom he had married.
mark:6:17.csb: For Herod himself had given orders to arrest John and to chain him in prison on account of Herodias, his brother Philip’s wife, because he had married her.
mark:6:17.nrsv: For Herod himself had sent men who arrested John, bound him, and put him in prison on account of Herodias, his brother Philip's wife, because Herod had married her.

mark:6:18.niv: For John had been saying to Herod, "It is not lawful for you to have your brother's wife."
mark:6:18.csb: John had been telling Herod, “It is not lawful for you to have your brother’s wife.”
mark:6:18.nrsv: For John had been telling Herod, "It is not lawful for you to have your brother's wife."

mark:6:19.niv: So Herodias nursed a grudge against John and wanted to kill him. But she was not able to,
mark:6:19.csb: So Herodias held a grudge against him and wanted to kill him. But she could not,
mark:6:19.nrsv: And Herodias had a grudge against him, and wanted to kill him. But she could not,

mark:6:20.niv: because Herod feared John and protected him, knowing him to be a righteous and holy man. When Herod heard John, he was greatly puzzled; yet he liked to listen to him.
mark:6:20.csb: because Herod feared John and protected him, knowing he was a righteous and holy man. When Herod heard him he would be very perplexed, and yet he liked to listen to him.
mark:6:20.nrsv: for Herod feared John, knowing that he was a righteous and holy man, and he protected him. When he heard him, he was greatly perplexed; and yet he liked to listen to him.

mark:6:21.niv: Finally the opportune time came. On his birthday Herod gave a banquet for his high officials and military commanders and the leading men of Galilee.
mark:6:21.csb: An opportune time came on his birthday, when Herod gave a banquet for his nobles, military commanders, and the leading men of Galilee.
mark:6:21.nrsv: But an opportunity came when Herod on his birthday gave a banquet for his courtiers and officers and for the leaders of Galilee.

mark:6:22.niv: When the daughter of Herodias came in and danced, she pleased Herod and his dinner guests.
mark:6:22.csb: When Herodias’s own daughter came in and danced, she pleased Herod and his guests. The king said to the girl, “Ask me whatever you want, and I’ll give it to you.”
mark:6:22.nrsv: When his daughter Herodias came in and danced, she pleased Herod and his guests; and the king said to the girl, "Ask me for whatever you wish, and I will give it."

mark:6:23.niv: The king said to the girl, "Ask me for anything you want, and I'll give it to you." And he promised her with an oath, "Whatever you ask I will give you, up to half my kingdom."
mark:6:23.csb: He promised her with an oath: “Whatever you ask me I will give you, up to half my kingdom.”
mark:6:23.nrsv: And he solemnly swore to her, "Whatever you ask me, I will give you, even half of my kingdom."

mark:6:24.niv: She went out and said to her mother, "What shall I ask for?The head of John the Baptist," she answered.
mark:6:24.csb: She went out and said to her mother, “What should I ask for?” “John the Baptist’s head,” she said.
mark:6:24.nrsv: She went out and said to her mother, "What should I ask for?" She replied, "The head of John the baptizer."

mark:6:25.niv: At once the girl hurried in to the king with the request: "I want you to give me right now the head of John the Baptist on a platter."
mark:6:25.csb: At once she hurried to the king and said, “I want you to give me John the Baptist’s head on a platter immediately.”
mark:6:25.nrsv: Immediately she rushed back to the king and requested, "I want you to give me at once the head of John the Baptist on a platter."

mark:6:26.niv: The king was greatly distressed, but because of his oaths and his dinner guests, he did not want to refuse her.
mark:6:26.csb: Although the king was deeply distressed, because of his oaths and the guests he did not want to refuse her.
mark:6:26.nrsv: The king was deeply grieved; yet out of regard for his oaths and for the guests, he did not want to refuse her.

mark:6:27.niv: So he immediately sent an executioner with orders to bring John's head. The man went, beheaded John in the prison,
mark:6:27.csb: The king immediately sent for an executioner and commanded him to bring John’s head. So he went and beheaded him in prison,
mark:6:27.nrsv: Immediately the king sent a soldier of the guard with orders to bring John's head. He went and beheaded him in the prison,

mark:6:28.niv: and brought back his head on a platter. He presented it to the girl, and she gave it to her mother.
mark:6:28.csb: brought his head on a platter, and gave it to the girl. Then the girl gave it to her mother.
mark:6:28.nrsv: brought his head on a platter, and gave it to the girl. Then the girl gave it to her mother.

mark:6:29.niv: On hearing of this, John's disciples came and took his body and laid it in a tomb.
mark:6:29.csb: When John’s disciples heard about it, they came and removed his corpse and placed it in a tomb.
mark:6:29.nrsv: When his disciples heard about it, they came and took his body, and laid it in a tomb.

mark:6:30.niv: The apostles gathered around Jesus and reported to him all they had done and taught.
mark:6:30.csb: The apostles gathered around Jesus and reported to him all that they had done and taught.
mark:6:30.nrsv: The apostles gathered around Jesus, and told him all that they had done and taught.

mark:6:31.niv: Then, because so many people were coming and going that they did not even have a chance to eat, he said to them, "Come with me by yourselves to a quiet place and get some rest."
mark:6:31.csb: He said to them, “Come away by yourselves to a remote place and rest for a while.” For many people were coming and going, and they did not even have time to eat.
mark:6:31.nrsv: He said to them, "Come away to a deserted place all by yourselves and rest a while." For many were coming and going, and they had no leisure even to eat.

mark:6:32.niv: So they went away by themselves in a boat to a solitary place.
mark:6:32.csb: So they went away in the boat by themselves to a remote place,
mark:6:32.nrsv: And they went away in the boat to a deserted place by themselves.

mark:6:33.niv: But many who saw them leaving recognized them and ran on foot from all the towns and got there ahead of them.
mark:6:33.csb: but many saw them leaving and recognized them, and they ran on foot from all the towns and arrived ahead of them.
mark:6:33.nrsv: Now many saw them going and recognized them, and they hurried there on foot from all the towns and arrived ahead of them.

mark:6:34.niv: When Jesus landed and saw a large crowd, he had compassion on them, because they were like sheep without a shepherd. So he began teaching them many things.
mark:6:34.csb: When he went ashore, he saw a large crowd and had compassion on them, because they were like sheep without a shepherd. Then he began to teach them many things.
mark:6:34.nrsv: As he went ashore, he saw a great crowd; and he had compassion for them, because they were like sheep without a shepherd; and he began to teach them many things.

mark:6:35.niv: By this time it was late in the day, so his disciples came to him. "This is a remote place," they said, "and it's already very late.
mark:6:35.csb: When it grew late, his disciples approached him and said, “This place is deserted, and it is already late.
mark:6:35.nrsv: When it grew late, his disciples came to him and said, "This is a deserted place, and the hour is now very late;

mark:6:36.niv: Send the people away so they can go to the surrounding countryside and villages and buy themselves something to eat."
mark:6:36.csb: Send them away so that they can go into the surrounding countryside and villages to buy themselves something to eat.”
mark:6:36.nrsv: send them away so that they may go into the surrounding country and villages and buy something for themselves to eat."

mark:6:37.niv: But he answered, "You give them something to eat." They said to him, "That would take eight months of a man's wages! Are we to go and spend that much on bread and give it to them to eat?"
mark:6:37.csb: “You give them something to eat,” he responded. They said to him, “Should we go and buy two hundred denarii worth of bread and give them something to eat?”
mark:6:37.nrsv: But he answered them, "You give them something to eat." They said to him, "Are we to go and buy two hundred denarii worth of bread, and give it to them to eat?"

mark:6:38.niv: "How many loaves do you have?" he asked. "Go and see." When they found out, they said, "Five--and two fish."
mark:6:38.csb: He asked them, “How many loaves do you have? Go and see.” When they found out they said, “Five, and two fish.”
mark:6:38.nrsv: And he said to them, "How many loaves have you? Go and see." When they had found out, they said, "Five, and two fish."

mark:6:39.niv: Then Jesus directed them to have all the people sit down in groups on the green grass.
mark:6:39.csb: Then he instructed them to have all the people sit down in groups on the green grass.
mark:6:39.nrsv: Then he ordered them to get all the people to sit down in groups on the green grass.

mark:6:40.niv: So they sat down in groups of hundreds and fifties.
mark:6:40.csb: So they sat down in groups of hundreds and fifties.
mark:6:40.nrsv: So they sat down in groups of hundreds and of fifties.

mark:6:41.niv: Taking the five loaves and the two fish and looking up to heaven, he gave thanks and broke the loaves. Then he gave them to his disciples to set before the people. He also divided the two fish among them all.
mark:6:41.csb: He took the five loaves and the two fish, and looking up to heaven, he blessed and broke the loaves. He kept giving them to his disciples to set before the people. He also divided the two fish among them all.
mark:6:41.nrsv: Taking the five loaves and the two fish, he looked up to heaven, and blessed and broke the loaves, and gave them to his disciples to set before the people; and he divided the two fish among them all.

mark:6:42.niv: They all ate and were satisfied,
mark:6:42.nrsv: And all ate and were filled;

mark:6:43.niv: and the disciples picked up twelve basketfuls of broken pieces of bread and fish.
mark:6:43.csb: They picked up twelve baskets full of pieces of bread and fish.
mark:6:43.nrsv: and they took up twelve baskets full of broken pieces and of the fish.

mark:6:44.niv: The number of the men who had eaten was five thousand.
mark:6:44.csb: Now those who had eaten the loaves were five thousand men.
mark:6:44.nrsv: Those who had eaten the loaves numbered five thousand men.

mark:6:45.niv: Immediately Jesus made his disciples get into the boat and go on ahead of him to Bethsaida, while he dismissed the crowd.
mark:6:45.csb: Immediately he made his disciples get into the boat and go ahead of him to the other side, to Bethsaida, while he dismissed the crowd.
mark:6:45.nrsv: Immediately he made his disciples get into the boat and go on ahead to the other side, to Bethsaida, while he dismissed the crowd.

mark:6:46.niv: After leaving them, he went up on a mountainside to pray.
mark:6:46.csb: After he said good-bye to them, he went away to the mountain to pray.
mark:6:46.nrsv: After saying farewell to them, he went up on the mountain to pray.

mark:6:47.niv: When evening came, the boat was in the middle of the lake, and he was alone on land.
mark:6:47.csb: Well into the night, the boat was in the middle of the sea, and he was alone on the land.
mark:6:47.nrsv: When evening came, the boat was out on the sea, and he was alone on the land.

mark:6:48.niv: He saw the disciples straining at the oars, because the wind was against them. About the fourth watch of the night he went out to them, walking on the lake. He was about to pass by them,
mark:6:48.csb: He saw them straining at the oars, because the wind was against them. Very early in the morning he came toward them walking on the sea and wanted to pass by them.
mark:6:48.nrsv: When he saw that they were straining at the oars against an adverse wind, he came towards them early in the morning, walking on the sea. He intended to pass them by.

mark:6:49.niv: but when they saw him walking on the lake, they thought he was a ghost. They cried out,
mark:6:49.csb: When they saw him walking on the sea, they thought it was a ghost and cried out,
mark:6:49.nrsv: But when they saw him walking on the sea, they thought it was a ghost and cried out;

mark:6:50.niv: because they all saw him and were terrified.
mark:6:50.csb: because they all saw him and were terrified. Immediately he spoke with them and said, “Have courage! It is I. Don’t be afraid.”
mark:6:50.nrsv: for they all saw him and were terrified. But immediately he spoke to them and said, "Take heart, it is I; do not be afraid."

mark:6:51.niv: Immediately he spoke to them and said, "Take courage! It is I. Don't be afraid." Then he climbed into the boat with them, and the wind died down. They were completely amazed,
mark:6:51.csb: Then he got into the boat with them, and the wind ceased. They were completely astounded,
mark:6:51.nrsv: Then he got into the boat with them and the wind ceased. And they were utterly astounded,

mark:6:52.niv: for they had not understood about the loaves; their hearts were hardened.
mark:6:52.csb: because they had not understood about the loaves. Instead, their hearts were hardened.
mark:6:52.nrsv: for they did not understand about the loaves, but their hearts were hardened.

mark:6:53.niv: When they had crossed over, they landed at Gennesaret and anchored there.
mark:6:53.csb: When they had crossed over, they came to shore at Gennesaret and anchored there.
mark:6:53.nrsv: When they had crossed over, they came to land at Gennesaret and moored the boat.

mark:6:54.niv: As soon as they got out of the boat, people recognized Jesus.
mark:6:54.csb: As they got out of the boat, people immediately recognized him.
mark:6:54.nrsv: When they got out of the boat, people at once recognized him,

mark:6:55.niv: They ran throughout that whole region and carried the sick on mats to wherever they heard he was.
mark:6:55.csb: They hurried throughout that region and began to carry the sick on mats to wherever they heard he was.
mark:6:55.nrsv: and rushed about that whole region and began to bring the sick on mats to wherever they heard he was.

mark:6:56.niv: And wherever he went--into villages, towns or countryside--they placed the sick in the marketplaces. They begged him to let them touch even the edge of his cloak, and all who touched him were healed.
mark:6:56.csb: Wherever he went, into villages, towns, or the country, they laid the sick in the marketplaces and begged him that they might touch just the end of his robe. And everyone who touched it was healed.
mark:6:56.nrsv: And wherever he went, into villages or cities or farms, they laid the sick in the marketplaces, and begged him that they might touch even the fringe of his cloak; and all who touched it were healed.

mark:7:1.niv: The Pharisees and some of the teachers of the law who had come from Jerusalem gathered around Jesus and
mark:7:1.csb: The Pharisees and some of the scribes who had come from Jerusalem gathered around him.
mark:7:1.nrsv: Now when the Pharisees and some of the scribes who had come from Jerusalem gathered around him,

mark:7:2.niv: saw some of his disciples eating food with hands that were "unclean," that is, unwashed.
mark:7:2.csb: They observed that some of his disciples were eating bread with unclean — that is, unwashed — hands.
mark:7:2.nrsv: they noticed that some of his disciples were eating with defiled hands, that is, without washing them.

mark:7:3.niv: (The Pharisees and all the Jews do not eat unless they give their hands a ceremonial washing, holding to the tradition of the elders.
mark:7:3.csb: (For the Pharisees and all the Jews do not eat unless they give their hands a ceremonial washing, keeping the tradition of the elders.
mark:7:3.nrsv: (For the Pharisees, and all the Jews, do not eat unless they thoroughly wash their hands, thus observing the tradition of the elders;

mark:7:4.niv: When they come from the marketplace they do not eat unless they wash. And they observe many other traditions, such as the washing of cups, pitchers and kettles. )
mark:7:4.csb: When they come from the marketplace, they do not eat unless they have washed. And there are many other customs they have received and keep, like the washing of cups, pitchers, kettles, and dining couches. )
mark:7:4.nrsv: and they do not eat anything from the market unless they wash it; and there are also many other traditions that they observe, the washing of cups, pots, and bronze kettles.)

mark:7:5.niv: So the Pharisees and teachers of the law asked Jesus, "Why don't your disciples live according to the tradition of the elders instead of eating their food with 'unclean' hands?"
mark:7:5.csb: So the Pharisees and the scribes asked him, “Why don’t your disciples live according to the tradition of the elders, instead of eating bread with ceremonially unclean hands?”
mark:7:5.nrsv: So the Pharisees and the scribes asked him, "Why do your disciples not live according to the tradition of the elders, but eat with defiled hands?"

mark:7:6.niv: He replied, "Isaiah was right when he prophesied about you hypocrites; as it is written: "'These people honor me with their lips, but their hearts are far from me.
mark:7:6.csb: He answered them, “Isaiah prophesied correctly about you hypocrites, as it is written: This people honors me with their lips, but their heart is far from me.
mark:7:6.nrsv: He said to them, "Isaiah prophesied rightly about you hypocrites, as it is written, 'This people honors me with their lips, but their hearts are far from me;

mark:7:7.niv: They worship me in vain; their teachings are but rules taught by men.'
mark:7:7.csb: They worship me in vain, teaching as doctrines human commands.
mark:7:7.nrsv: in vain do they worship me, teaching human precepts as doctrines.'

mark:7:8.niv: You have let go of the commands of God and are holding on to the traditions of men."
mark:7:8.csb: Abandoning the command of God, you hold on to human tradition.”
mark:7:8.nrsv: You abandon the commandment of God and hold to human tradition."

mark:7:9.niv: And he said to them: "You have a fine way of setting aside the commands of God in order to observe your own traditions!
mark:7:9.csb: He also said to them, “You have a fine way of invalidating God’s command in order to set up your tradition!
mark:7:9.nrsv: Then he said to them, "You have a fine way of rejecting the commandment of God in order to keep your tradition!

mark:7:10.niv: For Moses said, 'Honor your father and your mother,' and, 'Anyone who curses his father or mother must be put to death.'
mark:7:10.csb: For Moses said: Honor your father and your mother; and Whoever speaks evil of father or mother must be put to death.
mark:7:10.nrsv: For Moses said, 'Honor your father and your mother'; and, 'Whoever speaks evil of father or mother must surely die.'

mark:7:11.niv: But you say that if a man says to his father or mother: 'Whatever help you might otherwise have received from me is Corban' (that is, a gift devoted to God),
mark:7:11.csb: But you say, ‘If anyone tells his father or mother: Whatever benefit you might have received from me is corban ’” (that is, an offering devoted to God),
mark:7:11.nrsv: But you say that if anyone tells father or mother, 'Whatever support you might have had from me is Corban' (that is, an offering to God)--

mark:7:12.niv: then you no longer let him do anything for his father or mother.
mark:7:12.csb: “you no longer let him do anything for his father or mother.
mark:7:12.nrsv: then you no longer permit doing anything for a father or mother,

mark:7:13.niv: Thus you nullify the word of God by your tradition that you have handed down. And you do many things like that."
mark:7:13.csb: You nullify the word of God by your tradition that you have handed down. And you do many other similar things.”
mark:7:13.nrsv: thus making void the word of God through your tradition that you have handed on. And you do many things like this."

mark:7:14.niv: Again Jesus called the crowd to him and said, "Listen to me, everyone, and understand this.
mark:7:14.csb: Summoning the crowd again, he told them, “Listen to me, all of you, and understand:
mark:7:14.nrsv: Then he called the crowd again and said to them, "Listen to me, all of you, and understand:

mark:7:15.niv: Nothing outside a man can make him 'unclean' by going into him. Rather, it is what comes out of a man that makes him 'unclean.'"
mark:7:15.csb: Nothing that goes into a person from outside can defile him but the things that come out of a person are what defile him.”
mark:7:15.nrsv: there is nothing outside a person that by going in can defile, but the things that come out are what defile."

mark:7:17.niv: After he had left the crowd and entered the house, his disciples asked him about this parable.
mark:7:17.csb: When he went into the house away from the crowd, his disciples asked him about the parable.
mark:7:17.nrsv: When he had left the crowd and entered the house, his disciples asked him about the parable.

mark:7:18.niv: "Are you so dull?" he asked. "Don't you see that nothing that enters a man from the outside can make him 'unclean'?
mark:7:18.csb: He said to them, “Are you also as lacking in understanding? Don’t you realize that nothing going into a person from the outside can defile him?
mark:7:18.nrsv: He said to them, "Then do you also fail to understand? Do you not see that whatever goes into a person from outside cannot defile,

mark:7:19.niv: For it doesn't go into his heart but into his stomach, and then out of his body." (In saying this, Jesus declared all foods "clean.")
mark:7:19.csb: For it doesn’t go into his heart but into the stomach and is eliminated” (thus he declared all foods clean ).
mark:7:19.nrsv: since it enters, not the heart but the stomach, and goes out into the sewer?" (Thus he declared all foods clean.)

mark:7:20.niv: He went on: "What comes out of a man is what makes him 'unclean.'
mark:7:20.csb: And he said, “What comes out of a person is what defiles him.
mark:7:20.nrsv: And he said, "It is what comes out of a person that defiles.

mark:7:21.niv: For from within, out of men's hearts, come evil thoughts, sexual immorality, theft, murder, adultery,
mark:7:21.csb: For from within, out of people’s hearts, come evil thoughts, sexual immoralities, thefts, murders,
mark:7:21.nrsv: For it is from within, from the human heart, that evil intentions come: fornication, theft, murder,

mark:7:22.niv: greed, malice, deceit, lewdness, envy, slander, arrogance and folly.
mark:7:22.csb: adulteries, greed, evil actions, deceit, self-indulgence, envy, slander, pride, and foolishness.
mark:7:22.nrsv: adultery, avarice, wickedness, deceit, licentiousness, envy, slander, pride, folly.

mark:7:23.niv: All these evils come from inside and make a man 'unclean.'"
mark:7:23.csb: All these evil things come from within and defile a person.”
mark:7:23.nrsv: All these evil things come from within, and they defile a person."

mark:7:24.niv: Jesus left that place and went to the vicinity of Tyre. He entered a house and did not want anyone to know it; yet he could not keep his presence secret.
mark:7:24.csb: He got up and departed from there to the region of Tyre. He entered a house and did not want anyone to know it, but he could not escape notice.
mark:7:24.nrsv: From there he set out and went away to the region of Tyre. He entered a house and did not want anyone to know he was there. Yet he could not escape notice,

mark:7:25.niv: In fact, as soon as she heard about him, a woman whose little daughter was possessed by an evil spirit came and fell at his feet.
mark:7:25.csb: Instead, immediately after hearing about him, a woman whose little daughter had an unclean spirit came and fell at his feet.
mark:7:25.nrsv: but a woman whose little daughter had an unclean spirit immediately heard about him, and she came and bowed down at his feet.

mark:7:26.niv: The woman was a Greek, born in Syrian Phoenicia. She begged Jesus to drive the demon out of her daughter.
mark:7:26.csb: The woman was a Gentile, a Syrophoenician by birth, and she was asking him to cast the demon out of her daughter.
mark:7:26.nrsv: Now the woman was a Gentile, of Syrophoenician origin. She begged him to cast the demon out of her daughter.

mark:7:27.niv: "First let the children eat all they want," he told her, "for it is not right to take the children's bread and toss it to their dogs."
mark:7:27.csb: He said to her, “Let the children be fed first, because it isn’t right to take the children’s bread and throw it to the dogs.”
mark:7:27.nrsv: He said to her, "Let the children be fed first, for it is not fair to take the children's food and throw it to the dogs."

mark:7:28.niv: "Yes, Lord," she replied, "but even the dogs under the table eat the children's crumbs."
mark:7:28.csb: But she replied to him, “Lord, even the dogs under the table eat the children’s crumbs.”
mark:7:28.nrsv: But she answered him, "Sir, even the dogs under the table eat the children's crumbs."

mark:7:29.niv: Then he told her, "For such a reply, you may go; the demon has left your daughter."
mark:7:29.csb: Then he told her, “Because of this reply, you may go. The demon has left your daughter.”
mark:7:29.nrsv: Then he said to her, "For saying that, you may go-- the demon has left your daughter."

mark:7:30.niv: She went home and found her child lying on the bed, and the demon gone.
mark:7:30.csb: When she went back to her home, she found her child lying on the bed, and the demon was gone.
mark:7:30.nrsv: So she went home, found the child lying on the bed, and the demon gone.

mark:7:31.niv: Then Jesus left the vicinity of Tyre and went through Sidon, down to the Sea of Galilee and into the region of the Decapolis.
mark:7:31.csb: Again, leaving the region of Tyre, he went by way of Sidon to the Sea of Galilee, through the region of the Decapolis.
mark:7:31.nrsv: Then he returned from the region of Tyre, and went by way of Sidon towards the Sea of Galilee, in the region of the Decapolis.

mark:7:32.niv: There some people brought to him a man who was deaf and could hardly talk, and they begged him to place his hand on the man.
mark:7:32.csb: They brought to him a deaf man who had difficulty speaking and begged Jesus to lay his hand on him.
mark:7:32.nrsv: They brought to him a deaf man who had an impediment in his speech; and they begged him to lay his hand on him.

mark:7:33.niv: After he took him aside, away from the crowd, Jesus put his fingers into the man's ears. Then he spit and touched the man's tongue.
mark:7:33.csb: So he took him away from the crowd in private. After putting his fingers in the man’s ears and spitting, he touched his tongue.
mark:7:33.nrsv: He took him aside in private, away from the crowd, and put his fingers into his ears, and he spat and touched his tongue.

mark:7:34.niv: He looked up to heaven and with a deep sigh said to him, "Ephphatha!" (which means, "Be opened!" ).
mark:7:34.csb: Looking up to heaven, he sighed deeply and said to him, “Ephphatha!” (that is, “Be opened!” ).
mark:7:34.nrsv: Then looking up to heaven, he sighed and said to him, "Ephphatha," that is, "Be opened."

mark:7:35.niv: At this, the man's ears were opened, his tongue was loosened and he began to speak plainly.
mark:7:35.csb: Immediately his ears were opened, his tongue was loosened, and he began to speak clearly.
mark:7:35.nrsv: And immediately his ears were opened, his tongue was released, and he spoke plainly.

mark:7:36.niv: Jesus commanded them not to tell anyone. But the more he did so, the more they kept talking about it.
mark:7:36.csb: He ordered them to tell no one, but the more he ordered them, the more they proclaimed it.
mark:7:36.nrsv: Then Jesus ordered them to tell no one; but the more he ordered them, the more zealously they proclaimed it.

mark:7:37.niv: People were overwhelmed with amazement. "He has done everything well," they said. "He even makes the deaf hear and the mute speak."
mark:7:37.csb: They were extremely astonished and said, “He has done everything well. He even makes the deaf hear and the mute speak.”
mark:7:37.nrsv: They were astounded beyond measure, saying, "He has done everything well; he even makes the deaf to hear and the mute to speak."

mark:8:1.niv: During those days another large crowd gathered. Since they had nothing to eat, Jesus called his disciples to him and said,
mark:8:1.csb: In those days there was again a large crowd, and they had nothing to eat. He called the disciples and said to them,
mark:8:1.nrsv: In those days when there was again a great crowd without anything to eat, he called his disciples and said to them,

mark:8:2.niv: "I have compassion for these people; they have already been with me three days and have nothing to eat.
mark:8:2.csb: “I have compassion on the crowd, because they’ve already stayed with me three days and have nothing to eat.
mark:8:2.nrsv: "I have compassion for the crowd, because they have been with me now for three days and have nothing to eat.

mark:8:3.niv: If I send them home hungry, they will collapse on the way, because some of them have come a long distance."
mark:8:3.csb: If I send them home hungry, they will collapse on the way, and some of them have come a long distance.”
mark:8:3.nrsv: If I send them away hungry to their homes, they will faint on the way-- and some of them have come from a great distance."

mark:8:4.niv: His disciples answered, "But where in this remote place can anyone get enough bread to feed them?"
mark:8:4.csb: His disciples answered him, “Where can anyone get enough bread here in this desolate place to feed these people?”
mark:8:4.nrsv: His disciples replied, "How can one feed these people with bread here in the desert?"

mark:8:5.niv: "How many loaves do you have?" Jesus asked. "Seven," they replied.
mark:8:5.csb: “How many loaves do you have?” he asked them. “Seven,” they said.
mark:8:5.nrsv: He asked them, "How many loaves do you have?" They said, "Seven."

mark:8:6.niv: He told the crowd to sit down on the ground. When he had taken the seven loaves and given thanks, he broke them and gave them to his disciples to set before the people, and they did so.
mark:8:6.csb: He commanded the crowd to sit down on the ground. Taking the seven loaves, he gave thanks, broke them, and gave them to his disciples to set before the people. So they served them to the crowd.
mark:8:6.nrsv: Then he ordered the crowd to sit down on the ground; and he took the seven loaves, and after giving thanks he broke them and gave them to his disciples to distribute; and they distributed them to the crowd.

mark:8:7.niv: They had a few small fish as well; he gave thanks for them also and told the disciples to distribute them.
mark:8:7.csb: They also had a few small fish, and after he had blessed them, he said these were to be served as well.
mark:8:7.nrsv: They had also a few small fish; and after blessing them, he ordered that these too should be distributed.

mark:8:8.niv: The people ate and were satisfied. Afterward the disciples picked up seven basketfuls of broken pieces that were left over.
mark:8:8.csb: They ate and were satisfied. Then they collected seven large baskets of leftover pieces.
mark:8:8.nrsv: They ate and were filled; and they took up the broken pieces left over, seven baskets full.

mark:8:9.niv: About four thousand men were present. And having sent them away,
mark:8:9.csb: About four thousand were there. He dismissed them.
mark:8:9.nrsv: Now there were about four thousand people. And he sent them away.

mark:8:10.niv: he got into the boat with his disciples and went to the region of Dalmanutha.
mark:8:10.csb: And he immediately got into the boat with his disciples and went to the district of Dalmanutha.
mark:8:10.nrsv: And immediately he got into the boat with his disciples and went to the district of Dalmanutha.

mark:8:11.niv: The Pharisees came and began to question Jesus. To test him, they asked him for a sign from heaven.
mark:8:11.csb: The Pharisees came and began to argue with him, demanding of him a sign from heaven to test him.
mark:8:11.nrsv: The Pharisees came and began to argue with him, asking him for a sign from heaven, to test him.

mark:8:12.niv: He sighed deeply and said, "Why does this generation ask for a miraculous sign? I tell you the truth, no sign will be given to it."
mark:8:12.csb: Sighing deeply in his spirit, he said, “Why does this generation demand a sign? Truly I tell you, no sign will be given to this generation.”
mark:8:12.nrsv: And he sighed deeply in his spirit and said, "Why does this generation ask for a sign? Truly I tell you, no sign will be given to this generation."

mark:8:13.niv: Then he left them, got back into the boat and crossed to the other side.
mark:8:13.csb: Then he left them, got back into the boat, and went to the other side.
mark:8:13.nrsv: And he left them, and getting into the boat again, he went across to the other side.

mark:8:14.niv: The disciples had forgotten to bring bread, except for one loaf they had with them in the boat.
mark:8:14.csb: The disciples had forgotten to take bread and had only one loaf with them in the boat.
mark:8:14.nrsv: Now the disciples had forgotten to bring any bread; and they had only one loaf with them in the boat.

mark:8:15.niv: "Be careful," Jesus warned them. "Watch out for the yeast of the Pharisees and that of Herod."
mark:8:15.csb: Then he gave them strict orders: “Watch out! Beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and the leaven of Herod.”
mark:8:15.nrsv: And he cautioned them, saying, "Watch out-- beware of the yeast of the Pharisees and the yeast of Herod."

mark:8:16.niv: They discussed this with one another and said, "It is because we have no bread."
mark:8:16.csb: They were discussing among themselves that they did not have any bread.
mark:8:16.nrsv: They said to one another, "It is because we have no bread."

mark:8:17.niv: Aware of their discussion, Jesus asked them: "Why are you talking about having no bread? Do you still not see or understand? Are your hearts hardened?
mark:8:17.csb: Aware of this, he said to them, “Why are you discussing the fact you have no bread? Don’t you understand or comprehend? Do you have hardened hearts?
mark:8:17.nrsv: And becoming aware of it, Jesus said to them, "Why are you talking about having no bread? Do you still not perceive or understand? Are your hearts hardened?

mark:8:18.niv: Do you have eyes but fail to see, and ears but fail to hear? And don't you remember?
mark:8:18.csb: Do you have eyes and not see; do you have ears and not hear? And do you not remember?
mark:8:18.nrsv: Do you have eyes, and fail to see? Do you have ears, and fail to hear? And do you not remember?

mark:8:19.niv: When I broke the five loaves for the five thousand, how many basketfuls of pieces did you pick up?Twelve," they replied.
mark:8:19.csb: When I broke the five loaves for the five thousand, how many baskets full of leftovers did you collect?” “Twelve,” they told him.
mark:8:19.nrsv: When I broke the five loaves for the five thousand, how many baskets full of broken pieces did you collect?" They said to him, "Twelve."

mark:8:20.niv: "And when I broke the seven loaves for the four thousand, how many basketfuls of pieces did you pick up?" They answered, "Seven."
mark:8:20.csb: “When I broke the seven loaves for the four thousand, how many baskets full of pieces did you collect?” “Seven,” they said.
mark:8:20.nrsv: "And the seven for the four thousand, how many baskets full of broken pieces did you collect?" And they said to him, "Seven."

mark:8:21.niv: He said to them, "Do you still not understand?"
mark:8:21.csb: And he said to them, “Don’t you understand yet?”
mark:8:21.nrsv: Then he said to them, "Do you not yet understand?"

mark:8:22.niv: They came to Bethsaida, and some people brought a blind man and begged Jesus to touch him.
mark:8:22.csb: They came to Bethsaida. They brought a blind man to him and begged him to touch him.
mark:8:22.nrsv: They came to Bethsaida. Some people brought a blind man to him and begged him to touch him.

mark:8:23.niv: He took the blind man by the hand and led him outside the village. When he had spit on the man's eyes and put his hands on him, Jesus asked, "Do you see anything?"
mark:8:23.csb: He took the blind man by the hand and brought him out of the village. Spitting on his eyes and laying his hands on him, he asked him, “Do you see anything?”
mark:8:23.nrsv: He took the blind man by the hand and led him out of the village; and when he had put saliva on his eyes and laid his hands on him, he asked him, "Can you see anything?"

mark:8:24.niv: He looked up and said, "I see people; they look like trees walking around."
mark:8:24.csb: He looked up and said, “I see people — they look like trees walking.”
mark:8:24.nrsv: And the man looked up and said, "I can see people, but they look like trees, walking."

mark:8:25.niv: Once more Jesus put his hands on the man's eyes. Then his eyes were opened, his sight was restored, and he saw everything clearly.
mark:8:25.csb: Again Jesus placed his hands on the man’s eyes. The man looked intently and his sight was restored and he saw everything clearly.
mark:8:25.nrsv: Then Jesus laid his hands on his eyes again; and he looked intently and his sight was restored, and he saw everything clearly.

mark:8:26.niv: Jesus sent him home, saying, "Don't go into the village. "
mark:8:26.csb: Then he sent him home, saying, “Don’t even go into the village.”
mark:8:26.nrsv: Then he sent him away to his home, saying, "Do not even go into the village."

mark:8:27.niv: Jesus and his disciples went on to the villages around Caesarea Philippi. On the way he asked them, "Who do people say I am?"
mark:8:27.csb: Jesus went out with his disciples to the villages of Caesarea Philippi. And on the road he asked his disciples, “Who do people say that I am?”
mark:8:27.nrsv: Jesus went on with his disciples to the villages of Caesarea Philippi; and on the way he asked his disciples, "Who do people say that I am?"

mark:8:28.niv: They replied, "Some say John the Baptist; others say Elijah; and still others, one of the prophets."
mark:8:28.csb: They answered him, “John the Baptist; others, Elijah; still others, one of the prophets.”
mark:8:28.nrsv: And they answered him, "John the Baptist; and others, Elijah; and still others, one of the prophets."

mark:8:29.niv: "But what about you?" he asked. "Who do you say I am?" Peter answered, "You are the Christ. "
mark:8:29.csb: “But you,” he asked them, “who do you say that I am?” Peter answered him, “You are the Messiah.”
mark:8:29.nrsv: He asked them, "But who do you say that I am?" Peter answered him, "You are the Messiah."

mark:8:30.niv: Jesus warned them not to tell anyone about him.
mark:8:30.csb: And he strictly warned them to tell no one about him.
mark:8:30.nrsv: And he sternly ordered them not to tell anyone about him.

mark:8:31.niv: He then began to teach them that the Son of Man must suffer many things and be rejected by the elders, chief priests and teachers of the law, and that he must be killed and after three days rise again.
mark:8:31.csb: Then he began to teach them that it was necessary for the Son of Man to suffer many things and be rejected by the elders, chief priests, and scribes, be killed, and rise after three days.
mark:8:31.nrsv: Then he began to teach them that the Son of Man must undergo great suffering, and be rejected by the elders, the chief priests, and the scribes, and be killed, and after three days rise again.

mark:8:32.niv: He spoke plainly about this, and Peter took him aside and began to rebuke him.
mark:8:32.csb: He spoke openly about this. Peter took him aside and began to rebuke him.
mark:8:32.nrsv: He said all this quite openly. And Peter took him aside and began to rebuke him.

mark:8:33.niv: But when Jesus turned and looked at his disciples, he rebuked Peter. "Get behind me, Satan!" he said. "You do not have in mind the things of God, but the things of men."
mark:8:33.csb: But turning around and looking at his disciples, he rebuked Peter and said, “Get behind me, Satan! You are not thinking about God’s concerns but human concerns.”
mark:8:33.nrsv: But turning and looking at his disciples, he rebuked Peter and said, "Get behind me, Satan! For you are setting your mind not on divine things but on human things."

mark:8:34.niv: Then he called the crowd to him along with his disciples and said: "If anyone would come after me, he must deny himself and take up his cross and follow me.
mark:8:34.csb: Calling the crowd along with his disciples, he said to them, “If anyone wants to follow after me, let him deny himself, take up his cross, and follow me.
mark:8:34.nrsv: He called the crowd with his disciples, and said to them, "If any want to become my followers, let them deny themselves and take up their cross and follow me.

mark:8:35.niv: For whoever wants to save his life will lose it, but whoever loses his life for me and for the gospel will save it.
mark:8:35.csb: For whoever wants to save his life will lose it, but whoever loses his life because of me and the gospel will save it.
mark:8:35.nrsv: For those who want to save their life will lose it, and those who lose their life for my sake, and for the sake of the gospel, will save it.

mark:8:36.niv: What good is it for a man to gain the whole world, yet forfeit his soul?
mark:8:36.csb: For what does it benefit someone to gain the whole world and yet lose his life?
mark:8:36.nrsv: For what will it profit them to gain the whole world and forfeit their life?

mark:8:37.niv: Or what can a man give in exchange for his soul?
mark:8:37.csb: What can anyone give in exchange for his life?
mark:8:37.nrsv: Indeed, what can they give in return for their life?

mark:8:38.niv: If anyone is ashamed of me and my words in this adulterous and sinful generation, the Son of Man will be ashamed of him when he comes in his Father's glory with the holy angels."
mark:8:38.csb: For whoever is ashamed of me and my words in this adulterous and sinful generation, the Son of Man will also be ashamed of him when he comes in the glory of his Father with the holy angels.”
mark:8:38.nrsv: Those who are ashamed of me and of my words in this adulterous and sinful generation, of them the Son of Man will also be ashamed when he comes in the glory of his Father with the holy angels."

mark:9:1.niv: And he said to them, "I tell you the truth, some who are standing here will not taste death before they see the kingdom of God come with power."
mark:9:1.csb: Then he said to them, “Truly I tell you, there are some standing here who will not taste death until they see the kingdom of God come in power.”
mark:9:1.nrsv: And he said to them, "Truly I tell you, there are some standing here who will not taste death until they see that the kingdom of God has come with power."

mark:9:2.niv: After six days Jesus took Peter, James and John with him and led them up a high mountain, where they were all alone. There he was transfigured before them.
mark:9:2.csb: After six days Jesus took Peter, James, and John and led them up a high mountain by themselves to be alone. He was transfigured in front of them,
mark:9:2.nrsv: Six days later, Jesus took with him Peter and James and John, and led them up a high mountain apart, by themselves. And he was transfigured before them,

mark:9:3.niv: His clothes became dazzling white, whiter than anyone in the world could bleach them.
mark:9:3.csb: and his clothes became dazzling — extremely white as no launderer on earth could whiten them.
mark:9:3.nrsv: and his clothes became dazzling white, such as no one on earth could bleach them.

mark:9:4.niv: And there appeared before them Elijah and Moses, who were talking with Jesus.
mark:9:4.csb: Elijah appeared to them with Moses, and they were talking with Jesus.
mark:9:4.nrsv: And there appeared to them Elijah with Moses, who were talking with Jesus.

mark:9:5.niv: Peter said to Jesus, "Rabbi, it is good for us to be here. Let us put up three shelters--one for you, one for Moses and one for Elijah."
mark:9:5.csb: Peter said to Jesus, “Rabbi, it’s good for us to be here. Let us set up three shelters: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah” —
mark:9:5.nrsv: Then Peter said to Jesus, "Rabbi, it is good for us to be here; let us make three dwellings, one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah."

mark:9:6.niv: (He did not know what to say, they were so frightened.)
mark:9:6.csb: because he did not know what to say, since they were terrified.
mark:9:6.nrsv: He did not know what to say, for they were terrified.

mark:9:7.niv: Then a cloud appeared and enveloped them, and a voice came from the cloud: "This is my Son, whom I love. Listen to him!"
mark:9:7.csb: A cloud appeared, overshadowing them, and a voice came from the cloud: “This is my beloved Son; listen to him!”
mark:9:7.nrsv: Then a cloud overshadowed them, and from the cloud there came a voice, "This is my Son, the Beloved; listen to him!"

mark:9:8.niv: Suddenly, when they looked around, they no longer saw anyone with them except Jesus.
mark:9:8.csb: Suddenly, looking around, they no longer saw anyone with them except Jesus.
mark:9:8.nrsv: Suddenly when they looked around, they saw no one with them any more, but only Jesus.

mark:9:9.niv: As they were coming down the mountain, Jesus gave them orders not to tell anyone what they had seen until the Son of Man had risen from the dead.
mark:9:9.csb: As they were coming down the mountain, he ordered them to tell no one what they had seen until the Son of Man had risen from the dead.
mark:9:9.nrsv: As they were coming down the mountain, he ordered them to tell no one about what they had seen, until after the Son of Man had risen from the dead.

mark:9:10.niv: They kept the matter to themselves, discussing what "rising from the dead" meant.
mark:9:10.csb: They kept this word to themselves, questioning what “rising from the dead” meant.
mark:9:10.nrsv: So they kept the matter to themselves, questioning what this rising from the dead could mean.

mark:9:11.niv: And they asked him, "Why do the teachers of the law say that Elijah must come first?"
mark:9:11.csb: Then they asked him, “Why do the scribes say that Elijah must come first?”
mark:9:11.nrsv: Then they asked him, "Why do the scribes say that Elijah must come first?"

mark:9:12.niv: Jesus replied, "To be sure, Elijah does come first, and restores all things. Why then is it written that the Son of Man must suffer much and be rejected?
mark:9:12.csb: “Elijah does come first and restores all things,” he replied. “Why then is it written that the Son of Man must suffer many things and be treated with contempt?
mark:9:12.nrsv: He said to them, "Elijah is indeed coming first to restore all things. How then is it written about the Son of Man, that he is to go through many sufferings and be treated with contempt?

mark:9:13.niv: But I tell you, Elijah has come, and they have done to him everything they wished, just as it is written about him."
mark:9:13.csb: But I tell you that Elijah has come, and they did whatever they pleased to him, just as it is written about him.”
mark:9:13.nrsv: But I tell you that Elijah has come, and they did to him whatever they pleased, as it is written about him."

mark:9:14.niv: When they came to the other disciples, they saw a large crowd around them and the teachers of the law arguing with them.
mark:9:14.csb: When they came to the disciples, they saw a large crowd around them and scribes disputing with them.
mark:9:14.nrsv: When they came to the disciples, they saw a great crowd around them, and some scribes arguing with them.

mark:9:15.niv: As soon as all the people saw Jesus, they were overwhelmed with wonder and ran to greet him.
mark:9:15.csb: When the whole crowd saw him, they were amazed and ran to greet him.
mark:9:15.nrsv: When the whole crowd saw him, they were immediately overcome with awe, and they ran forward to greet him.

mark:9:16.niv: "What are you arguing with them about?" he asked.
mark:9:16.csb: He asked them, “What are you arguing with them about?”
mark:9:16.nrsv: He asked them, "What are you arguing about with them?"

mark:9:17.niv: A man in the crowd answered, "Teacher, I brought you my son, who is possessed by a spirit that has robbed him of speech.
mark:9:17.csb: Someone from the crowd answered him, “Teacher, I brought my son to you. He has a spirit that makes him unable to speak.
mark:9:17.nrsv: Someone from the crowd answered him, "Teacher, I brought you my son; he has a spirit that makes him unable to speak;

mark:9:18.niv: Whenever it seizes him, it throws him to the ground. He foams at the mouth, gnashes his teeth and becomes rigid. I asked your disciples to drive out the spirit, but they could not."
mark:9:18.csb: Whenever it seizes him, it throws him down, and he foams at the mouth, grinds his teeth, and becomes rigid. I asked your disciples to drive it out, but they couldn’t.”
mark:9:18.nrsv: and whenever it seizes him, it dashes him down; and he foams and grinds his teeth and becomes rigid; and I asked your disciples to cast it out, but they could not do so."

mark:9:19.niv: "O unbelieving generation," Jesus replied, "how long shall I stay with you? How long shall I put up with you? Bring the boy to me."
mark:9:19.csb: He replied to them, “You unbelieving generation, how long will I be with you? How long must I put up with you? Bring him to me.”
mark:9:19.nrsv: He answered them, "You faithless generation, how much longer must I be among you? How much longer must I put up with you? Bring him to me."

mark:9:20.niv: So they brought him. When the spirit saw Jesus, it immediately threw the boy into a convulsion. He fell to the ground and rolled around, foaming at the mouth.
mark:9:20.csb: So they brought the boy to him. When the spirit saw him, it immediately threw the boy into convulsions. He fell to the ground and rolled around, foaming at the mouth.
mark:9:20.nrsv: And they brought the boy to him. When the spirit saw him, immediately it convulsed the boy, and he fell on the ground and rolled about, foaming at the mouth.

mark:9:21.niv: Jesus asked the boy's father, "How long has he been like this?"
mark:9:21.csb: “How long has this been happening to him?” Jesus asked his father. “From childhood,” he said.
mark:9:21.nrsv: Jesus asked the father, "How long has this been happening to him?" And he said, "From childhood.

mark:9:22.niv: "From childhood," he answered. "It has often thrown him into fire or water to kill him. But if you can do anything, take pity on us and help us."
mark:9:22.csb: “And many times it has thrown him into fire or water to destroy him. But if you can do anything, have compassion on us and help us.”
mark:9:22.nrsv: It has often cast him into the fire and into the water, to destroy him; but if you are able to do anything, have pity on us and help us."

mark:9:23.niv: "'If you can'?" said Jesus. "Everything is possible for him who believes."
mark:9:23.csb: Jesus said to him, “‘If you can’? Everything is possible for the one who believes.”
mark:9:23.nrsv: Jesus said to him, "If you are able!-- All things can be done for the one who believes."

mark:9:24.niv: Immediately the boy's father exclaimed, "I do believe; help me overcome my unbelief!"
mark:9:24.csb: Immediately the father of the boy cried out, “I do believe; help my unbelief!”
mark:9:24.nrsv: Immediately the father of the child cried out, "I believe; help my unbelief!"

mark:9:25.niv: When Jesus saw that a crowd was running to the scene, he rebuked the evil spirit. "You deaf and mute spirit," he said, "I command you, come out of him and never enter him again."
mark:9:25.csb: When Jesus saw that a crowd was quickly gathering, he rebuked the unclean spirit, saying to it, “You mute and deaf spirit, I command you: Come out of him and never enter him again.”
mark:9:25.nrsv: When Jesus saw that a crowd came running together, he rebuked the unclean spirit, saying to it, "You spirit that keeps this boy from speaking and hearing, I command you, come out of him, and never enter him again!"

mark:9:26.niv: The spirit shrieked, convulsed him violently and came out. The boy looked so much like a corpse that many said, "He's dead."
mark:9:26.csb: Then it came out, shrieking and throwing him into terrible convulsions. The boy became like a corpse, so that many said, “He’s dead.”
mark:9:26.nrsv: After crying out and convulsing him terribly, it came out, and the boy was like a corpse, so that most of them said, "He is dead."

mark:9:27.niv: But Jesus took him by the hand and lifted him to his feet, and he stood up.
mark:9:27.csb: But Jesus, taking him by the hand, raised him, and he stood up.
mark:9:27.nrsv: But Jesus took him by the hand and lifted him up, and he was able to stand.

mark:9:28.niv: After Jesus had gone indoors, his disciples asked him privately, "Why couldn't we drive it out?"
mark:9:28.csb: After he had gone into the house, his disciples asked him privately, “Why couldn’t we drive it out?”
mark:9:28.nrsv: When he had entered the house, his disciples asked him privately, "Why could we not cast it out?"

mark:9:29.niv: He replied, "This kind can come out only by prayer. "
mark:9:29.csb: And he told them, “This kind can come out by nothing but prayer.”
mark:9:29.nrsv: He said to them, "This kind can come out only through prayer."

mark:9:30.niv: They left that place and passed through Galilee. Jesus did not want anyone to know where they were,
mark:9:30.csb: Then they left that place and made their way through Galilee, but he did not want anyone to know it.
mark:9:30.nrsv: They went on from there and passed through Galilee. He did not want anyone to know it;

mark:9:31.niv: because he was teaching his disciples. He said to them, "The Son of Man is going to be betrayed into the hands of men. They will kill him, and after three days he will rise."
mark:9:31.csb: For he was teaching his disciples and telling them, “The Son of Man is going to be betrayed into the hands of men. They will kill him, and after he is killed, he will rise three days later.”
mark:9:31.nrsv: for he was teaching his disciples, saying to them, "The Son of Man is to be betrayed into human hands, and they will kill him, and three days after being killed, he will rise again."

mark:9:32.niv: But they did not understand what he meant and were afraid to ask him about it.
mark:9:32.csb: But they did not understand this statement, and they were afraid to ask him.
mark:9:32.nrsv: But they did not understand what he was saying and were afraid to ask him.

mark:9:33.niv: They came to Capernaum. When he was in the house, he asked them, "What were you arguing about on the road?"
mark:9:33.csb: They came to Capernaum. When he was in the house, he asked them, “What were you arguing about on the way?”
mark:9:33.nrsv: Then they came to Capernaum; and when he was in the house he asked them, "What were you arguing about on the way?"

mark:9:34.niv: But they kept quiet because on the way they had argued about who was the greatest.
mark:9:34.csb: But they were silent, because on the way they had been arguing with one another about who was the greatest.
mark:9:34.nrsv: But they were silent, for on the way they had argued with one another who was the greatest.

mark:9:35.niv: Sitting down, Jesus called the Twelve and said, "If anyone wants to be first, he must be the very last, and the servant of all."
mark:9:35.csb: Sitting down, he called the Twelve and said to them, “If anyone wants to be first, he must be last and servant of all.”
mark:9:35.nrsv: He sat down, called the twelve, and said to them, "Whoever wants to be first must be last of all and servant of all."

mark:9:36.niv: He took a little child and had him stand among them. Taking him in his arms, he said to them,
mark:9:36.csb: He took a child, had him stand among them, and taking him in his arms, he said to them,
mark:9:36.nrsv: Then he took a little child and put it among them; and taking it in his arms, he said to them,

mark:9:37.niv: "Whoever welcomes one of these little children in my name welcomes me; and whoever welcomes me does not welcome me but the one who sent me."
mark:9:37.csb: “Whoever welcomes one little child such as this in my name welcomes me. And whoever welcomes me does not welcome me, but him who sent me.”
mark:9:37.nrsv: "Whoever welcomes one such child in my name welcomes me, and whoever welcomes me welcomes not me but the one who sent me."

mark:9:38.niv: "Teacher," said John, "we saw a man driving out demons in your name and we told him to stop, because he was not one of us."
mark:9:38.csb: John said to him, “Teacher, we saw someone driving out demons in your name, and we tried to stop him because he wasn’t following us.”
mark:9:38.nrsv: John said to him, "Teacher, we saw someone casting out demons in your name, and we tried to stop him, because he was not following us."

mark:9:39.niv: "Do not stop him," Jesus said. "No one who does a miracle in my name can in the next moment say anything bad about me,
mark:9:39.csb: “Don’t stop him,” said Jesus, “because there is no one who will perform a miracle in my name who can soon afterward speak evil of me.
mark:9:39.nrsv: But Jesus said, "Do not stop him; for no one who does a deed of power in my name will be able soon afterward to speak evil of me.

mark:9:40.niv: for whoever is not against us is for us.
mark:9:40.csb: For whoever is not against us is for us.
mark:9:40.nrsv: Whoever is not against us is for us.

mark:9:41.niv: I tell you the truth, anyone who gives you a cup of water in my name because you belong to Christ will certainly not lose his reward.
mark:9:41.csb: And whoever gives you a cup of water to drink in my name, because you belong to Christ — truly I tell you, he will never lose his reward.
mark:9:41.nrsv: For truly I tell you, whoever gives you a cup of water to drink because you bear the name of Christ will by no means lose the reward.

mark:9:42.niv: "And if anyone causes one of these little ones who believe in me to sin, it would be better for him to be thrown into the sea with a large millstone tied around his neck.
mark:9:42.csb: “But whoever causes one of these little ones who believe in me to fall away — it would be better for him if a heavy millstone were hung around his neck and he were thrown into the sea.
mark:9:42.nrsv: "If any of you put a stumbling block before one of these little ones who believe in me, it would be better for you if a great millstone were hung around your neck and you were thrown into the sea.

mark:9:43.niv: If your hand causes you to sin, cut it off. It is better for you to enter life maimed than with two hands to go into hell, where the fire never goes out.
mark:9:43.csb: “And if your hand causes you to fall away, cut it off. It is better for you to enter life maimed than to have two hands and go to hell, the unquenchable fire.
mark:9:43.nrsv: If your hand causes you to stumble, cut it off; it is better for you to enter life maimed than to have two hands and to go to hell, to the unquenchable fire.

mark:9:45.niv: And if your foot causes you to sin, cut it off. It is better for you to enter life crippled than to have two feet and be thrown into hell.
mark:9:45.csb: And if your foot causes you to fall away, cut it off. It is better for you to enter life lame than to have two feet and be thrown into hell.
mark:9:45.nrsv: And if your foot causes you to stumble, cut it off; it is better for you to enter life lame than to have two feet and to be thrown into hell.,

mark:9:47.niv: And if your eye causes you to sin, pluck it out. It is better for you to enter the kingdom of God with one eye than to have two eyes and be thrown into hell,
mark:9:47.csb: And if your eye causes you to fall away, gouge it out. It is better for you to enter the kingdom of God with one eye than to have two eyes and be thrown into hell,
mark:9:47.nrsv: And if your eye causes you to stumble, tear it out; it is better for you to enter the kingdom of God with one eye than to have two eyes and to be thrown into hell,

mark:9:48.niv: where "'their worm does not die, and the fire is not quenched.'
mark:9:48.csb: where their worm does not die, and the fire is not quenched.
mark:9:48.nrsv: where their worm never dies, and the fire is never quenched.

mark:9:49.niv: Everyone will be salted with fire.
mark:9:49.csb: For everyone will be salted with fire.
mark:9:49.nrsv: "For everyone will be salted with fire.

mark:9:50.niv: "Salt is good, but if it loses its saltiness, how can you make it salty again? Have salt in yourselves, and be at peace with each other."
mark:9:50.csb: Salt is good, but if the salt should lose its flavor, how can you season it? Have salt among yourselves, and be at peace with one another.”
mark:9:50.nrsv: Salt is good; but if salt has lost its saltiness, how can you season it? Have salt in yourselves, and be at peace with one another."

mark:10:1.niv: Jesus then left that place and went into the region of Judea and across the Jordan. Again crowds of people came to him, and as was his custom, he taught them.
mark:10:1.csb: He set out from there and went to the region of Judea and across the Jordan. Then crowds converged on him again, and as was his custom he taught them again.
mark:10:1.nrsv: He left that place and went to the region of Judea and beyond the Jordan. And crowds again gathered around him; and, as was his custom, he again taught them.

mark:10:2.niv: Some Pharisees came and tested him by asking, "Is it lawful for a man to divorce his wife?"
mark:10:2.csb: Some Pharisees came to test him, asking, “Is it lawful for a man to divorce his wife?”
mark:10:2.nrsv: Some Pharisees came, and to test him they asked, "Is it lawful for a man to divorce his wife?"

mark:10:3.niv: "What did Moses command you?" he replied.
mark:10:3.csb: He replied to them, “What did Moses command you?”
mark:10:3.nrsv: He answered them, "What did Moses command you?"

mark:10:4.niv: They said, "Moses permitted a man to write a certificate of divorce and send her away."
mark:10:4.csb: They said, “Moses permitted us to write divorce papers and send her away.”
mark:10:4.nrsv: They said, "Moses allowed a man to write a certificate of dismissal and to divorce her."

mark:10:5.niv: "It was because your hearts were hard that Moses wrote you this law," Jesus replied.
mark:10:5.csb: But Jesus told them, “He wrote this command for you because of the hardness of your hearts.
mark:10:5.nrsv: But Jesus said to them, "Because of your hardness of heart he wrote this commandment for you.

mark:10:6.niv: "But at the beginning of creation God 'made them male and female.'
mark:10:6.csb: But from the beginning of creation God made them male and female.
mark:10:6.nrsv: But from the beginning of creation, 'God made them male and female.'

mark:10:7.niv: 'For this reason a man will leave his father and mother and be united to his wife,
mark:10:7.csb: For this reason a man will leave his father and mother
mark:10:7.nrsv: 'For this reason a man shall leave his father and mother and be joined to his wife,

mark:10:8.niv: and the two will become one flesh.' So they are no longer two, but one.
mark:10:8.csb: and the two will become one flesh. So they are no longer two, but one flesh.
mark:10:8.nrsv: and the two shall become one flesh.' So they are no longer two, but one flesh.

mark:10:9.niv: Therefore what God has joined together, let man not separate."
mark:10:9.csb: Therefore what God has joined together, let no one separate.”
mark:10:9.nrsv: Therefore what God has joined together, let no one separate."

mark:10:10.niv: When they were in the house again, the disciples asked Jesus about this.
mark:10:10.csb: When they were in the house again, the disciples questioned him about this matter.
mark:10:10.nrsv: Then in the house the disciples asked him again about this matter.

mark:10:11.niv: He answered, "Anyone who divorces his wife and marries another woman commits adultery against her.
mark:10:11.csb: He said to them, “Whoever divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery against her.
mark:10:11.nrsv: He said to them, "Whoever divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery against her;

mark:10:12.niv: And if she divorces her husband and marries another man, she commits adultery."
mark:10:12.csb: Also, if she divorces her husband and marries another, she commits adultery.”
mark:10:12.nrsv: and if she divorces her husband and marries another, she commits adultery."

mark:10:13.niv: People were bringing little children to Jesus to have him touch them, but the disciples rebuked them.
mark:10:13.csb: People were bringing little children to him in order that he might touch them, but the disciples rebuked them.
mark:10:13.nrsv: People were bringing little children to him in order that he might touch them; and the disciples spoke sternly to them.

mark:10:14.niv: When Jesus saw this, he was indignant. He said to them, "Let the little children come to me, and do not hinder them, for the kingdom of God belongs to such as these.
mark:10:14.csb: When Jesus saw it, he was indignant and said to them, “Let the little children come to me. Don’t stop them, because the kingdom of God belongs to such as these.
mark:10:14.nrsv: But when Jesus saw this, he was indignant and said to them, "Let the little children come to me; do not stop them; for it is to such as these that the kingdom of God belongs.

mark:10:15.niv: I tell you the truth, anyone who will not receive the kingdom of God like a little child will never enter it."
mark:10:15.csb: Truly I tell you, whoever does not receive the kingdom of God like a little child will never enter it.”
mark:10:15.nrsv: Truly I tell you, whoever does not receive the kingdom of God as a little child will never enter it."

mark:10:16.niv: And he took the children in his arms, put his hands on them and blessed them.
mark:10:16.csb: After taking them in his arms, he laid his hands on them and blessed them.
mark:10:16.nrsv: And he took them up in his arms, laid his hands on them, and blessed them.

mark:10:17.niv: As Jesus started on his way, a man ran up to him and fell on his knees before him. "Good teacher," he asked, "what must I do to inherit eternal life?"
mark:10:17.csb: As he was setting out on a journey, a man ran up, knelt down before him, and asked him, “Good teacher, what must I do to inherit eternal life?”
mark:10:17.nrsv: As he was setting out on a journey, a man ran up and knelt before him, and asked him, "Good Teacher, what must I do to inherit eternal life?"

mark:10:18.niv: "Why do you call me good?" Jesus answered. "No one is good--except God alone.
mark:10:18.csb: “Why do you call me good?” Jesus asked him. “No one is good except God alone.
mark:10:18.nrsv: Jesus said to him, "Why do you call me good? No one is good but God alone.

mark:10:19.niv: You know the commandments: 'Do not murder, do not commit adultery, do not steal, do not give false testimony, do not defraud, honor your father and mother.'"
mark:10:19.csb: You know the commandments: Do not murder; do not commit adultery; do not steal; do not bear false witness; do not defraud; honor your father and mother. ”
mark:10:19.nrsv: You know the commandments: 'You shall not murder; You shall not commit adultery; You shall not steal; You shall not bear false witness; You shall not defraud; Honor your father and mother.'"

mark:10:20.niv: "Teacher," he declared, "all these I have kept since I was a boy."
mark:10:20.csb: He said to him, “Teacher, I have kept all these from my youth.”
mark:10:20.nrsv: He said to him, "Teacher, I have kept all these since my youth."

mark:10:21.niv: Jesus looked at him and loved him. "One thing you lack," he said. "Go, sell everything you have and give to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven. Then come, follow me."
mark:10:21.csb: Looking at him, Jesus loved him and said to him, “You lack one thing: Go, sell all you have and give to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven. Then come, follow me.”
mark:10:21.nrsv: Jesus, looking at him, loved him and said, "You lack one thing; go, sell what you own, and give the money to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven; then come, follow me."

mark:10:22.niv: At this the man's face fell. He went away sad, because he had great wealth.
mark:10:22.csb: But he was dismayed by this demand, and he went away grieving, because he had many possessions.
mark:10:22.nrsv: When he heard this, he was shocked and went away grieving, for he had many possessions.

mark:10:23.niv: Jesus looked around and said to his disciples, "How hard it is for the rich to enter the kingdom of God!"
mark:10:23.csb: Jesus looked around and said to his disciples, “How hard it is for those who have wealth to enter the kingdom of God!”
mark:10:23.nrsv: Then Jesus looked around and said to his disciples, "How hard it will be for those who have wealth to enter the kingdom of God!"

mark:10:24.niv: The disciples were amazed at his words. But Jesus said again, "Children, how hard it is to enter the kingdom of God!
mark:10:24.csb: The disciples were astonished at his words. Again Jesus said to them, “Children, how hard it is to enter the kingdom of God!
mark:10:24.nrsv: And the disciples were perplexed at these words. But Jesus said to them again, "Children, how hard it is to enter the kingdom of God!

mark:10:25.niv: It is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich man to enter the kingdom of God."
mark:10:25.csb: It is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter the kingdom of God.”
mark:10:25.nrsv: It is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for someone who is rich to enter the kingdom of God."

mark:10:26.niv: The disciples were even more amazed, and said to each other, "Who then can be saved?"
mark:10:26.csb: They were even more astonished, saying to one another, “Then who can be saved?”
mark:10:26.nrsv: They were greatly astounded and said to one another, "Then who can be saved?"

mark:10:27.niv: Jesus looked at them and said, "With man this is impossible, but not with God; all things are possible with God."
mark:10:27.csb: Looking at them, Jesus said, “With man it is impossible, but not with God, because all things are possible with God.”
mark:10:27.nrsv: Jesus looked at them and said, "For mortals it is impossible, but not for God; for God all things are possible."

mark:10:28.niv: Peter said to him, "We have left everything to follow you!"
