mark:10:28.csb: Peter began to tell him, “Look, we have left everything and followed you.”
mark:10:28.nrsv: Peter began to say to him, "Look, we have left everything and followed you."

mark:10:29.niv: "I tell you the truth," Jesus replied, "no one who has left home or brothers or sisters or mother or father or children or fields for me and the gospel
mark:10:29.csb: “Truly I tell you,” Jesus said, “there is no one who has left house or brothers or sisters or mother or father or children or fields for my sake and for the sake of the gospel,
mark:10:29.nrsv: Jesus said, "Truly I tell you, there is no one who has left house or brothers or sisters or mother or father or children or fields, for my sake and for the sake of the good news,

mark:10:30.niv: will fail to receive a hundred times as much in this present age (homes, brothers, sisters, mothers, children and fields--and with them, persecutions) and in the age to come, eternal life.
mark:10:30.csb: who will not receive a hundred times more, now at this time — houses, brothers and sisters, mothers and children, and fields, with persecutions — and eternal life in the age to come.
mark:10:30.nrsv: who will not receive a hundredfold now in this age-- houses, brothers and sisters, mothers and children, and fields with persecutions-- and in the age to come eternal life.

mark:10:31.niv: But many who are first will be last, and the last first."
mark:10:31.csb: But many who are first will be last, and the last first.”
mark:10:31.nrsv: But many who are first will be last, and the last will be first."

mark:10:32.niv: They were on their way up to Jerusalem, with Jesus leading the way, and the disciples were astonished, while those who followed were afraid. Again he took the Twelve aside and told them what was going to happen to him.
mark:10:32.csb: They were on the road, going up to Jerusalem, and Jesus was walking ahead of them. The disciples were astonished, but those who followed him were afraid. Taking the Twelve aside again, he began to tell them the things that would happen to him.
mark:10:32.nrsv: They were on the road, going up to Jerusalem, and Jesus was walking ahead of them; they were amazed, and those who followed were afraid. He took the twelve aside again and began to tell them what was to happen to him,

mark:10:33.niv: "We are going up to Jerusalem," he said, "and the Son of Man will be betrayed to the chief priests and teachers of the law. They will condemn him to death and will hand him over to the Gentiles,
mark:10:33.csb: “See, we are going up to Jerusalem. The Son of Man will be handed over to the chief priests and the scribes, and they will condemn him to death. Then they will hand him over to the Gentiles,
mark:10:33.nrsv: saying, "See, we are going up to Jerusalem, and the Son of Man will be handed over to the chief priests and the scribes, and they will condemn him to death; then they will hand him over to the Gentiles;

mark:10:34.niv: who will mock him and spit on him, flog him and kill him. Three days later he will rise."
mark:10:34.csb: and they will mock him, spit on him, flog him, and kill him, and he will rise after three days.”
mark:10:34.nrsv: they will mock him, and spit upon him, and flog him, and kill him; and after three days he will rise again."

mark:10:35.niv: Then James and John, the sons of Zebedee, came to him. "Teacher," they said, "we want you to do for us whatever we ask."
mark:10:35.csb: James and John, the sons of Zebedee, approached him and said, “Teacher, we want you to do whatever we ask you.”
mark:10:35.nrsv: James and John, the sons of Zebedee, came forward to him and said to him, "Teacher, we want you to do for us whatever we ask of you."

mark:10:36.niv: "What do you want me to do for you?" he asked.
mark:10:36.csb: “What do you want me to do for you?” he asked them.
mark:10:36.nrsv: And he said to them, "What is it you want me to do for you?"

mark:10:37.niv: They replied, "Let one of us sit at your right and the other at your left in your glory."
mark:10:37.csb: They answered him, “Allow us to sit at your right and at your left in your glory.”
mark:10:37.nrsv: And they said to him, "Grant us to sit, one at your right hand and one at your left, in your glory."

mark:10:38.niv: "You don't know what you are asking," Jesus said. "Can you drink the cup I drink or be baptized with the baptism I am baptized with?"
mark:10:38.csb: Jesus said to them, “You don’t know what you’re asking. Are you able to drink the cup I drink or to be baptized with the baptism I am baptized with?”
mark:10:38.nrsv: But Jesus said to them, "You do not know what you are asking. Are you able to drink the cup that I drink, or be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with?"

mark:10:39.niv: "We can," they answered. Jesus said to them, "You will drink the cup I drink and be baptized with the baptism I am baptized with,
mark:10:39.csb: “We are able,” they told him. Jesus said to them, “You will drink the cup I drink, and you will be baptized with the baptism I am baptized with.
mark:10:39.nrsv: They replied, "We are able." Then Jesus said to them, "The cup that I drink you will drink; and with the baptism with which I am baptized, you will be baptized;

mark:10:40.niv: but to sit at my right or left is not for me to grant. These places belong to those for whom they have been prepared."
mark:10:40.csb: But to sit at my right or left is not mine to give; instead, it is for those for whom it has been prepared.”
mark:10:40.nrsv: but to sit at my right hand or at my left is not mine to grant, but it is for those for whom it has been prepared."

mark:10:41.niv: When the ten heard about this, they became indignant with James and John.
mark:10:41.csb: When the ten disciples heard this, they began to be indignant with James and John.
mark:10:41.nrsv: When the ten heard this, they began to be angry with James and John.

mark:10:42.niv: Jesus called them together and said, "You know that those who are regarded as rulers of the Gentiles lord it over them, and their high officials exercise authority over them.
mark:10:42.csb: Jesus called them over and said to them, “You know that those who are regarded as rulers of the Gentiles lord it over them, and those in high positions act as tyrants over them.
mark:10:42.nrsv: So Jesus called them and said to them, "You know that among the Gentiles those whom they recognize as their rulers lord it over them, and their great ones are tyrants over them.

mark:10:43.niv: Not so with you. Instead, whoever wants to become great among you must be your servant,
mark:10:43.csb: But it is not so among you. On the contrary, whoever wants to become great among you will be your servant,
mark:10:43.nrsv: But it is not so among you; but whoever wishes to become great among you must be your servant,

mark:10:44.niv: and whoever wants to be first must be slave of all.
mark:10:44.csb: and whoever wants to be first among you will be a slave to all.
mark:10:44.nrsv: and whoever wishes to be first among you must be slave of all.

mark:10:45.niv: For even the Son of Man did not come to be served, but to serve, and to give his life as a ransom for many."
mark:10:45.csb: For even the Son of Man did not come to be served, but to serve, and to give his life as a ransom for many.”
mark:10:45.nrsv: For the Son of Man came not to be served but to serve, and to give his life a ransom for many."

mark:10:46.niv: Then they came to Jericho. As Jesus and his disciples, together with a large crowd, were leaving the city, a blind man, Bartimaeus (that is, the Son of Timaeus), was sitting by the roadside begging.
mark:10:46.csb: They came to Jericho. And as he was leaving Jericho with his disciples and a large crowd, Bartimaeus (the son of Timaeus), a blind beggar, was sitting by the road.
mark:10:46.nrsv: They came to Jericho. As he and his disciples and a large crowd were leaving Jericho, Bartimaeus son of Timaeus, a blind beggar, was sitting by the roadside.

mark:10:47.niv: When he heard that it was Jesus of Nazareth, he began to shout, "Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me!"
mark:10:47.csb: When he heard that it was Jesus of Nazareth, he began to cry out, “Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me!”
mark:10:47.nrsv: When he heard that it was Jesus of Nazareth, he began to shout out and say, "Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me!"

mark:10:48.niv: Many rebuked him and told him to be quiet, but he shouted all the more, "Son of David, have mercy on me!"
mark:10:48.csb: Many warned him to keep quiet, but he was crying out all the more, “Have mercy on me, Son of David!”
mark:10:48.nrsv: Many sternly ordered him to be quiet, but he cried out even more loudly, "Son of David, have mercy on me!"

mark:10:49.niv: Jesus stopped and said, "Call him." So they called to the blind man, "Cheer up! On your feet! He's calling you."
mark:10:49.csb: Jesus stopped and said, “Call him.” So they called the blind man and said to him, “Have courage! Get up; he’s calling for you.”
mark:10:49.nrsv: Jesus stood still and said, "Call him here." And they called the blind man, saying to him, "Take heart; get up, he is calling you."

mark:10:50.niv: Throwing his cloak aside, he jumped to his feet and came to Jesus.
mark:10:50.csb: He threw off his coat, jumped up, and came to Jesus.
mark:10:50.nrsv: So throwing off his cloak, he sprang up and came to Jesus.

mark:10:51.niv: "What do you want me to do for you?" Jesus asked him. The blind man said, "Rabbi, I want to see."
mark:10:51.csb: Then Jesus answered him, “What do you want me to do for you?” “Rabboni,” the blind man said to him, “I want to see.”
mark:10:51.nrsv: Then Jesus said to him, "What do you want me to do for you?" The blind man said to him, "My teacher, let me see again."

mark:10:52.niv: "Go," said Jesus, "your faith has healed you." Immediately he received his sight and followed Jesus along the road.
mark:10:52.csb: Jesus said to him, “Go, your faith has saved you.” Immediately he could see and began to follow Jesus on the road.
mark:10:52.nrsv: Jesus said to him, "Go; your faith has made you well." Immediately he regained his sight and followed him on the way.

mark:11:1.niv: As they approached Jerusalem and came to Bethphage and Bethany at the Mount of Olives, Jesus sent two of his disciples,
mark:11:1.csb: When they approached Jerusalem, at Bethphage and Bethany near the Mount of Olives, he sent two of his disciples
mark:11:1.nrsv: When they were approaching Jerusalem, at Bethphage and Bethany, near the Mount of Olives, he sent two of his disciples

mark:11:2.niv: saying to them, "Go to the village ahead of you, and just as you enter it, you will find a colt tied there, which no one has ever ridden. Untie it and bring it here.
mark:11:2.csb: and told them, “Go into the village ahead of you. As soon as you enter it, you will find a colt tied there, on which no one has ever sat. Untie it and bring it.
mark:11:2.nrsv: and said to them, "Go into the village ahead of you, and immediately as you enter it, you will find tied there a colt that has never been ridden; untie it and bring it.

mark:11:3.niv: If anyone asks you, 'Why are you doing this?' tell him, 'The Lord needs it and will send it back here shortly.'"
mark:11:3.csb: If anyone says to you, ‘Why are you doing this? ’ say, ‘The Lord needs it and will send it back here right away.’”
mark:11:3.nrsv: If anyone says to you, 'Why are you doing this?' just say this, 'The Lord needs it and will send it back here immediately.'"

mark:11:4.niv: They went and found a colt outside in the street, tied at a doorway. As they untied it,
mark:11:4.csb: So they went and found a colt outside in the street, tied by a door. They untied it,
mark:11:4.nrsv: They went away and found a colt tied near a door, outside in the street. As they were untying it,

mark:11:5.niv: some people standing there asked, "What are you doing, untying that colt?"
mark:11:5.csb: and some of those standing there said to them, “What are you doing, untying the colt?”
mark:11:5.nrsv: some of the bystanders said to them, "What are you doing, untying the colt?"

mark:11:6.niv: They answered as Jesus had told them to, and the people let them go.
mark:11:6.csb: They answered them just as Jesus had said; so they let them go.
mark:11:6.nrsv: They told them what Jesus had said; and they allowed them to take it.

mark:11:7.niv: When they brought the colt to Jesus and threw their cloaks over it, he sat on it.
mark:11:7.csb: They brought the donkey to Jesus and threw their clothes on it, and he sat on it.
mark:11:7.nrsv: Then they brought the colt to Jesus and threw their cloaks on it; and he sat on it.

mark:11:8.niv: Many people spread their cloaks on the road, while others spread branches they had cut in the fields.
mark:11:8.csb: Many people spread their clothes on the road, and others spread leafy branches cut from the fields.
mark:11:8.nrsv: Many people spread their cloaks on the road, and others spread leafy branches that they had cut in the fields.

mark:11:9.niv: Those who went ahead and those who followed shouted, "Hosanna! Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord!"
mark:11:9.csb: Those who went ahead and those who followed shouted: Hosanna! Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord!
mark:11:9.nrsv: Then those who went ahead and those who followed were shouting, "Hosanna! Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord!

mark:11:10.niv: "Blessed is the coming kingdom of our father David!Hosanna in the highest!"
mark:11:10.csb: Blessed is the coming kingdom of our father David! Hosanna in the highest heaven!
mark:11:10.nrsv: Blessed is the coming kingdom of our ancestor David! Hosanna in the highest heaven!"

mark:11:11.niv: Jesus entered Jerusalem and went to the temple. He looked around at everything, but since it was already late, he went out to Bethany with the Twelve.
mark:11:11.csb: He went into Jerusalem and into the temple. After looking around at everything, since it was already late, he went out to Bethany with the Twelve.
mark:11:11.nrsv: Then he entered Jerusalem and went into the temple; and when he had looked around at everything, as it was already late, he went out to Bethany with the twelve.

mark:11:12.niv: The next day as they were leaving Bethany, Jesus was hungry.
mark:11:12.csb: The next day when they went out from Bethany, he was hungry.
mark:11:12.nrsv: On the following day, when they came from Bethany, he was hungry.

mark:11:13.niv: Seeing in the distance a fig tree in leaf, he went to find out if it had any fruit. When he reached it, he found nothing but leaves, because it was not the season for figs.
mark:11:13.csb: Seeing in the distance a fig tree with leaves, he went to find out if there was anything on it. When he came to it, he found nothing but leaves; for it was not the season for figs.
mark:11:13.nrsv: Seeing in the distance a fig tree in leaf, he went to see whether perhaps he would find anything on it. When he came to it, he found nothing but leaves, for it was not the season for figs.

mark:11:14.niv: Then he said to the tree, "May no one ever eat fruit from you again." And his disciples heard him say it.
mark:11:14.csb: He said to it, “May no one ever eat fruit from you again!” And his disciples heard it.
mark:11:14.nrsv: He said to it, "May no one ever eat fruit from you again." And his disciples heard it.

mark:11:15.niv: On reaching Jerusalem, Jesus entered the temple area and began driving out those who were buying and selling there. He overturned the tables of the money changers and the benches of those selling doves,
mark:11:15.csb: They came to Jerusalem, and he went into the temple and began to throw out those buying and selling. He overturned the tables of the money changers and the chairs of those selling doves,
mark:11:15.nrsv: Then they came to Jerusalem. And he entered the temple and began to drive out those who were selling and those who were buying in the temple, and he overturned the tables of the money changers and the seats of those who sold doves;

mark:11:16.niv: and would not allow anyone to carry merchandise through the temple courts.
mark:11:16.csb: and would not permit anyone to carry goods through the temple.
mark:11:16.nrsv: and he would not allow anyone to carry anything through the temple.

mark:11:17.niv: And as he taught them, he said, "Is it not written: "'My house will be called a house of prayer for all nations'? But you have made it 'a den of robbers.'"
mark:11:17.csb: He was teaching them: “Is it not written, My house will be called a house of prayer for all nations? But you have made it a den of thieves!”
mark:11:17.nrsv: He was teaching and saying, "Is it not written, 'My house shall be called a house of prayer for all the nations'? But you have made it a den of robbers."

mark:11:18.niv: The chief priests and the teachers of the law heard this and began looking for a way to kill him, for they feared him, because the whole crowd was amazed at his teaching.
mark:11:18.csb: The chief priests and the scribes heard it and started looking for a way to kill him. For they were afraid of him, because the whole crowd was astonished by his teaching.
mark:11:18.nrsv: And when the chief priests and the scribes heard it, they kept looking for a way to kill him; for they were afraid of him, because the whole crowd was spellbound by his teaching.

mark:11:19.niv: When evening came, they went out of the city.
mark:11:19.csb: Whenever evening came, they would go out of the city.
mark:11:19.nrsv: And when evening came, Jesus and his disciples went out of the city.

mark:11:20.niv: In the morning, as they went along, they saw the fig tree withered from the roots.
mark:11:20.csb: Early in the morning, as they were passing by, they saw the fig tree withered from the roots up.
mark:11:20.nrsv: In the morning as they passed by, they saw the fig tree withered away to its roots.

mark:11:21.niv: Peter remembered and said to Jesus, "Rabbi, look! The fig tree you cursed has withered!"
mark:11:21.csb: Then Peter remembered and said to him, “Rabbi, look! The fig tree that you cursed has withered.”
mark:11:21.nrsv: Then Peter remembered and said to him, "Rabbi, look! The fig tree that you cursed has withered."

mark:11:22.csb: Jesus replied to them, “Have faith in God.
mark:11:22.nrsv: Jesus answered them, "Have faith in God.

mark:11:23.niv: "I tell you the truth, if anyone says to this mountain, 'Go, throw yourself into the sea,' and does not doubt in his heart but believes that what he says will happen, it will be done for him.
mark:11:23.csb: Truly I tell you, if anyone says to this mountain, ‘Be lifted up and thrown into the sea,’ and does not doubt in his heart, but believes that what he says will happen, it will be done for him.
mark:11:23.nrsv: Truly I tell you, if you say to this mountain, 'Be taken up and thrown into the sea,' and if you do not doubt in your heart, but believe that what you say will come to pass, it will be done for you.

mark:11:24.niv: Therefore I tell you, whatever you ask for in prayer, believe that you have received it, and it will be yours.
mark:11:24.csb: Therefore I tell you, everything you pray and ask for — believe that you have received it and it will be yours.
mark:11:24.nrsv: So I tell you, whatever you ask for in prayer, believe that you have received it, and it will be yours.

mark:11:25.niv: And when you stand praying, if you hold anything against anyone, forgive him, so that your Father in heaven may forgive you your sins."
mark:11:25.csb: And whenever you stand praying, if you have anything against anyone, forgive him, so that your Father in heaven will also forgive you your wrongdoing.”
mark:11:25.nrsv: "Whenever you stand praying, forgive, if you have anything against anyone; so that your Father in heaven may also forgive you your trespasses."

mark:11:27.niv: They arrived again in Jerusalem, and while Jesus was walking in the temple courts, the chief priests, the teachers of the law and the elders came to him.
mark:11:27.csb: They came again to Jerusalem. As he was walking in the temple, the chief priests, the scribes, and the elders came
mark:11:27.nrsv: Again they came to Jerusalem. As he was walking in the temple, the chief priests, the scribes, and the elders came to him

mark:11:28.niv: "By what authority are you doing these things?" they asked. "And who gave you authority to do this?"
mark:11:28.csb: and asked him, “By what authority are you doing these things? Who gave you this authority to do these things?”
mark:11:28.nrsv: and said, "By what authority are you doing these things? Who gave you this authority to do them?"

mark:11:29.niv: Jesus replied, "I will ask you one question. Answer me, and I will tell you by what authority I am doing these things.
mark:11:29.csb: Jesus said to them, “I will ask you one question; then answer me, and I will tell you by what authority I do these things.
mark:11:29.nrsv: Jesus said to them, "I will ask you one question; answer me, and I will tell you by what authority I do these things.

mark:11:30.niv: John's baptism--was it from heaven, or from men? Tell me!"
mark:11:30.csb: Was John’s baptism from heaven or of human origin? Answer me.”
mark:11:30.nrsv: Did the baptism of John come from heaven, or was it of human origin? Answer me."

mark:11:31.niv: They discussed it among themselves and said, "If we say, 'From heaven,' he will ask, 'Then why didn't you believe him?'
mark:11:31.csb: They discussed it among themselves: “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ he will say, ‘Then why didn’t you believe him?’
mark:11:31.nrsv: They argued with one another, "If we say, 'From heaven,' he will say, 'Why then did you not believe him?'

mark:11:32.niv: But if we say, 'From men'...." (They feared the people, for everyone held that John really was a prophet.)
mark:11:32.csb: But if we say, ‘Of human origin’” — they were afraid of the crowd, because everyone thought that John was truly a prophet.
mark:11:32.nrsv: But shall we say, 'Of human origin'?"-- they were afraid of the crowd, for all regarded John as truly a prophet.

mark:11:33.niv: So they answered Jesus, "We don't know." Jesus said, "Neither will I tell you by what authority I am doing these things."
mark:11:33.csb: So they answered Jesus, “We don’t know.” And Jesus said to them, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I do these things.”
mark:11:33.nrsv: So they answered Jesus, "We do not know." And Jesus said to them, "Neither will I tell you by what authority I am doing these things."

mark:12:1.niv: He then began to speak to them in parables: "A man planted a vineyard. He put a wall around it, dug a pit for the winepress and built a watchtower. Then he rented the vineyard to some farmers and went away on a journey.
mark:12:1.csb: He began to speak to them in parables: “A man planted a vineyard, put a fence around it, dug out a pit for a winepress, and built a watchtower. Then he leased it to tenant farmers and went away.
mark:12:1.nrsv: Then he began to speak to them in parables. "A man planted a vineyard, put a fence around it, dug a pit for the wine press, and built a watchtower; then he leased it to tenants and went to another country.

mark:12:2.niv: At harvest time he sent a servant to the tenants to collect from them some of the fruit of the vineyard.
mark:12:2.csb: At harvest time he sent a servant to the farmers to collect some of the fruit of the vineyard from them.
mark:12:2.nrsv: When the season came, he sent a slave to the tenants to collect from them his share of the produce of the vineyard.

mark:12:3.niv: But they seized him, beat him and sent him away empty-handed.
mark:12:3.csb: But they took him, beat him, and sent him away empty-handed.
mark:12:3.nrsv: But they seized him, and beat him, and sent him away empty-handed.

mark:12:4.niv: Then he sent another servant to them; they struck this man on the head and treated him shamefully.
mark:12:4.csb: Again he sent another servant to them, and they hit him on the head and treated him shamefully.
mark:12:4.nrsv: And again he sent another slave to them; this one they beat over the head and insulted.

mark:12:5.niv: He sent still another, and that one they killed. He sent many others; some of them they beat, others they killed.
mark:12:5.csb: Then he sent another, and they killed that one. He also sent many others; some they beat, and others they killed.
mark:12:5.nrsv: Then he sent another, and that one they killed. And so it was with many others; some they beat, and others they killed.

mark:12:6.niv: "He had one left to send, a son, whom he loved. He sent him last of all, saying, 'They will respect my son.'
mark:12:6.csb: He still had one to send, a beloved son. Finally he sent him to them, saying, ‘They will respect my son.’
mark:12:6.nrsv: He had still one other, a beloved son. Finally he sent him to them, saying, 'They will respect my son.'

mark:12:7.niv: "But the tenants said to one another, 'This is the heir. Come, let's kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.'
mark:12:7.csb: But those tenant farmers said to one another, ‘This is the heir. Come, let’s kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.’
mark:12:7.nrsv: But those tenants said to one another, 'This is the heir; come, let us kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.'

mark:12:8.niv: So they took him and killed him, and threw him out of the vineyard.
mark:12:8.csb: So they seized him, killed him, and threw him out of the vineyard.
mark:12:8.nrsv: So they seized him, killed him, and threw him out of the vineyard.

mark:12:9.niv: "What then will the owner of the vineyard do? He will come and kill those tenants and give the vineyard to others.
mark:12:9.csb: What then will the owner of the vineyard do? He will come and kill the farmers and give the vineyard to others.
mark:12:9.nrsv: What then will the owner of the vineyard do? He will come and destroy the tenants and give the vineyard to others.

mark:12:10.niv: Haven't you read this scripture: "'The stone the builders rejected has become the capstone;
mark:12:10.csb: Haven’t you read this Scripture: The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone.
mark:12:10.nrsv: Have you not read this scripture: 'The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone;

mark:12:11.niv: the Lord has done this, and it is marvelous in our eyes'?"
mark:12:11.csb: This came about from the Lord and is wonderful in our eyes?”
mark:12:11.nrsv: this was the Lord's doing, and it is amazing in our eyes'?"

mark:12:12.niv: Then they looked for a way to arrest him because they knew he had spoken the parable against them. But they were afraid of the crowd; so they left him and went away.
mark:12:12.csb: They were looking for a way to arrest him but feared the crowd because they knew he had spoken this parable against them. So they left him and went away.
mark:12:12.nrsv: When they realized that he had told this parable against them, they wanted to arrest him, but they feared the crowd. So they left him and went away.

mark:12:13.niv: Later they sent some of the Pharisees and Herodians to Jesus to catch him in his words.
mark:12:13.csb: Then they sent some of the Pharisees and the Herodians to Jesus to trap him in his words.
mark:12:13.nrsv: Then they sent to him some Pharisees and some Herodians to trap him in what he said.

mark:12:14.niv: They came to him and said, "Teacher, we know you are a man of integrity. You aren't swayed by men, because you pay no attention to who they are; but you teach the way of God in accordance with the truth. Is it right to pay taxes to Caesar or not?
mark:12:14.csb: When they came, they said to him, “Teacher, we know you are truthful and don’t care what anyone thinks, nor do you show partiality but teach the way of God truthfully. Is it lawful to pay taxes to Caesar or not? Should we pay or shouldn’t we?”
mark:12:14.nrsv: And they came and said to him, "Teacher, we know that you are sincere, and show deference to no one; for you do not regard people with partiality, but teach the way of God in accordance with truth. Is it lawful to pay taxes to the emperor, or not?

mark:12:15.niv: Should we pay or shouldn't we?"
mark:12:15.csb: But knowing their hypocrisy, he said to them, “Why are you testing me? Bring me a denarius to look at.”
mark:12:15.nrsv: Should we pay them, or should we not?" But knowing their hypocrisy, he said to them, "Why are you putting me to the test? Bring me a denarius and let me see it."

mark:12:16.niv: But Jesus knew their hypocrisy. "Why are you trying to trap me?" he asked. "Bring me a denarius and let me look at it." They brought the coin, and he asked them, "Whose portrait is this? And whose inscription?Caesar's," they replied.
mark:12:16.csb: They brought a coin. “Whose image and inscription is this?” he asked them. “Caesar’s,” they replied.
mark:12:16.nrsv: And they brought one. Then he said to them, "Whose head is this, and whose title?" They answered, "The emperor's."

mark:12:17.niv: Then Jesus said to them, "Give to Caesar what is Caesar's and to God what is God's." And they were amazed at him.
mark:12:17.csb: Jesus told them, “Give to Caesar the things that are Caesar’s, and to God the things that are God’s.” And they were utterly amazed at him.
mark:12:17.nrsv: Jesus said to them, "Give to the emperor the things that are the emperor's, and to God the things that are God's." And they were utterly amazed at him.

mark:12:18.niv: Then the Sadducees, who say there is no resurrection, came to him with a question.
mark:12:18.csb: Sadducees, who say there is no resurrection, came to him and questioned him:
mark:12:18.nrsv: Some Sadducees, who say there is no resurrection, came to him and asked him a question, saying,

mark:12:19.niv: "Teacher," they said, "Moses wrote for us that if a man's brother dies and leaves a wife but no children, the man must marry the widow and have children for his brother.
mark:12:19.csb: “Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a man’s brother dies, leaving a wife behind but no child, that man should take the wife and raise up offspring for his brother.
mark:12:19.nrsv: "Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a man's brother dies, leaving a wife but no child, the man shall marry the widow and raise up children for his brother.

mark:12:20.niv: Now there were seven brothers. The first one married and died without leaving any children.
mark:12:20.csb: There were seven brothers. The first married a woman, and dying, left no offspring.
mark:12:20.nrsv: There were seven brothers; the first married and, when he died, left no children;

mark:12:21.niv: The second one married the widow, but he also died, leaving no child. It was the same with the third.
mark:12:21.csb: The second also took her, and he died, leaving no offspring. And the third likewise.
mark:12:21.nrsv: and the second married her and died, leaving no children; and the third likewise;

mark:12:22.niv: In fact, none of the seven left any children. Last of all, the woman died too.
mark:12:22.csb: None of the seven left offspring. Last of all, the woman died too.
mark:12:22.nrsv: none of the seven left children. Last of all the woman herself died.

mark:12:23.niv: At the resurrection whose wife will she be, since the seven were married to her?"
mark:12:23.csb: In the resurrection, when they rise, whose wife will she be, since the seven had married her?”
mark:12:23.nrsv: In the resurrection whose wife will she be? For the seven had married her."

mark:12:24.niv: Jesus replied, "Are you not in error because you do not know the Scriptures or the power of God?
mark:12:24.csb: Jesus spoke to them, “Isn’t this the reason why you’re mistaken: you don’t know the Scriptures or the power of God?
mark:12:24.nrsv: Jesus said to them, "Is not this the reason you are wrong, that you know neither the scriptures nor the power of God?

mark:12:25.niv: When the dead rise, they will neither marry nor be given in marriage; they will be like the angels in heaven.
mark:12:25.csb: For when they rise from the dead, they neither marry nor are given in marriage but are like angels in heaven.
mark:12:25.nrsv: For when they rise from the dead, they neither marry nor are given in marriage, but are like angels in heaven.

mark:12:26.niv: Now about the dead rising--have you not read in the book of Moses, in the account of the bush, how God said to him, 'I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob'?
mark:12:26.csb: And as for the dead being raised — haven’t you read in the book of Moses, in the passage about the burning bush, how God said to him: I am the God of Abraham and the God of Isaac and the God of Jacob?
mark:12:26.nrsv: And as for the dead being raised, have you not read in the book of Moses, in the story about the bush, how God said to him, 'I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob'?

mark:12:27.niv: He is not the God of the dead, but of the living. You are badly mistaken!"
mark:12:27.csb: He is not the God of the dead but of the living. You are badly mistaken.”
mark:12:27.nrsv: He is God not of the dead, but of the living; you are quite wrong."

mark:12:28.niv: One of the teachers of the law came and heard them debating. Noticing that Jesus had given them a good answer, he asked him, "Of all the commandments, which is the most important?"
mark:12:28.csb: One of the scribes approached. When he heard them debating and saw that Jesus answered them well, he asked him, “Which command is the most important of all?”
mark:12:28.nrsv: One of the scribes came near and heard them disputing with one another, and seeing that he answered them well, he asked him, "Which commandment is the first of all?"

mark:12:29.niv: "The most important one," answered Jesus, "is this: 'Hear, O Israel, the Lord our God, the Lord is one.
mark:12:29.csb: Jesus answered, “The most important is Listen, O Israel! The Lord our God, the Lord is one.
mark:12:29.nrsv: Jesus answered, "The first is, 'Hear, O Israel: the Lord our God, the Lord is one;

mark:12:30.niv: Love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your mind and with all your strength.'
mark:12:30.csb: Love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your soul, with all your mind, and with all your strength.
mark:12:30.nrsv: you shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, and with all your soul, and with all your mind, and with all your strength.'

mark:12:31.niv: The second is this: 'Love your neighbor as yourself.' There is no commandment greater than these."
mark:12:31.csb: The second is, Love your neighbor as yourself. There is no other command greater than these.”
mark:12:31.nrsv: The second is this, 'You shall love your neighbor as yourself.' There is no other commandment greater than these."

mark:12:32.niv: "Well said, teacher," the man replied. "You are right in saying that God is one and there is no other but him.
mark:12:32.csb: Then the scribe said to him, “You are right, teacher. You have correctly said that he is one, and there is no one else except him.
mark:12:32.nrsv: Then the scribe said to him, "You are right, Teacher; you have truly said that 'he is one, and besides him there is no other';

mark:12:33.niv: To love him with all your heart, with all your understanding and with all your strength, and to love your neighbor as yourself is more important than all burnt offerings and sacrifices."
mark:12:33.csb: And to love him with all your heart, with all your understanding, and with all your strength, and to love your neighbor as yourself, is far more important than all the burnt offerings and sacrifices.”
mark:12:33.nrsv: and 'to love him with all the heart, and with all the understanding, and with all the strength,' and 'to love one's neighbor as oneself,'-- this is much more important than all whole burnt offerings and sacrifices."

mark:12:34.niv: When Jesus saw that he had answered wisely, he said to him, "You are not far from the kingdom of God." And from then on no one dared ask him any more questions.
mark:12:34.csb: When Jesus saw that he answered wisely, he said to him, “You are not far from the kingdom of God.” And no one dared to question him any longer.
mark:12:34.nrsv: When Jesus saw that he answered wisely, he said to him, "You are not far from the kingdom of God." After that no one dared to ask him any question.

mark:12:35.niv: While Jesus was teaching in the temple courts, he asked, "How is it that the teachers of the law say that the Christ is the son of David?
mark:12:35.csb: While Jesus was teaching in the temple, he asked, “How can the scribes say that the Messiah is the son of David?
mark:12:35.nrsv: While Jesus was teaching in the temple, he said, "How can the scribes say that the Messiah is the son of David?

mark:12:36.niv: David himself, speaking by the Holy Spirit, declared: "'The Lord said to my Lord: "Sit at my right hand until I put your enemies under your feet."'
mark:12:36.csb: David himself says by the Holy Spirit: The Lord declared to my Lord, ‘Sit at my right hand until I put your enemies under your feet.’
mark:12:36.nrsv: David himself, by the Holy Spirit, declared, 'The Lord said to my Lord, "Sit at my right hand, until I put your enemies under your feet."'

mark:12:37.niv: David himself calls him 'Lord.' How then can he be his son?" The large crowd listened to him with delight.
mark:12:37.csb: David himself calls him ‘Lord’; how then can he be his son?” And the large crowd was listening to him with delight.
mark:12:37.nrsv: David himself calls him Lord; so how can he be his son?" And the large crowd was listening to him with delight.

mark:12:38.niv: As he taught, Jesus said, "Watch out for the teachers of the law. They like to walk around in flowing robes and be greeted in the marketplaces,
mark:12:38.csb: He also said in his teaching, “Beware of the scribes, who want to go around in long robes and who want greetings in the marketplaces,
mark:12:38.nrsv: As he taught, he said, "Beware of the scribes, who like to walk around in long robes, and to be greeted with respect in the marketplaces,

mark:12:39.niv: and have the most important seats in the synagogues and the places of honor at banquets.
mark:12:39.csb: the best seats in the synagogues, and the places of honor at banquets.
mark:12:39.nrsv: and to have the best seats in the synagogues and places of honor at banquets!

mark:12:40.niv: They devour widows' houses and for a show make lengthy prayers. Such men will be punished most severely."
mark:12:40.csb: They devour widows’ houses and say long prayers just for show. These will receive harsher judgment.”
mark:12:40.nrsv: They devour widows' houses and for the sake of appearance say long prayers. They will receive the greater condemnation."

mark:12:41.niv: Jesus sat down opposite the place where the offerings were put and watched the crowd putting their money into the temple treasury. Many rich people threw in large amounts.
mark:12:41.csb: Sitting across from the temple treasury, he watched how the crowd dropped money into the treasury. Many rich people were putting in large sums.
mark:12:41.nrsv: He sat down opposite the treasury, and watched the crowd putting money into the treasury. Many rich people put in large sums.

mark:12:42.niv: But a poor widow came and put in two very small copper coins, worth only a fraction of a penny.
mark:12:42.csb: Then a poor widow came and dropped in two tiny coins worth very little.
mark:12:42.nrsv: A poor widow came and put in two small copper coins, which are worth a penny.

mark:12:43.niv: Calling his disciples to him, Jesus said, "I tell you the truth, this poor widow has put more into the treasury than all the others.
mark:12:43.csb: Summoning his disciples, he said to them, “Truly I tell you, this poor widow has put more into the treasury than all the others.
mark:12:43.nrsv: Then he called his disciples and said to them, "Truly I tell you, this poor widow has put in more than all those who are contributing to the treasury.

mark:12:44.niv: They all gave out of their wealth; but she, out of her poverty, put in everything--all she had to live on."
mark:12:44.csb: For they all gave out of their surplus, but she out of her poverty has put in everything she had — all she had to live on.”
mark:12:44.nrsv: For all of them have contributed out of their abundance; but she out of her poverty has put in everything she had, all she had to live on."

mark:13:1.niv: As he was leaving the temple, one of his disciples said to him, "Look, Teacher! What massive stones! What magnificent buildings!"
mark:13:1.csb: As he was going out of the temple, one of his disciples said to him, “Teacher, look! What massive stones! What impressive buildings!”
mark:13:1.nrsv: As he came out of the temple, one of his disciples said to him, "Look, Teacher, what large stones and what large buildings!"

mark:13:2.niv: "Do you see all these great buildings?" replied Jesus. "Not one stone here will be left on another; every one will be thrown down."
mark:13:2.csb: Jesus said to him, “Do you see these great buildings? Not one stone will be left upon another — all will be thrown down.”
mark:13:2.nrsv: Then Jesus asked him, "Do you see these great buildings? Not one stone will be left here upon another; all will be thrown down."

mark:13:3.niv: As Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives opposite the temple, Peter, James, John and Andrew asked him privately,
mark:13:3.csb: While he was sitting on the Mount of Olives across from the temple, Peter, James, John, and Andrew asked him privately,
mark:13:3.nrsv: When he was sitting on the Mount of Olives opposite the temple, Peter, James, John, and Andrew asked him privately,

mark:13:4.niv: "Tell us, when will these things happen? And what will be the sign that they are all about to be fulfilled?"
mark:13:4.csb: “Tell us, when will these things happen? And what will be the sign when all these things are about to be accomplished?”
mark:13:4.nrsv: "Tell us, when will this be, and what will be the sign that all these things are about to be accomplished?"

mark:13:5.niv: Jesus said to them: "Watch out that no one deceives you.
mark:13:5.csb: Jesus told them, “Watch out that no one deceives you.
mark:13:5.nrsv: Then Jesus began to say to them, "Beware that no one leads you astray.

mark:13:6.niv: Many will come in my name, claiming, 'I am he,' and will deceive many.
mark:13:6.csb: Many will come in my name, saying, ‘I am he,’ and they will deceive many.
mark:13:6.nrsv: Many will come in my name and say, 'I am he!' and they will lead many astray.

mark:13:7.niv: When you hear of wars and rumors of wars, do not be alarmed. Such things must happen, but the end is still to come.
mark:13:7.csb: When you hear of wars and rumors of wars, don’t be alarmed; these things must take place, but it is not yet the end.
mark:13:7.nrsv: When you hear of wars and rumors of wars, do not be alarmed; this must take place, but the end is still to come.

mark:13:8.niv: Nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom. There will be earthquakes in various places, and famines. These are the beginning of birth pains.
mark:13:8.csb: For nation will rise up against nation, and kingdom against kingdom. There will be earthquakes in various places, and famines. These are the beginning of birth pains.
mark:13:8.nrsv: For nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom; there will be earthquakes in various places; there will be famines. This is but the beginning of the birth pangs.

mark:13:9.niv: "You must be on your guard. You will be handed over to the local councils and flogged in the synagogues. On account of me you will stand before governors and kings as witnesses to them.
mark:13:9.csb: “But you, be on your guard! They will hand you over to local courts, and you will be flogged in the synagogues. You will stand before governors and kings because of me, as a witness to them.
mark:13:9.nrsv: "As for yourselves, beware; for they will hand you over to councils; and you will be beaten in synagogues; and you will stand before governors and kings because of me, as a testimony to them.

mark:13:10.niv: And the gospel must first be preached to all nations.
mark:13:10.csb: And it is necessary that the gospel be preached to all nations.
mark:13:10.nrsv: And the good news must first be proclaimed to all nations.

mark:13:11.niv: Whenever you are arrested and brought to trial, do not worry beforehand about what to say. Just say whatever is given you at the time, for it is not you speaking, but the Holy Spirit.
mark:13:11.csb: So when they arrest you and hand you over, don’t worry beforehand what you will say, but say whatever is given to you at that time, for it isn’t you speaking, but the Holy Spirit.
mark:13:11.nrsv: When they bring you to trial and hand you over, do not worry beforehand about what you are to say; but say whatever is given you at that time, for it is not you who speak, but the Holy Spirit.

mark:13:12.niv: "Brother will betray brother to death, and a father his child. Children will rebel against their parents and have them put to death.
mark:13:12.csb: “Brother will betray brother to death, and a father his child. Children will rise up against parents and have them put to death.
mark:13:12.nrsv: Brother will betray brother to death, and a father his child, and children will rise against parents and have them put to death;

mark:13:13.niv: All men will hate you because of me, but he who stands firm to the end will be saved.
mark:13:13.csb: You will be hated by everyone because of my name, but the one who endures to the end will be saved.
mark:13:13.nrsv: and you will be hated by all because of my name. But the one who endures to the end will be saved.

mark:13:14.niv: "When you see 'the abomination that causes desolation' standing where it does not belong--let the reader understand--then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains.
mark:13:14.csb: “When you see the abomination of desolation standing where it should not be” (let the reader understand), “then those in Judea must flee to the mountains.
mark:13:14.nrsv: "But when you see the desolating sacrilege set up where it ought not to be (let the reader understand), then those in Judea must flee to the mountains;

mark:13:15.niv: Let no one on the roof of his house go down or enter the house to take anything out.
mark:13:15.csb: A man on the housetop must not come down or go in to get anything out of his house,
mark:13:15.nrsv: the one on the housetop must not go down or enter the house to take anything away;

mark:13:16.niv: Let no one in the field go back to get his cloak.
mark:13:16.csb: and a man in the field must not go back to get his coat.
mark:13:16.nrsv: the one in the field must not turn back to get a coat.

mark:13:17.niv: How dreadful it will be in those days for pregnant women and nursing mothers!
mark:13:17.csb: Woe to pregnant women and nursing mothers in those days!
mark:13:17.nrsv: Woe to those who are pregnant and to those who are nursing infants in those days!

mark:13:18.niv: Pray that this will not take place in winter,
mark:13:18.nrsv: Pray that it may not be in winter.

mark:13:19.niv: because those will be days of distress unequaled from the beginning, when God created the world, until now--and never to be equaled again.
mark:13:19.csb: For those will be days of tribulation, the kind that hasn’t been from the beginning of creation until now and never will be again.
mark:13:19.nrsv: For in those days there will be suffering, such as has not been from the beginning of the creation that God created until now, no, and never will be.

mark:13:20.niv: If the Lord had not cut short those days, no one would survive. But for the sake of the elect, whom he has chosen, he has shortened them.
mark:13:20.csb: If the Lord had not cut those days short, no one would be saved. But he cut those days short for the sake of the elect, whom he chose.
mark:13:20.nrsv: And if the Lord had not cut short those days, no one would be saved; but for the sake of the elect, whom he chose, he has cut short those days.

mark:13:21.niv: At that time if anyone says to you, 'Look, here is the Christ!' or, 'Look, there he is!' do not believe it.
mark:13:21.csb: “Then if anyone tells you, ‘See, here is the Messiah! See, there! ’ do not believe it.
mark:13:21.nrsv: And if anyone says to you at that time, 'Look! Here is the Messiah!' or 'Look! There he is!'-- do not believe it.

mark:13:22.niv: For false Christs and false prophets will appear and perform signs and miracles to deceive the elect--if that were possible.
mark:13:22.csb: For false messiahs and false prophets will arise and will perform signs and wonders to lead astray, if possible, the elect.
mark:13:22.nrsv: False messiahs and false prophets will appear and produce signs and omens, to lead astray, if possible, the elect.

mark:13:23.niv: So be on your guard; I have told you everything ahead of time.
mark:13:23.csb: And you must watch! I have told you everything in advance.
mark:13:23.nrsv: But be alert; I have already told you everything.

mark:13:24.niv: "But in those days, following that distress, "'the sun will be darkened, and the moon will not give its light;
mark:13:24.csb: “But in those days, after that tribulation: The sun will be darkened, and the moon will not shed its light;
mark:13:24.nrsv: "But in those days, after that suffering, the sun will be darkened, and the moon will not give its light,

mark:13:25.niv: the stars will fall from the sky, and the heavenly bodies will be shaken.'
mark:13:25.csb: the stars will be falling from the sky, and the powers in the heavens will be shaken.
mark:13:25.nrsv: and the stars will be falling from heaven, and the powers in the heavens will be shaken.

mark:13:26.niv: "At that time men will see the Son of Man coming in clouds with great power and glory.
mark:13:26.csb: Then they will see the Son of Man coming in clouds with great power and glory.
mark:13:26.nrsv: Then they will see 'the Son of Man coming in clouds' with great power and glory.

mark:13:27.niv: And he will send his angels and gather his elect from the four winds, from the ends of the earth to the ends of the heavens.
mark:13:27.csb: He will send out the angels and gather his elect from the four winds, from the ends of the earth to the ends of heaven.
mark:13:27.nrsv: Then he will send out the angels, and gather his elect from the four winds, from the ends of the earth to the ends of heaven.

mark:13:28.niv: "Now learn this lesson from the fig tree: As soon as its twigs get tender and its leaves come out, you know that summer is near.
mark:13:28.csb: “Learn this lesson from the fig tree: As soon as its branch becomes tender and sprouts leaves, you know that summer is near.
mark:13:28.nrsv: "From the fig tree learn its lesson: as soon as its branch becomes tender and puts forth its leaves, you know that summer is near.

mark:13:29.niv: Even so, when you see these things happening, you know that it is near, right at the door.
mark:13:29.csb: In the same way, when you see these things happening, recognize that he is near — at the door.
mark:13:29.nrsv: So also, when you see these things taking place, you know that he is near, at the very gates.

mark:13:30.niv: I tell you the truth, this generation will certainly not pass away until all these things have happened.
mark:13:30.csb: “Truly I tell you, this generation will certainly not pass away until all these things take place.
mark:13:30.nrsv: Truly I tell you, this generation will not pass away until all these things have taken place.

mark:13:31.niv: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will never pass away.
mark:13:31.csb: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will never pass away.
mark:13:31.nrsv: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will not pass away.

mark:13:32.niv: "No one knows about that day or hour, not even the angels in heaven, nor the Son, but only the Father.
mark:13:32.csb: “Now concerning that day or hour no one knows — neither the angels in heaven nor the Son — but only the Father.
mark:13:32.nrsv: "But about that day or hour no one knows, neither the angels in heaven, nor the Son, but only the Father.

mark:13:33.niv: Be on guard! Be alert! You do not know when that time will come.
mark:13:33.csb: “Watch! Be alert! For you don’t know when the time is coming.
mark:13:33.nrsv: Beware, keep alert; for you do not know when the time will come.

mark:13:34.niv: It's like a man going away: He leaves his house and puts his servants in charge, each with his assigned task, and tells the one at the door to keep watch.
mark:13:34.csb: “It is like a man on a journey, who left his house, gave authority to his servants, gave each one his work, and commanded the doorkeeper to be alert.
mark:13:34.nrsv: It is like a man going on a journey, when he leaves home and puts his slaves in charge, each with his work, and commands the doorkeeper to be on the watch.

mark:13:35.niv: "Therefore keep watch because you do not know when the owner of the house will come back--whether in the evening, or at midnight, or when the rooster crows, or at dawn.
mark:13:35.csb: Therefore be alert, since you don’t know when the master of the house is coming — whether in the evening or at midnight or at the crowing of the rooster or early in the morning.
mark:13:35.nrsv: Therefore, keep awake-- for you do not know when the master of the house will come, in the evening, or at midnight, or at cockcrow, or at dawn,

mark:13:36.niv: If he comes suddenly, do not let him find you sleeping.
mark:13:36.csb: Otherwise, when he comes suddenly he might find you sleeping.
mark:13:36.nrsv: or else he may find you asleep when he comes suddenly.

mark:13:37.niv: What I say to you, I say to everyone: 'Watch!'"
mark:13:37.csb: And what I say to you, I say to everyone: Be alert!”
mark:13:37.nrsv: And what I say to you I say to all: Keep awake."

mark:14:1.niv: Now the Passover and the Feast of Unleavened Bread were only two days away, and the chief priests and the teachers of the law were looking for some sly way to arrest Jesus and kill him.
mark:14:1.csb: It was two days before the Passover and the Festival of Unleavened Bread. The chief priests and the scribes were looking for a cunning way to arrest Jesus and kill him.
mark:14:1.nrsv: It was two days before the Passover and the festival of Unleavened Bread. The chief priests and the scribes were looking for a way to arrest Jesus by stealth and kill him;

mark:14:2.niv: "But not during the Feast," they said, "or the people may riot."
mark:14:2.csb: “Not during the festival,” they said, “so that there won’t be a riot among the people.”
mark:14:2.nrsv: for they said, "Not during the festival, or there may be a riot among the people."

mark:14:3.niv: While he was in Bethany, reclining at the table in the home of a man known as Simon the Leper, a woman came with an alabaster jar of very expensive perfume, made of pure nard. She broke the jar and poured the perfume on his head.
mark:14:3.csb: While he was in Bethany at the house of Simon the leper, as he was reclining at the table, a woman came with an alabaster jar of very expensive perfume of pure nard. She broke the jar and poured it on his head.
mark:14:3.nrsv: While he was at Bethany in the house of Simon the leper, as he sat at the table, a woman came with an alabaster jar of very costly ointment of nard, and she broke open the jar and poured the ointment on his head.

mark:14:4.niv: Some of those present were saying indignantly to one another, "Why this waste of perfume?
mark:14:4.csb: But some were expressing indignation to one another: “Why has this perfume been wasted?
mark:14:4.nrsv: But some were there who said to one another in anger, "Why was the ointment wasted in this way?

mark:14:5.niv: It could have been sold for more than a year's wages and the money given to the poor." And they rebuked her harshly.
mark:14:5.csb: For this perfume might have been sold for more than three hundred denarii and given to the poor.” And they began to scold her.
mark:14:5.nrsv: For this ointment could have been sold for more than three hundred denarii, and the money given to the poor." And they scolded her.

mark:14:6.niv: "Leave her alone," said Jesus. "Why are you bothering her? She has done a beautiful thing to me.
mark:14:6.csb: Jesus replied, “Leave her alone. Why are you bothering her? She has done a noble thing for me.
mark:14:6.nrsv: But Jesus said, "Let her alone; why do you trouble her? She has performed a good service for me.

mark:14:7.niv: The poor you will always have with you, and you can help them any time you want. But you will not always have me.
mark:14:7.csb: You always have the poor with you, and you can do what is good for them whenever you want, but you do not always have me.
mark:14:7.nrsv: For you always have the poor with you, and you can show kindness to them whenever you wish; but you will not always have me.

mark:14:8.niv: She did what she could. She poured perfume on my body beforehand to prepare for my burial.
mark:14:8.csb: She has done what she could; she has anointed my body in advance for burial.
mark:14:8.nrsv: She has done what she could; she has anointed my body beforehand for its burial.

mark:14:9.niv: I tell you the truth, wherever the gospel is preached throughout the world, what she has done will also be told, in memory of her."
mark:14:9.csb: Truly I tell you, wherever the gospel is proclaimed in the whole world, what she has done will also be told in memory of her.”
mark:14:9.nrsv: Truly I tell you, wherever the good news is proclaimed in the whole world, what she has done will be told in remembrance of her."

mark:14:10.niv: Then Judas Iscariot, one of the Twelve, went to the chief priests to betray Jesus to them.
mark:14:10.csb: Then Judas Iscariot, one of the Twelve, went to the chief priests to betray Jesus to them.
mark:14:10.nrsv: Then Judas Iscariot, who was one of the twelve, went to the chief priests in order to betray him to them.

mark:14:11.niv: They were delighted to hear this and promised to give him money. So he watched for an opportunity to hand him over.
mark:14:11.csb: And when they heard this, they were glad and promised to give him money. So he started looking for a good opportunity to betray him.
mark:14:11.nrsv: When they heard it, they were greatly pleased, and promised to give him money. So he began to look for an opportunity to betray him.

mark:14:12.niv: On the first day of the Feast of Unleavened Bread, when it was customary to sacrifice the Passover lamb, Jesus' disciples asked him, "Where do you want us to go and make preparations for you to eat the Passover?"
mark:14:12.csb: On the first day of Unleavened Bread, when they sacrifice the Passover lamb, his disciples asked him, “Where do you want us to go and prepare the Passover so that you may eat it?”
mark:14:12.nrsv: On the first day of Unleavened Bread, when the Passover lamb is sacrificed, his disciples said to him, "Where do you want us to go and make the preparations for you to eat the Passover?"

mark:14:13.niv: So he sent two of his disciples, telling them, "Go into the city, and a man carrying a jar of water will meet you. Follow him.
mark:14:13.csb: So he sent two of his disciples and told them, “Go into the city, and a man carrying a jar of water will meet you. Follow him.
mark:14:13.nrsv: So he sent two of his disciples, saying to them, "Go into the city, and a man carrying a jar of water will meet you; follow him,

mark:14:14.niv: Say to the owner of the house he enters, 'The Teacher asks: Where is my guest room, where I may eat the Passover with my disciples?'
mark:14:14.csb: Wherever he enters, tell the owner of the house, ‘The Teacher says, “Where is my guest room where I may eat the Passover with my disciples?”’
mark:14:14.nrsv: and wherever he enters, say to the owner of the house, 'The Teacher asks, Where is my guest room where I may eat the Passover with my disciples?'

mark:14:15.niv: He will show you a large upper room, furnished and ready. Make preparations for us there."
mark:14:15.csb: He will show you a large room upstairs, furnished and ready. Make the preparations for us there.”
mark:14:15.nrsv: He will show you a large room upstairs, furnished and ready. Make preparations for us there."

mark:14:16.niv: The disciples left, went into the city and found things just as Jesus had told them. So they prepared the Passover.
mark:14:16.csb: So the disciples went out, entered the city, and found it just as he had told them, and they prepared the Passover.
mark:14:16.nrsv: So the disciples set out and went to the city, and found everything as he had told them; and they prepared the Passover meal.

mark:14:17.niv: When evening came, Jesus arrived with the Twelve.
mark:14:17.csb: When evening came, he arrived with the Twelve.
mark:14:17.nrsv: When it was evening, he came with the twelve.

mark:14:18.niv: While they were reclining at the table eating, he said, "I tell you the truth, one of you will betray me--one who is eating with me."
mark:14:18.csb: While they were reclining and eating, Jesus said, “Truly I tell you, one of you will betray me — one who is eating with me.”
mark:14:18.nrsv: And when they had taken their places and were eating, Jesus said, "Truly I tell you, one of you will betray me, one who is eating with me."

mark:14:19.niv: They were saddened, and one by one they said to him, "Surely not I?"
mark:14:19.csb: They began to be distressed and to say to him one by one, “Surely not I?”
mark:14:19.nrsv: They began to be distressed and to say to him one after another, "Surely, not I?"

mark:14:20.niv: "It is one of the Twelve," he replied, "one who dips bread into the bowl with me.
mark:14:20.csb: He said to them, “It is one of the Twelve — the one who is dipping bread in the bowl with me.
mark:14:20.nrsv: He said to them, "It is one of the twelve, one who is dipping bread into the bowl with me.

mark:14:21.niv: The Son of Man will go just as it is written about him. But woe to that man who betrays the Son of Man! It would be better for him if he had not been born."
mark:14:21.csb: For the Son of Man will go just as it is written about him, but woe to that man by whom the Son of Man is betrayed! It would have been better for him if he had not been born.”
mark:14:21.nrsv: For the Son of Man goes as it is written of him, but woe to that one by whom the Son of Man is betrayed! It would have been better for that one not to have been born."

mark:14:22.niv: While they were eating, Jesus took bread, gave thanks and broke it, and gave it to his disciples, saying, "Take it; this is my body."
mark:14:22.csb: As they were eating, he took bread, blessed and broke it, gave it to them, and said, “Take it; this is my body.”
mark:14:22.nrsv: While they were eating, he took a loaf of bread, and after blessing it he broke it, gave it to them, and said, "Take; this is my body."

mark:14:23.niv: Then he took the cup, gave thanks and offered it to them, and they all drank from it.
mark:14:23.csb: Then he took a cup, and after giving thanks, he gave it to them, and they all drank from it.
mark:14:23.nrsv: Then he took a cup, and after giving thanks he gave it to them, and all of them drank from it.

mark:14:24.niv: "This is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many," he said to them.
mark:14:24.csb: He said to them, “This is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many.
mark:14:24.nrsv: He said to them, "This is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many.

mark:14:25.niv: "I tell you the truth, I will not drink again of the fruit of the vine until that day when I drink it anew in the kingdom of God."
mark:14:25.csb: Truly I tell you, I will no longer drink of the fruit of the vine until that day when I drink it new in the kingdom of God.”
mark:14:25.nrsv: Truly I tell you, I will never again drink of the fruit of the vine until that day when I drink it new in the kingdom of God."

mark:14:26.niv: When they had sung a hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.
mark:14:26.csb: After singing a hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.
mark:14:26.nrsv: When they had sung the hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.

mark:14:27.niv: "You will all fall away," Jesus told them, "for it is written: "'I will strike the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered.'
mark:14:27.csb: Then Jesus said to them, “All of you will fall away, because it is written: I will strike the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered.
mark:14:27.nrsv: And Jesus said to them, "You will all become deserters; for it is written, 'I will strike the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered.'

mark:14:28.niv: But after I have risen, I will go ahead of you into Galilee."
mark:14:28.csb: But after I have risen, I will go ahead of you to Galilee.”
mark:14:28.nrsv: But after I am raised up, I will go before you to Galilee."

mark:14:29.niv: Peter declared, "Even if all fall away, I will not."
mark:14:29.csb: Peter told him, “Even if everyone falls away, I will not.”
mark:14:29.nrsv: Peter said to him, "Even though all become deserters, I will not."

mark:14:30.niv: "I tell you the truth," Jesus answered, "today--yes, tonight--before the rooster crows twice you yourself will disown me three times."
mark:14:30.csb: “Truly I tell you,” Jesus said to him, “today, this very night, before the rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times.”
mark:14:30.nrsv: Jesus said to him, "Truly I tell you, this day, this very night, before the cock crows twice, you will deny me three times."

mark:14:31.niv: But Peter insisted emphatically, "Even if I have to die with you, I will never disown you." And all the others said the same.
mark:14:31.csb: But he kept insisting, “If I have to die with you, I will never deny you.” And they all said the same thing.
mark:14:31.nrsv: But he said vehemently, "Even though I must die with you, I will not deny you." And all of them said the same.

mark:14:32.niv: They went to a place called Gethsemane, and Jesus said to his disciples, "Sit here while I pray."
mark:14:32.csb: Then they came to a place named Gethsemane, and he told his disciples, “Sit here while I pray.”
mark:14:32.nrsv: They went to a place called Gethsemane; and he said to his disciples, "Sit here while I pray."

mark:14:33.niv: He took Peter, James and John along with him, and he began to be deeply distressed and troubled.
mark:14:33.csb: He took Peter, James, and John with him, and he began to be deeply distressed and troubled.
mark:14:33.nrsv: He took with him Peter and James and John, and began to be distressed and agitated.

mark:14:34.niv: "My soul is overwhelmed with sorrow to the point of death," he said to them. "Stay here and keep watch."
mark:14:34.csb: He said to them, “I am deeply grieved to the point of death. Remain here and stay awake.”
mark:14:34.nrsv: And he said to them, "I am deeply grieved, even to death; remain here, and keep awake."

mark:14:35.niv: Going a little farther, he fell to the ground and prayed that if possible the hour might pass from him.
mark:14:35.csb: He went a little farther, fell to the ground, and prayed that if it were possible, the hour might pass from him.
mark:14:35.nrsv: And going a little farther, he threw himself on the ground and prayed that, if it were possible, the hour might pass from him.

mark:14:36.niv: "Abba, Father," he said, "everything is possible for you. Take this cup from me. Yet not what I will, but what you will."
mark:14:36.csb: And he said, “ Abba, Father! All things are possible for you. Take this cup away from me. Nevertheless, not what I will, but what you will.”
mark:14:36.nrsv: He said, "Abba, Father, for you all things are possible; remove this cup from me; yet, not what I want, but what you want."

mark:14:37.niv: Then he returned to his disciples and found them sleeping. "Simon," he said to Peter, "are you asleep? Could you not keep watch for one hour?
mark:14:37.csb: Then he came and found them sleeping. He said to Peter, “Simon, are you sleeping? Couldn’t you stay awake one hour?
mark:14:37.nrsv: He came and found them sleeping; and he said to Peter, "Simon, are you asleep? Could you not keep awake one hour?

mark:14:38.niv: Watch and pray so that you will not fall into temptation. The spirit is willing, but the body is weak."
mark:14:38.csb: Stay awake and pray so that you won’t enter into temptation. The spirit is willing, but the flesh is weak.”
mark:14:38.nrsv: Keep awake and pray that you may not come into the time of trial; the spirit indeed is willing, but the flesh is weak."

mark:14:39.niv: Once more he went away and prayed the same thing.
mark:14:39.csb: Once again he went away and prayed, saying the same thing.
mark:14:39.nrsv: And again he went away and prayed, saying the same words.

mark:14:40.niv: When he came back, he again found them sleeping, because their eyes were heavy. They did not know what to say to him.
mark:14:40.csb: And again he came and found them sleeping, because they could not keep their eyes open. They did not know what to say to him.
mark:14:40.nrsv: And once more he came and found them sleeping, for their eyes were very heavy; and they did not know what to say to him.

mark:14:41.niv: Returning the third time, he said to them, "Are you still sleeping and resting? Enough! The hour has come. Look, the Son of Man is betrayed into the hands of sinners.
mark:14:41.csb: Then he came a third time and said to them, “Are you still sleeping and resting? Enough! The time has come. See, the Son of Man is betrayed into the hands of sinners.
mark:14:41.nrsv: He came a third time and said to them, "Are you still sleeping and taking your rest? Enough! The hour has come; the Son of Man is betrayed into the hands of sinners.

mark:14:42.niv: Rise! Let us go! Here comes my betrayer!"
mark:14:42.csb: Get up; let’s go. See, my betrayer is near.”
mark:14:42.nrsv: Get up, let us be going. See, my betrayer is at hand."

mark:14:43.niv: Just as he was speaking, Judas, one of the Twelve, appeared. With him was a crowd armed with swords and clubs, sent from the chief priests, the teachers of the law, and the elders.
mark:14:43.csb: While he was still speaking, Judas, one of the Twelve, suddenly arrived. With him was a mob, with swords and clubs, from the chief priests, the scribes, and the elders.
mark:14:43.nrsv: Immediately, while he was still speaking, Judas, one of the twelve, arrived; and with him there was a crowd with swords and clubs, from the chief priests, the scribes, and the elders.

mark:14:44.niv: Now the betrayer had arranged a signal with them: "The one I kiss is the man; arrest him and lead him away under guard."
mark:14:44.csb: His betrayer had given them a signal. “The one I kiss,” he said, “he’s the one; arrest him and take him away under guard.”
mark:14:44.nrsv: Now the betrayer had given them a sign, saying, "The one I will kiss is the man; arrest him and lead him away under guard."

mark:14:45.niv: Going at once to Jesus, Judas said, "Rabbi!" and kissed him.
mark:14:45.csb: So when he came, immediately he went up to Jesus and said, “Rabbi!” and kissed him.
mark:14:45.nrsv: So when he came, he went up to him at once and said, "Rabbi!" and kissed him.

mark:14:46.niv: The men seized Jesus and arrested him.
mark:14:46.csb: They took hold of him and arrested him.
mark:14:46.nrsv: Then they laid hands on him and arrested him.

mark:14:47.niv: Then one of those standing near drew his sword and struck the servant of the high priest, cutting off his ear.
mark:14:47.csb: One of those who stood by drew his sword, struck the high priest’s servant, and cut off his ear.
mark:14:47.nrsv: But one of those who stood near drew his sword and struck the slave of the high priest, cutting off his ear.

mark:14:48.niv: "Am I leading a rebellion," said Jesus, "that you have come out with swords and clubs to capture me?
mark:14:48.csb: Jesus said to them, “Have you come out with swords and clubs, as if I were a criminal, to capture me?
mark:14:48.nrsv: Then Jesus said to them, "Have you come out with swords and clubs to arrest me as though I were a bandit?

mark:14:49.niv: Every day I was with you, teaching in the temple courts, and you did not arrest me. But the Scriptures must be fulfilled."
mark:14:49.csb: Every day I was among you, teaching in the temple, and you didn’t arrest me. But the Scriptures must be fulfilled.”
mark:14:49.nrsv: Day after day I was with you in the temple teaching, and you did not arrest me. But let the scriptures be fulfilled."

mark:14:50.niv: Then everyone deserted him and fled.
mark:14:50.csb: Then they all deserted him and ran away.
mark:14:50.nrsv: All of them deserted him and fled.

mark:14:51.niv: A young man, wearing nothing but a linen garment, was following Jesus. When they seized him,
mark:14:51.csb: Now a certain young man, wearing nothing but a linen cloth, was following him. They caught hold of him,
mark:14:51.nrsv: A certain young man was following him, wearing nothing but a linen cloth. They caught hold of him,

mark:14:52.niv: he fled naked, leaving his garment behind.
mark:14:52.csb: but he left the linen cloth behind and ran away naked.
mark:14:52.nrsv: but he left the linen cloth and ran off naked.

mark:14:53.niv: They took Jesus to the high priest, and all the chief priests, elders and teachers of the law came together.
mark:14:53.csb: They led Jesus away to the high priest, and all the chief priests, the elders, and the scribes assembled.
mark:14:53.nrsv: They took Jesus to the high priest; and all the chief priests, the elders, and the scribes were assembled.

mark:14:54.niv: Peter followed him at a distance, right into the courtyard of the high priest. There he sat with the guards and warmed himself at the fire.
mark:14:54.csb: Peter followed him at a distance, right into the high priest’s courtyard. He was sitting with the servants, warming himself by the fire.
mark:14:54.nrsv: Peter had followed him at a distance, right into the courtyard of the high priest; and he was sitting with the guards, warming himself at the fire.

mark:14:55.niv: The chief priests and the whole Sanhedrin were looking for evidence against Jesus so that they could put him to death, but they did not find any.
mark:14:55.csb: The chief priests and the whole Sanhedrin were looking for testimony against Jesus to put him to death, but they could not find any.
mark:14:55.nrsv: Now the chief priests and the whole council were looking for testimony against Jesus to put him to death; but they found none.

mark:14:56.niv: Many testified falsely against him, but their statements did not agree.
mark:14:56.csb: For many were giving false testimony against him, and the testimonies did not agree.
mark:14:56.nrsv: For many gave false testimony against him, and their testimony did not agree.

mark:14:57.niv: Then some stood up and gave this false testimony against him:
mark:14:57.csb: Some stood up and gave false testimony against him, stating,
mark:14:57.nrsv: Some stood up and gave false testimony against him, saying,

mark:14:58.niv: "We heard him say, 'I will destroy this man-made temple and in three days will build another, not made by man.'"
mark:14:58.csb: “We heard him say, ‘I will destroy this temple made with human hands, and in three days I will build another not made by hands.’”
mark:14:58.nrsv: "We heard him say, 'I will destroy this temple that is made with hands, and in three days I will build another, not made with hands.'"

mark:14:59.niv: Yet even then their testimony did not agree.
mark:14:59.csb: Yet their testimony did not agree even on this.
mark:14:59.nrsv: But even on this point their testimony did not agree.

mark:14:60.niv: Then the high priest stood up before them and asked Jesus, "Are you not going to answer? What is this testimony that these men are bringing against you?"
mark:14:60.csb: Then the high priest stood up before them all and questioned Jesus, “Don’t you have an answer to what these men are testifying against you?”
mark:14:60.nrsv: Then the high priest stood up before them and asked Jesus, "Have you no answer? What is it that they testify against you?"

mark:14:61.niv: But Jesus remained silent and gave no answer. Again the high priest asked him, "Are you the Christ, the Son of the Blessed One?"
mark:14:61.csb: But he kept silent and did not answer. Again the high priest questioned him, “Are you the Messiah, the Son of the Blessed One?”
mark:14:61.nrsv: But he was silent and did not answer. Again the high priest asked him, "Are you the Messiah, the Son of the Blessed One?"

mark:14:62.niv: "I am," said Jesus. "And you will see the Son of Man sitting at the right hand of the Mighty One and coming on the clouds of heaven."
mark:14:62.csb: “I am,” said Jesus, “and you will see the Son of Man seated at the right hand of Power and coming with the clouds of heaven.”
mark:14:62.nrsv: Jesus said, "I am; and 'you will see the Son of Man seated at the right hand of the Power,' and 'coming with the clouds of heaven.'"

mark:14:63.niv: The high priest tore his clothes. "Why do we need any more witnesses?" he asked.
mark:14:63.csb: Then the high priest tore his robes and said, “Why do we still need witnesses?
mark:14:63.nrsv: Then the high priest tore his clothes and said, "Why do we still need witnesses?

mark:14:64.niv: "You have heard the blasphemy. What do you think?"
mark:14:64.csb: You have heard the blasphemy. What is your decision?” They all condemned him as deserving death.
mark:14:64.nrsv: You have heard his blasphemy! What is your decision?" All of them condemned him as deserving death.

mark:14:65.niv: They all condemned him as worthy of death. Then some began to spit at him; they blindfolded him, struck him with their fists, and said, "Prophesy!" And the guards took him and beat him.
mark:14:65.csb: Then some began to spit on him, to blindfold him, and to beat him, saying, “Prophesy!” The temple servants also took him and slapped him.
mark:14:65.nrsv: Some began to spit on him, to blindfold him, and to strike him, saying to him, "Prophesy!" The guards also took him over and beat him.

mark:14:66.niv: While Peter was below in the courtyard, one of the servant girls of the high priest came by.
mark:14:66.csb: While Peter was in the courtyard below, one of the high priest’s maidservants came.
mark:14:66.nrsv: While Peter was below in the courtyard, one of the servant-girls of the high priest came by.

mark:14:67.niv: When she saw Peter warming himself, she looked closely at him. "You also were with that Nazarene, Jesus," she said.
mark:14:67.csb: When she saw Peter warming himself, she looked at him and said, “You also were with Jesus, the man from Nazareth.”
mark:14:67.nrsv: When she saw Peter warming himself, she stared at him and said, "You also were with Jesus, the man from Nazareth."

mark:14:68.niv: But he denied it. "I don't know or understand what you're talking about," he said, and went out into the entryway.
mark:14:68.csb: But he denied it: “I don’t know or understand what you’re talking about.” Then he went out to the entryway, and a rooster crowed.
mark:14:68.nrsv: But he denied it, saying, "I do not know or understand what you are talking about." And he went out into the forecourt. Then the cock crowed.

mark:14:69.niv: When the servant girl saw him there, she said again to those standing around, "This fellow is one of them."
mark:14:69.csb: When the maidservant saw him again, she began to tell those standing nearby, “This man is one of them.”
mark:14:69.nrsv: And the servant-girl, on seeing him, began again to say to the bystanders, "This man is one of them."

mark:14:70.niv: Again he denied it. After a little while, those standing near said to Peter, "Surely you are one of them, for you are a Galilean."
