mark:14:70.csb: But again he denied it. After a little while those standing there said to Peter again, “You certainly are one of them, since you’re also a Galilean.”
mark:14:70.nrsv: But again he denied it. Then after a little while the bystanders again said to Peter, "Certainly you are one of them; for you are a Galilean."

mark:14:71.niv: He began to call down curses on himself, and he swore to them, "I don't know this man you're talking about."
mark:14:71.csb: Then he started to curse and swear, “I don’t know this man you’re talking about!”
mark:14:71.nrsv: But he began to curse, and he swore an oath, "I do not know this man you are talking about."

mark:14:72.niv: Immediately the rooster crowed the second time. Then Peter remembered the word Jesus had spoken to him: "Before the rooster crows twice you will disown me three times." And he broke down and wept.
mark:14:72.csb: Immediately a rooster crowed a second time, and Peter remembered when Jesus had spoken the word to him, “Before the rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times.” And he broke down and wept.
mark:14:72.nrsv: At that moment the cock crowed for the second time. Then Peter remembered that Jesus had said to him, "Before the cock crows twice, you will deny me three times." And he broke down and wept.

mark:15:1.niv: Very early in the morning, the chief priests, with the elders, the teachers of the law and the whole Sanhedrin, reached a decision. They bound Jesus, led him away and handed him over to Pilate.
mark:15:1.csb: As soon as it was morning, having held a meeting with the elders, scribes, and the whole Sanhedrin, the chief priests tied Jesus up, led him away, and handed him over to Pilate.
mark:15:1.nrsv: As soon as it was morning, the chief priests held a consultation with the elders and scribes and the whole council. They bound Jesus, led him away, and handed him over to Pilate.

mark:15:2.niv: "Are you the king of the Jews?" asked Pilate. "Yes, it is as you say," Jesus replied.
mark:15:2.csb: So Pilate asked him, “Are you the King of the Jews?” He answered him, “You say so.”
mark:15:2.nrsv: Pilate asked him, "Are you the King of the Jews?" He answered him, "You say so."

mark:15:3.niv: The chief priests accused him of many things.
mark:15:3.csb: And the chief priests accused him of many things.
mark:15:3.nrsv: Then the chief priests accused him of many things.

mark:15:4.niv: So again Pilate asked him, "Aren't you going to answer? See how many things they are accusing you of."
mark:15:4.csb: Pilate questioned him again, “Aren’t you going to answer? Look how many things they are accusing you of!”
mark:15:4.nrsv: Pilate asked him again, "Have you no answer? See how many charges they bring against you."

mark:15:5.niv: But Jesus still made no reply, and Pilate was amazed.
mark:15:5.csb: But Jesus still did not answer, and so Pilate was amazed.
mark:15:5.nrsv: But Jesus made no further reply, so that Pilate was amazed.

mark:15:6.niv: Now it was the custom at the Feast to release a prisoner whom the people requested.
mark:15:6.csb: At the festival Pilate used to release for the people a prisoner whom they requested.
mark:15:6.nrsv: Now at the festival he used to release a prisoner for them, anyone for whom they asked.

mark:15:7.niv: A man called Barabbas was in prison with the insurrectionists who had committed murder in the uprising.
mark:15:7.csb: There was one named Barabbas, who was in prison with rebels who had committed murder during the rebellion.
mark:15:7.nrsv: Now a man called Barabbas was in prison with the rebels who had committed murder during the insurrection.

mark:15:8.niv: The crowd came up and asked Pilate to do for them what he usually did.
mark:15:8.csb: The crowd came up and began to ask Pilate to do for them as was his custom.
mark:15:8.nrsv: So the crowd came and began to ask Pilate to do for them according to his custom.

mark:15:9.niv: "Do you want me to release to you the king of the Jews?" asked Pilate,
mark:15:9.csb: Pilate answered them, “Do you want me to release the King of the Jews for you?”
mark:15:9.nrsv: Then he answered them, "Do you want me to release for you the King of the Jews?"

mark:15:10.niv: knowing it was out of envy that the chief priests had handed Jesus over to him.
mark:15:10.csb: For he knew it was because of envy that the chief priests had handed him over.
mark:15:10.nrsv: For he realized that it was out of jealousy that the chief priests had handed him over.

mark:15:11.niv: But the chief priests stirred up the crowd to have Pilate release Barabbas instead.
mark:15:11.csb: But the chief priests stirred up the crowd so that he would release Barabbas to them instead.
mark:15:11.nrsv: But the chief priests stirred up the crowd to have him release Barabbas for them instead.

mark:15:12.niv: "What shall I do, then, with the one you call the king of the Jews?" Pilate asked them.
mark:15:12.csb: Pilate asked them again, “Then what do you want me to do with the one you call the King of the Jews?”
mark:15:12.nrsv: Pilate spoke to them again, "Then what do you wish me to do with the man you call the King of the Jews?"

mark:15:14.niv: "Why? What crime has he committed?" asked Pilate. But they shouted all the louder, "Crucify him!"
mark:15:14.csb: Pilate said to them, “Why? What has he done wrong?” But they shouted all the more, “Crucify him!”
mark:15:14.nrsv: Pilate asked them, "Why, what evil has he done?" But they shouted all the more, "Crucify him!"

mark:15:15.niv: Wanting to satisfy the crowd, Pilate released Barabbas to them. He had Jesus flogged, and handed him over to be crucified.
mark:15:15.csb: Wanting to satisfy the crowd, Pilate released Barabbas to them; and after having Jesus flogged, he handed him over to be crucified.
mark:15:15.nrsv: So Pilate, wishing to satisfy the crowd, released Barabbas for them; and after flogging Jesus, he handed him over to be crucified.

mark:15:16.niv: The soldiers led Jesus away into the palace (that is, the Praetorium) and called together the whole company of soldiers.
mark:15:16.csb: The soldiers led him away into the palace (that is, the governor’s residence ) and called the whole company together.
mark:15:16.nrsv: Then the soldiers led him into the courtyard of the palace (that is, the governor's headquarters); and they called together the whole cohort.

mark:15:17.niv: They put a purple robe on him, then twisted together a crown of thorns and set it on him.
mark:15:17.csb: They dressed him in a purple robe, twisted together a crown of thorns, and put it on him.
mark:15:17.nrsv: And they clothed him in a purple cloak; and after twisting some thorns into a crown, they put it on him.

mark:15:18.niv: And they began to call out to him, "Hail, king of the Jews!"
mark:15:18.csb: And they began to salute him, “Hail, King of the Jews!”
mark:15:18.nrsv: And they began saluting him, "Hail, King of the Jews!"

mark:15:19.niv: Again and again they struck him on the head with a staff and spit on him. Falling on their knees, they paid homage to him.
mark:15:19.csb: They were hitting him on the head with a stick and spitting on him. Getting down on their knees, they were paying him homage.
mark:15:19.nrsv: They struck his head with a reed, spat upon him, and knelt down in homage to him.

mark:15:20.niv: And when they had mocked him, they took off the purple robe and put his own clothes on him. Then they led him out to crucify him.
mark:15:20.csb: After they had mocked him, they stripped him of the purple robe and put his clothes on him. They led him out to crucify him.
mark:15:20.nrsv: After mocking him, they stripped him of the purple cloak and put his own clothes on him. Then they led him out to crucify him.

mark:15:21.niv: A certain man from Cyrene, Simon, the father of Alexander and Rufus, was passing by on his way in from the country, and they forced him to carry the cross.
mark:15:21.csb: They forced a man coming in from the country, who was passing by, to carry Jesus’s cross. He was Simon of Cyrene, the father of Alexander and Rufus.
mark:15:21.nrsv: They compelled a passer-by, who was coming in from the country, to carry his cross; it was Simon of Cyrene, the father of Alexander and Rufus.

mark:15:22.niv: They brought Jesus to the place called Golgotha (which means The Place of the Skull).
mark:15:22.csb: They brought Jesus to the place called Golgotha (which means Place of the Skull ).
mark:15:22.nrsv: Then they brought Jesus to the place called Golgotha (which means the place of a skull).

mark:15:23.niv: Then they offered him wine mixed with myrrh, but he did not take it.
mark:15:23.csb: They tried to give him wine mixed with myrrh, but he did not take it.
mark:15:23.nrsv: And they offered him wine mixed with myrrh; but he did not take it.

mark:15:24.niv: And they crucified him. Dividing up his clothes, they cast lots to see what each would get.
mark:15:24.csb: Then they crucified him and divided his clothes, casting lots for them to decide what each would get.
mark:15:24.nrsv: And they crucified him, and divided his clothes among them, casting lots to decide what each should take.

mark:15:25.niv: It was the third hour when they crucified him.
mark:15:25.csb: Now it was nine in the morning when they crucified him.
mark:15:25.nrsv: It was nine o'clock in the morning when they crucified him.

mark:15:26.niv: The written notice of the charge against him read: THE KING OF THE JEWS.
mark:15:26.csb: The inscription of the charge written against him was: THE KING OF THE JEWS.
mark:15:26.nrsv: The inscription of the charge against him read, "The King of the Jews."

mark:15:27.niv: They crucified two robbers with him, one on his right and one on his left.
mark:15:27.csb: They crucified two criminals with him, one on his right and one on his left.
mark:15:27.nrsv: And with him they crucified two bandits, one on his right and one on his left.

mark:15:29.niv: Those who passed by hurled insults at him, shaking their heads and saying, "So! You who are going to destroy the temple and build it in three days,
mark:15:29.csb: Those who passed by were yelling insults at him, shaking their heads, and saying, “Ha! The one who would destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days,
mark:15:29.nrsv: Those who passed by derided him, shaking their heads and saying, "Aha! You who would destroy the temple and build it in three days,

mark:15:30.niv: come down from the cross and save yourself!"
mark:15:30.csb: save yourself by coming down from the cross!”
mark:15:30.nrsv: save yourself, and come down from the cross!"

mark:15:31.niv: In the same way the chief priests and the teachers of the law mocked him among themselves. "He saved others," they said, "but he can't save himself!
mark:15:31.csb: In the same way, the chief priests with the scribes were mocking him among themselves and saying, “He saved others, but he cannot save himself!
mark:15:31.nrsv: In the same way the chief priests, along with the scribes, were also mocking him among themselves and saying, "He saved others; he cannot save himself.

mark:15:32.niv: Let this Christ, this King of Israel, come down now from the cross, that we may see and believe." Those crucified with him also heaped insults on him.
mark:15:32.csb: Let the Messiah, the King of Israel, come down now from the cross, so that we may see and believe.” Even those who were crucified with him taunted him.
mark:15:32.nrsv: Let the Messiah, the King of Israel, come down from the cross now, so that we may see and believe." Those who were crucified with him also taunted him.

mark:15:33.niv: At the sixth hour darkness came over the whole land until the ninth hour.
mark:15:33.csb: When it was noon, darkness came over the whole land until three in the afternoon.
mark:15:33.nrsv: When it was noon, darkness came over the whole land until three in the afternoon.

mark:15:34.niv: And at the ninth hour Jesus cried out in a loud voice, "Eloi, Eloi, lama sabachthani?"--which means, "My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?"
mark:15:34.csb: And at three Jesus cried out with a loud voice, “Eloi, Eloi, lemá sabachtháni?” which is translated, “My God, my God, why have you abandoned me?”
mark:15:34.nrsv: At three o'clock Jesus cried out with a loud voice, "Eloi, Eloi, lema sabachthani?" which means, "My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?"

mark:15:35.niv: When some of those standing near heard this, they said, "Listen, he's calling Elijah."
mark:15:35.csb: When some of those standing there heard this, they said, “See, he’s calling for Elijah.”
mark:15:35.nrsv: When some of the bystanders heard it, they said, "Listen, he is calling for Elijah."

mark:15:36.niv: One man ran, filled a sponge with wine vinegar, put it on a stick, and offered it to Jesus to drink. "Now leave him alone. Let's see if Elijah comes to take him down," he said.
mark:15:36.csb: Someone ran and filled a sponge with sour wine, fixed it on a stick, offered him a drink, and said, “Let’s see if Elijah comes to take him down.”
mark:15:36.nrsv: And someone ran, filled a sponge with sour wine, put it on a stick, and gave it to him to drink, saying, "Wait, let us see whether Elijah will come to take him down."

mark:15:37.niv: With a loud cry, Jesus breathed his last.
mark:15:37.csb: Jesus let out a loud cry and breathed his last.
mark:15:37.nrsv: Then Jesus gave a loud cry and breathed his last.

mark:15:38.niv: The curtain of the temple was torn in two from top to bottom.
mark:15:38.csb: Then the curtain of the temple was torn in two from top to bottom.
mark:15:38.nrsv: And the curtain of the temple was torn in two, from top to bottom.

mark:15:39.niv: And when the centurion, who stood there in front of Jesus, heard his cry and saw how he died, he said, "Surely this man was the Son of God!"
mark:15:39.csb: When the centurion, who was standing opposite him, saw the way he breathed his last, he said, “Truly this man was the Son of God!”
mark:15:39.nrsv: Now when the centurion, who stood facing him, saw that in this way he breathed his last, he said, "Truly this man was God's Son!"

mark:15:40.niv: Some women were watching from a distance. Among them were Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James the younger and of Joses, and Salome.
mark:15:40.csb: There were also women watching from a distance. Among them were Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James the younger and of Joses, and Salome.
mark:15:40.nrsv: There were also women looking on from a distance; among them were Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James the younger and of Joses, and Salome.

mark:15:41.niv: In Galilee these women had followed him and cared for his needs. Many other women who had come up with him to Jerusalem were also there.
mark:15:41.csb: In Galilee these women followed him and took care of him. Many other women had come up with him to Jerusalem.
mark:15:41.nrsv: These used to follow him and provided for him when he was in Galilee; and there were many other women who had come up with him to Jerusalem.

mark:15:42.niv: It was Preparation Day (that is, the day before the Sabbath). So as evening approached,
mark:15:42.csb: When it was already evening, because it was the day of preparation (that is, the day before the Sabbath),
mark:15:42.nrsv: When evening had come, and since it was the day of Preparation, that is, the day before the sabbath,

mark:15:43.niv: Joseph of Arimathea, a prominent member of the Council, who was himself waiting for the kingdom of God, went boldly to Pilate and asked for Jesus' body.
mark:15:43.csb: Joseph of Arimathea, a prominent member of the Sanhedrin who was himself looking forward to the kingdom of God, came and boldly went to Pilate and asked for Jesus’s body.
mark:15:43.nrsv: Joseph of Arimathea, a respected member of the council, who was also himself waiting expectantly for the kingdom of God, went boldly to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus.

mark:15:44.niv: Pilate was surprised to hear that he was already dead. Summoning the centurion, he asked him if Jesus had already died.
mark:15:44.csb: Pilate was surprised that he was already dead. Summoning the centurion, he asked him whether he had already died.
mark:15:44.nrsv: Then Pilate wondered if he were already dead; and summoning the centurion, he asked him whether he had been dead for some time.

mark:15:45.niv: When he learned from the centurion that it was so, he gave the body to Joseph.
mark:15:45.csb: When he found out from the centurion, he gave the corpse to Joseph.
mark:15:45.nrsv: When he learned from the centurion that he was dead, he granted the body to Joseph.

mark:15:46.niv: So Joseph bought some linen cloth, took down the body, wrapped it in the linen, and placed it in a tomb cut out of rock. Then he rolled a stone against the entrance of the tomb.
mark:15:46.csb: After he bought some linen cloth, Joseph took him down and wrapped him in the linen. Then he laid him in a tomb cut out of the rock and rolled a stone against the entrance to the tomb.
mark:15:46.nrsv: Then Joseph bought a linen cloth, and taking down the body, wrapped it in the linen cloth, and laid it in a tomb that had been hewn out of the rock. He then rolled a stone against the door of the tomb.

mark:15:47.niv: Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of Joses saw where he was laid.
mark:15:47.csb: Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of Joses were watching where he was laid.
mark:15:47.nrsv: Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of Joses saw where the body was laid.

mark:16:1.niv: When the Sabbath was over, Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James, and Salome bought spices so that they might go to anoint Jesus' body.
mark:16:1.csb: When the Sabbath was over, Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James, and Salome bought spices, so that they could go and anoint him.
mark:16:1.nrsv: When the sabbath was over, Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James, and Salome bought spices, so that they might go and anoint him.

mark:16:2.niv: Very early on the first day of the week, just after sunrise, they were on their way to the tomb
mark:16:2.csb: Very early in the morning, on the first day of the week, they went to the tomb at sunrise.
mark:16:2.nrsv: And very early on the first day of the week, when the sun had risen, they went to the tomb.

mark:16:3.niv: and they asked each other, "Who will roll the stone away from the entrance of the tomb?"
mark:16:3.csb: They were saying to one another, “Who will roll away the stone from the entrance to the tomb for us?”
mark:16:3.nrsv: They had been saying to one another, "Who will roll away the stone for us from the entrance to the tomb?"

mark:16:4.niv: But when they looked up, they saw that the stone, which was very large, had been rolled away.
mark:16:4.csb: Looking up, they noticed that the stone — which was very large — had been rolled away.
mark:16:4.nrsv: When they looked up, they saw that the stone, which was very large, had already been rolled back.

mark:16:5.niv: As they entered the tomb, they saw a young man dressed in a white robe sitting on the right side, and they were alarmed.
mark:16:5.csb: When they entered the tomb, they saw a young man dressed in a white robe sitting on the right side; they were alarmed.
mark:16:5.nrsv: As they entered the tomb, they saw a young man, dressed in a white robe, sitting on the right side; and they were alarmed.

mark:16:6.niv: "Don't be alarmed," he said. "You are looking for Jesus the Nazarene, who was crucified. He has risen! He is not here. See the place where they laid him.
mark:16:6.csb: “Don’t be alarmed,” he told them. “You are looking for Jesus of Nazareth, who was crucified. He has risen! He is not here. See the place where they put him.
mark:16:6.nrsv: But he said to them, "Do not be alarmed; you are looking for Jesus of Nazareth, who was crucified. He has been raised; he is not here. Look, there is the place they laid him.

mark:16:7.niv: But go, tell his disciples and Peter, 'He is going ahead of you into Galilee. There you will see him, just as he told you.'"
mark:16:7.csb: But go, tell his disciples and Peter, ‘He is going ahead of you to Galilee; you will see him there just as he told you.’”
mark:16:7.nrsv: But go, tell his disciples and Peter that he is going ahead of you to Galilee; there you will see him, just as he told you."

mark:16:8.niv: Trembling and bewildered, the women went out and fled from the tomb. They said nothing to anyone, because they were afraid.
mark:16:8.csb: They went out and ran from the tomb, because trembling and astonishment overwhelmed them. And they said nothing to anyone, since they were afraid. [Some of the earliest mss conclude with 16:8.] [
mark:16:8.nrsv: So they went out and fled from the tomb, for terror and amazement had seized them; and they said nothing to anyone, for they were afraid.

mark:16:9.niv: When Jesus rose early on the first day of the week, he appeared first to Mary Magdalene, out of whom he had driven seven demons.
mark:16:9.csb: Early on the first day of the week, after he had risen, he appeared first to Mary Magdalene, out of whom he had driven seven demons.
mark:16:9.nrsv: Now after he rose early on the first day of the week, he appeared first to Mary Magdalene, from whom he had cast out seven demons.

mark:16:10.niv: She went and told those who had been with him and who were mourning and weeping.
mark:16:10.csb: She went and reported to those who had been with him, as they were mourning and weeping.
mark:16:10.nrsv: She went out and told those who had been with him, while they were mourning and weeping.

mark:16:11.niv: When they heard that Jesus was alive and that she had seen him, they did not believe it.
mark:16:11.csb: Yet, when they heard that he was alive and had been seen by her, they did not believe it.
mark:16:11.nrsv: But when they heard that he was alive and had been seen by her, they would not believe it.

mark:16:12.niv: Afterward Jesus appeared in a different form to two of them while they were walking in the country.
mark:16:12.csb: After this, he appeared in a different form to two of them walking on their way into the country.
mark:16:12.nrsv: After this he appeared in another form to two of them, as they were walking into the country.

mark:16:13.niv: These returned and reported it to the rest; but they did not believe them either.
mark:16:13.csb: And they went and reported it to the rest, who did not believe them either.
mark:16:13.nrsv: And they went back and told the rest, but they did not believe them.

mark:16:14.niv: Later Jesus appeared to the Eleven as they were eating; he rebuked them for their lack of faith and their stubborn refusal to believe those who had seen him after he had risen.
mark:16:14.csb: Later he appeared to the Eleven themselves as they were reclining at the table. He rebuked their unbelief and hardness of heart, because they did not believe those who saw him after he had risen.
mark:16:14.nrsv: Later he appeared to the eleven themselves as they were sitting at the table; and he upbraided them for their lack of faith and stubbornness, because they had not believed those who saw him after he had risen.

mark:16:15.niv: He said to them, "Go into all the world and preach the good news to all creation.
mark:16:15.csb: Then he said to them, “Go into all the world and preach the gospel to all creation.
mark:16:15.nrsv: And he said to them, "Go into all the world and proclaim the good news to the whole creation.

mark:16:16.niv: Whoever believes and is baptized will be saved, but whoever does not believe will be condemned.
mark:16:16.csb: Whoever believes and is baptized will be saved, but whoever does not believe will be condemned.
mark:16:16.nrsv: The one who believes and is baptized will be saved; but the one who does not believe will be condemned.

mark:16:17.niv: And these signs will accompany those who believe: In my name they will drive out demons; they will speak in new tongues;
mark:16:17.csb: And these signs will accompany those who believe: In my name they will drive out demons; they will speak in new tongues;
mark:16:17.nrsv: And these signs will accompany those who believe: by using my name they will cast out demons; they will speak in new tongues;

mark:16:18.niv: they will pick up snakes with their hands; and when they drink deadly poison, it will not hurt them at all; they will place their hands on sick people, and they will get well."
mark:16:18.csb: they will pick up snakes; if they should drink anything deadly, it will not harm them; they will lay hands on the sick, and they will get well.”
mark:16:18.nrsv: they will pick up snakes in their hands, and if they drink any deadly thing, it will not hurt them; they will lay their hands on the sick, and they will recover."

mark:16:19.niv: After the Lord Jesus had spoken to them, he was taken up into heaven and he sat at the right hand of God.
mark:16:19.csb: So the Lord Jesus, after speaking to them, was taken up into heaven and sat down at the right hand of God.
mark:16:19.nrsv: So then the Lord Jesus, after he had spoken to them, was taken up into heaven and sat down at the right hand of God.

mark:16:20.niv: Then the disciples went out and preached everywhere, and the Lord worked with them and confirmed his word by the signs that accompanied it.
mark:16:20.csb: And they went out and preached everywhere, while the Lord worked with them and confirmed the word by the accompanying signs.]
mark:16:20.nrsv: And they went out and proclaimed the good news everywhere, while the Lord worked with them and confirmed the message by the signs that accompanied it.

luke:1:1.niv: Many have undertaken to draw up an account of the things that have been fulfilled among us,
luke:1:1.csb: Many have undertaken to compile a narrative about the events that have been fulfilled among us,
luke:1:1.nrsv: Since many have undertaken to set down an orderly account of the events that have been fulfilled among us,

luke:1:2.niv: just as they were handed down to us by those who from the first were eyewitnesses and servants of the word.
luke:1:2.csb: just as the original eyewitnesses and servants of the word handed them down to us.
luke:1:2.nrsv: just as they were handed on to us by those who from the beginning were eyewitnesses and servants of the word,

luke:1:3.niv: Therefore, since I myself have carefully investigated everything from the beginning, it seemed good also to me to write an orderly account for you, most excellent Theophilus,
luke:1:3.csb: It also seemed good to me, since I have carefully investigated everything from the very first, to write to you in an orderly sequence, most honorable Theophilus,
luke:1:3.nrsv: I too decided, after investigating everything carefully from the very first, to write an orderly account for you, most excellent Theophilus,

luke:1:4.niv: so that you may know the certainty of the things you have been taught.
luke:1:4.csb: so that you may know the certainty of the things about which you have been instructed.
luke:1:4.nrsv: so that you may know the truth concerning the things about which you have been instructed.

luke:1:5.niv: In the time of Herod king of Judea there was a priest named Zechariah, who belonged to the priestly division of Abijah; his wife Elizabeth was also a descendant of Aaron.
luke:1:5.csb: In the days of King Herod of Judea, there was a priest of Abijah’s division named Zechariah. His wife was from the daughters of Aaron, and her name was Elizabeth.
luke:1:5.nrsv: In the days of King Herod of Judea, there was a priest named Zechariah, who belonged to the priestly order of Abijah. His wife was a descendant of Aaron, and her name was Elizabeth.

luke:1:6.niv: Both of them were upright in the sight of God, observing all the Lord's commandments and regulations blamelessly.
luke:1:6.csb: Both were righteous in God’s sight, living without blame according to all the commands and requirements of the Lord.
luke:1:6.nrsv: Both of them were righteous before God, living blamelessly according to all the commandments and regulations of the Lord.

luke:1:7.niv: But they had no children, because Elizabeth was barren; and they were both well along in years.
luke:1:7.csb: But they had no children because Elizabeth could not conceive, and both of them were well along in years.
luke:1:7.nrsv: But they had no children, because Elizabeth was barren, and both were getting on in years.

luke:1:8.niv: Once when Zechariah's division was on duty and he was serving as priest before God,
luke:1:8.csb: When his division was on duty and he was serving as priest before God,
luke:1:8.nrsv: Once when he was serving as priest before God and his section was on duty,

luke:1:9.niv: he was chosen by lot, according to the custom of the priesthood, to go into the temple of the Lord and burn incense.
luke:1:9.csb: it happened that he was chosen by lot, according to the custom of the priesthood, to enter the sanctuary of the Lord and burn incense.
luke:1:9.nrsv: he was chosen by lot, according to the custom of the priesthood, to enter the sanctuary of the Lord and offer incense.

luke:1:10.niv: And when the time for the burning of incense came, all the assembled worshipers were praying outside.
luke:1:10.csb: At the hour of incense the whole assembly of the people was praying outside.
luke:1:10.nrsv: Now at the time of the incense offering, the whole assembly of the people was praying outside.

luke:1:11.niv: Then an angel of the Lord appeared to him, standing at the right side of the altar of incense.
luke:1:11.csb: An angel of the Lord appeared to him, standing to the right of the altar of incense.
luke:1:11.nrsv: Then there appeared to him an angel of the Lord, standing at the right side of the altar of incense.

luke:1:12.niv: When Zechariah saw him, he was startled and was gripped with fear.
luke:1:12.csb: When Zechariah saw him, he was terrified and overcome with fear.
luke:1:12.nrsv: When Zechariah saw him, he was terrified; and fear overwhelmed him.

luke:1:13.niv: But the angel said to him: "Do not be afraid, Zechariah; your prayer has been heard. Your wife Elizabeth will bear you a son, and you are to give him the name John.
luke:1:13.csb: But the angel said to him: “Do not be afraid, Zechariah, because your prayer has been heard. Your wife Elizabeth will bear you a son, and you will name him John.
luke:1:13.nrsv: But the angel said to him, "Do not be afraid, Zechariah, for your prayer has been heard. Your wife Elizabeth will bear you a son, and you will name him John.

luke:1:14.niv: He will be a joy and delight to you, and many will rejoice because of his birth,
luke:1:14.csb: There will be joy and delight for you, and many will rejoice at his birth.
luke:1:14.nrsv: You will have joy and gladness, and many will rejoice at his birth,

luke:1:15.niv: for he will be great in the sight of the Lord. He is never to take wine or other fermented drink, and he will be filled with the Holy Spirit even from birth.
luke:1:15.csb: For he will be great in the sight of the Lord and will never drink wine or beer. He will be filled with the Holy Spirit while still in his mother’s womb.
luke:1:15.nrsv: for he will be great in the sight of the Lord. He must never drink wine or strong drink; even before his birth he will be filled with the Holy Spirit.

luke:1:16.niv: Many of the people of Israel will he bring back to the Lord their God.
luke:1:16.csb: He will turn many of the children of Israel to the Lord their God.
luke:1:16.nrsv: He will turn many of the people of Israel to the Lord their God.

luke:1:17.niv: And he will go on before the Lord, in the spirit and power of Elijah, to turn the hearts of the fathers to their children and the disobedient to the wisdom of the righteous--to make ready a people prepared for the Lord."
luke:1:17.csb: And he will go before him in the spirit and power of Elijah, to turn the hearts of fathers to their children, and the disobedient to the understanding of the righteous, to make ready for the Lord a prepared people.”
luke:1:17.nrsv: With the spirit and power of Elijah he will go before him, to turn the hearts of parents to their children, and the disobedient to the wisdom of the righteous, to make ready a people prepared for the Lord."

luke:1:18.niv: Zechariah asked the angel, "How can I be sure of this? I am an old man and my wife is well along in years."
luke:1:18.csb: “How can I know this?” Zechariah asked the angel. “For I am an old man, and my wife is well along in years.”
luke:1:18.nrsv: Zechariah said to the angel, "How will I know that this is so? For I am an old man, and my wife is getting on in years."

luke:1:19.niv: The angel answered, "I am Gabriel. I stand in the presence of God, and I have been sent to speak to you and to tell you this good news.
luke:1:19.csb: The angel answered him, “I am Gabriel, who stands in the presence of God, and I was sent to speak to you and tell you this good news.
luke:1:19.nrsv: The angel replied, "I am Gabriel. I stand in the presence of God, and I have been sent to speak to you and to bring you this good news.

luke:1:20.niv: And now you will be silent and not able to speak until the day this happens, because you did not believe my words, which will come true at their proper time."
luke:1:20.csb: Now listen. You will become silent and unable to speak until the day these things take place, because you did not believe my words, which will be fulfilled in their proper time.”
luke:1:20.nrsv: But now, because you did not believe my words, which will be fulfilled in their time, you will become mute, unable to speak, until the day these things occur."

luke:1:21.niv: Meanwhile, the people were waiting for Zechariah and wondering why he stayed so long in the temple.
luke:1:21.csb: Meanwhile, the people were waiting for Zechariah, amazed that he stayed so long in the sanctuary.
luke:1:21.nrsv: Meanwhile the people were waiting for Zechariah, and wondered at his delay in the sanctuary.

luke:1:22.niv: When he came out, he could not speak to them. They realized he had seen a vision in the temple, for he kept making signs to them but remained unable to speak.
luke:1:22.csb: When he did come out, he could not speak to them. Then they realized that he had seen a vision in the sanctuary. He was making signs to them and remained speechless.
luke:1:22.nrsv: When he did come out, he could not speak to them, and they realized that he had seen a vision in the sanctuary. He kept motioning to them and remained unable to speak.

luke:1:23.niv: When his time of service was completed, he returned home.
luke:1:23.csb: When the days of his ministry were completed, he went back home.
luke:1:23.nrsv: When his time of service was ended, he went to his home.

luke:1:24.niv: After this his wife Elizabeth became pregnant and for five months remained in seclusion.
luke:1:24.csb: After these days his wife Elizabeth conceived and kept herself in seclusion for five months. She said,
luke:1:24.nrsv: After those days his wife Elizabeth conceived, and for five months she remained in seclusion. She said,

luke:1:25.niv: "The Lord has done this for me," she said. "In these days he has shown his favor and taken away my disgrace among the people."
luke:1:25.csb: “The Lord has done this for me. He has looked with favor in these days to take away my disgrace among the people.”
luke:1:25.nrsv: "This is what the Lord has done for me when he looked favorably on me and took away the disgrace I have endured among my people."

luke:1:26.niv: In the sixth month, God sent the angel Gabriel to Nazareth, a town in Galilee,
luke:1:26.csb: In the sixth month, the angel Gabriel was sent by God to a town in Galilee called Nazareth,
luke:1:26.nrsv: In the sixth month the angel Gabriel was sent by God to a town in Galilee called Nazareth,

luke:1:27.niv: to a virgin pledged to be married to a man named Joseph, a descendant of David. The virgin's name was Mary.
luke:1:27.csb: to a virgin engaged to a man named Joseph, of the house of David. The virgin’s name was Mary.
luke:1:27.nrsv: to a virgin engaged to a man whose name was Joseph, of the house of David. The virgin's name was Mary.

luke:1:28.niv: The angel went to her and said, "Greetings, you who are highly favored! The Lord is with you."
luke:1:28.csb: And the angel came to her and said, “Greetings, favored woman! The Lord is with you.”
luke:1:28.nrsv: And he came to her and said, "Greetings, favored one! The Lord is with you."

luke:1:29.niv: Mary was greatly troubled at his words and wondered what kind of greeting this might be.
luke:1:29.csb: But she was deeply troubled by this statement, wondering what kind of greeting this could be.
luke:1:29.nrsv: But she was much perplexed by his words and pondered what sort of greeting this might be.

luke:1:30.niv: But the angel said to her, "Do not be afraid, Mary, you have found favor with God.
luke:1:30.csb: Then the angel told her: “Do not be afraid, Mary, for you have found favor with God.
luke:1:30.nrsv: The angel said to her, "Do not be afraid, Mary, for you have found favor with God.

luke:1:31.niv: You will be with child and give birth to a son, and you are to give him the name Jesus.
luke:1:31.csb: Now listen: You will conceive and give birth to a son, and you will name him Jesus.
luke:1:31.nrsv: And now, you will conceive in your womb and bear a son, and you will name him Jesus.

luke:1:32.niv: He will be great and will be called the Son of the Most High. The Lord God will give him the throne of his father David,
luke:1:32.csb: He will be great and will be called the Son of the Most High, and the Lord God will give him the throne of his father David.
luke:1:32.nrsv: He will be great, and will be called the Son of the Most High, and the Lord God will give to him the throne of his ancestor David.

luke:1:33.niv: and he will reign over the house of Jacob forever; his kingdom will never end."
luke:1:33.csb: He will reign over the house of Jacob forever, and his kingdom will have no end.”
luke:1:33.nrsv: He will reign over the house of Jacob forever, and of his kingdom there will be no end."

luke:1:34.niv: "How will this be," Mary asked the angel, "since I am a virgin?"
luke:1:34.csb: Mary asked the angel, “How can this be, since I have not had sexual relations with a man?”
luke:1:34.nrsv: Mary said to the angel, "How can this be, since I am a virgin?"

luke:1:35.niv: The angel answered, "The Holy Spirit will come upon you, and the power of the Most High will overshadow you. So the holy one to be born will be called the Son of God.
luke:1:35.csb: The angel replied to her: “The Holy Spirit will come upon you, and the power of the Most High will overshadow you. Therefore, the holy one to be born will be called the Son of God.
luke:1:35.nrsv: The angel said to her, "The Holy Spirit will come upon you, and the power of the Most High will overshadow you; therefore the child to be born will be holy; he will be called Son of God.

luke:1:36.niv: Even Elizabeth your relative is going to have a child in her old age, and she who was said to be barren is in her sixth month.
luke:1:36.csb: And consider your relative Elizabeth — even she has conceived a son in her old age, and this is the sixth month for her who was called childless.
luke:1:36.nrsv: And now, your relative Elizabeth in her old age has also conceived a son; and this is the sixth month for her who was said to be barren.

luke:1:37.niv: For nothing is impossible with God."
luke:1:37.csb: For nothing will be impossible with God.”
luke:1:37.nrsv: For nothing will be impossible with God."

luke:1:38.niv: "I am the Lord's servant," Mary answered. "May it be to me as you have said." Then the angel left her.
luke:1:38.csb: “I am the Lord’s servant,” said Mary. “May it be done to me according to your word.” Then the angel left her.
luke:1:38.nrsv: Then Mary said, "Here am I, the servant of the Lord; let it be with me according to your word." Then the angel departed from her.

luke:1:39.niv: At that time Mary got ready and hurried to a town in the hill country of Judea,
luke:1:39.csb: In those days Mary set out and hurried to a town in the hill country of Judah
luke:1:39.nrsv: In those days Mary set out and went with haste to a Judean town in the hill country,

luke:1:40.niv: where she entered Zechariah's home and greeted Elizabeth.
luke:1:40.csb: where she entered Zechariah’s house and greeted Elizabeth.
luke:1:40.nrsv: where she entered the house of Zechariah and greeted Elizabeth.

luke:1:41.niv: When Elizabeth heard Mary's greeting, the baby leaped in her womb, and Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit.
luke:1:41.csb: When Elizabeth heard Mary’s greeting, the baby leaped inside her, and Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit.
luke:1:41.nrsv: When Elizabeth heard Mary's greeting, the child leaped in her womb. And Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit

luke:1:42.niv: In a loud voice she exclaimed: "Blessed are you among women, and blessed is the child you will bear!
luke:1:42.csb: Then she exclaimed with a loud cry: “Blessed are you among women, and your child will be blessed!
luke:1:42.nrsv: and exclaimed with a loud cry, "Blessed are you among women, and blessed is the fruit of your womb.

luke:1:43.niv: But why am I so favored, that the mother of my Lord should come to me?
luke:1:43.csb: How could this happen to me, that the mother of my Lord should come to me?
luke:1:43.nrsv: And why has this happened to me, that the mother of my Lord comes to me?

luke:1:44.niv: As soon as the sound of your greeting reached my ears, the baby in my womb leaped for joy.
luke:1:44.csb: For you see, when the sound of your greeting reached my ears, the baby leaped for joy inside me.
luke:1:44.nrsv: For as soon as I heard the sound of your greeting, the child in my womb leaped for joy.

luke:1:45.niv: Blessed is she who has believed that what the Lord has said to her will be accomplished!"
luke:1:45.csb: Blessed is she who has believed that the Lord would fulfill what he has spoken to her!”
luke:1:45.nrsv: And blessed is she who believed that there would be a fulfillment of what was spoken to her by the Lord."

luke:1:46.niv: And Mary said: "My soul glorifies the Lord
luke:1:46.csb: And Mary said: My soul praises the greatness of the Lord,
luke:1:46.nrsv: And Mary said, "My soul magnifies the Lord,

luke:1:47.niv: and my spirit rejoices in God my Savior,
luke:1:47.csb: and my spirit rejoices in God my Savior,
luke:1:47.nrsv: and my spirit rejoices in God my Savior,

luke:1:48.niv: for he has been mindful of the humble state of his servant. From now on all generations will call me blessed,
luke:1:48.csb: because he has looked with favor on the humble condition of his servant. Surely, from now on all generations will call me blessed,
luke:1:48.nrsv: for he has looked with favor on the lowliness of his servant. Surely, from now on all generations will call me blessed;

luke:1:49.niv: for the Mighty One has done great things for me--holy is his name.
luke:1:49.csb: because the Mighty One has done great things for me, and his name is holy.
luke:1:49.nrsv: for the Mighty One has done great things for me, and holy is his name.

luke:1:50.niv: His mercy extends to those who fear him, from generation to generation.
luke:1:50.csb: His mercy is from generation to generation on those who fear him.
luke:1:50.nrsv: His mercy is for those who fear him from generation to generation.

luke:1:51.niv: He has performed mighty deeds with his arm; he has scattered those who are proud in their inmost thoughts.
luke:1:51.csb: He has done a mighty deed with his arm; he has scattered the proud because of the thoughts of their hearts;
luke:1:51.nrsv: He has shown strength with his arm; he has scattered the proud in the thoughts of their hearts.

luke:1:52.niv: He has brought down rulers from their thrones but has lifted up the humble.
luke:1:52.csb: he has toppled the mighty from their thrones and exalted the lowly.
luke:1:52.nrsv: He has brought down the powerful from their thrones, and lifted up the lowly;

luke:1:53.niv: He has filled the hungry with good things but has sent the rich away empty.
luke:1:53.csb: He has satisfied the hungry with good things and sent the rich away empty.
luke:1:53.nrsv: he has filled the hungry with good things, and sent the rich away empty.

luke:1:54.niv: He has helped his servant Israel, remembering to be merciful
luke:1:54.csb: He has helped his servant Israel, remembering his mercy
luke:1:54.nrsv: He has helped his servant Israel, in remembrance of his mercy,

luke:1:55.niv: to Abraham and his descendants forever, even as he said to our fathers."
luke:1:55.csb: to Abraham and his descendants forever, just as he spoke to our ancestors.
luke:1:55.nrsv: according to the promise he made to our ancestors, to Abraham and to his descendants forever."

luke:1:56.niv: Mary stayed with Elizabeth for about three months and then returned home.
luke:1:56.csb: And Mary stayed with her about three months; then she returned to her home.
luke:1:56.nrsv: And Mary remained with her about three months and then returned to her home.

luke:1:57.niv: When it was time for Elizabeth to have her baby, she gave birth to a son.
luke:1:57.csb: Now the time had come for Elizabeth to give birth, and she had a son.
luke:1:57.nrsv: Now the time came for Elizabeth to give birth, and she bore a son.

luke:1:58.niv: Her neighbors and relatives heard that the Lord had shown her great mercy, and they shared her joy.
luke:1:58.csb: Then her neighbors and relatives heard that the Lord had shown her his great mercy, and they rejoiced with her.
luke:1:58.nrsv: Her neighbors and relatives heard that the Lord had shown his great mercy to her, and they rejoiced with her.

luke:1:59.niv: On the eighth day they came to circumcise the child, and they were going to name him after his father Zechariah,
luke:1:59.csb: When they came to circumcise the child on the eighth day, they were going to name him Zechariah, after his father.
luke:1:59.nrsv: On the eighth day they came to circumcise the child, and they were going to name him Zechariah after his father.

luke:1:60.niv: but his mother spoke up and said, "No! He is to be called John."
luke:1:60.csb: But his mother responded, “No. He will be called John.”
luke:1:60.nrsv: But his mother said, "No; he is to be called John."

luke:1:61.niv: They said to her, "There is no one among your relatives who has that name."
luke:1:61.csb: Then they said to her, “None of your relatives has that name.”
luke:1:61.nrsv: They said to her, "None of your relatives has this name."

luke:1:62.niv: Then they made signs to his father, to find out what he would like to name the child.
luke:1:62.csb: So they motioned to his father to find out what he wanted him to be called.
luke:1:62.nrsv: Then they began motioning to his father to find out what name he wanted to give him.

luke:1:63.niv: He asked for a writing tablet, and to everyone's astonishment he wrote, "His name is John."
luke:1:63.csb: He asked for a writing tablet and wrote: “His name is John.” And they were all amazed.
luke:1:63.nrsv: He asked for a writing tablet and wrote, "His name is John." And all of them were amazed.

luke:1:64.niv: Immediately his mouth was opened and his tongue was loosed, and he began to speak, praising God.
luke:1:64.csb: Immediately his mouth was opened and his tongue set free, and he began to speak, praising God.
luke:1:64.nrsv: Immediately his mouth was opened and his tongue freed, and he began to speak, praising God.

luke:1:65.niv: The neighbors were all filled with awe, and throughout the hill country of Judea people were talking about all these things.
luke:1:65.csb: Fear came on all those who lived around them, and all these things were being talked about throughout the hill country of Judea.
luke:1:65.nrsv: Fear came over all their neighbors, and all these things were talked about throughout the entire hill country of Judea.

luke:1:66.niv: Everyone who heard this wondered about it, asking, "What then is this child going to be?" For the Lord's hand was with him.
luke:1:66.csb: All who heard about him took it to heart, saying, “What then will this child become?” For, indeed, the Lord’s hand was with him.
luke:1:66.nrsv: All who heard them pondered them and said, "What then will this child become?" For, indeed, the hand of the Lord was with him.

luke:1:67.niv: His father Zechariah was filled with the Holy Spirit and prophesied:
luke:1:67.csb: Then his father Zechariah was filled with the Holy Spirit and prophesied:
luke:1:67.nrsv: Then his father Zechariah was filled with the Holy Spirit and spoke this prophecy:

luke:1:68.niv: "Praise be to the Lord, the God of Israel, because he has come and has redeemed his people.
luke:1:68.csb: Blessed is the Lord, the God of Israel, because he has visited and provided redemption for his people.
luke:1:68.nrsv: "Blessed be the Lord God of Israel, for he has looked favorably on his people and redeemed them.

luke:1:69.niv: He has raised up a horn of salvation for us in the house of his servant David
luke:1:69.csb: He has raised up a horn of salvation for us in the house of his servant David,
luke:1:69.nrsv: He has raised up a mighty savior for us in the house of his servant David,

luke:1:70.niv: (as he said through his holy prophets of long ago),
luke:1:70.csb: just as he spoke by the mouth of his holy prophets in ancient times;
luke:1:70.nrsv: as he spoke through the mouth of his holy prophets from of old,

luke:1:71.niv: salvation from our enemies and from the hand of all who hate us--
luke:1:71.csb: salvation from our enemies and from the hand of those who hate us.
luke:1:71.nrsv: that we would be saved from our enemies and from the hand of all who hate us.

luke:1:72.niv: to show mercy to our fathers and to remember his holy covenant,
luke:1:72.csb: He has dealt mercifully with our fathers and remembered his holy covenant —
luke:1:72.nrsv: Thus he has shown the mercy promised to our ancestors, and has remembered his holy covenant,

luke:1:73.niv: the oath he swore to our father Abraham:
luke:1:73.csb: the oath that he swore to our father Abraham. He has given us the privilege,
luke:1:73.nrsv: the oath that he swore to our ancestor Abraham, to grant us

luke:1:74.niv: to rescue us from the hand of our enemies, and to enable us to serve him without fear
luke:1:74.csb: since we have been rescued from the hand of our enemies, to serve him without fear
luke:1:74.nrsv: that we, being rescued from the hands of our enemies, might serve him without fear,

luke:1:75.niv: in holiness and righteousness before him all our days.
luke:1:75.csb: in holiness and righteousness in his presence all our days.
luke:1:75.nrsv: in holiness and righteousness before him all our days.

luke:1:76.niv: And you, my child, will be called a prophet of the Most High; for you will go on before the Lord to prepare the way for him,
luke:1:76.csb: And you, child, will be called a prophet of the Most High, for you will go before the Lord to prepare his ways,
luke:1:76.nrsv: And you, child, will be called the prophet of the Most High; for you will go before the Lord to prepare his ways,

luke:1:77.niv: to give his people the knowledge of salvation through the forgiveness of their sins,
luke:1:77.csb: to give his people knowledge of salvation through the forgiveness of their sins.
luke:1:77.nrsv: to give knowledge of salvation to his people by the forgiveness of their sins.

luke:1:78.niv: because of the tender mercy of our God, by which the rising sun will come to us from heaven
luke:1:78.csb: Because of our God’s merciful compassion, the dawn from on high will visit us
luke:1:78.nrsv: By the tender mercy of our God, the dawn from on high will break upon us,

luke:1:79.niv: to shine on those living in darkness and in the shadow of death, to guide our feet into the path of peace."
luke:1:79.csb: to shine on those who live in darkness and the shadow of death, to guide our feet into the way of peace.
luke:1:79.nrsv: to give light to those who sit in darkness and in the shadow of death, to guide our feet into the way of peace."

luke:1:80.niv: And the child grew and became strong in spirit; and he lived in the desert until he appeared publicly to Israel.
luke:1:80.csb: The child grew up and became spiritually strong, and he was in the wilderness until the day of his public appearance to Israel.
luke:1:80.nrsv: The child grew and became strong in spirit, and he was in the wilderness until the day he appeared publicly to Israel.

luke:2:1.niv: In those days Caesar Augustus issued a decree that a census should be taken of the entire Roman world.
luke:2:1.csb: In those days a decree went out from Caesar Augustus that the whole empire should be registered.
luke:2:1.nrsv: In those days a decree went out from Emperor Augustus that all the world should be registered.

luke:2:2.niv: (This was the first census that took place while Quirinius was governor of Syria.)
luke:2:2.csb: This first registration took place while Quirinius was governing Syria.
luke:2:2.nrsv: This was the first registration and was taken while Quirinius was governor of Syria.

luke:2:3.niv: And everyone went to his own town to register.
luke:2:3.csb: So everyone went to be registered, each to his own town.
luke:2:3.nrsv: All went to their own towns to be registered.

luke:2:4.niv: So Joseph also went up from the town of Nazareth in Galilee to Judea, to Bethlehem the town of David, because he belonged to the house and line of David.
luke:2:4.csb: Joseph also went up from the town of Nazareth in Galilee, to Judea, to the city of David, which is called Bethlehem, because he was of the house and family line of David,
luke:2:4.nrsv: Joseph also went from the town of Nazareth in Galilee to Judea, to the city of David called Bethlehem, because he was descended from the house and family of David.

luke:2:5.niv: He went there to register with Mary, who was pledged to be married to him and was expecting a child.
luke:2:5.csb: to be registered along with Mary, who was engaged to him and was pregnant.
luke:2:5.nrsv: He went to be registered with Mary, to whom he was engaged and who was expecting a child.

luke:2:6.niv: While they were there, the time came for the baby to be born,
luke:2:6.csb: While they were there, the time came for her to give birth.
luke:2:6.nrsv: While they were there, the time came for her to deliver her child.

luke:2:7.niv: and she gave birth to her firstborn, a son. She wrapped him in cloths and placed him in a manger, because there was no room for them in the inn.
luke:2:7.csb: Then she gave birth to her firstborn Son, and she wrapped him tightly in cloth and laid him in a manger, because there was no guest room available for them.
luke:2:7.nrsv: And she gave birth to her firstborn son and wrapped him in bands of cloth, and laid him in a manger, because there was no place for them in the inn.

luke:2:8.niv: And there were shepherds living out in the fields nearby, keeping watch over their flocks at night.
luke:2:8.csb: In the same region, shepherds were staying out in the fields and keeping watch at night over their flock.
luke:2:8.nrsv: In that region there were shepherds living in the fields, keeping watch over their flock by night.

luke:2:9.niv: An angel of the Lord appeared to them, and the glory of the Lord shone around them, and they were terrified.
luke:2:9.csb: Then an angel of the Lord stood before them, and the glory of the Lord shone around them, and they were terrified.
luke:2:9.nrsv: Then an angel of the Lord stood before them, and the glory of the Lord shone around them, and they were terrified.

luke:2:10.niv: But the angel said to them, "Do not be afraid. I bring you good news of great joy that will be for all the people.
luke:2:10.csb: But the angel said to them, “Don’t be afraid, for look, I proclaim to you good news of great joy that will be for all the people:
luke:2:10.nrsv: But the angel said to them, "Do not be afraid; for see-- I am bringing you good news of great joy for all the people:

luke:2:11.niv: Today in the town of David a Savior has been born to you; he is Christ the Lord.
luke:2:11.csb: Today in the city of David a Savior was born for you, who is the Messiah, the Lord.
luke:2:11.nrsv: to you is born this day in the city of David a Savior, who is the Messiah, the Lord.

luke:2:12.niv: This will be a sign to you: You will find a baby wrapped in cloths and lying in a manger."
luke:2:12.csb: This will be the sign for you: You will find a baby wrapped tightly in cloth and lying in a manger.”
luke:2:12.nrsv: This will be a sign for you: you will find a child wrapped in bands of cloth and lying in a manger."

luke:2:13.niv: Suddenly a great company of the heavenly host appeared with the angel, praising God and saying,
luke:2:13.csb: Suddenly there was a multitude of the heavenly host with the angel, praising God and saying:
luke:2:13.nrsv: And suddenly there was with the angel a multitude of the heavenly host, praising God and saying,

luke:2:14.niv: "Glory to God in the highest, and on earth peace to men on whom his favor rests."
luke:2:14.csb: Glory to God in the highest heaven, and peace on earth to people he favors!
luke:2:14.nrsv: "Glory to God in the highest heaven, and on earth peace among those whom he favors!"

luke:2:15.niv: When the angels had left them and gone into heaven, the shepherds said to one another, "Let's go to Bethlehem and see this thing that has happened, which the Lord has told us about."
luke:2:15.csb: When the angels had left them and returned to heaven, the shepherds said to one another, “Let’s go straight to Bethlehem and see what has happened, which the Lord has made known to us.”
luke:2:15.nrsv: When the angels had left them and gone into heaven, the shepherds said to one another, "Let us go now to Bethlehem and see this thing that has taken place, which the Lord has made known to us."

luke:2:16.niv: So they hurried off and found Mary and Joseph, and the baby, who was lying in the manger.
luke:2:16.csb: They hurried off and found both Mary and Joseph, and the baby who was lying in the manger.
luke:2:16.nrsv: So they went with haste and found Mary and Joseph, and the child lying in the manger.

luke:2:17.niv: When they had seen him, they spread the word concerning what had been told them about this child,
luke:2:17.csb: After seeing them, they reported the message they were told about this child,
luke:2:17.nrsv: When they saw this, they made known what had been told them about this child;

luke:2:18.niv: and all who heard it were amazed at what the shepherds said to them.
luke:2:18.csb: and all who heard it were amazed at what the shepherds said to them.
luke:2:18.nrsv: and all who heard it were amazed at what the shepherds told them.

luke:2:19.niv: But Mary treasured up all these things and pondered them in her heart.
luke:2:19.csb: But Mary was treasuring up all these things in her heart and meditating on them.
luke:2:19.nrsv: But Mary treasured all these words and pondered them in her heart.

luke:2:20.niv: The shepherds returned, glorifying and praising God for all the things they had heard and seen, which were just as they had been told.
luke:2:20.csb: The shepherds returned, glorifying and praising God for all the things they had seen and heard, which were just as they had been told.
luke:2:20.nrsv: The shepherds returned, glorifying and praising God for all they had heard and seen, as it had been told them.

luke:2:21.niv: On the eighth day, when it was time to circumcise him, he was named Jesus, the name the angel had given him before he had been conceived.
luke:2:21.csb: When the eight days were completed for his circumcision, he was named Jesus — the name given by the angel before he was conceived.
luke:2:21.nrsv: After eight days had passed, it was time to circumcise the child; and he was called Jesus, the name given by the angel before he was conceived in the womb.

luke:2:22.niv: When the time of their purification according to the Law of Moses had been completed, Joseph and Mary took him to Jerusalem to present him to the Lord
luke:2:22.csb: And when the days of their purification according to the law of Moses were finished, they brought him up to Jerusalem to present him to the Lord
luke:2:22.nrsv: When the time came for their purification according to the law of Moses, they brought him up to Jerusalem to present him to the Lord

luke:2:23.niv: (as it is written in the Law of the Lord, "Every firstborn male is to be consecrated to the Lord" ),
luke:2:23.csb: (just as it is written in the law of the Lord, Every firstborn male will be dedicated to the Lord )
luke:2:23.nrsv: (as it is written in the law of the Lord, "Every firstborn male shall be designated as holy to the Lord"),

luke:2:24.niv: and to offer a sacrifice in keeping with what is said in the Law of the Lord: "a pair of doves or two young pigeons."
luke:2:24.csb: and to offer a sacrifice (according to what is stated in the law of the Lord, a pair of turtledoves or two young pigeons ).
luke:2:24.nrsv: and they offered a sacrifice according to what is stated in the law of the Lord, "a pair of turtledoves or two young pigeons."

luke:2:25.niv: Now there was a man in Jerusalem called Simeon, who was righteous and devout. He was waiting for the consolation of Israel, and the Holy Spirit was upon him.
luke:2:25.csb: There was a man in Jerusalem whose name was Simeon. This man was righteous and devout, looking forward to Israel’s consolation, and the Holy Spirit was on him.
luke:2:25.nrsv: Now there was a man in Jerusalem whose name was Simeon; this man was righteous and devout, looking forward to the consolation of Israel, and the Holy Spirit rested on him.

luke:2:26.niv: It had been revealed to him by the Holy Spirit that he would not die before he had seen the Lord's Christ.
luke:2:26.csb: It had been revealed to him by the Holy Spirit that he would not see death before he saw the Lord’s Messiah.
luke:2:26.nrsv: It had been revealed to him by the Holy Spirit that he would not see death before he had seen the Lord's Messiah.

luke:2:27.niv: Moved by the Spirit, he went into the temple courts. When the parents brought in the child Jesus to do for him what the custom of the Law required,
luke:2:27.csb: Guided by the Spirit, he entered the temple. When the parents brought in the child Jesus to perform for him what was customary under the law,
luke:2:27.nrsv: Guided by the Spirit, Simeon came into the temple; and when the parents brought in the child Jesus, to do for him what was customary under the law,

luke:2:28.niv: Simeon took him in his arms and praised God, saying:
luke:2:28.csb: Simeon took him up in his arms, praised God, and said,
luke:2:28.nrsv: Simeon took him in his arms and praised God, saying,

luke:2:29.niv: "Sovereign Lord, as you have promised, you now dismiss your servant in peace.
luke:2:29.csb: Now, Master, you can dismiss your servant in peace, as you promised.
luke:2:29.nrsv: "Master, now you are dismissing your servant in peace, according to your word;

luke:2:30.niv: For my eyes have seen your salvation,
luke:2:30.csb: For my eyes have seen your salvation.
luke:2:30.nrsv: for my eyes have seen your salvation,

luke:2:31.niv: which you have prepared in the sight of all people,
luke:2:31.csb: You have prepared it in the presence of all peoples —
luke:2:31.nrsv: which you have prepared in the presence of all peoples,

luke:2:32.niv: a light for revelation to the Gentiles and for glory to your people Israel."
luke:2:32.csb: a light for revelation to the Gentiles and glory to your people Israel.
luke:2:32.nrsv: a light for revelation to the Gentiles and for glory to your people Israel."

luke:2:33.niv: The child's father and mother marveled at what was said about him.
luke:2:33.csb: His father and mother were amazed at what was being said about him.
luke:2:33.nrsv: And the child's father and mother were amazed at what was being said about him.

luke:2:34.niv: Then Simeon blessed them and said to Mary, his mother: "This child is destined to cause the falling and rising of many in Israel, and to be a sign that will be spoken against,
luke:2:34.csb: Then Simeon blessed them and told his mother Mary: “Indeed, this child is destined to cause the fall and rise of many in Israel and to be a sign that will be opposed —
luke:2:34.nrsv: Then Simeon blessed them and said to his mother Mary, "This child is destined for the falling and the rising of many in Israel, and to be a sign that will be opposed

luke:2:35.niv: so that the thoughts of many hearts will be revealed. And a sword will pierce your own soul too."
luke:2:35.csb: and a sword will pierce your own soul — that the thoughts of many hearts may be revealed.”
luke:2:35.nrsv: so that the inner thoughts of many will be revealed-- and a sword will pierce your own soul too."

luke:2:36.niv: There was also a prophetess, Anna, the daughter of Phanuel, of the tribe of Asher. She was very old; she had lived with her husband seven years after her marriage,
luke:2:36.csb: There was also a prophetess, Anna, a daughter of Phanuel, of the tribe of Asher. She was well along in years, having lived with her husband seven years after her marriage,
luke:2:36.nrsv: There was also a prophet, Anna the daughter of Phanuel, of the tribe of Asher. She was of a great age, having lived with her husband seven years after her marriage,

luke:2:37.niv: and then was a widow until she was eighty-four. She never left the temple but worshiped night and day, fasting and praying.
luke:2:37.csb: and was a widow for eighty-four years. She did not leave the temple, serving God night and day with fasting and prayers.
luke:2:37.nrsv: then as a widow to the age of eighty-four. She never left the temple but worshiped there with fasting and prayer night and day.

luke:2:38.niv: Coming up to them at that very moment, she gave thanks to God and spoke about the child to all who were looking forward to the redemption of Jerusalem.
luke:2:38.csb: At that very moment, she came up and began to thank God and to speak about him to all who were looking forward to the redemption of Jerusalem.
luke:2:38.nrsv: At that moment she came, and began to praise God and to speak about the child to all who were looking for the redemption of Jerusalem.

luke:2:39.niv: When Joseph and Mary had done everything required by the Law of the Lord, they returned to Galilee to their own town of Nazareth.
luke:2:39.csb: When they had completed everything according to the law of the Lord, they returned to Galilee, to their own town of Nazareth.
luke:2:39.nrsv: When they had finished everything required by the law of the Lord, they returned to Galilee, to their own town of Nazareth.

luke:2:40.niv: And the child grew and became strong; he was filled with wisdom, and the grace of God was upon him.
luke:2:40.csb: The boy grew up and became strong, filled with wisdom, and God’s grace was on him.
luke:2:40.nrsv: The child grew and became strong, filled with wisdom; and the favor of God was upon him.

luke:2:41.niv: Every year his parents went to Jerusalem for the Feast of the Passover.
luke:2:41.csb: Every year his parents traveled to Jerusalem for the Passover Festival.
luke:2:41.nrsv: Now every year his parents went to Jerusalem for the festival of the Passover.

luke:2:42.niv: When he was twelve years old, they went up to the Feast, according to the custom.
luke:2:42.csb: When he was twelve years old, they went up according to the custom of the festival.
luke:2:42.nrsv: And when he was twelve years old, they went up as usual for the festival.

luke:2:43.niv: After the Feast was over, while his parents were returning home, the boy Jesus stayed behind in Jerusalem, but they were unaware of it.
luke:2:43.csb: After those days were over, as they were returning, the boy Jesus stayed behind in Jerusalem, but his parents did not know it.
luke:2:43.nrsv: When the festival was ended and they started to return, the boy Jesus stayed behind in Jerusalem, but his parents did not know it.

luke:2:44.niv: Thinking he was in their company, they traveled on for a day. Then they began looking for him among their relatives and friends.
luke:2:44.csb: Assuming he was in the traveling party, they went a day’s journey. Then they began looking for him among their relatives and friends.
luke:2:44.nrsv: Assuming that he was in the group of travelers, they went a day's journey. Then they started to look for him among their relatives and friends.

luke:2:45.niv: When they did not find him, they went back to Jerusalem to look for him.
luke:2:45.csb: When they did not find him, they returned to Jerusalem to search for him.
luke:2:45.nrsv: When they did not find him, they returned to Jerusalem to search for him.

luke:2:46.niv: After three days they found him in the temple courts, sitting among the teachers, listening to them and asking them questions.
luke:2:46.csb: After three days, they found him in the temple sitting among the teachers, listening to them and asking them questions.
luke:2:46.nrsv: After three days they found him in the temple, sitting among the teachers, listening to them and asking them questions.

luke:2:47.niv: Everyone who heard him was amazed at his understanding and his answers.
luke:2:47.csb: And all those who heard him were astounded at his understanding and his answers.
luke:2:47.nrsv: And all who heard him were amazed at his understanding and his answers.

luke:2:48.niv: When his parents saw him, they were astonished. His mother said to him, "Son, why have you treated us like this? Your father and I have been anxiously searching for you."
luke:2:48.csb: When his parents saw him, they were astonished, and his mother said to him, “Son, why have you treated us like this? Your father and I have been anxiously searching for you.”
luke:2:48.nrsv: When his parents saw him they were astonished; and his mother said to him, "Child, why have you treated us like this? Look, your father and I have been searching for you in great anxiety."

luke:2:49.niv: "Why were you searching for me?" he asked. "Didn't you know I had to be in my Father's house?"
luke:2:49.csb: “Why were you searching for me?” he asked them. “Didn’t you know that it was necessary for me to be in my Father’s house?”
luke:2:49.nrsv: He said to them, "Why were you searching for me? Did you not know that I must be in my Father's house?"

luke:2:50.niv: But they did not understand what he was saying to them.
luke:2:50.csb: But they did not understand what he said to them.
luke:2:50.nrsv: But they did not understand what he said to them.

luke:2:51.niv: Then he went down to Nazareth with them and was obedient to them. But his mother treasured all these things in her heart.
luke:2:51.csb: Then he went down with them and came to Nazareth and was obedient to them. His mother kept all these things in her heart.
luke:2:51.nrsv: Then he went down with them and came to Nazareth, and was obedient to them. His mother treasured all these things in her heart.

luke:2:52.niv: And Jesus grew in wisdom and stature, and in favor with God and men.
luke:2:52.csb: And Jesus increased in wisdom and stature, and in favor with God and with people.
luke:2:52.nrsv: And Jesus increased in wisdom and in years, and in divine and human favor.

luke:3:1.niv: In the fifteenth year of the reign of Tiberius Caesar--when Pontius Pilate was governor of Judea, Herod tetrarch of Galilee, his brother Philip tetrarch of Iturea and Traconitis, and Lysanias tetrarch of Abilene--
luke:3:1.csb: In the fifteenth year of the reign of Tiberius Caesar, while Pontius Pilate was governor of Judea, Herod was tetrarch of Galilee, his brother Philip tetrarch of the region of Iturea and Trachonitis, and Lysanias tetrarch of Abilene,
luke:3:1.nrsv: In the fifteenth year of the reign of Emperor Tiberius, when Pontius Pilate was governor of Judea, and Herod was ruler of Galilee, and his brother Philip ruler of the region of Ituraea and Trachonitis, and Lysanias ruler of Abilene,

luke:3:2.niv: during the high priesthood of Annas and Caiaphas, the word of God came to John son of Zechariah in the desert.
luke:3:2.csb: during the high priesthood of Annas and Caiaphas, God’s word came to John the son of Zechariah in the wilderness.
luke:3:2.nrsv: during the high priesthood of Annas and Caiaphas, the word of God came to John son of Zechariah in the wilderness.

luke:3:3.niv: He went into all the country around the Jordan, preaching a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins.
luke:3:3.csb: He went into all the vicinity of the Jordan, proclaiming a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins,
luke:3:3.nrsv: He went into all the region around the Jordan, proclaiming a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins,

luke:3:4.niv: As is written in the book of the words of Isaiah the prophet: "A voice of one calling in the desert, 'Prepare the way for the Lord, make straight paths for him.
luke:3:4.csb: as it is written in the book of the words of the prophet Isaiah: A voice of one crying out in the wilderness: Prepare the way for the Lord; make his paths straight!
luke:3:4.nrsv: as it is written in the book of the words of the prophet Isaiah, "The voice of one crying out in the wilderness: 'Prepare the way of the Lord, make his paths straight.

luke:3:5.niv: Every valley shall be filled in, every mountain and hill made low. The crooked roads shall become straight, the rough ways smooth.
luke:3:5.csb: Every valley will be filled, and every mountain and hill will be made low; the crooked will become straight, the rough ways smooth,
luke:3:5.nrsv: Every valley shall be filled, and every mountain and hill shall be made low, and the crooked shall be made straight, and the rough ways made smooth;

luke:3:6.niv: And all mankind will see God's salvation.'"
luke:3:6.csb: and everyone will see the salvation of God.
luke:3:6.nrsv: and all flesh shall see the salvation of God.'"

luke:3:7.niv: John said to the crowds coming out to be baptized by him, "You brood of vipers! Who warned you to flee from the coming wrath?
luke:3:7.csb: He then said to the crowds who came out to be baptized by him, “Brood of vipers! Who warned you to flee from the coming wrath?
luke:3:7.nrsv: John said to the crowds that came out to be baptized by him, "You brood of vipers! Who warned you to flee from the wrath to come?

luke:3:8.niv: Produce fruit in keeping with repentance. And do not begin to say to yourselves, 'We have Abraham as our father.' For I tell you that out of these stones God can raise up children for Abraham.
luke:3:8.csb: Therefore produce fruit consistent with repentance. And don’t start saying to yourselves, ‘We have Abraham as our father,’ for I tell you that God is able to raise up children for Abraham from these stones.
luke:3:8.nrsv: Bear fruits worthy of repentance. Do not begin to say to yourselves, 'We have Abraham as our ancestor'; for I tell you, God is able from these stones to raise up children to Abraham.

luke:3:9.niv: The ax is already at the root of the trees, and every tree that does not produce good fruit will be cut down and thrown into the fire."
luke:3:9.csb: The ax is already at the root of the trees. Therefore, every tree that doesn’t produce good fruit will be cut down and thrown into the fire.”
luke:3:9.nrsv: Even now the ax is lying at the root of the trees; every tree therefore that does not bear good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire."

luke:3:10.niv: "What should we do then?" the crowd asked.
luke:3:10.csb: “What then should we do?” the crowds were asking him.
luke:3:10.nrsv: And the crowds asked him, "What then should we do?"

luke:3:11.niv: John answered, "The man with two tunics should share with him who has none, and the one who has food should do the same."
luke:3:11.csb: He replied to them, “The one who has two shirts must share with someone who has none, and the one who has food must do the same.”
luke:3:11.nrsv: In reply he said to them, "Whoever has two coats must share with anyone who has none; and whoever has food must do likewise."

luke:3:12.niv: Tax collectors also came to be baptized. "Teacher," they asked, "what should we do?"
luke:3:12.csb: Tax collectors also came to be baptized, and they asked him, “Teacher, what should we do?”
luke:3:12.nrsv: Even tax collectors came to be baptized, and they asked him, "Teacher, what should we do?"

luke:3:13.niv: "Don't collect any more than you are required to," he told
luke:3:13.csb: He told them, “Don’t collect any more than what you have been authorized.”
luke:3:13.nrsv: He said to them, "Collect no more than the amount prescribed for you."

luke:3:14.niv: them. Then some soldiers asked him, "And what should we do?" He replied, "Don't extort money and don't accuse people falsely--be content with your pay."
luke:3:14.csb: Some soldiers also questioned him, “What should we do?” He said to them, “Don’t take money from anyone by force or false accusation, and be satisfied with your wages.”
luke:3:14.nrsv: Soldiers also asked him, "And we, what should we do?" He said to them, "Do not extort money from anyone by threats or false accusation, and be satisfied with your wages."

luke:3:15.niv: The people were waiting expectantly and were all wondering in their hearts if John might possibly be the Christ.
luke:3:15.csb: Now the people were waiting expectantly, and all of them were questioning in their hearts whether John might be the Messiah.
luke:3:15.nrsv: As the people were filled with expectation, and all were questioning in their hearts concerning John, whether he might be the Messiah,

luke:3:16.niv: John answered them all, "I baptize you with water. But one more powerful than I will come, the thongs of whose sandals I am not worthy to untie. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and with fire.
luke:3:16.csb: John answered them all, “I baptize you with water, but one who is more powerful than I am is coming. I am not worthy to untie the strap of his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and fire.
luke:3:16.nrsv: John answered all of them by saying, "I baptize you with water; but one who is more powerful than I is coming; I am not worthy to untie the thong of his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and fire.

luke:3:17.niv: His winnowing fork is in his hand to clear his threshing floor and to gather the wheat into his barn, but he will burn up the chaff with unquenchable fire."
luke:3:17.csb: His winnowing shovel is in his hand to clear his threshing floor and gather the wheat into his barn, but the chaff he will burn with fire that never goes out.”
luke:3:17.nrsv: His winnowing fork is in his hand, to clear his threshing floor and to gather the wheat into his granary; but the chaff he will burn with unquenchable fire."

luke:3:18.niv: And with many other words John exhorted the people and preached the good news to them.
luke:3:18.csb: Then, along with many other exhortations, he proclaimed good news to the people.
luke:3:18.nrsv: So, with many other exhortations, he proclaimed the good news to the people.

luke:3:19.niv: But when John rebuked Herod the tetrarch because of Herodias, his brother's wife, and all the other evil things he had done,
luke:3:19.csb: But when John rebuked Herod the tetrarch because of Herodias, his brother’s wife, and all the evil things he had done,
luke:3:19.nrsv: But Herod the ruler, who had been rebuked by him because of Herodias, his brother's wife, and because of all the evil things that Herod had done,

luke:3:20.niv: Herod added this to them all: He locked John up in prison.
luke:3:20.csb: Herod added this to everything else — he locked up John in prison.
luke:3:20.nrsv: added to them all by shutting up John in prison.

luke:3:21.niv: When all the people were being baptized, Jesus was baptized too. And as he was praying, heaven was opened
luke:3:21.csb: When all the people were baptized, Jesus also was baptized. As he was praying, heaven opened,
