luke:3:21.nrsv: Now when all the people were baptized, and when Jesus also had been baptized and was praying, the heaven was opened,

luke:3:22.niv: and the Holy Spirit descended on him in bodily form like a dove. And a voice came from heaven: "You are my Son, whom I love; with you I am well pleased."
luke:3:22.csb: and the Holy Spirit descended on him in a physical appearance like a dove. And a voice came from heaven: “You are my beloved Son; with you I am well-pleased.”
luke:3:22.nrsv: and the Holy Spirit descended upon him in bodily form like a dove. And a voice came from heaven, "You are my Son, the Beloved; with you I am well pleased."

luke:3:23.niv: Now Jesus himself was about thirty years old when he began his ministry. He was the son, so it was thought, of Joseph,
luke:3:23.csb: As he began his ministry, Jesus was about thirty years old and was thought to be the son of Joseph, son of Heli,
luke:3:23.nrsv: Jesus was about thirty years old when he began his work. He was the son (as was thought) of Joseph son of Heli,

luke:3:24.niv: the son of Heli, the son of Matthat, the son of Levi, the son of Melki, the son of Jannai, the son of Joseph,
luke:3:24.csb: son of Matthat, son of Levi, son of Melchi, son of Jannai, son of Joseph,
luke:3:24.nrsv: son of Matthat, son of Levi, son of Melchi, son of Jannai, son of Joseph,

luke:3:25.niv: the son of Mattathias, the son of Amos, the son of Nahum, the son of Esli,
luke:3:25.csb: son of Mattathias, son of Amos, son of Nahum, son of Esli, son of Naggai,
luke:3:25.nrsv: son of Mattathias, son of Amos, son of Nahum, son of Esli, son of Naggai,

luke:3:26.niv: the son of Naggai, the son of Maath, the son of Mattathias, the son of Semein, the son of Josech, the son of Joda,
luke:3:26.csb: son of Maath, son of Mattathias, son of Semein, son of Josech, son of Joda,
luke:3:26.nrsv: son of Maath, son of Mattathias, son of Semein, son of Josech, son of Joda,

luke:3:27.niv: the son of Joanan, the son of Rhesa, the son of Zerubbabel, the son of Shealtiel,
luke:3:27.csb: son of Joanan, son of Rhesa, son of Zerubbabel, son of Shealtiel, son of Neri,
luke:3:27.nrsv: son of Joanan, son of Rhesa, son of Zerubbabel, son of Shealtiel, son of Neri,

luke:3:28.niv: the son of Neri, the son of Melki, the son of Addi, the son of Cosam, the son of Elmadam, the son of Er,
luke:3:28.csb: son of Melchi, son of Addi, son of Cosam, son of Elmadam, son of Er,
luke:3:28.nrsv: son of Melchi, son of Addi, son of Cosam, son of Elmadam, son of Er,

luke:3:29.niv: the son of Joshua, the son of Eliezer, the son of Jorim, the son of Matthat,
luke:3:29.csb: son of Joshua, son of Eliezer, son of Jorim, son of Matthat, son of Levi,
luke:3:29.nrsv: son of Joshua, son of Eliezer, son of Jorim, son of Matthat, son of Levi,

luke:3:30.niv: the son of Levi, the son of Simeon, the son of Judah, the son of Joseph, the son of Jonam, the son of Eliakim,
luke:3:30.csb: son of Simeon, son of Judah, son of Joseph, son of Jonam, son of Eliakim,
luke:3:30.nrsv: son of Simeon, son of Judah, son of Joseph, son of Jonam, son of Eliakim,

luke:3:31.niv: the son of Melea, the son of Menna, the son of Mattatha, the son of Nathan,
luke:3:31.csb: son of Melea, son of Menna, son of Mattatha, son of Nathan, son of David,
luke:3:31.nrsv: son of Melea, son of Menna, son of Mattatha, son of Nathan, son of David,

luke:3:32.niv: the son of David, the son of Jesse, the son of Obed, the son of Boaz, the son of Salmon, the son of Nahshon,
luke:3:32.csb: son of Jesse, son of Obed, son of Boaz, son of Salmon, son of Nahshon,
luke:3:32.nrsv: son of Jesse, son of Obed, son of Boaz, son of Sala, son of Nahshon,

luke:3:33.niv: the son of Amminadab, the son of Ram, the son of Hezron, the son of Perez,
luke:3:33.csb: son of Amminadab, son of Ram, son of Hezron, son of Perez, son of Judah,
luke:3:33.nrsv: son of Amminadab, son of Admin, son of Arni, son of Hezron, son of Perez, son of Judah,

luke:3:34.niv: the son of Judah, the son of Jacob, the son of Isaac, the son of Abraham, the son of Terah, the son of Nahor,
luke:3:34.csb: son of Jacob, son of Isaac, son of Abraham, son of Terah, son of Nahor,
luke:3:34.nrsv: son of Jacob, son of Isaac, son of Abraham, son of Terah, son of Nahor,

luke:3:35.niv: the son of Serug, the son of Reu, the son of Peleg, the son of Eber,
luke:3:35.csb: son of Serug, son of Reu, son of Peleg, son of Eber, son of Shelah,
luke:3:35.nrsv: son of Serug, son of Reu, son of Peleg, son of Eber, son of Shelah,

luke:3:36.niv: the son of Shelah, the son of Cainan, the son of Arphaxad, the son of Shem, the son of Noah, the son of Lamech,
luke:3:36.csb: son of Cainan, son of Arphaxad, son of Shem, son of Noah, son of Lamech,
luke:3:36.nrsv: son of Cainan, son of Arphaxad, son of Shem, son of Noah, son of Lamech,

luke:3:37.niv: the son of Methuselah, the son of Enoch, the son of Jared, the son of Mahalalel,
luke:3:37.csb: son of Methuselah, son of Enoch, son of Jared, son of Mahalalel, son of Cainan,
luke:3:37.nrsv: son of Methuselah, son of Enoch, son of Jared, son of Mahalaleel, son of Cainan,

luke:3:38.niv: the son of Kenan, the son of Enosh, the son of Seth, the son of Adam, the son of God.
luke:3:38.csb: son of Enos, son of Seth, son of Adam, son of God.
luke:3:38.nrsv: son of Enos, son of Seth, son of Adam, son of God.

luke:4:1.niv: Jesus, full of the Holy Spirit, returned from the Jordan and was led by the Spirit in the desert,
luke:4:1.csb: Then Jesus left the Jordan, full of the Holy Spirit, and was led by the Spirit in the wilderness
luke:4:1.nrsv: Jesus, full of the Holy Spirit, returned from the Jordan and was led by the Spirit in the wilderness,

luke:4:2.niv: where for forty days he was tempted by the devil. He ate nothing during those days, and at the end of them he was hungry.
luke:4:2.csb: for forty days to be tempted by the devil. He ate nothing during those days, and when they were over, he was hungry.
luke:4:2.nrsv: where for forty days he was tempted by the devil. He ate nothing at all during those days, and when they were over, he was famished.

luke:4:3.niv: The devil said to him, "If you are the Son of God, tell this stone to become bread."
luke:4:3.csb: The devil said to him, “If you are the Son of God, tell this stone to become bread.”
luke:4:3.nrsv: The devil said to him, "If you are the Son of God, command this stone to become a loaf of bread."

luke:4:4.niv: Jesus answered, "It is written: 'Man does not live on bread alone.'"
luke:4:4.csb: But Jesus answered him, “It is written: Man must not live on bread alone. ”
luke:4:4.nrsv: Jesus answered him, "It is written, 'One does not live by bread alone.'"

luke:4:5.niv: The devil led him up to a high place and showed him in an instant all the kingdoms of the world.
luke:4:5.csb: So he took him up and showed him all the kingdoms of the world in a moment of time.
luke:4:5.nrsv: Then the devil led him up and showed him in an instant all the kingdoms of the world.

luke:4:6.niv: And he said to him, "I will give you all their authority and splendor, for it has been given to me, and I can give it to anyone I want to.
luke:4:6.csb: The devil said to him, “I will give you their splendor and all this authority, because it has been given over to me, and I can give it to anyone I want.
luke:4:6.nrsv: And the devil said to him, "To you I will give their glory and all this authority; for it has been given over to me, and I give it to anyone I please.

luke:4:7.niv: So if you worship me, it will all be yours."
luke:4:7.csb: If you, then, will worship me, all will be yours.”
luke:4:7.nrsv: If you, then, will worship me, it will all be yours."

luke:4:8.niv: Jesus answered, "It is written: 'Worship the Lord your God and serve him only.'"
luke:4:8.csb: And Jesus answered him, “It is written: Worship the Lord your God, and serve him only. ”
luke:4:8.nrsv: Jesus answered him, "It is written, 'Worship the Lord your God, and serve only him.'"

luke:4:9.niv: The devil led him to Jerusalem and had him stand on the highest point of the temple. "If you are the Son of God," he said, "throw yourself down from here.
luke:4:9.csb: So he took him to Jerusalem, had him stand on the pinnacle of the temple, and said to him, “If you are the Son of God, throw yourself down from here.
luke:4:9.nrsv: Then the devil took him to Jerusalem, and placed him on the pinnacle of the temple, saying to him, "If you are the Son of God, throw yourself down from here,

luke:4:10.niv: For it is written: "'He will command his angels concerning you to guard you carefully;
luke:4:10.csb: For it is written: He will give his angels orders concerning you, to protect you,
luke:4:10.nrsv: for it is written, 'He will command his angels concerning you, to protect you,'

luke:4:11.niv: they will lift you up in their hands, so that you will not strike your foot against a stone.'"
luke:4:11.csb: and they will support you with their hands, so that you will not strike your foot against a stone. ”
luke:4:11.nrsv: and 'On their hands they will bear you up, so that you will not dash your foot against a stone.'"

luke:4:12.niv: Jesus answered, "It says: 'Do not put the Lord your God to the test.'"
luke:4:12.csb: And Jesus answered him, “It is said: Do not test the Lord your God. ”
luke:4:12.nrsv: Jesus answered him, "It is said, 'Do not put the Lord your God to the test.'"

luke:4:13.niv: When the devil had finished all this tempting, he left him until an opportune time.
luke:4:13.csb: After the devil had finished every temptation, he departed from him for a time.
luke:4:13.nrsv: When the devil had finished every test, he departed from him until an opportune time.

luke:4:14.niv: Jesus returned to Galilee in the power of the Spirit, and news about him spread through the whole countryside.
luke:4:14.csb: Then Jesus returned to Galilee in the power of the Spirit, and news about him spread throughout the entire vicinity.
luke:4:14.nrsv: Then Jesus, filled with the power of the Spirit, returned to Galilee, and a report about him spread through all the surrounding country.

luke:4:15.niv: He taught in their synagogues, and everyone praised him.
luke:4:15.csb: He was teaching in their synagogues, being praised by everyone.
luke:4:15.nrsv: He began to teach in their synagogues and was praised by everyone.

luke:4:16.niv: He went to Nazareth, where he had been brought up, and on the Sabbath day he went into the synagogue, as was his custom. And he stood up to read.
luke:4:16.csb: He came to Nazareth, where he had been brought up. As usual, he entered the synagogue on the Sabbath day and stood up to read.
luke:4:16.nrsv: When he came to Nazareth, where he had been brought up, he went to the synagogue on the sabbath day, as was his custom. He stood up to read,

luke:4:17.niv: The scroll of the prophet Isaiah was handed to him. Unrolling it, he found the place where it is written:
luke:4:17.csb: The scroll of the prophet Isaiah was given to him, and unrolling the scroll, he found the place where it was written:
luke:4:17.nrsv: and the scroll of the prophet Isaiah was given to him. He unrolled the scroll and found the place where it was written:

luke:4:18.niv: "The Spirit of the Lord is on me, because he has anointed me to preach good news to the poor. He has sent me to proclaim freedom for the prisoners and recovery of sight for the blind, to release the oppressed,
luke:4:18.csb: The Spirit of the Lord is on me, because he has anointed me to preach good news to the poor. He has sent me to proclaim release to the captives and recovery of sight to the blind, to set free the oppressed,
luke:4:18.nrsv: "The Spirit of the Lord is upon me, because he has anointed me to bring good news to the poor. He has sent me to proclaim release to the captives and recovery of sight to the blind, to let the oppressed go free,

luke:4:19.niv: to proclaim the year of the Lord's favor."
luke:4:19.csb: to proclaim the year of the Lord’s favor.
luke:4:19.nrsv: to proclaim the year of the Lord's favor."

luke:4:20.niv: Then he rolled up the scroll, gave it back to the attendant and sat down. The eyes of everyone in the synagogue were fastened on him,
luke:4:20.csb: He then rolled up the scroll, gave it back to the attendant, and sat down. And the eyes of everyone in the synagogue were fixed on him.
luke:4:20.nrsv: And he rolled up the scroll, gave it back to the attendant, and sat down. The eyes of all in the synagogue were fixed on him.

luke:4:21.niv: and he began by saying to them, "Today this scripture is fulfilled in your hearing."
luke:4:21.csb: He began by saying to them, “Today as you listen, this Scripture has been fulfilled.”
luke:4:21.nrsv: Then he began to say to them, "Today this scripture has been fulfilled in your hearing."

luke:4:22.niv: All spoke well of him and were amazed at the gracious words that came from his lips. "Isn't this Joseph's son?" they asked.
luke:4:22.csb: They were all speaking well of him and were amazed by the gracious words that came from his mouth; yet they said, “Isn’t this Joseph’s son?”
luke:4:22.nrsv: All spoke well of him and were amazed at the gracious words that came from his mouth. They said, "Is not this Joseph's son?"

luke:4:23.niv: Jesus said to them, "Surely you will quote this proverb to me: 'Physician, heal yourself! Do here in your hometown what we have heard that you did in Capernaum.'"
luke:4:23.csb: Then he said to them, “No doubt you will quote this proverb to me: ‘Doctor, heal yourself. What we’ve heard that took place in Capernaum, do here in your hometown also.’”
luke:4:23.nrsv: He said to them, "Doubtless you will quote to me this proverb, 'Doctor, cure yourself!' And you will say, 'Do here also in your hometown the things that we have heard you did at Capernaum.'"

luke:4:24.niv: "I tell you the truth," he continued, "no prophet is accepted in his hometown.
luke:4:24.csb: He also said, “Truly I tell you, no prophet is accepted in his hometown.
luke:4:24.nrsv: And he said, "Truly I tell you, no prophet is accepted in the prophet's hometown.

luke:4:25.niv: I assure you that there were many widows in Israel in Elijah's time, when the sky was shut for three and a half years and there was a severe famine throughout the land.
luke:4:25.csb: But I say to you, there were certainly many widows in Israel in Elijah’s days, when the sky was shut up for three years and six months while a great famine came over all the land.
luke:4:25.nrsv: But the truth is, there were many widows in Israel in the time of Elijah, when the heaven was shut up three years and six months, and there was a severe famine over all the land;

luke:4:26.niv: Yet Elijah was not sent to any of them, but to a widow in Zarephath in the region of Sidon.
luke:4:26.csb: Yet Elijah was not sent to any of them except a widow at Zarephath in Sidon.
luke:4:26.nrsv: yet Elijah was sent to none of them except to a widow at Zarephath in Sidon.

luke:4:27.niv: And there were many in Israel with leprosy in the time of Elisha the prophet, yet not one of them was cleansed--only Naaman the Syrian."
luke:4:27.csb: And in the prophet Elisha’s time, there were many in Israel who had leprosy, and yet not one of them was cleansed except Naaman the Syrian.”
luke:4:27.nrsv: There were also many lepers in Israel in the time of the prophet Elisha, and none of them was cleansed except Naaman the Syrian."

luke:4:28.niv: All the people in the synagogue were furious when they heard this.
luke:4:28.csb: When they heard this, everyone in the synagogue was enraged.
luke:4:28.nrsv: When they heard this, all in the synagogue were filled with rage.

luke:4:29.niv: They got up, drove him out of the town, and took him to the brow of the hill on which the town was built, in order to throw him down the cliff.
luke:4:29.csb: They got up, drove him out of town, and brought him to the edge of the hill that their town was built on, intending to hurl him over the cliff.
luke:4:29.nrsv: They got up, drove him out of the town, and led him to the brow of the hill on which their town was built, so that they might hurl him off the cliff.

luke:4:30.niv: But he walked right through the crowd and went on his way.
luke:4:30.csb: But he passed right through the crowd and went on his way.
luke:4:30.nrsv: But he passed through the midst of them and went on his way.

luke:4:31.niv: Then he went down to Capernaum, a town in Galilee, and on the Sabbath began to teach the people.
luke:4:31.csb: Then he went down to Capernaum, a town in Galilee, and was teaching them on the Sabbath.
luke:4:31.nrsv: He went down to Capernaum, a city in Galilee, and was teaching them on the sabbath.

luke:4:32.niv: They were amazed at his teaching, because his message had authority.
luke:4:32.csb: They were astonished at his teaching because his message had authority.
luke:4:32.nrsv: They were astounded at his teaching, because he spoke with authority.

luke:4:33.niv: In the synagogue there was a man possessed by a demon, an evil spirit. He cried out at the top of his voice,
luke:4:33.csb: In the synagogue there was a man with an unclean demonic spirit who cried out with a loud voice,
luke:4:33.nrsv: In the synagogue there was a man who had the spirit of an unclean demon, and he cried out with a loud voice,

luke:4:34.niv: "Ha! What do you want with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are--the Holy One of God!"
luke:4:34.csb: “Leave us alone! What do you have to do with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are — the Holy One of God!”
luke:4:34.nrsv: "Let us alone! What have you to do with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are, the Holy One of God."

luke:4:35.niv: "Be quiet!" Jesus said sternly. "Come out of him!" Then the demon threw the man down before them all and came out without injuring him.
luke:4:35.csb: But Jesus rebuked him and said, “Be silent and come out of him!” And throwing him down before them, the demon came out of him without hurting him at all.
luke:4:35.nrsv: But Jesus rebuked him, saying, "Be silent, and come out of him!" When the demon had thrown him down before them, he came out of him without having done him any harm.

luke:4:36.niv: All the people were amazed and said to each other, "What is this teaching? With authority and power he gives orders to evil spirits and they come out!"
luke:4:36.csb: Amazement came over them all, and they were saying to one another, “What is this message? For he commands the unclean spirits with authority and power, and they come out!”
luke:4:36.nrsv: They were all amazed and kept saying to one another, "What kind of utterance is this? For with authority and power he commands the unclean spirits, and out they come!"

luke:4:37.niv: And the news about him spread throughout the surrounding area.
luke:4:37.csb: And news about him began to go out to every place in the vicinity.
luke:4:37.nrsv: And a report about him began to reach every place in the region.

luke:4:38.niv: Jesus left the synagogue and went to the home of Simon. Now Simon's mother-in-law was suffering from a high fever, and they asked Jesus to help her.
luke:4:38.csb: After he left the synagogue, he entered Simon’s house. Simon’s mother-in-law was suffering from a high fever, and they asked him about her.
luke:4:38.nrsv: After leaving the synagogue he entered Simon's house. Now Simon's mother-in-law was suffering from a high fever, and they asked him about her.

luke:4:39.niv: So he bent over her and rebuked the fever, and it left her. She got up at once and began to wait on them.
luke:4:39.csb: So he stood over her and rebuked the fever, and it left her. She got up immediately and began to serve them.
luke:4:39.nrsv: Then he stood over her and rebuked the fever, and it left her. Immediately she got up and began to serve them.

luke:4:40.niv: When the sun was setting, the people brought to Jesus all who had various kinds of sickness, and laying his hands on each one, he healed them.
luke:4:40.csb: When the sun was setting, all those who had anyone sick with various diseases brought them to him. As he laid his hands on each one of them, he healed them.
luke:4:40.nrsv: As the sun was setting, all those who had any who were sick with various kinds of diseases brought them to him; and he laid his hands on each of them and cured them.

luke:4:41.niv: Moreover, demons came out of many people, shouting, "You are the Son of God!" But he rebuked them and would not allow them to speak, because they knew he was the Christ.
luke:4:41.csb: Also, demons were coming out of many, shouting and saying, “You are the Son of God!” But he rebuked them and would not allow them to speak, because they knew he was the Christ.
luke:4:41.nrsv: Demons also came out of many, shouting, "You are the Son of God!" But he rebuked them and would not allow them to speak, because they knew that he was the Messiah.

luke:4:42.niv: At daybreak Jesus went out to a solitary place. The people were looking for him and when they came to where he was, they tried to keep him from leaving them.
luke:4:42.csb: When it was day, he went out and made his way to a deserted place. But the crowds were searching for him. They came to him and tried to keep him from leaving them.
luke:4:42.nrsv: At daybreak he departed and went into a deserted place. And the crowds were looking for him; and when they reached him, they wanted to prevent him from leaving them.

luke:4:43.niv: But he said, "I must preach the good news of the kingdom of God to the other towns also, because that is why I was sent."
luke:4:43.csb: But he said to them, “It is necessary for me to proclaim the good news about the kingdom of God to the other towns also, because I was sent for this purpose.”
luke:4:43.nrsv: But he said to them, "I must proclaim the good news of the kingdom of God to the other cities also; for I was sent for this purpose."

luke:4:44.niv: And he kept on preaching in the synagogues of Judea.
luke:4:44.csb: And he was preaching in the synagogues of Judea.
luke:4:44.nrsv: So he continued proclaiming the message in the synagogues of Judea.

luke:5:1.niv: One day as Jesus was standing by the Lake of Gennesaret, with the people crowding around him and listening to the word of God,
luke:5:1.csb: As the crowd was pressing in on Jesus to hear God’s word, he was standing by Lake Gennesaret.
luke:5:1.nrsv: Once while Jesus was standing beside the lake of Gennesaret, and the crowd was pressing in on him to hear the word of God,

luke:5:2.niv: he saw at the water's edge two boats, left there by the fishermen, who were washing their nets.
luke:5:2.csb: He saw two boats at the edge of the lake; the fishermen had left them and were washing their nets.
luke:5:2.nrsv: he saw two boats there at the shore of the lake; the fishermen had gone out of them and were washing their nets.

luke:5:3.niv: He got into one of the boats, the one belonging to Simon, and asked him to put out a little from shore. Then he sat down and taught the people from the boat.
luke:5:3.csb: He got into one of the boats, which belonged to Simon, and asked him to put out a little from the land. Then he sat down and was teaching the crowds from the boat.
luke:5:3.nrsv: He got into one of the boats, the one belonging to Simon, and asked him to put out a little way from the shore. Then he sat down and taught the crowds from the boat.

luke:5:4.niv: When he had finished speaking, he said to Simon, "Put out into deep water, and let down the nets for a catch."
luke:5:4.csb: When he had finished speaking, he said to Simon, “Put out into deep water and let down your nets for a catch.”
luke:5:4.nrsv: When he had finished speaking, he said to Simon, "Put out into the deep water and let down your nets for a catch."

luke:5:5.niv: Simon answered, "Master, we've worked hard all night and haven't caught anything. But because you say so, I will let down the nets."
luke:5:5.csb: “Master,” Simon replied, “we’ve worked hard all night long and caught nothing. But if you say so, I’ll let down the nets.”
luke:5:5.nrsv: Simon answered, "Master, we have worked all night long but have caught nothing. Yet if you say so, I will let down the nets."

luke:5:6.niv: When they had done so, they caught such a large number of fish that their nets began to break.
luke:5:6.csb: When they did this, they caught a great number of fish, and their nets began to tear.
luke:5:6.nrsv: When they had done this, they caught so many fish that their nets were beginning to break.

luke:5:7.niv: So they signaled their partners in the other boat to come and help them, and they came and filled both boats so full that they began to sink.
luke:5:7.csb: So they signaled to their partners in the other boat to come and help them; they came and filled both boats so full that they began to sink.
luke:5:7.nrsv: So they signaled their partners in the other boat to come and help them. And they came and filled both boats, so that they began to sink.

luke:5:8.niv: When Simon Peter saw this, he fell at Jesus' knees and said, "Go away from me, Lord; I am a sinful man!"
luke:5:8.csb: When Simon Peter saw this, he fell at Jesus’s knees and said, “Go away from me, because I’m a sinful man, Lord!”
luke:5:8.nrsv: But when Simon Peter saw it, he fell down at Jesus' knees, saying, "Go away from me, Lord, for I am a sinful man!"

luke:5:9.niv: For he and all his companions were astonished at the catch of fish they had taken,
luke:5:9.csb: For he and all those with him were amazed at the catch of fish they had taken,
luke:5:9.nrsv: For he and all who were with him were amazed at the catch of fish that they had taken;

luke:5:10.niv: and so were James and John, the sons of Zebedee, Simon's partners.
luke:5:10.csb: and so were James and John, Zebedee’s sons, who were Simon’s partners. “Don’t be afraid,” Jesus told Simon. “From now on you will be catching people.”
luke:5:10.nrsv: and so also were James and John, sons of Zebedee, who were partners with Simon. Then Jesus said to Simon, "Do not be afraid; from now on you will be catching people."

luke:5:11.niv: Then Jesus said to Simon, "Don't be afraid; from now on you will catch men." So they pulled their boats up on shore, left everything and followed him.
luke:5:11.csb: Then they brought the boats to land, left everything, and followed him.
luke:5:11.nrsv: When they had brought their boats to shore, they left everything and followed him.

luke:5:12.niv: While Jesus was in one of the towns, a man came along who was covered with leprosy. When he saw Jesus, he fell with his face to the ground and begged him, "Lord, if you are willing, you can make me clean."
luke:5:12.csb: While he was in one of the towns, a man was there who had leprosy all over him. He saw Jesus, fell facedown, and begged him: “Lord, if you are willing, you can make me clean.”
luke:5:12.nrsv: Once, when he was in one of the cities, there was a man covered with leprosy. When he saw Jesus, he bowed with his face to the ground and begged him, "Lord, if you choose, you can make me clean."

luke:5:13.niv: Jesus reached out his hand and touched the man. "I am willing," he said. "Be clean!" And immediately the leprosy left him.
luke:5:13.csb: Reaching out his hand, Jesus touched him, saying, “I am willing; be made clean,” and immediately the leprosy left him.
luke:5:13.nrsv: Then Jesus stretched out his hand, touched him, and said, "I do choose. Be made clean." Immediately the leprosy left him.

luke:5:14.niv: Then Jesus ordered him, "Don't tell anyone, but go, show yourself to the priest and offer the sacrifices that Moses commanded for your cleansing, as a testimony to them."
luke:5:14.csb: Then he ordered him to tell no one: “But go and show yourself to the priest, and offer what Moses commanded for your cleansing as a testimony to them.”
luke:5:14.nrsv: And he ordered him to tell no one. "Go," he said, "and show yourself to the priest, and, as Moses commanded, make an offering for your cleansing, for a testimony to them."

luke:5:15.niv: Yet the news about him spread all the more, so that crowds of people came to hear him and to be healed of their sicknesses.
luke:5:15.csb: But the news about him spread even more, and large crowds would come together to hear him and to be healed of their sicknesses.
luke:5:15.nrsv: But now more than ever the word about Jesus spread abroad; many crowds would gather to hear him and to be cured of their diseases.

luke:5:16.niv: But Jesus often withdrew to lonely places and prayed.
luke:5:16.csb: Yet he often withdrew to deserted places and prayed.
luke:5:16.nrsv: But he would withdraw to deserted places and pray.

luke:5:17.niv: One day as he was teaching, Pharisees and teachers of the law, who had come from every village of Galilee and from Judea and Jerusalem, were sitting there. And the power of the Lord was present for him to heal the sick.
luke:5:17.csb: On one of those days while he was teaching, Pharisees and teachers of the law were sitting there who had come from every village of Galilee and Judea, and also from Jerusalem. And the Lord’s power to heal was in him.
luke:5:17.nrsv: One day, while he was teaching, Pharisees and teachers of the law were sitting near by (they had come from every village of Galilee and Judea and from Jerusalem); and the power of the Lord was with him to heal.

luke:5:18.niv: Some men came carrying a paralytic on a mat and tried to take him into the house to lay him before Jesus.
luke:5:18.csb: Just then some men came, carrying on a stretcher a man who was paralyzed. They tried to bring him in and set him down before him.
luke:5:18.nrsv: Just then some men came, carrying a paralyzed man on a bed. They were trying to bring him in and lay him before Jesus;

luke:5:19.niv: When they could not find a way to do this because of the crowd, they went up on the roof and lowered him on his mat through the tiles into the middle of the crowd, right in front of Jesus.
luke:5:19.csb: Since they could not find a way to bring him in because of the crowd, they went up on the roof and lowered him on the stretcher through the roof tiles into the middle of the crowd before Jesus.
luke:5:19.nrsv: but finding no way to bring him in because of the crowd, they went up on the roof and let him down with his bed through the tiles into the middle of the crowd in front of Jesus.

luke:5:20.niv: When Jesus saw their faith, he said, "Friend, your sins are forgiven."
luke:5:20.csb: Seeing their faith he said, “Friend, your sins are forgiven.”
luke:5:20.nrsv: When he saw their faith, he said, "Friend, your sins are forgiven you."

luke:5:21.niv: The Pharisees and the teachers of the law began thinking to themselves, "Who is this fellow who speaks blasphemy? Who can forgive sins but God alone?"
luke:5:21.csb: Then the scribes and the Pharisees began to think to themselves: “Who is this man who speaks blasphemies? Who can forgive sins but God alone?”
luke:5:21.nrsv: Then the scribes and the Pharisees began to question, "Who is this who is speaking blasphemies? Who can forgive sins but God alone?"

luke:5:22.niv: Jesus knew what they were thinking and asked, "Why are you thinking these things in your hearts?
luke:5:22.csb: But perceiving their thoughts, Jesus replied to them, “Why are you thinking this in your hearts?
luke:5:22.nrsv: When Jesus perceived their questionings, he answered them, "Why do you raise such questions in your hearts?

luke:5:23.niv: Which is easier: to say, 'Your sins are forgiven,' or to say, 'Get up and walk'?
luke:5:23.csb: Which is easier: to say, ‘Your sins are forgiven you,’ or to say, ‘Get up and walk’?
luke:5:23.nrsv: Which is easier, to say, 'Your sins are forgiven you,' or to say, 'Stand up and walk'?

luke:5:24.niv: But that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins...." He said to the paralyzed man, "I tell you, get up, take your mat and go home."
luke:5:24.csb: But so that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins” — he told the paralyzed man, “I tell you: Get up, take your stretcher, and go home.”
luke:5:24.nrsv: But so that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins"-- he said to the one who was paralyzed-- "I say to you, stand up and take your bed and go to your home."

luke:5:25.niv: Immediately he stood up in front of them, took what he had been lying on and went home praising God.
luke:5:25.csb: Immediately he got up before them, picked up what he had been lying on, and went home glorifying God.
luke:5:25.nrsv: Immediately he stood up before them, took what he had been lying on, and went to his home, glorifying God.

luke:5:26.niv: Everyone was amazed and gave praise to God. They were filled with awe and said, "We have seen remarkable things today."
luke:5:26.csb: Then everyone was astounded, and they were giving glory to God. And they were filled with awe and said, “We have seen incredible things today.”
luke:5:26.nrsv: Amazement seized all of them, and they glorified God and were filled with awe, saying, "We have seen strange things today."

luke:5:27.niv: After this, Jesus went out and saw a tax collector by the name of Levi sitting at his tax booth. "Follow me," Jesus said to him,
luke:5:27.csb: After this, Jesus went out and saw a tax collector named Levi sitting at the tax office, and he said to him, “Follow me.”
luke:5:27.nrsv: After this he went out and saw a tax collector named Levi, sitting at the tax booth; and he said to him, "Follow me."

luke:5:28.niv: and Levi got up, left everything and followed him.
luke:5:28.csb: So, leaving everything behind, he got up and began to follow him.
luke:5:28.nrsv: And he got up, left everything, and followed him.

luke:5:29.niv: Then Levi held a great banquet for Jesus at his house, and a large crowd of tax collectors and others were eating with them.
luke:5:29.csb: Then Levi hosted a grand banquet for him at his house. Now there was a large crowd of tax collectors and others who were guests with them.
luke:5:29.nrsv: Then Levi gave a great banquet for him in his house; and there was a large crowd of tax collectors and others sitting at the table with them.

luke:5:30.niv: But the Pharisees and the teachers of the law who belonged to their sect complained to his disciples, "Why do you eat and drink with tax collectors and 'sinners'?"
luke:5:30.csb: But the Pharisees and their scribes were complaining to his disciples, “Why do you eat and drink with tax collectors and sinners?”
luke:5:30.nrsv: The Pharisees and their scribes were complaining to his disciples, saying, "Why do you eat and drink with tax collectors and sinners?"

luke:5:31.niv: Jesus answered them, "It is not the healthy who need a doctor, but the sick.
luke:5:31.csb: Jesus replied to them, “It is not those who are healthy who need a doctor, but those who are sick.
luke:5:31.nrsv: Jesus answered, "Those who are well have no need of a physician, but those who are sick;

luke:5:32.niv: I have not come to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance."
luke:5:32.csb: I have not come to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.”
luke:5:32.nrsv: I have come to call not the righteous but sinners to repentance."

luke:5:33.niv: They said to him, "John's disciples often fast and pray, and so do the disciples of the Pharisees, but yours go on eating and drinking."
luke:5:33.csb: Then they said to him, “John’s disciples fast often and say prayers, and those of the Pharisees do the same, but yours eat and drink.”
luke:5:33.nrsv: Then they said to him, "John's disciples, like the disciples of the Pharisees, frequently fast and pray, but your disciples eat and drink.

luke:5:34.niv: Jesus answered, "Can you make the guests of the bridegroom fast while he is with them?
luke:5:34.csb: Jesus said to them, “You can’t make the wedding guests fast while the groom is with them, can you?
luke:5:34.nrsv: Jesus said to them, "You cannot make wedding guests fast while the bridegroom is with them, can you?

luke:5:35.niv: But the time will come when the bridegroom will be taken from them; in those days they will fast."
luke:5:35.csb: But the time will come when the groom will be taken away from them — then they will fast in those days.”
luke:5:35.nrsv: The days will come when the bridegroom will be taken away from them, and then they will fast in those days."

luke:5:36.niv: He told them this parable: "No one tears a patch from a new garment and sews it on an old one. If he does, he will have torn the new garment, and the patch from the new will not match the old.
luke:5:36.csb: He also told them a parable: “No one tears a patch from a new garment and puts it on an old garment. Otherwise, not only will he tear the new, but also the piece from the new garment will not match the old.
luke:5:36.nrsv: He also told them a parable: "No one tears a piece from a new garment and sews it on an old garment; otherwise the new will be torn, and the piece from the new will not match the old.

luke:5:37.niv: And no one pours new wine into old wineskins. If he does, the new wine will burst the skins, the wine will run out and the wineskins will be ruined.
luke:5:37.csb: And no one puts new wine into old wineskins. Otherwise, the new wine will burst the skins, it will spill, and the skins will be ruined.
luke:5:37.nrsv: And no one puts new wine into old wineskins; otherwise the new wine will burst the skins and will be spilled, and the skins will be destroyed.

luke:5:38.niv: No, new wine must be poured into new wineskins.
luke:5:38.csb: No, new wine is put into fresh wineskins.
luke:5:38.nrsv: But new wine must be put into fresh wineskins.

luke:5:39.niv: And no one after drinking old wine wants the new, for he says, 'The old is better.'"
luke:5:39.csb: And no one, after drinking old wine, wants new, because he says, ‘The old is better.’”
luke:5:39.nrsv: And no one after drinking old wine desires new wine, but says, 'The old is good.'"

luke:6:1.niv: One Sabbath Jesus was going through the grainfields, and his disciples began to pick some heads of grain, rub them in their hands and eat the kernels.
luke:6:1.csb: On a Sabbath, he passed through the grainfields. His disciples were picking heads of grain, rubbing them in their hands, and eating them.
luke:6:1.nrsv: One sabbath while Jesus was going through the grainfields, his disciples plucked some heads of grain, rubbed them in their hands, and ate them.

luke:6:2.niv: Some of the Pharisees asked, "Why are you doing what is unlawful on the Sabbath?"
luke:6:2.csb: But some of the Pharisees said, “Why are you doing what is not lawful on the Sabbath?”
luke:6:2.nrsv: But some of the Pharisees said, "Why are you doing what is not lawful on the sabbath?"

luke:6:3.niv: Jesus answered them, "Have you never read what David did when he and his companions were hungry?
luke:6:3.csb: Jesus answered them, “Haven’t you read what David and those who were with him did when he was hungry —
luke:6:3.nrsv: Jesus answered, "Have you not read what David did when he and his companions were hungry?

luke:6:4.niv: He entered the house of God, and taking the consecrated bread, he ate what is lawful only for priests to eat. And he also gave some to his companions."
luke:6:4.csb: how he entered the house of God and took and ate the bread of the Presence, which is not lawful for any but the priests to eat? He even gave some to those who were with him.”
luke:6:4.nrsv: He entered the house of God and took and ate the bread of the Presence, which it is not lawful for any but the priests to eat, and gave some to his companions?"

luke:6:5.niv: Then Jesus said to them, "The Son of Man is Lord of the Sabbath."
luke:6:5.csb: Then he told them, “The Son of Man is Lord of the Sabbath.”
luke:6:5.nrsv: Then he said to them, "The Son of Man is lord of the sabbath."

luke:6:6.niv: On another Sabbath he went into the synagogue and was teaching, and a man was there whose right hand was shriveled.
luke:6:6.csb: On another Sabbath he entered the synagogue and was teaching. A man was there whose right hand was shriveled.
luke:6:6.nrsv: On another sabbath he entered the synagogue and taught, and there was a man there whose right hand was withered.

luke:6:7.niv: The Pharisees and the teachers of the law were looking for a reason to accuse Jesus, so they watched him closely to see if he would heal on the Sabbath.
luke:6:7.csb: The scribes and Pharisees were watching him closely, to see if he would heal on the Sabbath, so that they could find a charge against him.
luke:6:7.nrsv: The scribes and the Pharisees watched him to see whether he would cure on the sabbath, so that they might find an accusation against him.

luke:6:8.niv: But Jesus knew what they were thinking and said to the man with the shriveled hand, "Get up and stand in front of everyone." So he got up and stood there.
luke:6:8.csb: But he knew their thoughts and told the man with the shriveled hand, “Get up and stand here.” So he got up and stood there.
luke:6:8.nrsv: Even though he knew what they were thinking, he said to the man who had the withered hand, "Come and stand here." He got up and stood there.

luke:6:9.niv: Then Jesus said to them, "I ask you, which is lawful on the Sabbath: to do good or to do evil, to save life or to destroy it?"
luke:6:9.csb: Then Jesus said to them, “I ask you: Is it lawful to do good on the Sabbath or to do evil, to save life or to destroy it?”
luke:6:9.nrsv: Then Jesus said to them, "I ask you, is it lawful to do good or to do harm on the sabbath, to save life or to destroy it?"

luke:6:10.niv: He looked around at them all, and then said to the man, "Stretch out your hand." He did so, and his hand was completely restored.
luke:6:10.csb: After looking around at them all, he told him, “Stretch out your hand.” He did, and his hand was restored.
luke:6:10.nrsv: After looking around at all of them, he said to him, "Stretch out your hand." He did so, and his hand was restored.

luke:6:11.niv: But they were furious and began to discuss with one another what they might do to Jesus.
luke:6:11.csb: They, however, were filled with rage and started discussing with one another what they might do to Jesus.
luke:6:11.nrsv: But they were filled with fury and discussed with one another what they might do to Jesus.

luke:6:12.niv: One of those days Jesus went out to a mountainside to pray, and spent the night praying to God.
luke:6:12.csb: During those days he went out to the mountain to pray and spent all night in prayer to God.
luke:6:12.nrsv: Now during those days he went out to the mountain to pray; and he spent the night in prayer to God.

luke:6:13.niv: When morning came, he called his disciples to him and chose twelve of them, whom he also designated apostles:
luke:6:13.csb: When daylight came, he summoned his disciples, and he chose twelve of them, whom he also named apostles:
luke:6:13.nrsv: And when day came, he called his disciples and chose twelve of them, whom he also named apostles:

luke:6:14.niv: Simon (whom he named Peter), his brother Andrew, James, John, Philip, Bartholomew,
luke:6:14.csb: Simon, whom he also named Peter, and Andrew his brother; James and John; Philip and Bartholomew;
luke:6:14.nrsv: Simon, whom he named Peter, and his brother Andrew, and James, and John, and Philip, and Bartholomew,

luke:6:15.niv: Matthew, Thomas, James son of Alphaeus, Simon who was called the Zealot,
luke:6:15.csb: Matthew and Thomas; James the son of Alphaeus, and Simon called the Zealot;
luke:6:15.nrsv: and Matthew, and Thomas, and James son of Alphaeus, and Simon, who was called the Zealot,

luke:6:16.niv: Judas son of James, and Judas Iscariot, who became a traitor.
luke:6:16.csb: Judas the son of James, and Judas Iscariot, who became a traitor.
luke:6:16.nrsv: and Judas son of James, and Judas Iscariot, who became a traitor.

luke:6:17.niv: He went down with them and stood on a level place. A large crowd of his disciples was there and a great number of people from all over Judea, from Jerusalem, and from the coast of Tyre and Sidon,
luke:6:17.csb: After coming down with them, he stood on a level place with a large crowd of his disciples and a great number of people from all Judea and Jerusalem and from the seacoast of Tyre and Sidon.
luke:6:17.nrsv: He came down with them and stood on a level place, with a great crowd of his disciples and a great multitude of people from all Judea, Jerusalem, and the coast of Tyre and Sidon.

luke:6:18.niv: who had come to hear him and to be healed of their diseases. Those troubled by evil spirits were cured,
luke:6:18.csb: They came to hear him and to be healed of their diseases; and those tormented by unclean spirits were made well.
luke:6:18.nrsv: They had come to hear him and to be healed of their diseases; and those who were troubled with unclean spirits were cured.

luke:6:19.niv: and the people all tried to touch him, because power was coming from him and healing them all.
luke:6:19.csb: The whole crowd was trying to touch him, because power was coming out from him and healing them all.
luke:6:19.nrsv: And all in the crowd were trying to touch him, for power came out from him and healed all of them.

luke:6:20.niv: Looking at his disciples, he said: "Blessed are you who are poor, for yours is the kingdom of God.
luke:6:20.csb: Then looking up at his disciples, he said: Blessed are you who are poor, because the kingdom of God is yours.
luke:6:20.nrsv: Then he looked up at his disciples and said: "Blessed are you who are poor, for yours is the kingdom of God.

luke:6:21.niv: Blessed are you who hunger now, for you will be satisfied. Blessed are you who weep now, for you will laugh.
luke:6:21.csb: Blessed are you who are now hungry, because you will be filled. Blessed are you who weep now, because you will laugh.
luke:6:21.nrsv: "Blessed are you who are hungry now, for you will be filled. "Blessed are you who weep now, for you will laugh.

luke:6:22.niv: Blessed are you when men hate you, when they exclude you and insult you and reject your name as evil, because of the Son of Man.
luke:6:22.csb: Blessed are you when people hate you, when they exclude you, insult you, and slander your name as evil because of the Son of Man.
luke:6:22.nrsv: "Blessed are you when people hate you, and when they exclude you, revile you, and defame you on account of the Son of Man.

luke:6:23.niv: "Rejoice in that day and leap for joy, because great is your reward in heaven. For that is how their fathers treated the prophets.
luke:6:23.csb: “Rejoice in that day and leap for joy. Take note — your reward is great in heaven, for this is the way their ancestors used to treat the prophets.
luke:6:23.nrsv: Rejoice in that day and leap for joy, for surely your reward is great in heaven; for that is what their ancestors did to the prophets.

luke:6:24.niv: "But woe to you who are rich, for you have already received your comfort.
luke:6:24.csb: But woe to you who are rich, for you have received your comfort.
luke:6:24.nrsv: "But woe to you who are rich, for you have received your consolation.

luke:6:25.niv: Woe to you who are well fed now, for you will go hungry. Woe to you who laugh now, for you will mourn and weep.
luke:6:25.csb: Woe to you who are now full, for you will be hungry. Woe to you who are now laughing, for you will mourn and weep.
luke:6:25.nrsv: "Woe to you who are full now, for you will be hungry. "Woe to you who are laughing now, for you will mourn and weep.

luke:6:26.niv: Woe to you when all men speak well of you, for that is how their fathers treated the false prophets.
luke:6:26.csb: Woe to you when all people speak well of you, for this is the way their ancestors used to treat the false prophets.
luke:6:26.nrsv: "Woe to you when all speak well of you, for that is what their ancestors did to the false prophets.

luke:6:27.niv: "But I tell you who hear me: Love your enemies, do good to those who hate you,
luke:6:27.csb: “But I say to you who listen: Love your enemies, do what is good to those who hate you,
luke:6:27.nrsv: "But I say to you that listen, Love your enemies, do good to those who hate you,

luke:6:28.niv: bless those who curse you, pray for those who mistreat you.
luke:6:28.csb: bless those who curse you, pray for those who mistreat you.
luke:6:28.nrsv: bless those who curse you, pray for those who abuse you.

luke:6:29.niv: If someone strikes you on one cheek, turn to him the other also. If someone takes your cloak, do not stop him from taking your tunic.
luke:6:29.csb: If anyone hits you on the cheek, offer the other also. And if anyone takes away your coat, don’t hold back your shirt either.
luke:6:29.nrsv: If anyone strikes you on the cheek, offer the other also; and from anyone who takes away your coat do not withhold even your shirt.

luke:6:30.niv: Give to everyone who asks you, and if anyone takes what belongs to you, do not demand it back.
luke:6:30.csb: Give to everyone who asks you, and from someone who takes your things, don’t ask for them back.
luke:6:30.nrsv: Give to everyone who begs from you; and if anyone takes away your goods, do not ask for them again.

luke:6:31.niv: Do to others as you would have them do to you.
luke:6:31.csb: Just as you want others to do for you, do the same for them.
luke:6:31.nrsv: Do to others as you would have them do to you.

luke:6:32.niv: "If you love those who love you, what credit is that to you? Even 'sinners' love those who love them.
luke:6:32.csb: If you love those who love you, what credit is that to you? Even sinners love those who love them.
luke:6:32.nrsv: "If you love those who love you, what credit is that to you? For even sinners love those who love them.

luke:6:33.niv: And if you do good to those who are good to you, what credit is that to you? Even 'sinners' do that.
luke:6:33.csb: If you do what is good to those who are good to you, what credit is that to you? Even sinners do that.
luke:6:33.nrsv: If you do good to those who do good to you, what credit is that to you? For even sinners do the same.

luke:6:34.niv: And if you lend to those from whom you expect repayment, what credit is that to you? Even 'sinners' lend to 'sinners,' expecting to be repaid in full.
luke:6:34.csb: And if you lend to those from whom you expect to receive, what credit is that to you? Even sinners lend to sinners to be repaid in full.
luke:6:34.nrsv: If you lend to those from whom you hope to receive, what credit is that to you? Even sinners lend to sinners, to receive as much again.

luke:6:35.niv: But love your enemies, do good to them, and lend to them without expecting to get anything back. Then your reward will be great, and you will be sons of the Most High, because he is kind to the ungrateful and wicked.
luke:6:35.csb: But love your enemies, do what is good, and lend, expecting nothing in return. Then your reward will be great, and you will be children of the Most High. For he is gracious to the ungrateful and evil.
luke:6:35.nrsv: But love your enemies, do good, and lend, expecting nothing in return. Your reward will be great, and you will be children of the Most High; for he is kind to the ungrateful and the wicked.

luke:6:36.niv: Be merciful, just as your Father is merciful.
luke:6:36.csb: Be merciful, just as your Father also is merciful.
luke:6:36.nrsv: Be merciful, just as your Father is merciful.

luke:6:37.niv: "Do not judge, and you will not be judged. Do not condemn, and you will not be condemned. Forgive, and you will be forgiven.
luke:6:37.csb: “Do not judge, and you will not be judged. Do not condemn, and you will not be condemned. Forgive, and you will be forgiven.
luke:6:37.nrsv: "Do not judge, and you will not be judged; do not condemn, and you will not be condemned. Forgive, and you will be forgiven;

luke:6:38.niv: Give, and it will be given to you. A good measure, pressed down, shaken together and running over, will be poured into your lap. For with the measure you use, it will be measured to you."
luke:6:38.csb: Give, and it will be given to you; a good measure — pressed down, shaken together, and running over — will be poured into your lap. For with the measure you use, it will be measured back to you.”
luke:6:38.nrsv: give, and it will be given to you. A good measure, pressed down, shaken together, running over, will be put into your lap; for the measure you give will be the measure you get back."

luke:6:39.niv: He also told them this parable: "Can a blind man lead a blind man? Will they not both fall into a pit?
luke:6:39.csb: He also told them a parable: “Can the blind guide the blind? Won’t they both fall into a pit?
luke:6:39.nrsv: He also told them a parable: "Can a blind person guide a blind person? Will not both fall into a pit?

luke:6:40.niv: A student is not above his teacher, but everyone who is fully trained will be like his teacher.
luke:6:40.csb: A disciple is not above his teacher, but everyone who is fully trained will be like his teacher.
luke:6:40.nrsv: A disciple is not above the teacher, but everyone who is fully qualified will be like the teacher.

luke:6:41.niv: "Why do you look at the speck of sawdust in your brother's eye and pay no attention to the plank in your own eye?
luke:6:41.csb: “Why do you look at the splinter in your brother’s eye, but don’t notice the beam of wood in your own eye?
luke:6:41.nrsv: Why do you see the speck in your neighbor's eye, but do not notice the log in your own eye?

luke:6:42.niv: How can you say to your brother, 'Brother, let me take the speck out of your eye,' when you yourself fail to see the plank in your own eye? You hypocrite, first take the plank out of your eye, and then you will see clearly to remove the speck from your brother's eye.
luke:6:42.csb: Or how can you say to your brother, ‘Brother, let me take out the splinter that is in your eye,’ when you yourself don’t see the beam of wood in your eye? Hypocrite! First take the beam of wood out of your eye, and then you will see clearly to take out the splinter in your brother’s eye.
luke:6:42.nrsv: Or how can you say to your neighbor, 'Friend, let me take out the speck in your eye,' when you yourself do not see the log in your own eye? You hypocrite, first take the log out of your own eye, and then you will see clearly to take the speck out of your neighbor's eye.

luke:6:43.niv: "No good tree bears bad fruit, nor does a bad tree bear good fruit.
luke:6:43.csb: “A good tree doesn’t produce bad fruit; on the other hand, a bad tree doesn’t produce good fruit.
luke:6:43.nrsv: "No good tree bears bad fruit, nor again does a bad tree bear good fruit;

luke:6:44.niv: Each tree is recognized by its own fruit. People do not pick figs from thornbushes, or grapes from briers.
luke:6:44.csb: For each tree is known by its own fruit. Figs aren’t gathered from thornbushes, or grapes picked from a bramble bush.
luke:6:44.nrsv: for each tree is known by its own fruit. Figs are not gathered from thorns, nor are grapes picked from a bramble bush.

luke:6:45.niv: The good man brings good things out of the good stored up in his heart, and the evil man brings evil things out of the evil stored up in his heart. For out of the overflow of his heart his mouth speaks.
luke:6:45.csb: A good person produces good out of the good stored up in his heart. An evil person produces evil out of the evil stored up in his heart, for his mouth speaks from the overflow of the heart.
luke:6:45.nrsv: The good person out of the good treasure of the heart produces good, and the evil person out of evil treasure produces evil; for it is out of the abundance of the heart that the mouth speaks.

luke:6:46.niv: "Why do you call me, 'Lord, Lord,' and do not do what I say?
luke:6:46.csb: “Why do you call me ‘Lord, Lord,’ and don’t do the things I say?
luke:6:46.nrsv: "Why do you call me 'Lord, Lord,' and do not do what I tell you?

luke:6:47.niv: I will show you what he is like who comes to me and hears my words and puts them into practice.
luke:6:47.csb: I will show you what someone is like who comes to me, hears my words, and acts on them:
luke:6:47.nrsv: I will show you what someone is like who comes to me, hears my words, and acts on them.

luke:6:48.niv: He is like a man building a house, who dug down deep and laid the foundation on rock. When a flood came, the torrent struck that house but could not shake it, because it was well built.
luke:6:48.csb: He is like a man building a house, who dug deep and laid the foundation on the rock. When the flood came, the river crashed against that house and couldn’t shake it, because it was well built.
luke:6:48.nrsv: That one is like a man building a house, who dug deeply and laid the foundation on rock; when a flood arose, the river burst against that house but could not shake it, because it had been well built.

luke:6:49.niv: But the one who hears my words and does not put them into practice is like a man who built a house on the ground without a foundation. The moment the torrent struck that house, it collapsed and its destruction was complete."
luke:6:49.csb: But the one who hears and does not act is like a man who built a house on the ground without a foundation. The river crashed against it, and immediately it collapsed. And the destruction of that house was great.”
luke:6:49.nrsv: But the one who hears and does not act is like a man who built a house on the ground without a foundation. When the river burst against it, immediately it fell, and great was the ruin of that house."

luke:7:1.niv: When Jesus had finished saying all this in the hearing of the people, he entered Capernaum.
luke:7:1.csb: When he had concluded saying all this to the people who were listening, he entered Capernaum.
luke:7:1.nrsv: After Jesus had finished all his sayings in the hearing of the people, he entered Capernaum.

luke:7:2.niv: There a centurion's servant, whom his master valued highly, was sick and about to die.
luke:7:2.csb: A centurion’s servant, who was highly valued by him, was sick and about to die.
luke:7:2.nrsv: A centurion there had a slave whom he valued highly, and who was ill and close to death.

luke:7:3.niv: The centurion heard of Jesus and sent some elders of the Jews to him, asking him to come and heal his servant.
luke:7:3.csb: When the centurion heard about Jesus, he sent some Jewish elders to him, requesting him to come and save the life of his servant.
luke:7:3.nrsv: When he heard about Jesus, he sent some Jewish elders to him, asking him to come and heal his slave.

luke:7:4.niv: When they came to Jesus, they pleaded earnestly with him, "This man deserves to have you do this,
luke:7:4.csb: When they reached Jesus, they pleaded with him earnestly, saying, “He is worthy for you to grant this,
luke:7:4.nrsv: When they came to Jesus, they appealed to him earnestly, saying, "He is worthy of having you do this for him,

luke:7:5.niv: because he loves our nation and has built our synagogue."
luke:7:5.csb: because he loves our nation and has built us a synagogue.”
luke:7:5.nrsv: for he loves our people, and it is he who built our synagogue for us."

luke:7:6.niv: So Jesus went with them. He was not far from the house when the centurion sent friends to say to him: "Lord, don't trouble yourself, for I do not deserve to have you come under my roof.
luke:7:6.csb: Jesus went with them, and when he was not far from the house, the centurion sent friends to tell him, “Lord, don’t trouble yourself, since I am not worthy to have you come under my roof.
luke:7:6.nrsv: And Jesus went with them, but when he was not far from the house, the centurion sent friends to say to him, "Lord, do not trouble yourself, for I am not worthy to have you come under my roof;

luke:7:7.niv: That is why I did not even consider myself worthy to come to you. But say the word, and my servant will be healed.
luke:7:7.csb: That is why I didn’t even consider myself worthy to come to you. But say the word, and my servant will be healed.
luke:7:7.nrsv: therefore I did not presume to come to you. But only speak the word, and let my servant be healed.

luke:7:8.niv: For I myself am a man under authority, with soldiers under me. I tell this one, 'Go,' and he goes; and that one, 'Come,' and he comes. I say to my servant, 'Do this,' and he does it."
luke:7:8.csb: For I too am a man placed under authority, having soldiers under my command. I say to this one, ‘Go,’ and he goes; and to another, ‘Come,’ and he comes; and to my servant, ‘Do this,’ and he does it.”
luke:7:8.nrsv: For I also am a man set under authority, with soldiers under me; and I say to one, 'Go,' and he goes, and to another, 'Come,' and he comes, and to my slave, 'Do this,' and the slave does it."

luke:7:9.niv: When Jesus heard this, he was amazed at him, and turning to the crowd following him, he said, "I tell you, I have not found such great faith even in Israel."
luke:7:9.csb: Jesus heard this and was amazed at him, and turning to the crowd following him, he said, “I tell you, I have not found so great a faith even in Israel.”
luke:7:9.nrsv: When Jesus heard this he was amazed at him, and turning to the crowd that followed him, he said, "I tell you, not even in Israel have I found such faith."

luke:7:10.niv: Then the men who had been sent returned to the house and found the servant well.
luke:7:10.csb: When those who had been sent returned to the house, they found the servant in good health.
luke:7:10.nrsv: When those who had been sent returned to the house, they found the slave in good health.

luke:7:11.niv: Soon afterward, Jesus went to a town called Nain, and his disciples and a large crowd went along with him.
luke:7:11.csb: Afterward he was on his way to a town called Nain. His disciples and a large crowd were traveling with him.
luke:7:11.nrsv: Soon afterwards he went to a town called Nain, and his disciples and a large crowd went with him.

luke:7:12.niv: As he approached the town gate, a dead person was being carried out--the only son of his mother, and she was a widow. And a large crowd from the town was with her.
luke:7:12.csb: Just as he neared the gate of the town, a dead man was being carried out. He was his mother’s only son, and she was a widow. A large crowd from the city was also with her.
luke:7:12.nrsv: As he approached the gate of the town, a man who had died was being carried out. He was his mother's only son, and she was a widow; and with her was a large crowd from the town.

luke:7:13.niv: When the Lord saw her, his heart went out to her and he said, "Don't cry."
luke:7:13.csb: When the Lord saw her, he had compassion on her and said, “Don’t weep.”
luke:7:13.nrsv: When the Lord saw her, he had compassion for her and said to her, "Do not weep."

luke:7:14.niv: Then he went up and touched the coffin, and those carrying it stood still. He said, "Young man, I say to you, get up!"
luke:7:14.csb: Then he came up and touched the open coffin, and the pallbearers stopped. And he said, “Young man, I tell you, get up!”
luke:7:14.nrsv: Then he came forward and touched the bier, and the bearers stood still. And he said, "Young man, I say to you, rise!"

luke:7:15.niv: The dead man sat up and began to talk, and Jesus gave him back to his mother.
luke:7:15.csb: The dead man sat up and began to speak, and Jesus gave him to his mother.
luke:7:15.nrsv: The dead man sat up and began to speak, and Jesus gave him to his mother.

luke:7:16.niv: They were all filled with awe and praised God. "A great prophet has appeared among us," they said. "God has come to help his people."
luke:7:16.csb: Then fear came over everyone, and they glorified God, saying, “A great prophet has risen among us,” and “God has visited his people.”
luke:7:16.nrsv: Fear seized all of them; and they glorified God, saying, "A great prophet has risen among us!" and "God has looked favorably on his people!"

luke:7:17.niv: This news about Jesus spread throughout Judea and the surrounding country.
luke:7:17.csb: This report about him went throughout Judea and all the vicinity.
luke:7:17.nrsv: This word about him spread throughout Judea and all the surrounding country.

luke:7:18.niv: John's disciples told him about all these things. Calling two of them,
luke:7:18.csb: Then John’s disciples told him about all these things. So John summoned two of his disciples
luke:7:18.nrsv: The disciples of John reported all these things to him. So John summoned two of his disciples

luke:7:19.niv: he sent them to the Lord to ask, "Are you the one who was to come, or should we expect someone else?"
luke:7:19.csb: and sent them to the Lord, asking, “Are you the one who is to come, or should we expect someone else?”
luke:7:19.nrsv: and sent them to the Lord to ask, "Are you the one who is to come, or are we to wait for another?"

luke:7:20.niv: When the men came to Jesus, they said, "John the Baptist sent us to you to ask, 'Are you the one who was to come, or should we expect someone else?'"
luke:7:20.csb: When the men reached him, they said, “John the Baptist sent us to ask you, ‘Are you the one who is to come, or should we expect someone else?’”
luke:7:20.nrsv: When the men had come to him, they said, "John the Baptist has sent us to you to ask, 'Are you the one who is to come, or are we to wait for another?'"

luke:7:21.niv: At that very time Jesus cured many who had diseases, sicknesses and evil spirits, and gave sight to many who were blind.
luke:7:21.csb: At that time Jesus healed many people of diseases, afflictions, and evil spirits, and he granted sight to many blind people.
luke:7:21.nrsv: Jesus had just then cured many people of diseases, plagues, and evil spirits, and had given sight to many who were blind.

luke:7:22.niv: So he replied to the messengers, "Go back and report to John what you have seen and heard: The blind receive sight, the lame walk, those who have leprosy are cured, the deaf hear, the dead are raised, and the good news is preached to the poor.
luke:7:22.csb: He replied to them, “Go and report to John what you have seen and heard: The blind receive their sight, the lame walk, those with leprosy are cleansed, the deaf hear, the dead are raised, and the poor are told the good news,
luke:7:22.nrsv: And he answered them, "Go and tell John what you have seen and heard: the blind receive their sight, the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, the deaf hear, the dead are raised, the poor have good news brought to them.

luke:7:23.niv: Blessed is the man who does not fall away on account of me."
luke:7:23.csb: and blessed is the one who isn’t offended by me.”
luke:7:23.nrsv: And blessed is anyone who takes no offense at me."

luke:7:24.niv: After John's messengers left, Jesus began to speak to the crowd about John: "What did you go out into the desert to see? A reed swayed by the wind?
luke:7:24.csb: After John’s messengers left, he began to speak to the crowds about John: “What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A reed swaying in the wind?
luke:7:24.nrsv: When John's messengers had gone, Jesus began to speak to the crowds about John: "What did you go out into the wilderness to look at? A reed shaken by the wind?

luke:7:25.niv: If not, what did you go out to see? A man dressed in fine clothes? No, those who wear expensive clothes and indulge in luxury are in palaces.
luke:7:25.csb: What then did you go out to see? A man dressed in soft clothes? See, those who are splendidly dressed and live in luxury are in royal palaces.
luke:7:25.nrsv: What then did you go out to see? Someone dressed in soft robes? Look, those who put on fine clothing and live in luxury are in royal palaces.

luke:7:26.niv: But what did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
luke:7:26.csb: What then did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
luke:7:26.nrsv: What then did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.

luke:7:27.niv: This is the one about whom it is written: "'I will send my messenger ahead of you, who will prepare your way before you.'
luke:7:27.csb: This is the one about whom it is written: See, I am sending my messenger ahead of you; he will prepare your way before you.
luke:7:27.nrsv: This is the one about whom it is written, 'See, I am sending my messenger ahead of you, who will prepare your way before you.'

luke:7:28.niv: I tell you, among those born of women there is no one greater than John; yet the one who is least in the kingdom of God is greater than he."
luke:7:28.csb: I tell you, among those born of women no one is greater than John, but the least in the kingdom of God is greater than he.”
luke:7:28.nrsv: I tell you, among those born of women no one is greater than John; yet the least in the kingdom of God is greater than he."

luke:7:29.niv: (All the people, even the tax collectors, when they heard Jesus' words, acknowledged that God's way was right, because they had been baptized by John.
luke:7:29.csb: (And when all the people, including the tax collectors, heard this, they acknowledged God’s way of righteousness, because they had been baptized with John’s baptism.
luke:7:29.nrsv: (And all the people who heard this, including the tax collectors, acknowledged the justice of God, because they had been baptized with John's baptism.

luke:7:30.niv: But the Pharisees and experts in the law rejected God's purpose for themselves, because they had not been baptized by John.)
luke:7:30.csb: But since the Pharisees and experts in the law had not been baptized by him, they rejected the plan of God for themselves. )
luke:7:30.nrsv: But by refusing to be baptized by him, the Pharisees and the lawyers rejected God's purpose for themselves.)

luke:7:31.niv: "To what, then, can I compare the people of this generation? What are they like?
luke:7:31.csb: “To what then should I compare the people of this generation, and what are they like?
luke:7:31.nrsv: "To what then will I compare the people of this generation, and what are they like?

luke:7:32.niv: They are like children sitting in the marketplace and calling out to each other: "'We played the flute for you, and you did not dance; we sang a dirge, and you did not cry.'
luke:7:32.csb: They are like children sitting in the marketplace and calling to each other: We played the flute for you, but you didn’t dance; we sang a lament, but you didn’t weep!
luke:7:32.nrsv: They are like children sitting in the marketplace and calling to one another, 'We played the flute for you, and you did not dance; we wailed, and you did not weep.'

luke:7:33.niv: For John the Baptist came neither eating bread nor drinking wine, and you say, 'He has a demon.'
luke:7:33.csb: For John the Baptist did not come eating bread or drinking wine, and you say, ‘He has a demon!’
luke:7:33.nrsv: For John the Baptist has come eating no bread and drinking no wine, and you say, 'He has a demon';

luke:7:34.niv: The Son of Man came eating and drinking, and you say, 'Here is a glutton and a drunkard, a friend of tax collectors and "sinners."'
luke:7:34.csb: The Son of Man has come eating and drinking, and you say, ‘Look, a glutton and a drunkard, a friend of tax collectors and sinners!’
luke:7:34.nrsv: the Son of Man has come eating and drinking, and you say, 'Look, a glutton and a drunkard, a friend of tax collectors and sinners!'

luke:7:35.niv: But wisdom is proved right by all her children."
luke:7:35.csb: Yet wisdom is vindicated by all her children.”
luke:7:35.nrsv: Nevertheless, wisdom is vindicated by all her children."

luke:7:36.niv: Now one of the Pharisees invited Jesus to have dinner with him, so he went to the Pharisee's house and reclined at the table.
luke:7:36.csb: Then one of the Pharisees invited him to eat with him. He entered the Pharisee’s house and reclined at the table.
luke:7:36.nrsv: One of the Pharisees asked Jesus to eat with him, and he went into the Pharisee's house and took his place at the table.

luke:7:37.niv: When a woman who had lived a sinful life in that town learned that Jesus was eating at the Pharisee's house, she brought an alabaster jar of perfume,
luke:7:37.csb: And a woman in the town who was a sinner found out that Jesus was reclining at the table in the Pharisee’s house. She brought an alabaster jar of perfume
luke:7:37.nrsv: And a woman in the city, who was a sinner, having learned that he was eating in the Pharisee's house, brought an alabaster jar of ointment.

luke:7:38.niv: and as she stood behind him at his feet weeping, she began to wet his feet with her tears. Then she wiped them with her hair, kissed them and poured perfume on them.
luke:7:38.csb: and stood behind him at his feet, weeping, and began to wash his feet with her tears. She wiped his feet with her hair, kissing them and anointing them with the perfume.
luke:7:38.nrsv: She stood behind him at his feet, weeping, and began to bathe his feet with her tears and to dry them with her hair. Then she continued kissing his feet and anointing them with the ointment.

luke:7:39.niv: When the Pharisee who had invited him saw this, he said to himself, "If this man were a prophet, he would know who is touching him and what kind of woman she is--that she is a sinner."
luke:7:39.csb: When the Pharisee who had invited him saw this, he said to himself, “This man, if he were a prophet, would know who and what kind of woman this is who is touching him — she’s a sinner!”
luke:7:39.nrsv: Now when the Pharisee who had invited him saw it, he said to himself, "If this man were a prophet, he would have known who and what kind of woman this is who is touching him-- that she is a sinner."

luke:7:40.niv: Jesus answered him, "Simon, I have something to tell you.Tell me, teacher," he said.
luke:7:40.csb: Jesus replied to him, “Simon, I have something to say to you.” He said, “Say it, teacher.”
luke:7:40.nrsv: Jesus spoke up and said to him, "Simon, I have something to say to you." "Teacher," he replied, "Speak."

luke:7:41.niv: "Two men owed money to a certain moneylender. One owed him five hundred denarii, and the other fifty.
luke:7:41.csb: “A creditor had two debtors. One owed five hundred denarii, and the other fifty.
luke:7:41.nrsv: "A certain creditor had two debtors; one owed five hundred denarii, and the other fifty.

luke:7:42.niv: Neither of them had the money to pay him back, so he canceled the debts of both. Now which of them will love him more?"
luke:7:42.csb: Since they could not pay it back, he graciously forgave them both. So, which of them will love him more?”
luke:7:42.nrsv: When they could not pay, he canceled the debts for both of them. Now which of them will love him more?"

luke:7:43.niv: Simon replied, "I suppose the one who had the bigger debt canceled.You have judged correctly," Jesus said.
luke:7:43.csb: Simon answered, “I suppose the one he forgave more.” “You have judged correctly,” he told him.
luke:7:43.nrsv: Simon answered, "I suppose the one for whom he canceled the greater debt." And Jesus said to him, "You have judged rightly."

luke:7:44.niv: Then he turned toward the woman and said to Simon, "Do you see this woman? I came into your house. You did not give me any water for my feet, but she wet my feet with her tears and wiped them with her hair.
luke:7:44.csb: Turning to the woman, he said to Simon, “Do you see this woman? I entered your house; you gave me no water for my feet, but she, with her tears, has washed my feet and wiped them with her hair.
luke:7:44.nrsv: Then turning toward the woman, he said to Simon, "Do you see this woman? I entered your house; you gave me no water for my feet, but she has bathed my feet with her tears and dried them with her hair.

luke:7:45.niv: You did not give me a kiss, but this woman, from the time I entered, has not stopped kissing my feet.
luke:7:45.csb: You gave me no kiss, but she hasn’t stopped kissing my feet since I came in.
luke:7:45.nrsv: You gave me no kiss, but from the time I came in she has not stopped kissing my feet.

luke:7:46.niv: You did not put oil on my head, but she has poured perfume on my feet.
luke:7:46.csb: You didn’t anoint my head with olive oil, but she has anointed my feet with perfume.
luke:7:46.nrsv: You did not anoint my head with oil, but she has anointed my feet with ointment.

luke:7:47.niv: Therefore, I tell you, her many sins have been forgiven--for she loved much. But he who has been forgiven little loves little."
luke:7:47.csb: Therefore I tell you, her many sins have been forgiven; that’s why she loved much. But the one who is forgiven little, loves little.”
luke:7:47.nrsv: Therefore, I tell you, her sins, which were many, have been forgiven; hence she has shown great love. But the one to whom little is forgiven, loves little."

luke:7:48.niv: Then Jesus said to her, "Your sins are forgiven."
luke:7:48.csb: Then he said to her, “Your sins are forgiven.”
luke:7:48.nrsv: Then he said to her, "Your sins are forgiven."

luke:7:49.niv: The other guests began to say among themselves, "Who is this who even forgives sins?"
luke:7:49.csb: Those who were at the table with him began to say among themselves, “Who is this man who even forgives sins?”
luke:7:49.nrsv: But those who were at the table with him began to say among themselves, "Who is this who even forgives sins?"

luke:7:50.niv: Jesus said to the woman, "Your faith has saved you; go in peace."
luke:7:50.csb: And he said to the woman, “Your faith has saved you. Go in peace.”
luke:7:50.nrsv: And he said to the woman, "Your faith has saved you; go in peace."

luke:8:1.niv: After this, Jesus traveled about from one town and village to another, proclaiming the good news of the kingdom of God. The Twelve were with him,
luke:8:1.csb: Afterward he was traveling from one town and village to another, preaching and telling the good news of the kingdom of God. The Twelve were with him,
luke:8:1.nrsv: Soon afterwards he went on through cities and villages, proclaiming and bringing the good news of the kingdom of God. The twelve were with him,

luke:8:2.niv: and also some women who had been cured of evil spirits and diseases: Mary (called Magdalene) from whom seven demons had come out;
luke:8:2.csb: and also some women who had been healed of evil spirits and sicknesses: Mary, called Magdalene (seven demons had come out of her);
luke:8:2.nrsv: as well as some women who had been cured of evil spirits and infirmities: Mary, called Magdalene, from whom seven demons had gone out,

luke:8:3.niv: Joanna the wife of Cuza, the manager of Herod's household; Susanna; and many others. These women were helping to support them out of their own means.
luke:8:3.csb: Joanna the wife of Chuza, Herod’s steward; Susanna; and many others who were supporting them from their possessions.
luke:8:3.nrsv: and Joanna, the wife of Herod's steward Chuza, and Susanna, and many others, who provided for them out of their resources.

luke:8:4.niv: While a large crowd was gathering and people were coming to Jesus from town after town, he told this parable:
luke:8:4.csb: As a large crowd was gathering, and people were coming to Jesus from every town, he said in a parable:
luke:8:4.nrsv: When a great crowd gathered and people from town after town came to him, he said in a parable:

luke:8:5.niv: "A farmer went out to sow his seed. As he was scattering the seed, some fell along the path; it was trampled on, and the birds of the air ate it up.
luke:8:5.csb: “A sower went out to sow his seed. As he sowed, some seed fell along the path; it was trampled on, and the birds of the sky devoured it.
luke:8:5.nrsv: "A sower went out to sow his seed; and as he sowed, some fell on the path and was trampled on, and the birds of the air ate it up.

luke:8:6.niv: Some fell on rock, and when it came up, the plants withered because they had no moisture.
luke:8:6.csb: Other seed fell on the rock; when it grew up, it withered away, since it lacked moisture.
luke:8:6.nrsv: Some fell on the rock; and as it grew up, it withered for lack of moisture.
