luke:16:29.csb: “But Abraham said, ‘They have Moses and the prophets; they should listen to them.’
luke:16:29.nrsv: Abraham replied, 'They have Moses and the prophets; they should listen to them.'

luke:16:30.niv: "'No, father Abraham,' he said, 'but if someone from the dead goes to them, they will repent.'
luke:16:30.csb: “‘No, father Abraham,’ he said. ‘But if someone from the dead goes to them, they will repent.’
luke:16:30.nrsv: He said, 'No, father Abraham; but if someone goes to them from the dead, they will repent.'

luke:16:31.niv: "He said to him, 'If they do not listen to Moses and the Prophets, they will not be convinced even if someone rises from the dead.'"
luke:16:31.csb: “But he told him, ‘If they don’t listen to Moses and the prophets, they will not be persuaded if someone rises from the dead.’”
luke:16:31.nrsv: He said to him, 'If they do not listen to Moses and the prophets, neither will they be convinced even if someone rises from the dead.'"

luke:17:1.niv: Jesus said to his disciples: "Things that cause people to sin are bound to come, but woe to that person through whom they come.
luke:17:1.csb: He said to his disciples, “Offenses will certainly come, but woe to the one through whom they come!
luke:17:1.nrsv: Jesus said to his disciples, "Occasions for stumbling are bound to come, but woe to anyone by whom they come!

luke:17:2.niv: It would be better for him to be thrown into the sea with a millstone tied around his neck than for him to cause one of these little ones to sin.
luke:17:2.csb: It would be better for him if a millstone were hung around his neck and he were thrown into the sea than for him to cause one of these little ones to stumble.
luke:17:2.nrsv: It would be better for you if a millstone were hung around your neck and you were thrown into the sea than for you to cause one of these little ones to stumble.

luke:17:3.niv: So watch yourselves. "If your brother sins, rebuke him, and if he repents, forgive him.
luke:17:3.csb: Be on your guard. If your brother sins, rebuke him, and if he repents, forgive him.
luke:17:3.nrsv: Be on your guard! If another disciple sins, you must rebuke the offender, and if there is repentance, you must forgive.

luke:17:4.niv: If he sins against you seven times in a day, and seven times comes back to you and says, 'I repent,' forgive him."
luke:17:4.csb: And if he sins against you seven times in a day, and comes back to you seven times, saying, ‘I repent,’ you must forgive him.”
luke:17:4.nrsv: And if the same person sins against you seven times a day, and turns back to you seven times and says, 'I repent,' you must forgive."

luke:17:5.niv: The apostles said to the Lord, "Increase our faith!"
luke:17:5.csb: The apostles said to the Lord, “Increase our faith.”
luke:17:5.nrsv: The apostles said to the Lord, "Increase our faith!"

luke:17:6.niv: He replied, "If you have faith as small as a mustard seed, you can say to this mulberry tree, 'Be uprooted and planted in the sea,' and it will obey you.
luke:17:6.csb: “If you have faith the size of a mustard seed,” the Lord said, “you can say to this mulberry tree, ‘Be uprooted and planted in the sea,’ and it will obey you.
luke:17:6.nrsv: The Lord replied, "If you had faith the size of a mustard seed, you could say to this mulberry tree, 'Be uprooted and planted in the sea,' and it would obey you.

luke:17:7.niv: "Suppose one of you had a servant plowing or looking after the sheep. Would he say to the servant when he comes in from the field, 'Come along now and sit down to eat'?
luke:17:7.csb: “Which one of you having a servant tending sheep or plowing will say to him when he comes in from the field, ‘Come at once and sit down to eat’?
luke:17:7.nrsv: "Who among you would say to your slave who has just come in from plowing or tending sheep in the field, 'Come here at once and take your place at the table'?

luke:17:8.niv: Would he not rather say, 'Prepare my supper, get yourself ready and wait on me while I eat and drink; after that you may eat and drink'?
luke:17:8.csb: Instead, will he not tell him, ‘Prepare something for me to eat, get ready, and serve me while I eat and drink; later you can eat and drink’?
luke:17:8.nrsv: Would you not rather say to him, 'Prepare supper for me, put on your apron and serve me while I eat and drink; later you may eat and drink'?

luke:17:9.niv: Would he thank the servant because he did what he was told to do?
luke:17:9.csb: Does he thank that servant because he did what was commanded?
luke:17:9.nrsv: Do you thank the slave for doing what was commanded?

luke:17:10.niv: So you also, when you have done everything you were told to do, should say, 'We are unworthy servants; we have only done our duty.'"
luke:17:10.csb: In the same way, when you have done all that you were commanded, you should say, ‘We are worthless servants; we’ve only done our duty.’”
luke:17:10.nrsv: So you also, when you have done all that you were ordered to do, say, 'We are worthless slaves; we have done only what we ought to have done!'"

luke:17:11.niv: Now on his way to Jerusalem, Jesus traveled along the border between Samaria and Galilee.
luke:17:11.csb: While traveling to Jerusalem, he passed between Samaria and Galilee.
luke:17:11.nrsv: On the way to Jerusalem Jesus was going through the region between Samaria and Galilee.

luke:17:12.niv: As he was going into a village, ten men who had leprosy met him. They stood at a distance
luke:17:12.csb: As he entered a village, ten men with leprosy met him. They stood at a distance
luke:17:12.nrsv: As he entered a village, ten lepers approached him. Keeping their distance,

luke:17:13.niv: and called out in a loud voice, "Jesus, Master, have pity on us!"
luke:17:13.csb: and raised their voices, saying, “Jesus, Master, have mercy on us!”
luke:17:13.nrsv: they called out, saying, "Jesus, Master, have mercy on us!"

luke:17:14.niv: When he saw them, he said, "Go, show yourselves to the priests." And as they went, they were cleansed.
luke:17:14.csb: When he saw them, he told them, “Go and show yourselves to the priests.” And while they were going, they were cleansed.
luke:17:14.nrsv: When he saw them, he said to them, "Go and show yourselves to the priests." And as they went, they were made clean.

luke:17:15.niv: One of them, when he saw he was healed, came back, praising God in a loud voice.
luke:17:15.csb: But one of them, seeing that he was healed, returned and, with a loud voice, gave glory to God.
luke:17:15.nrsv: Then one of them, when he saw that he was healed, turned back, praising God with a loud voice.

luke:17:16.niv: He threw himself at Jesus' feet and thanked him--and he was a Samaritan.
luke:17:16.csb: He fell facedown at his feet, thanking him. And he was a Samaritan.
luke:17:16.nrsv: He prostrated himself at Jesus' feet and thanked him. And he was a Samaritan.

luke:17:17.niv: Jesus asked, "Were not all ten cleansed? Where are the other nine?
luke:17:17.csb: Then Jesus said, “Were not ten cleansed? Where are the nine?
luke:17:17.nrsv: Then Jesus asked, "Were not ten made clean? But the other nine, where are they?

luke:17:18.niv: Was no one found to return and give praise to God except this foreigner?"
luke:17:18.csb: Didn’t any return to give glory to God except this foreigner?”
luke:17:18.nrsv: Was none of them found to return and give praise to God except this foreigner?"

luke:17:19.niv: Then he said to him, "Rise and go; your faith has made you well."
luke:17:19.csb: And he told him, “Get up and go on your way. Your faith has saved you.”
luke:17:19.nrsv: Then he said to him, "Get up and go on your way; your faith has made you well."

luke:17:20.niv: Once, having been asked by the Pharisees when the kingdom of God would come, Jesus replied, "The kingdom of God does not come with your careful observation,
luke:17:20.csb: Being asked by the Pharisees when the kingdom of God would come, he answered them, “The kingdom of God is not coming with something observable;
luke:17:20.nrsv: Once Jesus was asked by the Pharisees when the kingdom of God was coming, and he answered, "The kingdom of God is not coming with things that can be observed;

luke:17:21.niv: nor will people say, 'Here it is,' or 'There it is,' because the kingdom of God is within you."
luke:17:21.csb: no one will say, ‘See here! ’ or ‘There! ’ For you see, the kingdom of God is in your midst.”
luke:17:21.nrsv: nor will they say, 'Look, here it is!' or 'There it is!' For, in fact, the kingdom of God is among you."

luke:17:22.niv: Then he said to his disciples, "The time is coming when you will long to see one of the days of the Son of Man, but you will not see it.
luke:17:22.csb: Then he told the disciples: “The days are coming when you will long to see one of the days of the Son of Man, but you won’t see it.
luke:17:22.nrsv: Then he said to the disciples, "The days are coming when you will long to see one of the days of the Son of Man, and you will not see it.

luke:17:23.niv: Men will tell you, 'There he is!' or 'Here he is!' Do not go running off after them.
luke:17:23.csb: They will say to you, ‘See there! ’ or ‘See here! ’ Don’t follow or run after them.
luke:17:23.nrsv: They will say to you, 'Look there!' or 'Look here!' Do not go, do not set off in pursuit.

luke:17:24.niv: For the Son of Man in his day will be like the lightning, which flashes and lights up the sky from one end to the other.
luke:17:24.csb: For as the lightning flashes from horizon to horizon and lights up the sky, so the Son of Man will be in his day.
luke:17:24.nrsv: For as the lightning flashes and lights up the sky from one side to the other, so will the Son of Man be in his day.

luke:17:25.niv: But first he must suffer many things and be rejected by this generation.
luke:17:25.csb: But first it is necessary that he suffer many things and be rejected by this generation.
luke:17:25.nrsv: But first he must endure much suffering and be rejected by this generation.

luke:17:26.niv: "Just as it was in the days of Noah, so also will it be in the days of the Son of Man.
luke:17:26.csb: “Just as it was in the days of Noah, so it will be in the days of the Son of Man:
luke:17:26.nrsv: Just as it was in the days of Noah, so too it will be in the days of the Son of Man.

luke:17:27.niv: People were eating, drinking, marrying and being given in marriage up to the day Noah entered the ark. Then the flood came and destroyed them all.
luke:17:27.csb: People went on eating, drinking, marrying and giving in marriage until the day Noah boarded the ark, and the flood came and destroyed them all.
luke:17:27.nrsv: They were eating and drinking, and marrying and being given in marriage, until the day Noah entered the ark, and the flood came and destroyed all of them.

luke:17:28.niv: "It was the same in the days of Lot. People were eating and drinking, buying and selling, planting and building.
luke:17:28.csb: It will be the same as it was in the days of Lot: People went on eating, drinking, buying, selling, planting, building.
luke:17:28.nrsv: Likewise, just as it was in the days of Lot: they were eating and drinking, buying and selling, planting and building,

luke:17:29.niv: But the day Lot left Sodom, fire and sulfur rained down from heaven and destroyed them all.
luke:17:29.csb: But on the day Lot left Sodom, fire and sulfur rained from heaven and destroyed them all.
luke:17:29.nrsv: but on the day that Lot left Sodom, it rained fire and sulfur from heaven and destroyed all of them

luke:17:30.niv: "It will be just like this on the day the Son of Man is revealed.
luke:17:30.csb: It will be like that on the day the Son of Man is revealed.
luke:17:30.nrsv: -- it will be like that on the day that the Son of Man is revealed.

luke:17:31.niv: On that day no one who is on the roof of his house, with his goods inside, should go down to get them. Likewise, no one in the field should go back for anything.
luke:17:31.csb: On that day, a man on the housetop, whose belongings are in the house, must not come down to get them. Likewise the man who is in the field must not turn back.
luke:17:31.nrsv: On that day, anyone on the housetop who has belongings in the house must not come down to take them away; and likewise anyone in the field must not turn back.

luke:17:33.niv: Whoever tries to keep his life will lose it, and whoever loses his life will preserve it.
luke:17:33.csb: Whoever tries to make his life secure will lose it, and whoever loses his life will preserve it.
luke:17:33.nrsv: Those who try to make their life secure will lose it, but those who lose their life will keep it.

luke:17:34.niv: I tell you, on that night two people will be in one bed; one will be taken and the other left.
luke:17:34.csb: I tell you, on that night two will be in one bed; one will be taken and the other will be left.
luke:17:34.nrsv: I tell you, on that night there will be two in one bed; one will be taken and the other left.

luke:17:35.niv: Two women will be grinding grain together; one will be taken and the other left."
luke:17:35.csb: Two women will be grinding grain together; one will be taken and the other left.”
luke:17:35.nrsv: There will be two women grinding meal together; one will be taken and the other left."

luke:17:37.niv: "Where, Lord?" they asked. He replied, "Where there is a dead body, there the vultures will gather."
luke:17:37.csb: “Where, Lord?” they asked him. He said to them, “Where the corpse is, there also the vultures will be gathered.”
luke:17:37.nrsv: Then they asked him, "Where, Lord?" He said to them, "Where the corpse is, there the vultures will gather."

luke:18:1.niv: Then Jesus told his disciples a parable to show them that they should always pray and not give up.
luke:18:1.csb: Now he told them a parable on the need for them to pray always and not give up.
luke:18:1.nrsv: Then Jesus told them a parable about their need to pray always and not to lose heart.

luke:18:2.niv: He said: "In a certain town there was a judge who neither feared God nor cared about men.
luke:18:2.csb: “There was a judge in a certain town who didn’t fear God or respect people.
luke:18:2.nrsv: He said, "In a certain city there was a judge who neither feared God nor had respect for people.

luke:18:3.niv: And there was a widow in that town who kept coming to him with the plea, 'Grant me justice against my adversary.'
luke:18:3.csb: And a widow in that town kept coming to him, saying, ‘Give me justice against my adversary.’
luke:18:3.nrsv: In that city there was a widow who kept coming to him and saying, 'Grant me justice against my opponent.'

luke:18:4.niv: "For some time he refused. But finally he said to himself, 'Even though I don't fear God or care about men,
luke:18:4.csb: “For a while he was unwilling, but later he said to himself, ‘Even though I don’t fear God or respect people,
luke:18:4.nrsv: For a while he refused; but later he said to himself, 'Though I have no fear of God and no respect for anyone,

luke:18:5.niv: yet because this widow keeps bothering me, I will see that she gets justice, so that she won't eventually wear me out with her coming!'"
luke:18:5.csb: yet because this widow keeps pestering me, I will give her justice, so that she doesn’t wear me out by her persistent coming.’”
luke:18:5.nrsv: yet because this widow keeps bothering me, I will grant her justice, so that she may not wear me out by continually coming.'"

luke:18:6.niv: And the Lord said, "Listen to what the unjust judge says.
luke:18:6.csb: Then the Lord said, “Listen to what the unjust judge says.
luke:18:6.nrsv: And the Lord said, "Listen to what the unjust judge says.

luke:18:7.niv: And will not God bring about justice for his chosen ones, who cry out to him day and night? Will he keep putting them off?
luke:18:7.csb: Will not God grant justice to his elect who cry out to him day and night? Will he delay helping them?
luke:18:7.nrsv: And will not God grant justice to his chosen ones who cry to him day and night? Will he delay long in helping them?

luke:18:8.niv: I tell you, he will see that they get justice, and quickly. However, when the Son of Man comes, will he find faith on the earth?"
luke:18:8.csb: I tell you that he will swiftly grant them justice. Nevertheless, when the Son of Man comes, will he find faith on earth?”
luke:18:8.nrsv: I tell you, he will quickly grant justice to them. And yet, when the Son of Man comes, will he find faith on earth?"

luke:18:9.niv: To some who were confident of their own righteousness and looked down on everybody else, Jesus told this parable:
luke:18:9.csb: He also told this parable to some who trusted in themselves that they were righteous and looked down on everyone else:
luke:18:9.nrsv: He also told this parable to some who trusted in themselves that they were righteous and regarded others with contempt:

luke:18:10.niv: "Two men went up to the temple to pray, one a Pharisee and the other a tax collector.
luke:18:10.csb: “Two men went up to the temple to pray, one a Pharisee and the other a tax collector.
luke:18:10.nrsv: "Two men went up to the temple to pray, one a Pharisee and the other a tax collector.

luke:18:11.niv: The Pharisee stood up and prayed about himself: 'God, I thank you that I am not like other men--robbers, evildoers, adulterers--or even like this tax collector.
luke:18:11.csb: The Pharisee was standing and praying like this about himself: ‘God, I thank you that I’m not like other people — greedy, unrighteous, adulterers, or even like this tax collector.
luke:18:11.nrsv: The Pharisee, standing by himself, was praying thus, 'God, I thank you that I am not like other people: thieves, rogues, adulterers, or even like this tax collector.

luke:18:12.niv: I fast twice a week and give a tenth of all I get.'
luke:18:12.csb: I fast twice a week; I give a tenth of everything I get.’
luke:18:12.nrsv: I fast twice a week; I give a tenth of all my income.'

luke:18:13.niv: "But the tax collector stood at a distance. He would not even look up to heaven, but beat his breast and said, 'God, have mercy on me, a sinner.'
luke:18:13.csb: “But the tax collector, standing far off, would not even raise his eyes to heaven but kept striking his chest and saying, ‘God, have mercy on me, a sinner! ’
luke:18:13.nrsv: But the tax collector, standing far off, would not even look up to heaven, but was beating his breast and saying, 'God, be merciful to me, a sinner!'

luke:18:14.niv: "I tell you that this man, rather than the other, went home justified before God. For everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, and he who humbles himself will be exalted."
luke:18:14.csb: I tell you, this one went down to his house justified rather than the other; because everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, but the one who humbles himself will be exalted.”
luke:18:14.nrsv: I tell you, this man went down to his home justified rather than the other; for all who exalt themselves will be humbled, but all who humble themselves will be exalted."

luke:18:15.niv: People were also bringing babies to Jesus to have him touch them. When the disciples saw this, they rebuked them.
luke:18:15.csb: People were bringing infants to him so he might touch them, but when the disciples saw it, they rebuked them.
luke:18:15.nrsv: People were bringing even infants to him that he might touch them; and when the disciples saw it, they sternly ordered them not to do it.

luke:18:16.niv: But Jesus called the children to him and said, "Let the little children come to me, and do not hinder them, for the kingdom of God belongs to such as these.
luke:18:16.csb: Jesus, however, invited them: “Let the little children come to me, and don’t stop them, because the kingdom of God belongs to such as these.
luke:18:16.nrsv: But Jesus called for them and said, "Let the little children come to me, and do not stop them; for it is to such as these that the kingdom of God belongs.

luke:18:17.niv: I tell you the truth, anyone who will not receive the kingdom of God like a little child will never enter it."
luke:18:17.csb: Truly I tell you, whoever does not receive the kingdom of God like a little child will never enter it.”
luke:18:17.nrsv: Truly I tell you, whoever does not receive the kingdom of God as a little child will never enter it."

luke:18:18.niv: A certain ruler asked him, "Good teacher, what must I do to inherit eternal life?"
luke:18:18.csb: A ruler asked him, “Good teacher, what must I do to inherit eternal life?”
luke:18:18.nrsv: A certain ruler asked him, "Good Teacher, what must I do to inherit eternal life?"

luke:18:19.niv: "Why do you call me good?" Jesus answered. "No one is good--except God alone.
luke:18:19.csb: “Why do you call me good?” Jesus asked him. “No one is good except God alone.
luke:18:19.nrsv: Jesus said to him, "Why do you call me good? No one is good but God alone.

luke:18:20.niv: You know the commandments: 'Do not commit adultery, do not murder, do not steal, do not give false testimony, honor your father and mother.'"
luke:18:20.csb: You know the commandments: Do not commit adultery; do not murder; do not steal; do not bear false witness; honor your father and mother. ”
luke:18:20.nrsv: You know the commandments: 'You shall not commit adultery; You shall not murder; You shall not steal; You shall not bear false witness; Honor your father and mother.'"

luke:18:21.niv: "All these I have kept since I was a boy," he said.
luke:18:21.csb: “I have kept all these from my youth,” he said.
luke:18:21.nrsv: He replied, "I have kept all these since my youth."

luke:18:22.niv: When Jesus heard this, he said to him, "You still lack one thing. Sell everything you have and give to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven. Then come, follow me."
luke:18:22.csb: When Jesus heard this, he told him, “You still lack one thing: Sell all you have and distribute it to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven. Then come, follow me.”
luke:18:22.nrsv: When Jesus heard this, he said to him, "There is still one thing lacking. Sell all that you own and distribute the money to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven; then come, follow me."

luke:18:23.niv: When he heard this, he became very sad, because he was a man of great wealth.
luke:18:23.csb: After he heard this, he became extremely sad, because he was very rich.
luke:18:23.nrsv: But when he heard this, he became sad; for he was very rich.

luke:18:24.niv: Jesus looked at him and said, "How hard it is for the rich to enter the kingdom of God!
luke:18:24.csb: Seeing that he became sad, Jesus said, “How hard it is for those who have wealth to enter the kingdom of God!
luke:18:24.nrsv: Jesus looked at him and said, "How hard it is for those who have wealth to enter the kingdom of God!

luke:18:25.niv: Indeed, it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich man to enter the kingdom of God."
luke:18:25.csb: For it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter the kingdom of God.”
luke:18:25.nrsv: Indeed, it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for someone who is rich to enter the kingdom of God."

luke:18:26.niv: Those who heard this asked, "Who then can be saved?"
luke:18:26.csb: Those who heard this asked, “Then who can be saved?”
luke:18:26.nrsv: Those who heard it said, "Then who can be saved?"

luke:18:27.niv: Jesus replied, "What is impossible with men is possible with God."
luke:18:27.csb: He replied, “What is impossible with man is possible with God.”
luke:18:27.nrsv: He replied, "What is impossible for mortals is possible for God."

luke:18:28.niv: Peter said to him, "We have left all we had to follow you!"
luke:18:28.csb: Then Peter said, “Look, we have left what we had and followed you.”
luke:18:28.nrsv: Then Peter said, "Look, we have left our homes and followed you."

luke:18:29.niv: "I tell you the truth," Jesus said to them, "no one who has left home or wife or brothers or parents or children for the sake of the kingdom of God
luke:18:29.csb: So he said to them, “Truly I tell you, there is no one who has left a house, wife or brothers or sisters, parents or children because of the kingdom of God,
luke:18:29.nrsv: And he said to them, "Truly I tell you, there is no one who has left house or wife or brothers or parents or children, for the sake of the kingdom of God,

luke:18:30.niv: will fail to receive many times as much in this age and, in the age to come, eternal life."
luke:18:30.csb: who will not receive many times more at this time, and eternal life in the age to come.”
luke:18:30.nrsv: who will not get back very much more in this age, and in the age to come eternal life."

luke:18:31.niv: Jesus took the Twelve aside and told them, "We are going up to Jerusalem, and everything that is written by the prophets about the Son of Man will be fulfilled.
luke:18:31.csb: Then he took the Twelve aside and told them, “See, we are going up to Jerusalem. Everything that is written through the prophets about the Son of Man will be accomplished.
luke:18:31.nrsv: Then he took the twelve aside and said to them, "See, we are going up to Jerusalem, and everything that is written about the Son of Man by the prophets will be accomplished.

luke:18:32.niv: He will be handed over to the Gentiles. They will mock him, insult him, spit on him, flog him and kill him.
luke:18:32.csb: For he will be handed over to the Gentiles, and he will be mocked, insulted, spit on;
luke:18:32.nrsv: For he will be handed over to the Gentiles; and he will be mocked and insulted and spat upon.

luke:18:33.niv: On the third day he will rise again."
luke:18:33.csb: and after they flog him, they will kill him, and he will rise on the third day.”
luke:18:33.nrsv: After they have flogged him, they will kill him, and on the third day he will rise again."

luke:18:34.niv: The disciples did not understand any of this. Its meaning was hidden from them, and they did not know what he was talking about.
luke:18:34.csb: They understood none of these things. The meaning of the saying was hidden from them, and they did not grasp what was said.
luke:18:34.nrsv: But they understood nothing about all these things; in fact, what he said was hidden from them, and they did not grasp what was said.

luke:18:35.niv: As Jesus approached Jericho, a blind man was sitting by the roadside begging.
luke:18:35.csb: As he approached Jericho, a blind man was sitting by the road begging.
luke:18:35.nrsv: As he approached Jericho, a blind man was sitting by the roadside begging.

luke:18:36.niv: When he heard the crowd going by, he asked what was happening.
luke:18:36.csb: Hearing a crowd passing by, he inquired what was happening.
luke:18:36.nrsv: When he heard a crowd going by, he asked what was happening.

luke:18:37.niv: They told him, "Jesus of Nazareth is passing by."
luke:18:37.csb: “Jesus of Nazareth is passing by,” they told him.
luke:18:37.nrsv: They told him, "Jesus of Nazareth is passing by."

luke:18:38.niv: He called out, "Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me!"
luke:18:38.csb: So he called out, “Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me!”
luke:18:38.nrsv: Then he shouted, "Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me!"

luke:18:39.niv: Those who led the way rebuked him and told him to be quiet, but he shouted all the more, "Son of David, have mercy on me!"
luke:18:39.csb: Then those in front told him to keep quiet, but he kept crying out all the more, “Son of David, have mercy on me!”
luke:18:39.nrsv: Those who were in front sternly ordered him to be quiet; but he shouted even more loudly, "Son of David, have mercy on me!"

luke:18:40.niv: Jesus stopped and ordered the man to be brought to him. When he came near, Jesus asked him,
luke:18:40.csb: Jesus stopped and commanded that he be brought to him. When he came closer, he asked him,
luke:18:40.nrsv: Jesus stood still and ordered the man to be brought to him; and when he came near, he asked him,

luke:18:41.niv: "What do you want me to do for you?Lord, I want to see," he replied.
luke:18:41.csb: “What do you want me to do for you?” “Lord,” he said, “I want to see.”
luke:18:41.nrsv: "What do you want me to do for you?" He said, "Lord, let me see again."

luke:18:42.niv: Jesus said to him, "Receive your sight; your faith has healed you."
luke:18:42.csb: “Receive your sight.” Jesus told him. “Your faith has saved you.”
luke:18:42.nrsv: Jesus said to him, "Receive your sight; your faith has saved you."

luke:18:43.niv: Immediately he received his sight and followed Jesus, praising God. When all the people saw it, they also praised God.
luke:18:43.csb: Instantly he could see, and he began to follow him, glorifying God. All the people, when they saw it, gave praise to God.
luke:18:43.nrsv: Immediately he regained his sight and followed him, glorifying God; and all the people, when they saw it, praised God.

luke:19:1.niv: Jesus entered Jericho and was passing through.
luke:19:1.csb: He entered Jericho and was passing through.
luke:19:1.nrsv: He entered Jericho and was passing through it.

luke:19:2.niv: A man was there by the name of Zacchaeus; he was a chief tax collector and was wealthy.
luke:19:2.csb: There was a man named Zacchaeus who was a chief tax collector, and he was rich.
luke:19:2.nrsv: A man was there named Zacchaeus; he was a chief tax collector and was rich.

luke:19:3.niv: He wanted to see who Jesus was, but being a short man he could not, because of the crowd.
luke:19:3.csb: He was trying to see who Jesus was, but he was not able because of the crowd, since he was a short man.
luke:19:3.nrsv: He was trying to see who Jesus was, but on account of the crowd he could not, because he was short in stature.

luke:19:4.niv: So he ran ahead and climbed a sycamore-fig tree to see him, since Jesus was coming that way.
luke:19:4.csb: So running ahead, he climbed up a sycamore tree to see Jesus, since he was about to pass that way.
luke:19:4.nrsv: So he ran ahead and climbed a sycamore tree to see him, because he was going to pass that way.

luke:19:5.niv: When Jesus reached the spot, he looked up and said to him, "Zacchaeus, come down immediately. I must stay at your house today."
luke:19:5.csb: When Jesus came to the place, he looked up and said to him, “Zacchaeus, hurry and come down because today it is necessary for me to stay at your house.”
luke:19:5.nrsv: When Jesus came to the place, he looked up and said to him, "Zacchaeus, hurry and come down; for I must stay at your house today."

luke:19:6.niv: So he came down at once and welcomed him gladly.
luke:19:6.csb: So he quickly came down and welcomed him joyfully.
luke:19:6.nrsv: So he hurried down and was happy to welcome him.

luke:19:7.niv: All the people saw this and began to mutter, "He has gone to be the guest of a 'sinner.'"
luke:19:7.csb: All who saw it began to complain, “He’s gone to stay with a sinful man.”
luke:19:7.nrsv: All who saw it began to grumble and said, "He has gone to be the guest of one who is a sinner."

luke:19:8.niv: But Zacchaeus stood up and said to the Lord, "Look, Lord! Here and now I give half of my possessions to the poor, and if I have cheated anybody out of anything, I will pay back four times the amount."
luke:19:8.csb: But Zacchaeus stood there and said to the Lord, “Look, I’ll give half of my possessions to the poor, Lord. And if I have extorted anything from anyone, I’ll pay back four times as much.”
luke:19:8.nrsv: Zacchaeus stood there and said to the Lord, "Look, half of my possessions, Lord, I will give to the poor; and if I have defrauded anyone of anything, I will pay back four times as much."

luke:19:9.niv: Jesus said to him, "Today salvation has come to this house, because this man, too, is a son of Abraham.
luke:19:9.csb: “Today salvation has come to this house,” Jesus told him, “because he too is a son of Abraham.
luke:19:9.nrsv: Then Jesus said to him, "Today salvation has come to this house, because he too is a son of Abraham.

luke:19:10.niv: For the Son of Man came to seek and to save what was lost."
luke:19:10.csb: For the Son of Man has come to seek and to save the lost.”
luke:19:10.nrsv: For the Son of Man came to seek out and to save the lost."

luke:19:11.niv: While they were listening to this, he went on to tell them a parable, because he was near Jerusalem and the people thought that the kingdom of God was going to appear at once.
luke:19:11.csb: As they were listening to this, he went on to tell a parable because he was near Jerusalem, and they thought the kingdom of God was going to appear right away.
luke:19:11.nrsv: As they were listening to this, he went on to tell a parable, because he was near Jerusalem, and because they supposed that the kingdom of God was to appear immediately.

luke:19:12.niv: He said: "A man of noble birth went to a distant country to have himself appointed king and then to return.
luke:19:12.csb: Therefore he said: “A nobleman traveled to a far country to receive for himself authority to be king and then to return.
luke:19:12.nrsv: So he said, "A nobleman went to a distant country to get royal power for himself and then return.

luke:19:13.niv: So he called ten of his servants and gave them ten minas. 'Put this money to work,' he said, 'until I come back.'
luke:19:13.csb: He called ten of his servants, gave them ten minas, and told them, ‘Engage in business until I come back.’
luke:19:13.nrsv: He summoned ten of his slaves, and gave them ten pounds, and said to them, 'Do business with these until I come back.'

luke:19:14.niv: "But his subjects hated him and sent a delegation after him to say, 'We don't want this man to be our king.'
luke:19:14.csb: “But his subjects hated him and sent a delegation after him, saying, ‘We don’t want this man to rule over us.’
luke:19:14.nrsv: But the citizens of his country hated him and sent a delegation after him, saying, 'We do not want this man to rule over us.'

luke:19:15.niv: "He was made king, however, and returned home. Then he sent for the servants to whom he had given the money, in order to find out what they had gained with it.
luke:19:15.csb: “At his return, having received the authority to be king, he summoned those servants he had given the money to, so that he could find out how much they had made in business.
luke:19:15.nrsv: When he returned, having received royal power, he ordered these slaves, to whom he had given the money, to be summoned so that he might find out what they had gained by trading.

luke:19:16.niv: "The first one came and said, 'Sir, your mina has earned ten more.'
luke:19:16.csb: The first came forward and said, ‘Master, your mina has earned ten more minas.’
luke:19:16.nrsv: The first came forward and said, 'Lord, your pound has made ten more pounds.'

luke:19:17.niv: "'Well done, my good servant!' his master replied. 'Because you have been trustworthy in a very small matter, take charge of ten cities.'
luke:19:17.csb: “‘Well done, good servant! ’ he told him. ‘Because you have been faithful in a very small matter, have authority over ten towns.’
luke:19:17.nrsv: He said to him, 'Well done, good slave! Because you have been trustworthy in a very small thing, take charge of ten cities.'

luke:19:18.niv: "The second came and said, 'Sir, your mina has earned five more.'
luke:19:18.csb: “The second came and said, ‘Master, your mina has made five minas.’
luke:19:18.nrsv: Then the second came, saying, 'Lord, your pound has made five pounds.'

luke:19:19.niv: "His master answered, 'You take charge of five cities.'
luke:19:19.csb: “So he said to him, ‘You will be over five towns.’
luke:19:19.nrsv: He said to him, 'And you, rule over five cities.'

luke:19:20.niv: "Then another servant came and said, 'Sir, here is your mina; I have kept it laid away in a piece of cloth.
luke:19:20.csb: “And another came and said, ‘Master, here is your mina. I have kept it safe in a cloth
luke:19:20.nrsv: Then the other came, saying, 'Lord, here is your pound. I wrapped it up in a piece of cloth,

luke:19:21.niv: I was afraid of you, because you are a hard man. You take out what you did not put in and reap what you did not sow.'
luke:19:21.csb: because I was afraid of you since you’re a harsh man: you collect what you didn’t deposit and reap what you didn’t sow.’
luke:19:21.nrsv: for I was afraid of you, because you are a harsh man; you take what you did not deposit, and reap what you did not sow.'

luke:19:22.niv: "His master replied, 'I will judge you by your own words, you wicked servant! You knew, did you, that I am a hard man, taking out what I did not put in, and reaping what I did not sow?
luke:19:22.csb: “He told him, ‘I will condemn you by what you have said, you evil servant! If you knew I was a harsh man, collecting what I didn’t deposit and reaping what I didn’t sow,
luke:19:22.nrsv: He said to him, 'I will judge you by your own words, you wicked slave! You knew, did you, that I was a harsh man, taking what I did not deposit and reaping what I did not sow?

luke:19:23.niv: Why then didn't you put my money on deposit, so that when I came back, I could have collected it with interest?'
luke:19:23.csb: why, then, didn’t you put my money in the bank? And when I returned, I would have collected it with interest.’
luke:19:23.nrsv: Why then did you not put my money into the bank? Then when I returned, I could have collected it with interest.'

luke:19:24.niv: "Then he said to those standing by, 'Take his mina away from him and give it to the one who has ten minas.'
luke:19:24.csb: So he said to those standing there, ‘Take the mina away from him and give it to the one who has ten minas.’
luke:19:24.nrsv: He said to the bystanders, 'Take the pound from him and give it to the one who has ten pounds.'

luke:19:25.csb: “But they said to him, ‘Master, he has ten minas.’
luke:19:25.nrsv: (And they said to him, 'Lord, he has ten pounds!')

luke:19:26.niv: "He replied, 'I tell you that to everyone who has, more will be given, but as for the one who has nothing, even what he has will be taken away.
luke:19:26.csb: “‘I tell you, that to everyone who has, more will be given; and from the one who does not have, even what he does have will be taken away.
luke:19:26.nrsv: 'I tell you, to all those who have, more will be given; but from those who have nothing, even what they have will be taken away.

luke:19:27.niv: But those enemies of mine who did not want me to be king over them--bring them here and kill them in front of me.'"
luke:19:27.csb: But bring here these enemies of mine, who did not want me to rule over them, and slaughter them in my presence.’”
luke:19:27.nrsv: But as for these enemies of mine who did not want me to be king over them-- bring them here and slaughter them in my presence.'"

luke:19:28.niv: After Jesus had said this, he went on ahead, going up to Jerusalem.
luke:19:28.csb: When he had said these things, he went on ahead, going up to Jerusalem.
luke:19:28.nrsv: After he had said this, he went on ahead, going up to Jerusalem.

luke:19:29.niv: As he approached Bethphage and Bethany at the hill called the Mount of Olives, he sent two of his disciples, saying to them,
luke:19:29.csb: As he approached Bethphage and Bethany, at the place called the Mount of Olives, he sent two of the disciples
luke:19:29.nrsv: When he had come near Bethphage and Bethany, at the place called the Mount of Olives, he sent two of the disciples,

luke:19:30.niv: "Go to the village ahead of you, and as you enter it, you will find a colt tied there, which no one has ever ridden. Untie it and bring it here.
luke:19:30.csb: and said, “Go into the village ahead of you. As you enter it, you will find a young donkey tied there, on which no one has ever sat. Untie it and bring it.
luke:19:30.nrsv: saying, "Go into the village ahead of you, and as you enter it you will find tied there a colt that has never been ridden. Untie it and bring it here.

luke:19:31.niv: If anyone asks you, 'Why are you untying it?' tell him, 'The Lord needs it.'"
luke:19:31.csb: If anyone asks you, ‘Why are you untying it? ’ say this: ‘The Lord needs it.’”
luke:19:31.nrsv: If anyone asks you, 'Why are you untying it?' just say this, 'The Lord needs it.'"

luke:19:32.niv: Those who were sent ahead went and found it just as he had told them.
luke:19:32.csb: So those who were sent left and found it just as he had told them.
luke:19:32.nrsv: So those who were sent departed and found it as he had told them.

luke:19:33.niv: As they were untying the colt, its owners asked them, "Why are you untying the colt?"
luke:19:33.csb: As they were untying the young donkey, its owners said to them, “Why are you untying the donkey?”
luke:19:33.nrsv: As they were untying the colt, its owners asked them, "Why are you untying the colt?"

luke:19:35.niv: They brought it to Jesus, threw their cloaks on the colt and put Jesus on it.
luke:19:35.csb: Then they brought it to Jesus, and after throwing their clothes on the donkey, they helped Jesus get on it.
luke:19:35.nrsv: Then they brought it to Jesus; and after throwing their cloaks on the colt, they set Jesus on it.

luke:19:36.niv: As he went along, people spread their cloaks on the road.
luke:19:36.csb: As he was going along, they were spreading their clothes on the road.
luke:19:36.nrsv: As he rode along, people kept spreading their cloaks on the road.

luke:19:37.niv: When he came near the place where the road goes down the Mount of Olives, the whole crowd of disciples began joyfully to praise God in loud voices for all the miracles they had seen:
luke:19:37.csb: Now he came near the path down the Mount of Olives, and the whole crowd of the disciples began to praise God joyfully with a loud voice for all the miracles they had seen:
luke:19:37.nrsv: As he was now approaching the path down from the Mount of Olives, the whole multitude of the disciples began to praise God joyfully with a loud voice for all the deeds of power that they had seen,

luke:19:38.niv: "Blessed is the king who comes in the name of the Lord!Peace in heaven and glory in the highest!"
luke:19:38.csb: Blessed is the King who comes in the name of the Lord. Peace in heaven and glory in the highest heaven!
luke:19:38.nrsv: saying, "Blessed is the king who comes in the name of the Lord! Peace in heaven, and glory in the highest heaven!"

luke:19:39.niv: Some of the Pharisees in the crowd said to Jesus, "Teacher, rebuke your disciples!"
luke:19:39.csb: Some of the Pharisees from the crowd told him, “Teacher, rebuke your disciples.”
luke:19:39.nrsv: Some of the Pharisees in the crowd said to him, "Teacher, order your disciples to stop."

luke:19:40.niv: "I tell you," he replied, "if they keep quiet, the stones will cry out."
luke:19:40.csb: He answered, “I tell you, if they were to keep silent, the stones would cry out.”
luke:19:40.nrsv: He answered, "I tell you, if these were silent, the stones would shout out."

luke:19:41.niv: As he approached Jerusalem and saw the city, he wept over it
luke:19:41.csb: As he approached and saw the city, he wept for it,
luke:19:41.nrsv: As he came near and saw the city, he wept over it,

luke:19:42.niv: and said, "If you, even you, had only known on this day what would bring you peace--but now it is hidden from your eyes.
luke:19:42.csb: saying, “If you knew this day what would bring peace — but now it is hidden from your eyes.
luke:19:42.nrsv: saying, "If you, even you, had only recognized on this day the things that make for peace! But now they are hidden from your eyes.

luke:19:43.niv: The days will come upon you when your enemies will build an embankment against you and encircle you and hem you in on every side.
luke:19:43.csb: For the days will come on you when your enemies will build a barricade around you, surround you, and hem you in on every side.
luke:19:43.nrsv: Indeed, the days will come upon you, when your enemies will set up ramparts around you and surround you, and hem you in on every side.

luke:19:44.niv: They will dash you to the ground, you and the children within your walls. They will not leave one stone on another, because you did not recognize the time of God's coming to you."
luke:19:44.csb: They will crush you and your children among you to the ground, and they will not leave one stone on another in your midst, because you did not recognize the time when God visited you.”
luke:19:44.nrsv: They will crush you to the ground, you and your children within you, and they will not leave within you one stone upon another; because you did not recognize the time of your visitation from God."

luke:19:45.niv: Then he entered the temple area and began driving out those who were selling.
luke:19:45.csb: He went into the temple and began to throw out those who were selling,
luke:19:45.nrsv: Then he entered the temple and began to drive out those who were selling things there;

luke:19:46.niv: "It is written," he said to them, "'My house will be a house of prayer'; but you have made it 'a den of robbers.'"
luke:19:46.csb: and he said, “It is written, my house will be a house of prayer, but you have made it a den of thieves!”
luke:19:46.nrsv: and he said, "It is written, 'My house shall be a house of prayer'; but you have made it a den of robbers."

luke:19:47.niv: Every day he was teaching at the temple. But the chief priests, the teachers of the law and the leaders among the people were trying to kill him.
luke:19:47.csb: Every day he was teaching in the temple. The chief priests, the scribes, and the leaders of the people were looking for a way to kill him,
luke:19:47.nrsv: Every day he was teaching in the temple. The chief priests, the scribes, and the leaders of the people kept looking for a way to kill him;

luke:19:48.niv: Yet they could not find any way to do it, because all the people hung on his words.
luke:19:48.csb: but they could not find a way to do it, because all the people were captivated by what they heard.
luke:19:48.nrsv: but they did not find anything they could do, for all the people were spellbound by what they heard.

luke:20:1.niv: One day as he was teaching the people in the temple courts and preaching the gospel, the chief priests and the teachers of the law, together with the elders, came up to him.
luke:20:1.csb: One day as he was teaching the people in the temple and proclaiming the good news, the chief priests and the scribes, with the elders, came
luke:20:1.nrsv: One day, as he was teaching the people in the temple and telling the good news, the chief priests and the scribes came with the elders

luke:20:2.niv: "Tell us by what authority you are doing these things," they said. "Who gave you this authority?"
luke:20:2.csb: and said to him: “Tell us, by what authority are you doing these things? Who is it who gave you this authority?”
luke:20:2.nrsv: and said to him, "Tell us, by what authority are you doing these things? Who is it who gave you this authority?"

luke:20:3.niv: He replied, "I will also ask you a question. Tell me,
luke:20:3.csb: He answered them, “I will also ask you a question. Tell me,
luke:20:3.nrsv: He answered them, "I will also ask you a question, and you tell me:

luke:20:4.niv: John's baptism--was it from heaven, or from men?"
luke:20:4.csb: was the baptism of John from heaven or of human origin?”
luke:20:4.nrsv: Did the baptism of John come from heaven, or was it of human origin?"

luke:20:5.niv: They discussed it among themselves and said, "If we say, 'From heaven,' he will ask, 'Why didn't you believe him?'
luke:20:5.csb: They discussed it among themselves: “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ he will say, ‘Why didn’t you believe him?’
luke:20:5.nrsv: They discussed it with one another, saying, "If we say, 'From heaven,' he will say, 'Why did you not believe him?'

luke:20:6.niv: But if we say, 'From men,' all the people will stone us, because they are persuaded that John was a prophet."
luke:20:6.csb: But if we say, ‘Of human origin,’ all the people will stone us, because they are convinced that John was a prophet.”
luke:20:6.nrsv: But if we say, 'Of human origin,' all the people will stone us; for they are convinced that John was a prophet."

luke:20:7.niv: So they answered, "We don't know where it was from."
luke:20:7.csb: So they answered that they did not know its origin.
luke:20:7.nrsv: So they answered that they did not know where it came from.

luke:20:8.niv: Jesus said, "Neither will I tell you by what authority I am doing these things."
luke:20:8.csb: And Jesus said to them, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I do these things.”
luke:20:8.nrsv: Then Jesus said to them, "Neither will I tell you by what authority I am doing these things."

luke:20:9.niv: He went on to tell the people this parable: "A man planted a vineyard, rented it to some farmers and went away for a long time.
luke:20:9.csb: Now he began to tell the people this parable: “A man planted a vineyard, leased it to tenant farmers, and went away for a long time.
luke:20:9.nrsv: He began to tell the people this parable: "A man planted a vineyard, and leased it to tenants, and went to another country for a long time.

luke:20:10.niv: At harvest time he sent a servant to the tenants so they would give him some of the fruit of the vineyard. But the tenants beat him and sent him away empty-handed.
luke:20:10.csb: At harvest time he sent a servant to the farmers so that they might give him some fruit from the vineyard. But the farmers beat him and sent him away empty-handed.
luke:20:10.nrsv: When the season came, he sent a slave to the tenants in order that they might give him his share of the produce of the vineyard; but the tenants beat him and sent him away empty-handed.

luke:20:11.niv: He sent another servant, but that one also they beat and treated shamefully and sent away empty-handed.
luke:20:11.csb: He sent yet another servant, but they beat that one too, treated him shamefully, and sent him away empty-handed.
luke:20:11.nrsv: Next he sent another slave; that one also they beat and insulted and sent away empty-handed.

luke:20:12.niv: He sent still a third, and they wounded him and threw him out.
luke:20:12.csb: And he sent yet a third, but they wounded this one too and threw him out.
luke:20:12.nrsv: And he sent still a third; this one also they wounded and threw out.

luke:20:13.niv: "Then the owner of the vineyard said, 'What shall I do? I will send my son, whom I love; perhaps they will respect him.'
luke:20:13.csb: “Then the owner of the vineyard said, ‘What should I do? I will send my beloved son. Perhaps they will respect him.’
luke:20:13.nrsv: Then the owner of the vineyard said, 'What shall I do? I will send my beloved son; perhaps they will respect him.'

luke:20:14.niv: "But when the tenants saw him, they talked the matter over. 'This is the heir,' they said. 'Let's kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.'
luke:20:14.csb: “But when the tenant farmers saw him, they discussed it among themselves and said, ‘This is the heir. Let’s kill him, so that the inheritance will be ours.’
luke:20:14.nrsv: But when the tenants saw him, they discussed it among themselves and said, 'This is the heir; let us kill him so that the inheritance may be ours.'

luke:20:15.niv: So they threw him out of the vineyard and killed him.
luke:20:15.csb: So they threw him out of the vineyard and killed him. “What then will the owner of the vineyard do to them?
luke:20:15.nrsv: So they threw him out of the vineyard and killed him. What then will the owner of the vineyard do to them?

luke:20:16.niv: "What then will the owner of the vineyard do to them? He will come and kill those tenants and give the vineyard to others." When the people heard this, they said, "May this never be!"
luke:20:16.csb: He will come and kill those farmers and give the vineyard to others.” But when they heard this they said, “That must never happen!”
luke:20:16.nrsv: He will come and destroy those tenants and give the vineyard to others." When they heard this, they said, "Heaven forbid!"

luke:20:17.niv: Jesus looked directly at them and asked, "Then what is the meaning of that which is written: "'The stone the builders rejected has become the capstone '?
luke:20:17.csb: But he looked at them and said, “Then what is the meaning of this Scripture: The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone?
luke:20:17.nrsv: But he looked at them and said, "What then does this text mean: 'The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone'?

luke:20:18.niv: Everyone who falls on that stone will be broken to pieces, but he on whom it falls will be crushed."
luke:20:18.csb: Everyone who falls on that stone will be broken to pieces, but on whomever it falls, it will shatter him.”
luke:20:18.nrsv: Everyone who falls on that stone will be broken to pieces; and it will crush anyone on whom it falls."

luke:20:19.niv: The teachers of the law and the chief priests looked for a way to arrest him immediately, because they knew he had spoken this parable against them. But they were afraid of the people.
luke:20:19.csb: Then the scribes and the chief priests looked for a way to get their hands on him that very hour, because they knew he had told this parable against them, but they feared the people.
luke:20:19.nrsv: When the scribes and chief priests realized that he had told this parable against them, they wanted to lay hands on him at that very hour, but they feared the people.

luke:20:20.niv: Keeping a close watch on him, they sent spies, who pretended to be honest. They hoped to catch Jesus in something he said so that they might hand him over to the power and authority of the governor.
luke:20:20.csb: They watched closely and sent spies who pretended to be righteous, so that they could catch him in what he said, to hand him over to the governor’s rule and authority.
luke:20:20.nrsv: So they watched him and sent spies who pretended to be honest, in order to trap him by what he said, so as to hand him over to the jurisdiction and authority of the governor.

luke:20:21.niv: So the spies questioned him: "Teacher, we know that you speak and teach what is right, and that you do not show partiality but teach the way of God in accordance with the truth.
luke:20:21.csb: They questioned him, “Teacher, we know that you speak and teach correctly, and you don’t show partiality but teach truthfully the way of God.
luke:20:21.nrsv: So they asked him, "Teacher, we know that you are right in what you say and teach, and you show deference to no one, but teach the way of God in accordance with truth.

luke:20:22.niv: Is it right for us to pay taxes to Caesar or not?"
luke:20:22.csb: Is it lawful for us to pay taxes to Caesar or not?”
luke:20:22.nrsv: Is it lawful for us to pay taxes to the emperor, or not?"

luke:20:23.niv: He saw through their duplicity and said to them,
luke:20:23.csb: But detecting their craftiness, he said to them,
luke:20:23.nrsv: But he perceived their craftiness and said to them,

luke:20:24.niv: "Show me a denarius. Whose portrait and inscription are on it?"
luke:20:24.csb: “Show me a denarius. Whose image and inscription does it have?” “Caesar’s,” they said.
luke:20:24.nrsv: "Show me a denarius. Whose head and whose title does it bear?" They said, "The emperor's."

luke:20:25.niv: "Caesar's," they replied. He said to them, "Then give to Caesar what is Caesar's, and to God what is God's."
luke:20:25.csb: “Well then,” he told them, “give to Caesar the things that are Caesar’s, and to God the things that are God’s.”
luke:20:25.nrsv: He said to them, "Then give to the emperor the things that are the emperor's, and to God the things that are God's."

luke:20:26.niv: They were unable to trap him in what he had said there in public. And astonished by his answer, they became silent.
luke:20:26.csb: They were not able to catch him in what he said in public, and being amazed at his answer, they became silent.
luke:20:26.nrsv: And they were not able in the presence of the people to trap him by what he said; and being amazed by his answer, they became silent.

luke:20:27.niv: Some of the Sadducees, who say there is no resurrection, came to Jesus with a question.
luke:20:27.csb: Some of the Sadducees, who say there is no resurrection, came up and questioned him:
luke:20:27.nrsv: Some Sadducees, those who say there is no resurrection, came to him

luke:20:28.niv: "Teacher," they said, "Moses wrote for us that if a man's brother dies and leaves a wife but no children, the man must marry the widow and have children for his brother.
luke:20:28.csb: “Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a man’s brother has a wife, and dies childless, his brother should take the wife and produce offspring for his brother.
luke:20:28.nrsv: and asked him a question, "Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a man's brother dies, leaving a wife but no children, the man shall marry the widow and raise up children for his brother.

luke:20:29.niv: Now there were seven brothers. The first one married a woman and died childless.
luke:20:29.csb: Now there were seven brothers. The first took a wife and died without children.
luke:20:29.nrsv: Now there were seven brothers; the first married, and died childless;

luke:20:31.niv: and then the third married her, and in the same way the seven died, leaving no children.
luke:20:31.csb: and the third took her. In the same way, all seven died and left no children.
luke:20:31.nrsv: and the third married her, and so in the same way all seven died childless.

luke:20:33.niv: Now then, at the resurrection whose wife will she be, since the seven were married to her?"
luke:20:33.csb: In the resurrection, therefore, whose wife will the woman be? For all seven had married her.”
luke:20:33.nrsv: In the resurrection, therefore, whose wife will the woman be? For the seven had married her."

luke:20:34.niv: Jesus replied, "The people of this age marry and are given in marriage.
luke:20:34.csb: Jesus told them, “The children of this age marry and are given in marriage.
luke:20:34.nrsv: Jesus said to them, "Those who belong to this age marry and are given in marriage;

luke:20:35.niv: But those who are considered worthy of taking part in that age and in the resurrection from the dead will neither marry nor be given in marriage,
luke:20:35.csb: But those who are counted worthy to take part in that age and in the resurrection from the dead neither marry nor are given in marriage.
luke:20:35.nrsv: but those who are considered worthy of a place in that age and in the resurrection from the dead neither marry nor are given in marriage.

luke:20:36.niv: and they can no longer die; for they are like the angels. They are God's children, since they are children of the resurrection.
luke:20:36.csb: For they can no longer die, because they are like angels and are children of God, since they are children of the resurrection.
luke:20:36.nrsv: Indeed they cannot die anymore, because they are like angels and are children of God, being children of the resurrection.

luke:20:37.niv: But in the account of the bush, even Moses showed that the dead rise, for he calls the Lord 'the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.'
luke:20:37.csb: Moses even indicated in the passage about the burning bush that the dead are raised, where he calls the Lord the God of Abraham and the God of Isaac and the God of Jacob.
luke:20:37.nrsv: And the fact that the dead are raised Moses himself showed, in the story about the bush, where he speaks of the Lord as the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.

luke:20:38.niv: He is not the God of the dead, but of the living, for to him all are alive."
luke:20:38.csb: He is not the God of the dead but of the living, because all are living to him.”
luke:20:38.nrsv: Now he is God not of the dead, but of the living; for to him all of them are alive."

luke:20:39.niv: Some of the teachers of the law responded, "Well said, teacher!"
luke:20:39.csb: Some of the scribes answered, “Teacher, you have spoken well.”
luke:20:39.nrsv: Then some of the scribes answered, "Teacher, you have spoken well."

luke:20:40.niv: And no one dared to ask him any more questions.
luke:20:40.csb: And they no longer dared to ask him anything.
luke:20:40.nrsv: For they no longer dared to ask him another question.

luke:20:41.niv: Then Jesus said to them, "How is it that they say the Christ is the Son of David?
luke:20:41.csb: Then he said to them, “How can they say that the Christ is the son of David?
luke:20:41.nrsv: Then he said to them, "How can they say that the Messiah is David's son?

luke:20:42.niv: David himself declares in the Book of Psalms: "'The Lord said to my Lord: "Sit at my right hand
luke:20:42.csb: For David himself says in the Book of Psalms: The Lord declared to my Lord, ‘Sit at my right hand
luke:20:42.nrsv: For David himself says in the book of Psalms, 'The Lord said to my Lord, "Sit at my right hand,

luke:20:43.niv: until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet."'
luke:20:43.csb: until I make your enemies your footstool.’
luke:20:43.nrsv: until I make your enemies your footstool."'

luke:20:44.niv: David calls him 'Lord.' How then can he be his son?"
luke:20:44.csb: David calls him ‘Lord’ how then can the Christ be his son?”
luke:20:44.nrsv: David thus calls him Lord; so how can he be his son?"

luke:20:45.niv: While all the people were listening, Jesus said to his disciples,
luke:20:45.csb: While all the people were listening, he said to his disciples,
luke:20:45.nrsv: In the hearing of all the people he said to the disciples,

luke:20:46.niv: "Beware of the teachers of the law. They like to walk around in flowing robes and love to be greeted in the marketplaces and have the most important seats in the synagogues and the places of honor at banquets.
luke:20:46.csb: “Beware of the scribes, who want to go around in long robes and who love greetings in the marketplaces, the best seats in the synagogues, and the places of honor at banquets.
luke:20:46.nrsv: "Beware of the scribes, who like to walk around in long robes, and love to be greeted with respect in the marketplaces, and to have the best seats in the synagogues and places of honor at banquets.

luke:20:47.niv: They devour widows' houses and for a show make lengthy prayers. Such men will be punished most severely."
luke:20:47.csb: They devour widows’ houses and say long prayers just for show. These will receive harsher judgment.”
luke:20:47.nrsv: They devour widows' houses and for the sake of appearance say long prayers. They will receive the greater condemnation."

luke:21:1.niv: As he looked up, Jesus saw the rich putting their gifts into the temple treasury.
luke:21:1.csb: He looked up and saw the rich dropping their offerings into the temple treasury.
luke:21:1.nrsv: He looked up and saw rich people putting their gifts into the treasury;

luke:21:2.niv: He also saw a poor widow put in two very small copper coins.
luke:21:2.csb: He also saw a poor widow dropping in two tiny coins.
luke:21:2.nrsv: he also saw a poor widow put in two small copper coins.

luke:21:3.niv: "I tell you the truth," he said, "this poor widow has put in more than all the others.
luke:21:3.csb: “Truly I tell you,” he said. “This poor widow has put in more than all of them.
luke:21:3.nrsv: He said, "Truly I tell you, this poor widow has put in more than all of them;

luke:21:4.niv: All these people gave their gifts out of their wealth; but she out of her poverty put in all she had to live on."
luke:21:4.csb: For all these people have put in gifts out of their surplus, but she out of her poverty has put in all she had to live on.”
luke:21:4.nrsv: for all of them have contributed out of their abundance, but she out of her poverty has put in all she had to live on."

luke:21:5.niv: Some of his disciples were remarking about how the temple was adorned with beautiful stones and with gifts dedicated to God. But Jesus said,
luke:21:5.csb: As some were talking about the temple, how it was adorned with beautiful stones and gifts dedicated to God, he said,
luke:21:5.nrsv: When some were speaking about the temple, how it was adorned with beautiful stones and gifts dedicated to God, he said,

luke:21:6.niv: "As for what you see here, the time will come when not one stone will be left on another; every one of them will be thrown down."
luke:21:6.csb: “These things that you see — the days will come when not one stone will be left on another that will not be thrown down.”
luke:21:6.nrsv: "As for these things that you see, the days will come when not one stone will be left upon another; all will be thrown down."

luke:21:7.niv: "Teacher," they asked, "when will these things happen? And what will be the sign that they are about to take place?"
luke:21:7.csb: “Teacher,” they asked him, “so when will these things happen? And what will be the sign when these things are about to take place?”
luke:21:7.nrsv: They asked him, "Teacher, when will this be, and what will be the sign that this is about to take place?"

luke:21:8.niv: He replied: "Watch out that you are not deceived. For many will come in my name, claiming, 'I am he,' and, 'The time is near.' Do not follow them.
luke:21:8.csb: Then he said, “Watch out that you are not deceived. For many will come in my name, saying, ‘I am he,’ and, ‘The time is near.’ Don’t follow them.
luke:21:8.nrsv: And he said, "Beware that you are not led astray; for many will come in my name and say, 'I am he!' and, 'The time is near!' Do not go after them.

luke:21:9.niv: When you hear of wars and revolutions, do not be frightened. These things must happen first, but the end will not come right away."
luke:21:9.csb: When you hear of wars and rebellions, don’t be alarmed. Indeed, it is necessary that these things take place first, but the end won’t come right away.”
luke:21:9.nrsv: "When you hear of wars and insurrections, do not be terrified; for these things must take place first, but the end will not follow immediately."

luke:21:10.niv: Then he said to them: "Nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom.
luke:21:10.csb: Then he told them: “Nation will be raised up against nation, and kingdom against kingdom.
luke:21:10.nrsv: Then he said to them, "Nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom;

luke:21:11.niv: There will be great earthquakes, famines and pestilences in various places, and fearful events and great signs from heaven.
luke:21:11.csb: There will be violent earthquakes, and famines and plagues in various places, and there will be terrifying sights and great signs from heaven.
luke:21:11.nrsv: there will be great earthquakes, and in various places famines and plagues; and there will be dreadful portents and great signs from heaven.

luke:21:12.niv: "But before all this, they will lay hands on you and persecute you. They will deliver you to synagogues and prisons, and you will be brought before kings and governors, and all on account of my name.
luke:21:12.csb: But before all these things, they will lay their hands on you and persecute you. They will hand you over to the synagogues and prisons, and you will be brought before kings and governors because of my name.
luke:21:12.nrsv: "But before all this occurs, they will arrest you and persecute you; they will hand you over to synagogues and prisons, and you will be brought before kings and governors because of my name.

luke:21:13.niv: This will result in your being witnesses to them.
luke:21:13.csb: This will give you an opportunity to bear witness.
luke:21:13.nrsv: This will give you an opportunity to testify.

luke:21:14.niv: But make up your mind not to worry beforehand how you will defend yourselves.
luke:21:14.csb: Therefore make up your minds not to prepare your defense ahead of time,
luke:21:14.nrsv: So make up your minds not to prepare your defense in advance;

luke:21:15.niv: For I will give you words and wisdom that none of your adversaries will be able to resist or contradict.
luke:21:15.csb: for I will give you such words and a wisdom that none of your adversaries will be able to resist or contradict.
luke:21:15.nrsv: for I will give you words and a wisdom that none of your opponents will be able to withstand or contradict.

luke:21:16.niv: You will be betrayed even by parents, brothers, relatives and friends, and they will put some of you to death.
luke:21:16.csb: You will even be betrayed by parents, brothers, relatives, and friends. They will kill some of you.
luke:21:16.nrsv: You will be betrayed even by parents and brothers, by relatives and friends; and they will put some of you to death.

luke:21:17.niv: All men will hate you because of me.
luke:21:17.csb: You will be hated by everyone because of my name,
luke:21:17.nrsv: You will be hated by all because of my name.

luke:21:18.niv: But not a hair of your head will perish.
luke:21:18.csb: but not a hair of your head will be lost.
luke:21:18.nrsv: But not a hair of your head will perish.

luke:21:19.niv: By standing firm you will gain life.
luke:21:19.csb: By your endurance, gain your lives.
luke:21:19.nrsv: By your endurance you will gain your souls.

luke:21:20.niv: "When you see Jerusalem being surrounded by armies, you will know that its desolation is near.
luke:21:20.csb: “When you see Jerusalem surrounded by armies, then recognize that its desolation has come near.
luke:21:20.nrsv: "When you see Jerusalem surrounded by armies, then know that its desolation has come near.

luke:21:21.niv: Then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains, let those in the city get out, and let those in the country not enter the city.
luke:21:21.csb: Then those in Judea must flee to the mountains. Those inside the city must leave it, and those who are in the country must not enter it,
luke:21:21.nrsv: Then those in Judea must flee to the mountains, and those inside the city must leave it, and those out in the country must not enter it;

luke:21:22.niv: For this is the time of punishment in fulfillment of all that has been written.
luke:21:22.csb: because these are days of vengeance to fulfill all the things that are written.
luke:21:22.nrsv: for these are days of vengeance, as a fulfillment of all that is written.

luke:21:23.niv: How dreadful it will be in those days for pregnant women and nursing mothers! There will be great distress in the land and wrath against this people.
luke:21:23.csb: Woe to pregnant women and nursing mothers in those days, for there will be great distress in the land and wrath against this people.
luke:21:23.nrsv: Woe to those who are pregnant and to those who are nursing infants in those days! For there will be great distress on the earth and wrath against this people;

luke:21:24.niv: They will fall by the sword and will be taken as prisoners to all the nations. Jerusalem will be trampled on by the Gentiles until the times of the Gentiles are fulfilled.
luke:21:24.csb: They will be killed by the sword and be led captive into all the nations, and Jerusalem will be trampled by the Gentiles until the times of the Gentiles are fulfilled.
luke:21:24.nrsv: they will fall by the edge of the sword and be taken away as captives among all nations; and Jerusalem will be trampled on by the Gentiles, until the times of the Gentiles are fulfilled.

luke:21:25.niv: "There will be signs in the sun, moon and stars. On the earth, nations will be in anguish and perplexity at the roaring and tossing of the sea.
luke:21:25.csb: “Then there will be signs in the sun, moon, and stars; and there will be anguish on the earth among nations bewildered by the roaring of the sea and the waves.
luke:21:25.nrsv: "There will be signs in the sun, the moon, and the stars, and on the earth distress among nations confused by the roaring of the sea and the waves.

luke:21:26.niv: Men will faint from terror, apprehensive of what is coming on the world, for the heavenly bodies will be shaken.
luke:21:26.csb: People will faint from fear and expectation of the things that are coming on the world, because the powers of the heavens will be shaken.
luke:21:26.nrsv: People will faint from fear and foreboding of what is coming upon the world, for the powers of the heavens will be shaken.

luke:21:27.niv: At that time they will see the Son of Man coming in a cloud with power and great glory.
luke:21:27.csb: Then they will see the Son of Man coming in a cloud with power and great glory.
luke:21:27.nrsv: Then they will see 'the Son of Man coming in a cloud' with power and great glory.

luke:21:28.niv: When these things begin to take place, stand up and lift up your heads, because your redemption is drawing near."
luke:21:28.csb: But when these things begin to take place, stand up and lift up your heads, because your redemption is near.”
luke:21:28.nrsv: Now when these things begin to take place, stand up and raise your heads, because your redemption is drawing near."

luke:21:29.niv: He told them this parable: "Look at the fig tree and all the trees.
luke:21:29.csb: Then he told them a parable: “Look at the fig tree, and all the trees.
luke:21:29.nrsv: Then he told them a parable: "Look at the fig tree and all the trees;

luke:21:30.niv: When they sprout leaves, you can see for yourselves and know that summer is near.
luke:21:30.csb: As soon as they put out leaves you can see for yourselves and recognize that summer is already near.
luke:21:30.nrsv: as soon as they sprout leaves you can see for yourselves and know that summer is already near.

luke:21:31.niv: Even so, when you see these things happening, you know that the kingdom of God is near.
luke:21:31.csb: In the same way, when you see these things happening, recognize that the kingdom of God is near.
luke:21:31.nrsv: So also, when you see these things taking place, you know that the kingdom of God is near.

luke:21:32.niv: "I tell you the truth, this generation will certainly not pass away until all these things have happened.
luke:21:32.csb: Truly I tell you, this generation will certainly not pass away until all things take place.
luke:21:32.nrsv: Truly I tell you, this generation will not pass away until all things have taken place.

luke:21:33.niv: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will never pass away.
luke:21:33.csb: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will never pass away.
luke:21:33.nrsv: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will not pass away.

luke:21:34.niv: "Be careful, or your hearts will be weighed down with dissipation, drunkenness and the anxieties of life, and that day will close on you unexpectedly like a trap.
luke:21:34.csb: “Be on your guard, so that your minds are not dulled from carousing, drunkenness, and worries of life, or that day will come on you unexpectedly
luke:21:34.nrsv: "Be on guard so that your hearts are not weighed down with dissipation and drunkenness and the worries of this life, and that day catch you unexpectedly,

luke:21:35.niv: For it will come upon all those who live on the face of the whole earth.
luke:21:35.csb: like a trap. For it will come on all who live on the face of the whole earth.
luke:21:35.nrsv: like a trap. For it will come upon all who live on the face of the whole earth.

luke:21:36.niv: Be always on the watch, and pray that you may be able to escape all that is about to happen, and that you may be able to stand before the Son of Man."
luke:21:36.csb: But be alert at all times, praying that you may have strength to escape all these things that are going to take place and to stand before the Son of Man.”
luke:21:36.nrsv: Be alert at all times, praying that you may have the strength to escape all these things that will take place, and to stand before the Son of Man."

luke:21:37.niv: Each day Jesus was teaching at the temple, and each evening he went out to spend the night on the hill called the Mount of Olives,
luke:21:37.csb: During the day, he was teaching in the temple, but in the evening he would go out and spend the night on what is called the Mount of Olives.
luke:21:37.nrsv: Every day he was teaching in the temple, and at night he would go out and spend the night on the Mount of Olives, as it was called.

luke:21:38.niv: and all the people came early in the morning to hear him at the temple.
luke:21:38.csb: Then all the people would come early in the morning to hear him in the temple.
luke:21:38.nrsv: And all the people would get up early in the morning to listen to him in the temple.

luke:22:1.niv: Now the Feast of Unleavened Bread, called the Passover, was approaching,
luke:22:1.csb: The Festival of Unleavened Bread, which is called Passover, was approaching.
luke:22:1.nrsv: Now the festival of Unleavened Bread, which is called the Passover, was near.

luke:22:2.niv: and the chief priests and the teachers of the law were looking for some way to get rid of Jesus, for they were afraid of the people.
luke:22:2.csb: The chief priests and the scribes were looking for a way to put him to death, because they were afraid of the people.
luke:22:2.nrsv: The chief priests and the scribes were looking for a way to put Jesus to death, for they were afraid of the people.

luke:22:3.niv: Then Satan entered Judas, called Iscariot, one of the Twelve.
luke:22:3.csb: Then Satan entered Judas, called Iscariot, who was numbered among the Twelve.
luke:22:3.nrsv: Then Satan entered into Judas called Iscariot, who was one of the twelve;

luke:22:4.niv: And Judas went to the chief priests and the officers of the temple guard and discussed with them how he might betray Jesus.
luke:22:4.csb: He went away and discussed with the chief priests and temple police how he could hand him over to them.
luke:22:4.nrsv: he went away and conferred with the chief priests and officers of the temple police about how he might betray him to them.

luke:22:5.niv: They were delighted and agreed to give him money.
luke:22:5.csb: They were glad and agreed to give him silver.
luke:22:5.nrsv: They were greatly pleased and agreed to give him money.

luke:22:6.niv: He consented, and watched for an opportunity to hand Jesus over to them when no crowd was present.
luke:22:6.csb: So he accepted the offer and started looking for a good opportunity to betray him to them when the crowd was not present.
luke:22:6.nrsv: So he consented and began to look for an opportunity to betray him to them when no crowd was present.

luke:22:7.niv: Then came the day of Unleavened Bread on which the Passover lamb had to be sacrificed.
luke:22:7.csb: Then the Day of Unleavened Bread came when the Passover lamb had to be sacrificed.
luke:22:7.nrsv: Then came the day of Unleavened Bread, on which the Passover lamb had to be sacrificed.

luke:22:8.niv: Jesus sent Peter and John, saying, "Go and make preparations for us to eat the Passover."
luke:22:8.csb: Jesus sent Peter and John, saying, “Go and make preparations for us to eat the Passover.”
luke:22:8.nrsv: So Jesus sent Peter and John, saying, "Go and prepare the Passover meal for us that we may eat it."

luke:22:9.niv: "Where do you want us to prepare for it?" they asked.
luke:22:9.csb: “Where do you want us to prepare it?” they asked him.
luke:22:9.nrsv: They asked him, "Where do you want us to make preparations for it?"

luke:22:10.niv: He replied, "As you enter the city, a man carrying a jar of water will meet you. Follow him to the house that he enters,
luke:22:10.csb: “Listen,” he said to them, “when you’ve entered the city, a man carrying a water jug will meet you. Follow him into the house he enters.
luke:22:10.nrsv: "Listen," he said to them, "when you have entered the city, a man carrying a jar of water will meet you; follow him into the house he enters

luke:22:11.niv: and say to the owner of the house, 'The Teacher asks: Where is the guest room, where I may eat the Passover with my disciples?'
luke:22:11.csb: Tell the owner of the house, ‘The Teacher asks you, “Where is the guest room where I can eat the Passover with my disciples?”’
