luke:22:11.nrsv: and say to the owner of the house, 'The teacher asks you, "Where is the guest room, where I may eat the Passover with my disciples?"'

luke:22:12.niv: He will show you a large upper room, all furnished. Make preparations there."
luke:22:12.csb: Then he will show you a large, furnished room upstairs. Make the preparations there.”
luke:22:12.nrsv: He will show you a large room upstairs, already furnished. Make preparations for us there."

luke:22:13.niv: They left and found things just as Jesus had told them. So they prepared the Passover.
luke:22:13.csb: So they went and found it just as he had told them, and they prepared the Passover.
luke:22:13.nrsv: So they went and found everything as he had told them; and they prepared the Passover meal.

luke:22:14.niv: When the hour came, Jesus and his apostles reclined at the table.
luke:22:14.csb: When the hour came, he reclined at the table, and the apostles with him.
luke:22:14.nrsv: When the hour came, he took his place at the table, and the apostles with him.

luke:22:15.niv: And he said to them, "I have eagerly desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer.
luke:22:15.csb: Then he said to them, “I have fervently desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer.
luke:22:15.nrsv: He said to them, "I have eagerly desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer;

luke:22:16.niv: For I tell you, I will not eat it again until it finds fulfillment in the kingdom of God."
luke:22:16.csb: For I tell you, I will not eat it again until it is fulfilled in the kingdom of God.”
luke:22:16.nrsv: for I tell you, I will not eat it until it is fulfilled in the kingdom of God."

luke:22:17.niv: After taking the cup, he gave thanks and said, "Take this and divide it among you.
luke:22:17.csb: Then he took a cup, and after giving thanks, he said, “Take this and share it among yourselves.
luke:22:17.nrsv: Then he took a cup, and after giving thanks he said, "Take this and divide it among yourselves;

luke:22:18.niv: For I tell you I will not drink again of the fruit of the vine until the kingdom of God comes."
luke:22:18.csb: For I tell you, from now on I will not drink of the fruit of the vine until the kingdom of God comes.”
luke:22:18.nrsv: for I tell you that from now on I will not drink of the fruit of the vine until the kingdom of God comes."

luke:22:19.niv: And he took bread, gave thanks and broke it, and gave it to them, saying, "This is my body given for you; do this in remembrance of me."
luke:22:19.csb: And he took bread, gave thanks, broke it, gave it to them, and said, “This is my body, which is given for you. Do this in remembrance of me.”
luke:22:19.nrsv: Then he took a loaf of bread, and when he had given thanks, he broke it and gave it to them, saying, "This is my body, which is given for you. Do this in remembrance of me."

luke:22:20.niv: In the same way, after the supper he took the cup, saying, "This cup is the new covenant in my blood, which is poured out for you.
luke:22:20.csb: In the same way he also took the cup after supper and said, “This cup is the new covenant in my blood, which is poured out for you.
luke:22:20.nrsv: And he did the same with the cup after supper, saying, "This cup that is poured out for you is the new covenant in my blood.

luke:22:21.niv: But the hand of him who is going to betray me is with mine on the table.
luke:22:21.csb: But look, the hand of the one betraying me is at the table with me.
luke:22:21.nrsv: But see, the one who betrays me is with me, and his hand is on the table.

luke:22:22.niv: The Son of Man will go as it has been decreed, but woe to that man who betrays him."
luke:22:22.csb: For the Son of Man will go away as it has been determined, but woe to that man by whom he is betrayed!”
luke:22:22.nrsv: For the Son of Man is going as it has been determined, but woe to that one by whom he is betrayed!"

luke:22:23.niv: They began to question among themselves which of them it might be who would do this.
luke:22:23.csb: So they began to argue among themselves which of them it could be who was going to do it.
luke:22:23.nrsv: Then they began to ask one another, which one of them it could be who would do this.

luke:22:24.niv: Also a dispute arose among them as to which of them was considered to be greatest.
luke:22:24.csb: Then a dispute also arose among them about who should be considered the greatest.
luke:22:24.nrsv: A dispute also arose among them as to which one of them was to be regarded as the greatest.

luke:22:25.niv: Jesus said to them, "The kings of the Gentiles lord it over them; and those who exercise authority over them call themselves Benefactors.
luke:22:25.csb: But he said to them, “The kings of the Gentiles lord it over them, and those who have authority over them have themselves called ‘Benefactors.’
luke:22:25.nrsv: But he said to them, "The kings of the Gentiles lord it over them; and those in authority over them are called benefactors.

luke:22:26.niv: But you are not to be like that. Instead, the greatest among you should be like the youngest, and the one who rules like the one who serves.
luke:22:26.csb: It is not to be like that among you. On the contrary, whoever is greatest among you should become like the youngest, and whoever leads, like the one serving.
luke:22:26.nrsv: But not so with you; rather the greatest among you must become like the youngest, and the leader like one who serves.

luke:22:27.niv: For who is greater, the one who is at the table or the one who serves? Is it not the one who is at the table? But I am among you as one who serves.
luke:22:27.csb: For who is greater, the one at the table or the one serving? Isn’t it the one at the table? But I am among you as the one who serves.
luke:22:27.nrsv: For who is greater, the one who is at the table or the one who serves? Is it not the one at the table? But I am among you as one who serves.

luke:22:28.niv: You are those who have stood by me in my trials.
luke:22:28.csb: You are those who stood by me in my trials.
luke:22:28.nrsv: "You are those who have stood by me in my trials;

luke:22:29.niv: And I confer on you a kingdom, just as my Father conferred one on me,
luke:22:29.csb: I bestow on you a kingdom, just as my Father bestowed one on me,
luke:22:29.nrsv: and I confer on you, just as my Father has conferred on me, a kingdom,

luke:22:30.niv: so that you may eat and drink at my table in my kingdom and sit on thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
luke:22:30.csb: so that you may eat and drink at my table in my kingdom. And you will sit on thrones judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
luke:22:30.nrsv: so that you may eat and drink at my table in my kingdom, and you will sit on thrones judging the twelve tribes of Israel.

luke:22:31.niv: "Simon, Simon, Satan has asked to sift you as wheat.
luke:22:31.csb: “Simon, Simon, look out. Satan has asked to sift you like wheat.
luke:22:31.nrsv: "Simon, Simon, listen! Satan has demanded to sift all of you like wheat,

luke:22:32.niv: But I have prayed for you, Simon, that your faith may not fail. And when you have turned back, strengthen your brothers."
luke:22:32.csb: But I have prayed for you that your faith may not fail. And you, when you have turned back, strengthen your brothers.”
luke:22:32.nrsv: but I have prayed for you that your own faith may not fail; and you, when once you have turned back, strengthen your brothers."

luke:22:33.niv: But he replied, "Lord, I am ready to go with you to prison and to death."
luke:22:33.csb: “Lord,” he told him, “I’m ready to go with you both to prison and to death.”
luke:22:33.nrsv: And he said to him, "Lord, I am ready to go with you to prison and to death!"

luke:22:34.niv: Jesus answered, "I tell you, Peter, before the rooster crows today, you will deny three times that you know me."
luke:22:34.csb: “I tell you, Peter,” he said, “the rooster will not crow today until you deny three times that you know me.”
luke:22:34.nrsv: Jesus said, "I tell you, Peter, the cock will not crow this day, until you have denied three times that you know me."

luke:22:35.niv: Then Jesus asked them, "When I sent you without purse, bag or sandals, did you lack anything?Nothing," they answered.
luke:22:35.csb: He also said to them, “When I sent you out without money-bag, traveling bag, or sandals, did you lack anything?” “Not a thing,” they said.
luke:22:35.nrsv: He said to them, "When I sent you out without a purse, bag, or sandals, did you lack anything?" They said, "No, not a thing."

luke:22:36.niv: He said to them, "But now if you have a purse, take it, and also a bag; and if you don't have a sword, sell your cloak and buy one.
luke:22:36.csb: Then he said to them, “But now, whoever has a money-bag should take it, and also a traveling bag. And whoever doesn’t have a sword should sell his robe and buy one.
luke:22:36.nrsv: He said to them, "But now, the one who has a purse must take it, and likewise a bag. And the one who has no sword must sell his cloak and buy one.

luke:22:37.niv: It is written: 'And he was numbered with the transgressors'; and I tell you that this must be fulfilled in me. Yes, what is written about me is reaching its fulfillment."
luke:22:37.csb: For I tell you, what is written must be fulfilled in me: And he was counted among the lawless. Yes, what is written about me is coming to its fulfillment.”
luke:22:37.nrsv: For I tell you, this scripture must be fulfilled in me, 'And he was counted among the lawless'; and indeed what is written about me is being fulfilled."

luke:22:38.niv: The disciples said, "See, Lord, here are two swords.That is enough," he replied.
luke:22:38.csb: “Lord,” they said, “look, here are two swords.” “That is enough!” he told them.
luke:22:38.nrsv: They said, "Lord, look, here are two swords." He replied, "It is enough."

luke:22:39.niv: Jesus went out as usual to the Mount of Olives, and his disciples followed him.
luke:22:39.csb: He went out and made his way as usual to the Mount of Olives, and the disciples followed him.
luke:22:39.nrsv: He came out and went, as was his custom, to the Mount of Olives; and the disciples followed him.

luke:22:40.niv: On reaching the place, he said to them, "Pray that you will not fall into temptation."
luke:22:40.csb: When he reached the place, he told them, “Pray that you may not fall into temptation.”
luke:22:40.nrsv: When he reached the place, he said to them, "Pray that you may not come into the time of trial."

luke:22:41.niv: He withdrew about a stone's throw beyond them, knelt down and prayed,
luke:22:41.csb: Then he withdrew from them about a stone’s throw, knelt down, and began to pray,
luke:22:41.nrsv: Then he withdrew from them about a stone's throw, knelt down, and prayed,

luke:22:42.niv: "Father, if you are willing, take this cup from me; yet not my will, but yours be done."
luke:22:42.csb: “Father, if you are willing, take this cup away from me — nevertheless, not my will, but yours, be done.”
luke:22:42.nrsv: "Father, if you are willing, remove this cup from me; yet, not my will but yours be done."

luke:22:43.niv: An angel from heaven appeared to him and strengthened him.
luke:22:43.csb: Then an angel from heaven appeared to him, strengthening him.
luke:22:43.nrsv: Then an angel from heaven appeared to him and gave him strength.

luke:22:44.niv: And being in anguish, he prayed more earnestly, and his sweat was like drops of blood falling to the ground.
luke:22:44.csb: Being in anguish, he prayed more fervently, and his sweat became like drops of blood falling to the ground.
luke:22:44.nrsv: In his anguish he prayed more earnestly, and his sweat became like great drops of blood falling down on the ground.

luke:22:45.niv: When he rose from prayer and went back to the disciples, he found them asleep, exhausted from sorrow.
luke:22:45.csb: When he got up from prayer and came to the disciples, he found them sleeping, exhausted from their grief.
luke:22:45.nrsv: When he got up from prayer, he came to the disciples and found them sleeping because of grief,

luke:22:46.niv: "Why are you sleeping?" he asked them. "Get up and pray so that you will not fall into temptation."
luke:22:46.csb: “Why are you sleeping?” he asked them. “Get up and pray, so that you won’t fall into temptation.”
luke:22:46.nrsv: and he said to them, "Why are you sleeping? Get up and pray that you may not come into the time of trial."

luke:22:47.niv: While he was still speaking a crowd came up, and the man who was called Judas, one of the Twelve, was leading them. He approached Jesus to kiss him,
luke:22:47.csb: While he was still speaking, suddenly a mob came, and one of the Twelve named Judas was leading them. He came near Jesus to kiss him,
luke:22:47.nrsv: While he was still speaking, suddenly a crowd came, and the one called Judas, one of the twelve, was leading them. He approached Jesus to kiss him;

luke:22:48.niv: but Jesus asked him, "Judas, are you betraying the Son of Man with a kiss?"
luke:22:48.csb: but Jesus said to him, “Judas, are you betraying the Son of Man with a kiss?”
luke:22:48.nrsv: but Jesus said to him, "Judas, is it with a kiss that you are betraying the Son of Man?"

luke:22:49.niv: When Jesus' followers saw what was going to happen, they said, "Lord, should we strike with our swords?"
luke:22:49.csb: When those around him saw what was going to happen, they asked, “Lord, should we strike with the sword?”
luke:22:49.nrsv: When those who were around him saw what was coming, they asked, "Lord, should we strike with the sword?"

luke:22:50.niv: And one of them struck the servant of the high priest, cutting off his right ear.
luke:22:50.csb: Then one of them struck the high priest’s servant and cut off his right ear.
luke:22:50.nrsv: Then one of them struck the slave of the high priest and cut off his right ear.

luke:22:51.niv: But Jesus answered, "No more of this!" And he touched the man's ear and healed him.
luke:22:51.csb: But Jesus responded, “No more of this!” And touching his ear, he healed him.
luke:22:51.nrsv: But Jesus said, "No more of this!" And he touched his ear and healed him.

luke:22:52.niv: Then Jesus said to the chief priests, the officers of the temple guard, and the elders, who had come for him, "Am I leading a rebellion, that you have come with swords and clubs?
luke:22:52.csb: Then Jesus said to the chief priests, temple police, and the elders who had come for him, “Have you come out with swords and clubs as if I were a criminal?
luke:22:52.nrsv: Then Jesus said to the chief priests, the officers of the temple police, and the elders who had come for him, "Have you come out with swords and clubs as if I were a bandit?

luke:22:53.niv: Every day I was with you in the temple courts, and you did not lay a hand on me. But this is your hour--when darkness reigns."
luke:22:53.csb: Every day while I was with you in the temple, you never laid a hand on me. But this is your hour — and the dominion of darkness.”
luke:22:53.nrsv: When I was with you day after day in the temple, you did not lay hands on me. But this is your hour, and the power of darkness!"

luke:22:54.niv: Then seizing him, they led him away and took him into the house of the high priest. Peter followed at a distance.
luke:22:54.csb: They seized him, led him away, and brought him into the high priest’s house. Meanwhile Peter was following at a distance.
luke:22:54.nrsv: Then they seized him and led him away, bringing him into the high priest's house. But Peter was following at a distance.

luke:22:55.niv: But when they had kindled a fire in the middle of the courtyard and had sat down together, Peter sat down with them.
luke:22:55.csb: They lit a fire in the middle of the courtyard and sat down together, and Peter sat among them.
luke:22:55.nrsv: When they had kindled a fire in the middle of the courtyard and sat down together, Peter sat among them.

luke:22:56.niv: A servant girl saw him seated there in the firelight. She looked closely at him and said, "This man was with him."
luke:22:56.csb: When a servant saw him sitting in the light, and looked closely at him, she said, “This man was with him too.”
luke:22:56.nrsv: Then a servant-girl, seeing him in the firelight, stared at him and said, "This man also was with him."

luke:22:57.niv: But he denied it. "Woman, I don't know him," he said.
luke:22:57.csb: But he denied it: “Woman, I don’t know him.”
luke:22:57.nrsv: But he denied it, saying, "Woman, I do not know him."

luke:22:58.niv: A little later someone else saw him and said, "You also are one of them.Man, I am not!" Peter replied.
luke:22:58.csb: After a little while, someone else saw him and said, “You’re one of them too.” “Man, I am not!” Peter said.
luke:22:58.nrsv: A little later someone else, on seeing him, said, "You also are one of them." But Peter said, "Man, I am not!"

luke:22:59.niv: About an hour later another asserted, "Certainly this fellow was with him, for he is a Galilean."
luke:22:59.csb: About an hour later, another kept insisting, “This man was certainly with him, since he’s also a Galilean.”
luke:22:59.nrsv: Then about an hour later still another kept insisting, "Surely this man also was with him; for he is a Galilean."

luke:22:60.niv: Peter replied, "Man, I don't know what you're talking about!" Just as he was speaking, the rooster crowed.
luke:22:60.csb: But Peter said, “Man, I don’t know what you’re talking about!” Immediately, while he was still speaking, a rooster crowed.
luke:22:60.nrsv: But Peter said, "Man, I do not know what you are talking about!" At that moment, while he was still speaking, the cock crowed.

luke:22:61.niv: The Lord turned and looked straight at Peter. Then Peter remembered the word the Lord had spoken to him: "Before the rooster crows today, you will disown me three times."
luke:22:61.csb: Then the Lord turned and looked at Peter. So Peter remembered the word of the Lord, how he had said to him, “Before the rooster crows today, you will deny me three times.”
luke:22:61.nrsv: The Lord turned and looked at Peter. Then Peter remembered the word of the Lord, how he had said to him, "Before the cock crows today, you will deny me three times."

luke:22:62.niv: And he went outside and wept bitterly.
luke:22:62.csb: And he went outside and wept bitterly.
luke:22:62.nrsv: And he went out and wept bitterly.

luke:22:63.niv: The men who were guarding Jesus began mocking and beating him.
luke:22:63.csb: The men who were holding Jesus started mocking and beating him.
luke:22:63.nrsv: Now the men who were holding Jesus began to mock him and beat him;

luke:22:64.niv: They blindfolded him and demanded, "Prophesy! Who hit you?"
luke:22:64.csb: After blindfolding him, they kept asking, “Prophesy! Who was it that hit you?”
luke:22:64.nrsv: they also blindfolded him and kept asking him, "Prophesy! Who is it that struck you?"

luke:22:65.niv: And they said many other insulting things to him.
luke:22:65.csb: And they were saying many other blasphemous things to him.
luke:22:65.nrsv: They kept heaping many other insults on him.

luke:22:66.niv: At daybreak the council of the elders of the people, both the chief priests and teachers of the law, met together, and Jesus was led before them.
luke:22:66.csb: When daylight came, the elders of the people, both the chief priests and the scribes, convened and brought him before their Sanhedrin.
luke:22:66.nrsv: When day came, the assembly of the elders of the people, both chief priests and scribes, gathered together, and they brought him to their council.

luke:22:67.niv: "If you are the Christ, "they said, "tell us."
luke:22:67.csb: They said, “If you are the Messiah, tell us.” But he said to them, “If I do tell you, you will not believe.
luke:22:67.nrsv: They said, "If you are the Messiah, tell us." He replied, "If I tell you, you will not believe;

luke:22:68.niv: Jesus answered, "If I tell you, you will not believe me, and if I asked you, you would not answer.
luke:22:68.csb: And if I ask you, you will not answer.
luke:22:68.nrsv: and if I question you, you will not answer.

luke:22:69.niv: But from now on, the Son of Man will be seated at the right hand of the mighty God."
luke:22:69.csb: But from now on, the Son of Man will be seated at the right hand of the power of God.”
luke:22:69.nrsv: But from now on the Son of Man will be seated at the right hand of the power of God."

luke:22:70.niv: They all asked, "Are you then the Son of God?" He replied, "You are right in saying I am."
luke:22:70.csb: They all asked, “Are you, then, the Son of God?” And he said to them, “You say that I am.”
luke:22:70.nrsv: All of them asked, "Are you, then, the Son of God?" He said to them, "You say that I am."

luke:22:71.niv: Then they said, "Why do we need any more testimony? We have heard it from his own lips."
luke:22:71.csb: “Why do we need any more testimony,” they said, “since we’ve heard it ourselves from his mouth?”
luke:22:71.nrsv: Then they said, "What further testimony do we need? We have heard it ourselves from his own lips!"

luke:23:1.niv: Then the whole assembly rose and led him off to Pilate.
luke:23:1.csb: Then their whole assembly rose up and brought him before Pilate.
luke:23:1.nrsv: Then the assembly rose as a body and brought Jesus before Pilate.

luke:23:2.niv: And they began to accuse him, saying, "We have found this man subverting our nation. He opposes payment of taxes to Caesar and claims to be Christ, a king."
luke:23:2.csb: They began to accuse him, saying, “We found this man misleading our nation, opposing payment of taxes to Caesar, and saying that he himself is the Messiah, a king.”
luke:23:2.nrsv: They began to accuse him, saying, "We found this man perverting our nation, forbidding us to pay taxes to the emperor, and saying that he himself is the Messiah, a king."

luke:23:3.niv: So Pilate asked Jesus, "Are you the king of the Jews?Yes, it is as you say," Jesus replied.
luke:23:3.csb: So Pilate asked him, “Are you the king of the Jews?” He answered him, “You say so.”
luke:23:3.nrsv: Then Pilate asked him, "Are you the king of the Jews?" He answered, "You say so."

luke:23:4.niv: Then Pilate announced to the chief priests and the crowd, "I find no basis for a charge against this man."
luke:23:4.csb: Pilate then told the chief priests and the crowds, “I find no grounds for charging this man.”
luke:23:4.nrsv: Then Pilate said to the chief priests and the crowds, "I find no basis for an accusation against this man."

luke:23:5.niv: But they insisted, "He stirs up the people all over Judea by his teaching. He started in Galilee and has come all the way here."
luke:23:5.csb: But they kept insisting, “He stirs up the people, teaching throughout all Judea, from Galilee where he started even to here.”
luke:23:5.nrsv: But they were insistent and said, "He stirs up the people by teaching throughout all Judea, from Galilee where he began even to this place."

luke:23:6.niv: On hearing this, Pilate asked if the man was a Galilean.
luke:23:6.csb: When Pilate heard this, he asked if the man was a Galilean.
luke:23:6.nrsv: When Pilate heard this, he asked whether the man was a Galilean.

luke:23:7.niv: When he learned that Jesus was under Herod's jurisdiction, he sent him to Herod, who was also in Jerusalem at that time.
luke:23:7.csb: Finding that he was under Herod’s jurisdiction, he sent him to Herod, who was also in Jerusalem during those days.
luke:23:7.nrsv: And when he learned that he was under Herod's jurisdiction, he sent him off to Herod, who was himself in Jerusalem at that time.

luke:23:8.niv: When Herod saw Jesus, he was greatly pleased, because for a long time he had been wanting to see him. From what he had heard about him, he hoped to see him perform some miracle.
luke:23:8.csb: Herod was very glad to see Jesus; for a long time he had wanted to see him because he had heard about him and was hoping to see some miracle performed by him.
luke:23:8.nrsv: When Herod saw Jesus, he was very glad, for he had been wanting to see him for a long time, because he had heard about him and was hoping to see him perform some sign.

luke:23:9.niv: He plied him with many questions, but Jesus gave him no answer.
luke:23:9.csb: So he kept asking him questions, but Jesus did not answer him.
luke:23:9.nrsv: He questioned him at some length, but Jesus gave him no answer.

luke:23:10.niv: The chief priests and the teachers of the law were standing there, vehemently accusing him.
luke:23:10.csb: The chief priests and the scribes stood by, vehemently accusing him.
luke:23:10.nrsv: The chief priests and the scribes stood by, vehemently accusing him.

luke:23:11.niv: Then Herod and his soldiers ridiculed and mocked him. Dressing him in an elegant robe, they sent him back to Pilate.
luke:23:11.csb: Then Herod, with his soldiers, treated him with contempt, mocked him, dressed him in bright clothing, and sent him back to Pilate.
luke:23:11.nrsv: Even Herod with his soldiers treated him with contempt and mocked him; then he put an elegant robe on him, and sent him back to Pilate.

luke:23:12.niv: That day Herod and Pilate became friends--before this they had been enemies.
luke:23:12.csb: That very day Herod and Pilate became friends. Previously, they had been enemies.
luke:23:12.nrsv: That same day Herod and Pilate became friends with each other; before this they had been enemies.

luke:23:13.niv: Pilate called together the chief priests, the rulers and the people,
luke:23:13.csb: Pilate called together the chief priests, the leaders, and the people,
luke:23:13.nrsv: Pilate then called together the chief priests, the leaders, and the people,

luke:23:14.niv: and said to them, "You brought me this man as one who was inciting the people to rebellion. I have examined him in your presence and have found no basis for your charges against him.
luke:23:14.csb: and said to them, “You have brought me this man as one who misleads the people. But in fact, after examining him in your presence, I have found no grounds to charge this man with those things you accuse him of.
luke:23:14.nrsv: and said to them, "You brought me this man as one who was perverting the people; and here I have examined him in your presence and have not found this man guilty of any of your charges against him.

luke:23:15.niv: Neither has Herod, for he sent him back to us; as you can see, he has done nothing to deserve death.
luke:23:15.csb: Neither has Herod, because he sent him back to us. Clearly, he has done nothing to deserve death.
luke:23:15.nrsv: Neither has Herod, for he sent him back to us. Indeed, he has done nothing to deserve death.

luke:23:16.niv: Therefore, I will punish him and then release him."
luke:23:16.csb: Therefore, I will have him whipped and then release him.”
luke:23:16.nrsv: I will therefore have him flogged and release him."

luke:23:18.niv: With one voice they cried out, "Away with this man! Release Barabbas to us!"
luke:23:18.csb: Then they all cried out together, “Take this man away! Release Barabbas to us!”
luke:23:18.nrsv: Then they all shouted out together, "Away with this fellow! Release Barabbas for us!"

luke:23:19.niv: (Barabbas had been thrown into prison for an insurrection in the city, and for murder.)
luke:23:19.csb: (He had been thrown into prison for a rebellion that had taken place in the city, and for murder.)
luke:23:19.nrsv: (This was a man who had been put in prison for an insurrection that had taken place in the city, and for murder.)

luke:23:20.niv: Wanting to release Jesus, Pilate appealed to them again.
luke:23:20.csb: Wanting to release Jesus, Pilate addressed them again,
luke:23:20.nrsv: Pilate, wanting to release Jesus, addressed them again;

luke:23:21.niv: But they kept shouting, "Crucify him! Crucify him!"
luke:23:21.csb: but they kept shouting, “Crucify! Crucify him!”
luke:23:21.nrsv: but they kept shouting, "Crucify, crucify him!"

luke:23:22.niv: For the third time he spoke to them: "Why? What crime has this man committed? I have found in him no grounds for the death penalty. Therefore I will have him punished and then release him."
luke:23:22.csb: A third time he said to them, “Why? What has this man done wrong? I have found in him no grounds for the death penalty. Therefore, I will have him whipped and then release him.”
luke:23:22.nrsv: A third time he said to them, "Why, what evil has he done? I have found in him no ground for the sentence of death; I will therefore have him flogged and then release him."

luke:23:23.niv: But with loud shouts they insistently demanded that he be crucified, and their shouts prevailed.
luke:23:23.csb: But they kept up the pressure, demanding with loud voices that he be crucified, and their voices won out.
luke:23:23.nrsv: But they kept urgently demanding with loud shouts that he should be crucified; and their voices prevailed.

luke:23:24.niv: So Pilate decided to grant their demand.
luke:23:24.csb: So Pilate decided to grant their demand
luke:23:24.nrsv: So Pilate gave his verdict that their demand should be granted.

luke:23:25.niv: He released the man who had been thrown into prison for insurrection and murder, the one they asked for, and surrendered Jesus to their will.
luke:23:25.csb: and released the one they were asking for, who had been thrown into prison for rebellion and murder. But he handed Jesus over to their will.
luke:23:25.nrsv: He released the man they asked for, the one who had been put in prison for insurrection and murder, and he handed Jesus over as they wished.

luke:23:26.niv: As they led him away, they seized Simon from Cyrene, who was on his way in from the country, and put the cross on him and made him carry it behind Jesus.
luke:23:26.csb: As they led him away, they seized Simon, a Cyrenian, who was coming in from the country, and laid the cross on him to carry behind Jesus.
luke:23:26.nrsv: As they led him away, they seized a man, Simon of Cyrene, who was coming from the country, and they laid the cross on him, and made him carry it behind Jesus.

luke:23:27.niv: A large number of people followed him, including women who mourned and wailed for him.
luke:23:27.csb: A large crowd of people followed him, including women who were mourning and lamenting him.
luke:23:27.nrsv: A great number of the people followed him, and among them were women who were beating their breasts and wailing for him.

luke:23:28.niv: Jesus turned and said to them, "Daughters of Jerusalem, do not weep for me; weep for yourselves and for your children.
luke:23:28.csb: But turning to them, Jesus said, “Daughters of Jerusalem, do not weep for me, but weep for yourselves and your children.
luke:23:28.nrsv: But Jesus turned to them and said, "Daughters of Jerusalem, do not weep for me, but weep for yourselves and for your children.

luke:23:29.niv: For the time will come when you will say, 'Blessed are the barren women, the wombs that never bore and the breasts that never nursed!'
luke:23:29.csb: Look, the days are coming when they will say, ‘Blessed are the women without children, the wombs that never bore, and the breasts that never nursed!’
luke:23:29.nrsv: For the days are surely coming when they will say, 'Blessed are the barren, and the wombs that never bore, and the breasts that never nursed.'

luke:23:30.niv: Then "'they will say to the mountains, "Fall on us!" and to the hills, "Cover us!"'
luke:23:30.csb: Then they will begin to say to the mountains, ‘Fall on us! ’ and to the hills, ‘Cover us!’
luke:23:30.nrsv: Then they will begin to say to the mountains, 'Fall on us'; and to the hills, 'Cover us.'

luke:23:31.niv: For if men do these things when the tree is green, what will happen when it is dry?"
luke:23:31.csb: For if they do these things when the wood is green, what will happen when it is dry?”
luke:23:31.nrsv: For if they do this when the wood is green, what will happen when it is dry?"

luke:23:32.niv: Two other men, both criminals, were also led out with him to be executed.
luke:23:32.csb: Two others — criminals — were also led away to be executed with him.
luke:23:32.nrsv: Two others also, who were criminals, were led away to be put to death with him.

luke:23:33.niv: When they came to the place called the Skull, there they crucified him, along with the criminals--one on his right, the other on his left.
luke:23:33.csb: When they arrived at the place called The Skull, they crucified him there, along with the criminals, one on the right and one on the left.
luke:23:33.nrsv: When they came to the place that is called The Skull, they crucified Jesus there with the criminals, one on his right and one on his left.

luke:23:34.niv: Jesus said, "Father, forgive them, for they do not know what they are doing." And they divided up his clothes by casting lots.
luke:23:34.csb: Then Jesus said, “Father, forgive them, because they do not know what they are doing.” And they divided his clothes and cast lots.
luke:23:34.nrsv: Then Jesus said, "Father, forgive them; for they do not know what they are doing." And they cast lots to divide his clothing.

luke:23:35.niv: The people stood watching, and the rulers even sneered at him. They said, "He saved others; let him save himself if he is the Christ of God, the Chosen One."
luke:23:35.csb: The people stood watching, and even the leaders were scoffing: “He saved others; let him save himself if this is God’s Messiah, the Chosen One!”
luke:23:35.nrsv: And the people stood by, watching; but the leaders scoffed at him, saying, "He saved others; let him save himself if he is the Messiah of God, his chosen one!"

luke:23:36.niv: The soldiers also came up and mocked him. They offered him wine vinegar
luke:23:36.csb: The soldiers also mocked him. They came offering him sour wine
luke:23:36.nrsv: The soldiers also mocked him, coming up and offering him sour wine,

luke:23:37.niv: and said, "If you are the king of the Jews, save yourself."
luke:23:37.csb: and said, “If you are the King of the Jews, save yourself!”
luke:23:37.nrsv: and saying, "If you are the King of the Jews, save yourself!"

luke:23:38.niv: There was a written notice above him, which read:|sc THIS IS THE KING OF THE JEWS.
luke:23:38.csb: An inscription was above him: THIS IS THE KING OF THE JEWS.
luke:23:38.nrsv: There was also an inscription over him, "This is the King of the Jews."

luke:23:39.niv: One of the criminals who hung there hurled insults at him: "Aren't you the Christ? Save yourself and us!"
luke:23:39.csb: Then one of the criminals hanging there began to yell insults at him: “Aren’t you the Messiah? Save yourself and us!”
luke:23:39.nrsv: One of the criminals who were hanged there kept deriding him and saying, "Are you not the Messiah? Save yourself and us!"

luke:23:40.niv: But the other criminal rebuked him. "Don't you fear God," he said, "since you are under the same sentence?
luke:23:40.csb: But the other answered, rebuking him: “Don’t you even fear God, since you are undergoing the same punishment?
luke:23:40.nrsv: But the other rebuked him, saying, "Do you not fear God, since you are under the same sentence of condemnation?

luke:23:41.niv: We are punished justly, for we are getting what our deeds deserve. But this man has done nothing wrong."
luke:23:41.csb: We are punished justly, because we’re getting back what we deserve for the things we did, but this man has done nothing wrong.”
luke:23:41.nrsv: And we indeed have been condemned justly, for we are getting what we deserve for our deeds, but this man has done nothing wrong."

luke:23:42.niv: Then he said, "Jesus, remember me when you come into your kingdom. "
luke:23:42.csb: Then he said, “Jesus, remember me when you come into your kingdom.”
luke:23:42.nrsv: Then he said, "Jesus, remember me when you come into your kingdom."

luke:23:43.niv: Jesus answered him, "I tell you the truth, today you will be with me in paradise."
luke:23:43.csb: And he said to him, “Truly I tell you, today you will be with me in paradise.”
luke:23:43.nrsv: He replied, "Truly I tell you, today you will be with me in Paradise."

luke:23:44.niv: It was now about the sixth hour, and darkness came over the whole land until the ninth hour,
luke:23:44.csb: It was now about noon, and darkness came over the whole land until three,
luke:23:44.nrsv: It was now about noon, and darkness came over the whole land until three in the afternoon,

luke:23:45.niv: for the sun stopped shining. And the curtain of the temple was torn in two.
luke:23:45.csb: because the sun’s light failed. The curtain of the sanctuary was split down the middle.
luke:23:45.nrsv: while the sun's light failed; and the curtain of the temple was torn in two.

luke:23:46.niv: Jesus called out with a loud voice, "Father, into your hands I commit my spirit." When he had said this, he breathed his last.
luke:23:46.csb: And Jesus called out with a loud voice, “Father, into your hands I entrust my spirit. ” Saying this, he breathed his last.
luke:23:46.nrsv: Then Jesus, crying with a loud voice, said, "Father, into your hands I commend my spirit." Having said this, he breathed his last.

luke:23:47.niv: The centurion, seeing what had happened, praised God and said, "Surely this was a righteous man."
luke:23:47.csb: When the centurion saw what happened, he began to glorify God, saying, “This man really was righteous!”
luke:23:47.nrsv: When the centurion saw what had taken place, he praised God and said, "Certainly this man was innocent."

luke:23:48.niv: When all the people who had gathered to witness this sight saw what took place, they beat their breasts and went away.
luke:23:48.csb: All the crowds that had gathered for this spectacle, when they saw what had taken place, went home, striking their chests.
luke:23:48.nrsv: And when all the crowds who had gathered there for this spectacle saw what had taken place, they returned home, beating their breasts.

luke:23:49.niv: But all those who knew him, including the women who had followed him from Galilee, stood at a distance, watching these things.
luke:23:49.csb: But all who knew him, including the women who had followed him from Galilee, stood at a distance, watching these things.
luke:23:49.nrsv: But all his acquaintances, including the women who had followed him from Galilee, stood at a distance, watching these things.

luke:23:50.niv: Now there was a man named Joseph, a member of the Council, a good and upright man,
luke:23:50.csb: There was a good and righteous man named Joseph, a member of the Sanhedrin,
luke:23:50.nrsv: Now there was a good and righteous man named Joseph, who, though a member of the council,

luke:23:51.niv: who had not consented to their decision and action. He came from the Judean town of Arimathea and he was waiting for the kingdom of God.
luke:23:51.csb: who had not agreed with their plan and action. He was from Arimathea, a Judean town, and was looking forward to the kingdom of God.
luke:23:51.nrsv: had not agreed to their plan and action. He came from the Jewish town of Arimathea, and he was waiting expectantly for the kingdom of God.

luke:23:52.niv: Going to Pilate, he asked for Jesus' body.
luke:23:52.csb: He approached Pilate and asked for Jesus’s body.
luke:23:52.nrsv: This man went to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus.

luke:23:53.niv: Then he took it down, wrapped it in linen cloth and placed it in a tomb cut in the rock, one in which no one had yet been laid.
luke:23:53.csb: Taking it down, he wrapped it in fine linen and placed it in a tomb cut into the rock, where no one had ever been placed.
luke:23:53.nrsv: Then he took it down, wrapped it in a linen cloth, and laid it in a rock-hewn tomb where no one had ever been laid.

luke:23:54.niv: It was Preparation Day, and the Sabbath was about to begin.
luke:23:54.csb: It was the preparation day, and the Sabbath was about to begin.
luke:23:54.nrsv: It was the day of Preparation, and the sabbath was beginning.

luke:23:55.niv: The women who had come with Jesus from Galilee followed Joseph and saw the tomb and how his body was laid in it.
luke:23:55.csb: The women who had come with him from Galilee followed along and observed the tomb and how his body was placed.
luke:23:55.nrsv: The women who had come with him from Galilee followed, and they saw the tomb and how his body was laid.

luke:23:56.niv: Then they went home and prepared spices and perfumes. But they rested on the Sabbath in obedience to the commandment.
luke:23:56.csb: Then they returned and prepared spices and perfumes. And they rested on the Sabbath according to the commandment.
luke:23:56.nrsv: Then they returned, and prepared spices and ointments. On the sabbath they rested according to the commandment.

luke:24:1.niv: On the first day of the week, very early in the morning, the women took the spices they had prepared and went to the tomb.
luke:24:1.csb: On the first day of the week, very early in the morning, they came to the tomb, bringing the spices they had prepared.
luke:24:1.nrsv: But on the first day of the week, at early dawn, they came to the tomb, taking the spices that they had prepared.

luke:24:2.niv: They found the stone rolled away from the tomb,
luke:24:2.csb: They found the stone rolled away from the tomb.
luke:24:2.nrsv: They found the stone rolled away from the tomb,

luke:24:3.niv: but when they entered, they did not find the body of the Lord Jesus.
luke:24:3.csb: They went in but did not find the body of the Lord Jesus.
luke:24:3.nrsv: but when they went in, they did not find the body.

luke:24:4.niv: While they were wondering about this, suddenly two men in clothes that gleamed like lightning stood beside them.
luke:24:4.csb: While they were perplexed about this, suddenly two men stood by them in dazzling clothes.
luke:24:4.nrsv: While they were perplexed about this, suddenly two men in dazzling clothes stood beside them.

luke:24:5.niv: In their fright the women bowed down with their faces to the ground, but the men said to them, "Why do you look for the living among the dead?
luke:24:5.csb: So the women were terrified and bowed down to the ground. “Why are you looking for the living among the dead?” asked the men.
luke:24:5.nrsv: The women were terrified and bowed their faces to the ground, but the men said to them, "Why do you look for the living among the dead? He is not here, but has risen.

luke:24:6.niv: He is not here; he has risen! Remember how he told you, while he was still with you in Galilee:
luke:24:6.csb: “He is not here, but he has risen! Remember how he spoke to you when he was still in Galilee,
luke:24:6.nrsv: Remember how he told you, while he was still in Galilee,

luke:24:7.niv: 'The Son of Man must be delivered into the hands of sinful men, be crucified and on the third day be raised again.'"
luke:24:7.csb: saying, ‘It is necessary that the Son of Man be betrayed into the hands of sinful men, be crucified, and rise on the third day’?”
luke:24:7.nrsv: that the Son of Man must be handed over to sinners, and be crucified, and on the third day rise again."

luke:24:9.niv: When they came back from the tomb, they told all these things to the Eleven and to all the others.
luke:24:9.csb: Returning from the tomb, they reported all these things to the Eleven and to all the rest.
luke:24:9.nrsv: and returning from the tomb, they told all this to the eleven and to all the rest.

luke:24:10.niv: It was Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary the mother of James, and the others with them who told this to the apostles.
luke:24:10.csb: Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary the mother of James, and the other women with them were telling the apostles these things.
luke:24:10.nrsv: Now it was Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary the mother of James, and the other women with them who told this to the apostles.

luke:24:11.niv: But they did not believe the women, because their words seemed to them like nonsense.
luke:24:11.csb: But these words seemed like nonsense to them, and they did not believe the women.
luke:24:11.nrsv: But these words seemed to them an idle tale, and they did not believe them.

luke:24:12.niv: Peter, however, got up and ran to the tomb. Bending over, he saw the strips of linen lying by themselves, and he went away, wondering to himself what had happened.
luke:24:12.csb: Peter, however, got up and ran to the tomb. When he stooped to look in, he saw only the linen cloths. So he went away, amazed at what had happened.
luke:24:12.nrsv: But Peter got up and ran to the tomb; stooping and looking in, he saw the linen cloths by themselves; then he went home, amazed at what had happened.

luke:24:13.niv: Now that same day two of them were going to a village called Emmaus, about seven miles from Jerusalem.
luke:24:13.csb: Now that same day two of them were on their way to a village called Emmaus, which was about seven miles from Jerusalem.
luke:24:13.nrsv: Now on that same day two of them were going to a village called Emmaus, about seven miles from Jerusalem,

luke:24:14.niv: They were talking with each other about everything that had happened.
luke:24:14.csb: Together they were discussing everything that had taken place.
luke:24:14.nrsv: and talking with each other about all these things that had happened.

luke:24:15.niv: As they talked and discussed these things with each other, Jesus himself came up and walked along with them;
luke:24:15.csb: And while they were discussing and arguing, Jesus himself came near and began to walk along with them.
luke:24:15.nrsv: While they were talking and discussing, Jesus himself came near and went with them,

luke:24:16.niv: but they were kept from recognizing him.
luke:24:16.csb: But they were prevented from recognizing him.
luke:24:16.nrsv: but their eyes were kept from recognizing him.

luke:24:17.niv: He asked them, "What are you discussing together as you walk along?"
luke:24:17.csb: Then he asked them, “What is this dispute that you’re having with each other as you are walking?” And they stopped walking and looked discouraged.
luke:24:17.nrsv: And he said to them, "What are you discussing with each other while you walk along?" They stood still, looking sad.

luke:24:18.niv: They stood still, their faces downcast. One of them, named Cleopas, asked him, "Are you only a visitor to Jerusalem and do not know the things that have happened there in these days?"
luke:24:18.csb: The one named Cleopas answered him, “Are you the only visitor in Jerusalem who doesn’t know the things that happened there in these days?”
luke:24:18.nrsv: Then one of them, whose name was Cleopas, answered him, "Are you the only stranger in Jerusalem who does not know the things that have taken place there in these days?"

luke:24:19.csb: “What things?” he asked them. So they said to him, “The things concerning Jesus of Nazareth, who was a prophet powerful in action and speech before God and all the people,
luke:24:19.nrsv: He asked them, "What things?" They replied, "The things about Jesus of Nazareth, who was a prophet mighty in deed and word before God and all the people,

luke:24:20.niv: "About Jesus of Nazareth," they replied. "He was a prophet, powerful in word and deed before God and all the people. The chief priests and our rulers handed him over to be sentenced to death, and they crucified him;
luke:24:20.csb: and how our chief priests and leaders handed him over to be sentenced to death, and they crucified him.
luke:24:20.nrsv: and how our chief priests and leaders handed him over to be condemned to death and crucified him.

luke:24:21.niv: but we had hoped that he was the one who was going to redeem Israel. And what is more, it is the third day since all this took place.
luke:24:21.csb: But we were hoping that he was the one who was about to redeem Israel. Besides all this, it’s the third day since these things happened.
luke:24:21.nrsv: But we had hoped that he was the one to redeem Israel. Yes, and besides all this, it is now the third day since these things took place.

luke:24:22.niv: In addition, some of our women amazed us. They went to the tomb early this morning
luke:24:22.csb: Moreover, some women from our group astounded us. They arrived early at the tomb,
luke:24:22.nrsv: Moreover, some women of our group astounded us. They were at the tomb early this morning,

luke:24:23.niv: but didn't find his body. They came and told us that they had seen a vision of angels, who said he was alive.
luke:24:23.csb: and when they didn’t find his body, they came and reported that they had seen a vision of angels who said he was alive.
luke:24:23.nrsv: and when they did not find his body there, they came back and told us that they had indeed seen a vision of angels who said that he was alive.

luke:24:24.niv: Then some of our companions went to the tomb and found it just as the women had said, but him they did not see."
luke:24:24.csb: Some of those who were with us went to the tomb and found it just as the women had said, but they didn’t see him.”
luke:24:24.nrsv: Some of those who were with us went to the tomb and found it just as the women had said; but they did not see him."

luke:24:25.niv: He said to them, "How foolish you are, and how slow of heart to believe all that the prophets have spoken!
luke:24:25.csb: He said to them, “How foolish and slow you are to believe all that the prophets have spoken!
luke:24:25.nrsv: Then he said to them, "Oh, how foolish you are, and how slow of heart to believe all that the prophets have declared!

luke:24:26.niv: Did not the Christ have to suffer these things and then enter his glory?"
luke:24:26.csb: Wasn’t it necessary for the Messiah to suffer these things and enter into his glory?”
luke:24:26.nrsv: Was it not necessary that the Messiah should suffer these things and then enter into his glory?"

luke:24:27.niv: And beginning with Moses and all the Prophets, he explained to them what was said in all the Scriptures concerning himself.
luke:24:27.csb: Then beginning with Moses and all the Prophets, he interpreted for them the things concerning himself in all the Scriptures.
luke:24:27.nrsv: Then beginning with Moses and all the prophets, he interpreted to them the things about himself in all the scriptures.

luke:24:28.niv: As they approached the village to which they were going, Jesus acted as if he were going farther.
luke:24:28.csb: They came near the village where they were going, and he gave the impression that he was going farther.
luke:24:28.nrsv: As they came near the village to which they were going, he walked ahead as if he were going on.

luke:24:29.niv: But they urged him strongly, "Stay with us, for it is nearly evening; the day is almost over." So he went in to stay with them.
luke:24:29.csb: But they urged him, “Stay with us, because it’s almost evening, and now the day is almost over.” So he went in to stay with them.
luke:24:29.nrsv: But they urged him strongly, saying, "Stay with us, because it is almost evening and the day is now nearly over." So he went in to stay with them.

luke:24:30.niv: When he was at the table with them, he took bread, gave thanks, broke it and began to give it to them.
luke:24:30.csb: It was as he reclined at the table with them that he took the bread, blessed and broke it, and gave it to them.
luke:24:30.nrsv: When he was at the table with them, he took bread, blessed and broke it, and gave it to them.

luke:24:31.niv: Then their eyes were opened and they recognized him, and he disappeared from their sight.
luke:24:31.csb: Then their eyes were opened, and they recognized him, but he disappeared from their sight.
luke:24:31.nrsv: Then their eyes were opened, and they recognized him; and he vanished from their sight.

luke:24:32.niv: They asked each other, "Were not our hearts burning within us while he talked with us on the road and opened the Scriptures to us?"
luke:24:32.csb: They said to each other, “Weren’t our hearts burning within us while he was talking with us on the road and explaining the Scriptures to us?”
luke:24:32.nrsv: They said to each other, "Were not our hearts burning within us while he was talking to us on the road, while he was opening the scriptures to us?"

luke:24:33.niv: They got up and returned at once to Jerusalem. There they found the Eleven and those with them, assembled together
luke:24:33.csb: That very hour they got up and returned to Jerusalem. They found the Eleven and those with them gathered together,
luke:24:33.nrsv: That same hour they got up and returned to Jerusalem; and they found the eleven and their companions gathered together.

luke:24:34.niv: and saying, "It is true! The Lord has risen and has appeared to Simon."
luke:24:34.csb: who said, “The Lord has truly been raised and has appeared to Simon!”
luke:24:34.nrsv: They were saying, "The Lord has risen indeed, and he has appeared to Simon!"

luke:24:35.niv: Then the two told what had happened on the way, and how Jesus was recognized by them when he broke the bread.
luke:24:35.csb: Then they began to describe what had happened on the road and how he was made known to them in the breaking of the bread.
luke:24:35.nrsv: Then they told what had happened on the road, and how he had been made known to them in the breaking of the bread.

luke:24:36.niv: While they were still talking about this, Jesus himself stood among them and said to them, "Peace be with you."
luke:24:36.csb: As they were saying these things, he himself stood in their midst. He said to them, “Peace to you!”
luke:24:36.nrsv: While they were talking about this, Jesus himself stood among them and said to them, "Peace be with you."

luke:24:37.niv: They were startled and frightened, thinking they saw a ghost.
luke:24:37.csb: But they were startled and terrified and thought they were seeing a ghost.
luke:24:37.nrsv: They were startled and terrified, and thought that they were seeing a ghost.

luke:24:38.niv: He said to them, "Why are you troubled, and why do doubts rise in your minds?
luke:24:38.csb: “Why are you troubled?” he asked them. “And why do doubts arise in your hearts?
luke:24:38.nrsv: He said to them, "Why are you frightened, and why do doubts arise in your hearts?

luke:24:39.niv: Look at my hands and my feet. It is I myself! Touch me and see; a ghost does not have flesh and bones, as you see I have."
luke:24:39.csb: Look at my hands and my feet, that it is I myself! Touch me and see, because a ghost does not have flesh and bones as you can see I have.”
luke:24:39.nrsv: Look at my hands and my feet; see that it is I myself. Touch me and see; for a ghost does not have flesh and bones as you see that I have."

luke:24:40.niv: When he had said this, he showed them his hands and feet.
luke:24:40.csb: Having said this, he showed them his hands and feet.
luke:24:40.nrsv: And when he had said this, he showed them his hands and his feet.

luke:24:41.niv: And while they still did not believe it because of joy and amazement, he asked them, "Do you have anything here to eat?"
luke:24:41.csb: But while they still were amazed and in disbelief because of their joy, he asked them, “Do you have anything here to eat?”
luke:24:41.nrsv: While in their joy they were disbelieving and still wondering, he said to them, "Have you anything here to eat?"

luke:24:42.niv: They gave him a piece of broiled fish,
luke:24:42.csb: So they gave him a piece of a broiled fish,
luke:24:42.nrsv: They gave him a piece of broiled fish,

luke:24:43.niv: and he took it and ate it in their presence.
luke:24:43.csb: and he took it and ate in their presence.
luke:24:43.nrsv: and he took it and ate in their presence.

luke:24:44.niv: He said to them, "This is what I told you while I was still with you: Everything must be fulfilled that is written about me in the Law of Moses, the Prophets and the Psalms."
luke:24:44.csb: He told them, “These are my words that I spoke to you while I was still with you — that everything written about me in the Law of Moses, the Prophets, and the Psalms must be fulfilled.”
luke:24:44.nrsv: Then he said to them, "These are my words that I spoke to you while I was still with you-- that everything written about me in the law of Moses, the prophets, and the psalms must be fulfilled."

luke:24:45.niv: Then he opened their minds so they could understand the Scriptures.
luke:24:45.csb: Then he opened their minds to understand the Scriptures.
luke:24:45.nrsv: Then he opened their minds to understand the scriptures,

luke:24:46.niv: He told them, "This is what is written: The Christ will suffer and rise from the dead on the third day,
luke:24:46.csb: He also said to them, “This is what is written: The Messiah would suffer and rise from the dead the third day,
luke:24:46.nrsv: and he said to them, "Thus it is written, that the Messiah is to suffer and to rise from the dead on the third day,

luke:24:47.niv: and repentance and forgiveness of sins will be preached in his name to all nations, beginning at Jerusalem.
luke:24:47.csb: and repentance for forgiveness of sins would be proclaimed in his name to all the nations, beginning at Jerusalem.
luke:24:47.nrsv: and that repentance and forgiveness of sins is to be proclaimed in his name to all nations, beginning from Jerusalem.

luke:24:48.niv: You are witnesses of these things.
luke:24:48.csb: You are witnesses of these things.
luke:24:48.nrsv: You are witnesses of these things.

luke:24:49.niv: I am going to send you what my Father has promised; but stay in the city until you have been clothed with power from on high."
luke:24:49.csb: And look, I am sending you what my Father promised. As for you, stay in the city until you are empowered from on high.”
luke:24:49.nrsv: And see, I am sending upon you what my Father promised; so stay here in the city until you have been clothed with power from on high."

luke:24:50.niv: When he had led them out to the vicinity of Bethany, he lifted up his hands and blessed them.
luke:24:50.csb: Then he led them out to the vicinity of Bethany, and lifting up his hands he blessed them.
luke:24:50.nrsv: Then he led them out as far as Bethany, and, lifting up his hands, he blessed them.

luke:24:51.niv: While he was blessing them, he left them and was taken up into heaven.
luke:24:51.csb: And while he was blessing them, he left them and was carried up into heaven.
luke:24:51.nrsv: While he was blessing them, he withdrew from them and was carried up into heaven.

luke:24:52.niv: Then they worshiped him and returned to Jerusalem with great joy.
luke:24:52.csb: After worshiping him, they returned to Jerusalem with great joy.
luke:24:52.nrsv: And they worshiped him, and returned to Jerusalem with great joy;

luke:24:53.niv: And they stayed continually at the temple, praising God.
luke:24:53.csb: And they were continually in the temple praising God.
luke:24:53.nrsv: and they were continually in the temple blessing God.

john:1:1.niv: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.
john:1:1.csb: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.
john:1:1.nrsv: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.

john:1:2.niv: He was with God in the beginning.
john:1:2.csb: He was with God in the beginning.
john:1:2.nrsv: He was in the beginning with God.

john:1:3.niv: Through him all things were made; without him nothing was made that has been made.
john:1:3.csb: All things were created through him, and apart from him not one thing was created that has been created.
john:1:3.nrsv: All things came into being through him, and without him not one thing came into being. What has come into being

john:1:4.niv: In him was life, and that life was the light of men.
john:1:4.csb: In him was life, and that life was the light of men.
john:1:4.nrsv: in him was life, and the life was the light of all people.

john:1:5.niv: The light shines in the darkness, but the darkness has not understood it.
john:1:5.csb: That light shines in the darkness, and yet the darkness did not overcome it.
john:1:5.nrsv: The light shines in the darkness, and the darkness did not overcome it.

john:1:6.niv: There came a man who was sent from God; his name was John.
john:1:6.csb: There was a man sent from God whose name was John.
john:1:6.nrsv: There was a man sent from God, whose name was John.

john:1:7.niv: He came as a witness to testify concerning that light, so that through him all men might believe.
john:1:7.csb: He came as a witness to testify about the light, so that all might believe through him.
john:1:7.nrsv: He came as a witness to testify to the light, so that all might believe through him.

john:1:8.niv: He himself was not the light; he came only as a witness to the light.
john:1:8.csb: He was not the light, but he came to testify about the light.
john:1:8.nrsv: He himself was not the light, but he came to testify to the light.

john:1:9.niv: The true light that gives light to every man was coming into the world.
john:1:9.csb: The true light that gives light to everyone, was coming into the world.
john:1:9.nrsv: The true light, which enlightens everyone, was coming into the world.

john:1:10.niv: He was in the world, and though the world was made through him, the world did not recognize him.
john:1:10.csb: He was in the world, and the world was created through him, and yet the world did not recognize him.
john:1:10.nrsv: He was in the world, and the world came into being through him; yet the world did not know him.

john:1:11.niv: He came to that which was his own, but his own did not receive him.
john:1:11.csb: He came to his own, and his own people did not receive him.
john:1:11.nrsv: He came to what was his own, and his own people did not accept him.

john:1:12.niv: Yet to all who received him, to those who believed in his name, he gave the right to become children of God--
john:1:12.csb: But to all who did receive him, he gave them the right to be children of God, to those who believe in his name,
john:1:12.nrsv: But to all who received him, who believed in his name, he gave power to become children of God,

john:1:13.niv: children born not of natural descent, nor of human decision or a husband's will, but born of God.
john:1:13.csb: who were born, not of natural descent, or of the will of the flesh, or of the will of man, but of God.
john:1:13.nrsv: who were born, not of blood or of the will of the flesh or of the will of man, but of God.

john:1:14.niv: The Word became flesh and made his dwelling among us. We have seen his glory, the glory of the One and Only, who came from the Father, full of grace and truth.
john:1:14.csb: The Word became flesh and dwelt among us. We observed his glory, the glory as the one and only Son from the Father, full of grace and truth.
john:1:14.nrsv: And the Word became flesh and lived among us, and we have seen his glory, the glory as of a father's only son, full of grace and truth.

john:1:15.niv: John testifies concerning him. He cries out, saying, "This was he of whom I said, 'He who comes after me has surpassed me because he was before me.'"
john:1:15.csb: (John testified concerning him and exclaimed, “This was the one of whom I said, ‘The one coming after me ranks ahead of me, because he existed before me.’”)
john:1:15.nrsv: (John testified to him and cried out, "This was he of whom I said, 'He who comes after me ranks ahead of me because he was before me.'")

john:1:16.niv: From the fullness of his grace we have all received one blessing after another.
john:1:16.csb: Indeed, we have all received grace upon grace from his fullness,
john:1:16.nrsv: From his fullness we have all received, grace upon grace.

john:1:17.niv: For the law was given through Moses; grace and truth came through Jesus Christ.
john:1:17.csb: for the law was given through Moses; grace and truth came through Jesus Christ.
john:1:17.nrsv: The law indeed was given through Moses; grace and truth came through Jesus Christ.

john:1:18.niv: No one has ever seen God, but God the One and Only,, who is at the Father's side, has made him known.
john:1:18.csb: No one has ever seen God. The one and only Son, who is himself God and is at the Father’s side —he has revealed him.
john:1:18.nrsv: No one has ever seen God. It is God the only Son, who is close to the Father's heart, who has made him known.

john:1:19.niv: Now this was John's testimony when the Jews of Jerusalem sent priests and Levites to ask him who he was.
john:1:19.csb: This was John’s testimony when the Jews from Jerusalem sent priests and Levites to ask him, “Who are you?”
john:1:19.nrsv: This is the testimony given by John when the Jews sent priests and Levites from Jerusalem to ask him, "Who are you?"

john:1:20.niv: He did not fail to confess, but confessed freely, "I am not the Christ. "
john:1:20.csb: He didn’t deny it but confessed: “I am not the Messiah.”
john:1:20.nrsv: He confessed and did not deny it, but confessed, "I am not the Messiah."

john:1:21.niv: They asked him, "Then who are you? Are you Elijah?" He said, "I am not.Are you the Prophet?" He answered, "No."
john:1:21.csb: “What then?” they asked him. “Are you Elijah?” “I am not,” he said. “Are you the Prophet?” “No,” he answered.
john:1:21.nrsv: And they asked him, "What then? Are you Elijah?" He said, "I am not." "Are you the prophet?" He answered, "No."

john:1:22.niv: Finally they said, "Who are you? Give us an answer to take back to those who sent us. What do you say about yourself?"
john:1:22.csb: “Who are you, then?” they asked. “We need to give an answer to those who sent us. What can you tell us about yourself?”
john:1:22.nrsv: Then they said to him, "Who are you? Let us have an answer for those who sent us. What do you say about yourself?"

john:1:23.niv: John replied in the words of Isaiah the prophet, "I am the voice of one calling in the desert, 'Make straight the way for the Lord.'"
john:1:23.csb: He said, “I am a voice of one crying out in the wilderness: Make straight the way of the Lord — just as Isaiah the prophet said.”
john:1:23.nrsv: He said, "I am the voice of one crying out in the wilderness, 'Make straight the way of the Lord,'" as the prophet Isaiah said.

john:1:24.niv: Now some Pharisees who had been sent
john:1:24.csb: Now they had been sent from the Pharisees.
john:1:24.nrsv: Now they had been sent from the Pharisees.

john:1:25.niv: questioned him, "Why then do you baptize if you are not the Christ, nor Elijah, nor the Prophet?"
john:1:25.csb: So they asked him, “Why then do you baptize if you aren’t the Messiah, or Elijah, or the Prophet?”
john:1:25.nrsv: They asked him, "Why then are you baptizing if you are neither the Messiah, nor Elijah, nor the prophet?"

john:1:26.niv: "I baptize with water," John replied, "but among you stands one you do not know.
john:1:26.csb: “I baptize with water,” John answered them. “Someone stands among you, but you don’t know him.
john:1:26.nrsv: John answered them, "I baptize with water. Among you stands one whom you do not know,

john:1:27.niv: He is the one who comes after me, the thongs of whose sandals I am not worthy to untie."
john:1:27.csb: He is the one coming after me, whose sandal strap I’m not worthy to untie.”
john:1:27.nrsv: the one who is coming after me; I am not worthy to untie the thong of his sandal."

john:1:28.niv: This all happened at Bethany on the other side of the Jordan, where John was baptizing.
john:1:28.csb: All this happened in Bethany across the Jordan, where John was baptizing.
john:1:28.nrsv: This took place in Bethany across the Jordan where John was baptizing.

john:1:29.niv: The next day John saw Jesus coming toward him and said, "Look, the Lamb of God, who takes away the sin of the world!
john:1:29.csb: The next day John saw Jesus coming toward him and said, “Here is the Lamb of God, who takes away the sin of the world!
john:1:29.nrsv: The next day he saw Jesus coming toward him and declared, "Here is the Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world!

john:1:30.niv: This is the one I meant when I said, 'A man who comes after me has surpassed me because he was before me.'
john:1:30.csb: This is the one I told you about: ‘After me comes a man who ranks ahead of me, because he existed before me.’
john:1:30.nrsv: This is he of whom I said, 'After me comes a man who ranks ahead of me because he was before me.'

john:1:31.niv: I myself did not know him, but the reason I came baptizing with water was that he might be revealed to Israel."
john:1:31.csb: I didn’t know him, but I came baptizing with water so he might be revealed to Israel.”
john:1:31.nrsv: I myself did not know him; but I came baptizing with water for this reason, that he might be revealed to Israel."

john:1:32.niv: Then John gave this testimony: "I saw the Spirit come down from heaven as a dove and remain on him.
john:1:32.csb: And John testified, “I saw the Spirit descending from heaven like a dove, and he rested on him.
john:1:32.nrsv: And John testified, "I saw the Spirit descending from heaven like a dove, and it remained on him.

john:1:33.niv: I would not have known him, except that the one who sent me to baptize with water told me, 'The man on whom you see the Spirit come down and remain is he who will baptize with the Holy Spirit.'
john:1:33.csb: I didn’t know him, but he who sent me to baptize with water told me, ‘The one you see the Spirit descending and resting on — he is the one who baptizes with the Holy Spirit.’
john:1:33.nrsv: I myself did not know him, but the one who sent me to baptize with water said to me, 'He on whom you see the Spirit descend and remain is the one who baptizes with the Holy Spirit.'

john:1:34.niv: I have seen and I testify that this is the Son of God."
john:1:34.csb: I have seen and testified that this is the Son of God.”
john:1:34.nrsv: And I myself have seen and have testified that this is the Son of God."

john:1:35.niv: The next day John was there again with two of his disciples.
john:1:35.csb: The next day, John was standing with two of his disciples.
john:1:35.nrsv: The next day John again was standing with two of his disciples,

john:1:36.niv: When he saw Jesus passing by, he said, "Look, the Lamb of God!"
john:1:36.csb: When he saw Jesus passing by, he said, “Look, the Lamb of God!”
john:1:36.nrsv: and as he watched Jesus walk by, he exclaimed, "Look, here is the Lamb of God!"

john:1:37.niv: When the two disciples heard him say this, they followed Jesus.
john:1:37.csb: The two disciples heard him say this and followed Jesus.
john:1:37.nrsv: The two disciples heard him say this, and they followed Jesus.

john:1:38.niv: Turning around, Jesus saw them following and asked, "What do you want?" They said, "Rabbi" (which means Teacher), "where are you staying?"
john:1:38.csb: When Jesus turned and noticed them following him, he asked them, “What are you looking for?” They said to him, “Rabbi” (which means “Teacher”), “where are you staying?”
john:1:38.nrsv: When Jesus turned and saw them following, he said to them, "What are you looking for?" They said to him, "Rabbi" (which translated means Teacher), "where are you staying?"

john:1:39.niv: "Come," he replied, "and you will see." So they went and saw where he was staying, and spent that day with him. It was about the tenth hour.
john:1:39.csb: “Come and you’ll see,” he replied. So they went and saw where he was staying, and they stayed with him that day. It was about four in the afternoon.
john:1:39.nrsv: He said to them, "Come and see." They came and saw where he was staying, and they remained with him that day. It was about four o'clock in the afternoon.

john:1:40.niv: Andrew, Simon Peter's brother, was one of the two who heard what John had said and who had followed Jesus.
john:1:40.csb: Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother, was one of the two who heard John and followed him.
john:1:40.nrsv: One of the two who heard John speak and followed him was Andrew, Simon Peter's brother.

john:1:41.niv: The first thing Andrew did was to find his brother Simon and tell him, "We have found the Messiah" (that is, the Christ).
john:1:41.csb: He first found his own brother Simon and told him, “We have found the Messiah” (which is translated “the Christ”),
john:1:41.nrsv: He first found his brother Simon and said to him, "We have found the Messiah" (which is translated Anointed).

john:1:42.niv: And he brought him to Jesus. Jesus looked at him and said, "You are Simon son of John. You will be called Cephas" (which, when translated, is Peter ).
john:1:42.csb: and he brought Simon to Jesus. When Jesus saw him, he said, “You are Simon, son of John. You will be called Cephas” (which is translated “Peter” ).
john:1:42.nrsv: He brought Simon to Jesus, who looked at him and said, "You are Simon son of John. You are to be called Cephas" (which is translated Peter).

john:1:43.niv: The next day Jesus decided to leave for Galilee. Finding Philip, he said to him, "Follow me."
john:1:43.csb: The next day Jesus decided to leave for Galilee. He found Philip and told him, “Follow me.”
john:1:43.nrsv: The next day Jesus decided to go to Galilee. He found Philip and said to him, "Follow me."

john:1:44.niv: Philip, like Andrew and Peter, was from the town of Bethsaida.
john:1:44.csb: Now Philip was from Bethsaida, the hometown of Andrew and Peter.
john:1:44.nrsv: Now Philip was from Bethsaida, the city of Andrew and Peter.

john:1:45.niv: Philip found Nathanael and told him, "We have found the one Moses wrote about in the Law, and about whom the prophets also wrote--Jesus of Nazareth, the son of Joseph."
john:1:45.csb: Philip found Nathanael and told him, “We have found the one Moses wrote about in the law (and so did the prophets ): Jesus the son of Joseph, from Nazareth.”
john:1:45.nrsv: Philip found Nathanael and said to him, "We have found him about whom Moses in the law and also the prophets wrote, Jesus son of Joseph from Nazareth."

john:1:46.niv: "Nazareth! Can anything good come from there?" Nathanael asked. "Come and see," said Philip.
john:1:46.csb: “Can anything good come out of Nazareth?” Nathanael asked him. “Come and see,” Philip answered.
john:1:46.nrsv: Nathanael said to him, "Can anything good come out of Nazareth?" Philip said to him, "Come and see."

john:1:47.niv: When Jesus saw Nathanael approaching, he said of him, "Here is a true Israelite, in whom there is nothing false."
john:1:47.csb: Then Jesus saw Nathanael coming toward him and said about him, “Here truly is an Israelite in whom there is no deceit.”
john:1:47.nrsv: When Jesus saw Nathanael coming toward him, he said of him, "Here is truly an Israelite in whom there is no deceit!"

john:1:48.niv: "How do you know me?" Nathanael asked. Jesus answered, "I saw you while you were still under the fig tree before Philip called you."
john:1:48.csb: “How do you know me?” Nathanael asked. “Before Philip called you, when you were under the fig tree, I saw you,” Jesus answered.
john:1:48.nrsv: Nathanael asked him, "Where did you get to know me?" Jesus answered, "I saw you under the fig tree before Philip called you."

john:1:49.niv: Then Nathanael declared, "Rabbi, you are the Son of God; you are the King of Israel."
john:1:49.csb: “Rabbi,” Nathanael replied, “You are the Son of God; you are the King of Israel!”
john:1:49.nrsv: Nathanael replied, "Rabbi, you are the Son of God! You are the King of Israel!"

john:1:50.niv: Jesus said, "You believe because I told you I saw you under the fig tree. You shall see greater things than that."
john:1:50.csb: Jesus responded to him, “Do you believe because I told you I saw you under the fig tree? You will see greater things than this.”
john:1:50.nrsv: Jesus answered, "Do you believe because I told you that I saw you under the fig tree? You will see greater things than these."

john:1:51.niv: He then added, "I tell you the truth, you shall see heaven open, and the angels of God ascending and descending on the Son of Man."
john:1:51.csb: Then he said, “Truly I tell you, you will see heaven opened and the angels of God ascending and descending on the Son of Man.”
john:1:51.nrsv: And he said to him, "Very truly, I tell you, you will see heaven opened and the angels of God ascending and descending upon the Son of Man."

john:2:1.niv: On the third day a wedding took place at Cana in Galilee. Jesus' mother was there,
john:2:1.csb: On the third day a wedding took place in Cana of Galilee. Jesus’s mother was there, and
john:2:1.nrsv: On the third day there was a wedding in Cana of Galilee, and the mother of Jesus was there.

john:2:2.niv: and Jesus and his disciples had also been invited to the wedding.
john:2:2.csb: Jesus and his disciples were invited to the wedding as well.
john:2:2.nrsv: Jesus and his disciples had also been invited to the wedding.

john:2:3.niv: When the wine was gone, Jesus' mother said to him, "They have no more wine."
john:2:3.csb: When the wine ran out, Jesus’s mother told him, “They don’t have any wine.”
john:2:3.nrsv: When the wine gave out, the mother of Jesus said to him, "They have no wine."

john:2:4.niv: "Dear woman, why do you involve me?" Jesus replied, "My time has not yet come."
john:2:4.csb: “What does that have to do with you and me, woman?” Jesus asked. “My hour has not yet come.”
john:2:4.nrsv: And Jesus said to her, "Woman, what concern is that to you and to me? My hour has not yet come."

john:2:5.niv: His mother said to the servants, "Do whatever he tells you."
john:2:5.csb: “Do whatever he tells you,” his mother told the servants.
john:2:5.nrsv: His mother said to the servants, "Do whatever he tells you."

john:2:6.niv: Nearby stood six stone water jars, the kind used by the Jews for ceremonial washing, each holding from twenty to thirty gallons.
john:2:6.csb: Now six stone water jars had been set there for Jewish purification. Each contained twenty or thirty gallons.
john:2:6.nrsv: Now standing there were six stone water jars for the Jewish rites of purification, each holding twenty or thirty gallons.

john:2:7.niv: Jesus said to the servants, "Fill the jars with water"; so they filled them to the brim.
john:2:7.csb: “Fill the jars with water,” Jesus told them. So they filled them to the brim.
john:2:7.nrsv: Jesus said to them, "Fill the jars with water." And they filled them up to the brim.

john:2:8.niv: Then he told them, "Now draw some out and take it to the master of the banquet."
john:2:8.csb: Then he said to them, “Now draw some out and take it to the headwaiter.” And they did.
john:2:8.nrsv: He said to them, "Now draw some out, and take it to the chief steward." So they took it.

john:2:9.niv: They did so, and the master of the banquet tasted the water that had been turned into wine. He did not realize where it had come from, though the servants who had drawn the water knew. Then he called the bridegroom aside
john:2:9.csb: When the headwaiter tasted the water (after it had become wine), he did not know where it came from — though the servants who had drawn the water knew. He called the groom
john:2:9.nrsv: When the steward tasted the water that had become wine, and did not know where it came from (though the servants who had drawn the water knew), the steward called the bridegroom

john:2:10.niv: and said, "Everyone brings out the choice wine first and then the cheaper wine after the guests have had too much to drink; but you have saved the best till now."
john:2:10.csb: and told him, “Everyone sets out the fine wine first, then, after people are drunk, the inferior. But you have kept the fine wine until now.”
john:2:10.nrsv: and said to him, "Everyone serves the good wine first, and then the inferior wine after the guests have become drunk. But you have kept the good wine until now."

john:2:11.niv: This, the first of his miraculous signs, Jesus performed in Cana of Galilee. He thus revealed his glory, and his disciples put their faith in him.
john:2:11.csb: Jesus did this, the first of his signs, in Cana of Galilee. He revealed his glory, and his disciples believed in him.
