john:7:4.csb: For no one does anything in secret while he’s seeking public recognition. If you do these things, show yourself to the world.”
john:7:4.nrsv: for no one who wants to be widely known acts in secret. If you do these things, show yourself to the world."

john:7:5.niv: For even his own brothers did not believe in him.
john:7:5.csb: (For not even his brothers believed in him.)
john:7:5.nrsv: (For not even his brothers believed in him.)

john:7:6.niv: Therefore Jesus told them, "The right time for me has not yet come; for you any time is right.
john:7:6.csb: Jesus told them, “My time has not yet arrived, but your time is always at hand.
john:7:6.nrsv: Jesus said to them, "My time has not yet come, but your time is always here.

john:7:7.niv: The world cannot hate you, but it hates me because I testify that what it does is evil.
john:7:7.csb: The world cannot hate you, but it does hate me because I testify about it — that its works are evil.
john:7:7.nrsv: The world cannot hate you, but it hates me because I testify against it that its works are evil.

john:7:8.niv: You go to the Feast. I am not yet going up to this Feast, because for me the right time has not yet come."
john:7:8.csb: Go up to the festival yourselves. I’m not going up to this festival, because my time has not yet fully come.”
john:7:8.nrsv: Go to the festival yourselves. I am not going to this festival, for my time has not yet fully come."

john:7:9.niv: Having said this, he stayed in Galilee.
john:7:9.csb: After he had said these things, he stayed in Galilee.
john:7:9.nrsv: After saying this, he remained in Galilee.

john:7:10.niv: However, after his brothers had left for the Feast, he went also, not publicly, but in secret.
john:7:10.csb: After his brothers had gone up to the festival, then he also went up, not openly but secretly.
john:7:10.nrsv: But after his brothers had gone to the festival, then he also went, not publicly but as it were in secret.

john:7:11.niv: Now at the Feast the Jews were watching for him and asking, "Where is that man?"
john:7:11.csb: The Jews were looking for him at the festival and saying, “Where is he?”
john:7:11.nrsv: The Jews were looking for him at the festival and saying, "Where is he?"

john:7:12.niv: Among the crowds there was widespread whispering about him. Some said, "He is a good man."
john:7:12.csb: And there was a lot of murmuring about him among the crowds. Some were saying, “He’s a good man.” Others were saying, “No, on the contrary, he’s deceiving the people.”
john:7:12.nrsv: And there was considerable complaining about him among the crowds. While some were saying, "He is a good man," others were saying, "No, he is deceiving the crowd."

john:7:13.niv: Others replied, "No, he deceives the people." But no one would say anything publicly about him for fear of the Jews.
john:7:13.csb: Still, nobody was talking publicly about him for fear of the Jews.
john:7:13.nrsv: Yet no one would speak openly about him for fear of the Jews.

john:7:14.niv: Not until halfway through the Feast did Jesus go up to the temple courts and begin to teach.
john:7:14.csb: When the festival was already half over, Jesus went up into the temple and began to teach.
john:7:14.nrsv: About the middle of the festival Jesus went up into the temple and began to teach.

john:7:15.niv: The Jews were amazed and asked, "How did this man get such learning without having studied?"
john:7:15.csb: Then the Jews were amazed and said, “How is this man so learned, since he hasn’t been trained?”
john:7:15.nrsv: The Jews were astonished at it, saying, "How does this man have such learning, when he has never been taught?"

john:7:16.niv: Jesus answered, "My teaching is not my own. It comes from him who sent me.
john:7:16.csb: Jesus answered them, “My teaching isn’t mine but is from the one who sent me.
john:7:16.nrsv: Then Jesus answered them, "My teaching is not mine but his who sent me.

john:7:17.niv: If anyone chooses to do God's will, he will find out whether my teaching comes from God or whether I speak on my own.
john:7:17.csb: If anyone wants to do his will, he will know whether the teaching is from God or whether I am speaking on my own.
john:7:17.nrsv: Anyone who resolves to do the will of God will know whether the teaching is from God or whether I am speaking on my own.

john:7:18.niv: He who speaks on his own does so to gain honor for himself, but he who works for the honor of the one who sent him is a man of truth; there is nothing false about him.
john:7:18.csb: The one who speaks on his own seeks his own glory; but he who seeks the glory of the one who sent him is true, and there is no unrighteousness in him.
john:7:18.nrsv: Those who speak on their own seek their own glory; but the one who seeks the glory of him who sent him is true, and there is nothing false in him.

john:7:19.niv: Has not Moses given you the law? Yet not one of you keeps the law. Why are you trying to kill me?"
john:7:19.csb: Didn’t Moses give you the law? Yet none of you keeps the law. Why are you trying to kill me?”
john:7:19.nrsv: "Did not Moses give you the law? Yet none of you keeps the law. Why are you looking for an opportunity to kill me?"

john:7:20.niv: "You are demon-possessed," the crowd answered. "Who is trying to kill you?"
john:7:20.csb: “You have a demon!” the crowd responded. “Who is trying to kill you?”
john:7:20.nrsv: The crowd answered, "You have a demon! Who is trying to kill you?"

john:7:21.niv: Jesus said to them, "I did one miracle, and you are all astonished.
john:7:21.csb: “I performed one work, and you are all amazed,” Jesus answered.
john:7:21.nrsv: Jesus answered them, "I performed one work, and all of you are astonished.

john:7:22.niv: Yet, because Moses gave you circumcision (though actually it did not come from Moses, but from the patriarchs), you circumcise a child on the Sabbath.
john:7:22.csb: “This is why Moses has given you circumcision — not that it comes from Moses but from the fathers — and you circumcise a man on the Sabbath.
john:7:22.nrsv: Moses gave you circumcision (it is, of course, not from Moses, but from the patriarchs), and you circumcise a man on the sabbath.

john:7:23.niv: Now if a child can be circumcised on the Sabbath so that the law of Moses may not be broken, why are you angry with me for healing the whole man on the Sabbath?
john:7:23.csb: If a man receives circumcision on the Sabbath so that the law of Moses won’t be broken, are you angry at me because I made a man entirely well on the Sabbath?
john:7:23.nrsv: If a man receives circumcision on the sabbath in order that the law of Moses may not be broken, are you angry with me because I healed a man's whole body on the sabbath?

john:7:24.niv: Stop judging by mere appearances, and make a right judgment."
john:7:24.csb: Stop judging according to outward appearances; rather judge according to righteous judgment.”
john:7:24.nrsv: Do not judge by appearances, but judge with right judgment."

john:7:25.niv: At that point some of the people of Jerusalem began to ask, "Isn't this the man they are trying to kill?
john:7:25.csb: Some of the people of Jerusalem were saying, “Isn’t this the man they are trying to kill?
john:7:25.nrsv: Now some of the people of Jerusalem were saying, "Is not this the man whom they are trying to kill?

john:7:26.niv: Here he is, speaking publicly, and they are not saying a word to him. Have the authorities really concluded that he is the Christ?
john:7:26.csb: Yet, look, he’s speaking publicly and they’re saying nothing to him. Can it be true that the authorities know he is the Messiah?
john:7:26.nrsv: And here he is, speaking openly, but they say nothing to him! Can it be that the authorities really know that this is the Messiah?

john:7:27.niv: But we know where this man is from; when the Christ comes, no one will know where he is from."
john:7:27.csb: But we know where this man is from. When the Messiah comes, nobody will know where he is from.”
john:7:27.nrsv: Yet we know where this man is from; but when the Messiah comes, no one will know where he is from."

john:7:28.niv: Then Jesus, still teaching in the temple courts, cried out, "Yes, you know me, and you know where I am from. I am not here on my own, but he who sent me is true. You do not know him,
john:7:28.csb: As he was teaching in the temple, Jesus cried out, “You know me and you know where I am from. Yet I have not come on my own, but the one who sent me is true. You don’t know him;
john:7:28.nrsv: Then Jesus cried out as he was teaching in the temple, "You know me, and you know where I am from. I have not come on my own. But the one who sent me is true, and you do not know him.

john:7:29.niv: but I know him because I am from him and he sent me."
john:7:29.csb: I know him because I am from him, and he sent me.”
john:7:29.nrsv: I know him, because I am from him, and he sent me."

john:7:30.niv: At this they tried to seize him, but no one laid a hand on him, because his time had not yet come.
john:7:30.csb: Then they tried to seize him. Yet no one laid a hand on him because his hour had not yet come.
john:7:30.nrsv: Then they tried to arrest him, but no one laid hands on him, because his hour had not yet come.

john:7:31.niv: Still, many in the crowd put their faith in him. They said, "When the Christ comes, will he do more miraculous signs than this man?"
john:7:31.csb: However, many from the crowd believed in him and said, “When the Messiah comes, he won’t perform more signs than this man has done, will he?”
john:7:31.nrsv: Yet many in the crowd believed in him and were saying, "When the Messiah comes, will he do more signs than this man has done?"

john:7:32.niv: The Pharisees heard the crowd whispering such things about him. Then the chief priests and the Pharisees sent temple guards to arrest him.
john:7:32.csb: The Pharisees heard the crowd murmuring these things about him, and so the chief priests and the Pharisees sent servants to arrest him.
john:7:32.nrsv: The Pharisees heard the crowd muttering such things about him, and the chief priests and Pharisees sent temple police to arrest him.

john:7:33.niv: Jesus said, "I am with you for only a short time, and then I go to the one who sent me.
john:7:33.csb: Then Jesus said, “I am only with you for a short time. Then I’m going to the one who sent me.
john:7:33.nrsv: Jesus then said, "I will be with you a little while longer, and then I am going to him who sent me.

john:7:34.niv: You will look for me, but you will not find me; and where I am, you cannot come."
john:7:34.csb: You will look for me, but you will not find me; and where I am, you cannot come.”
john:7:34.nrsv: You will search for me, but you will not find me; and where I am, you cannot come."

john:7:35.niv: The Jews said to one another, "Where does this man intend to go that we cannot find him? Will he go where our people live scattered among the Greeks, and teach the Greeks?
john:7:35.csb: Then the Jews said to one another, “Where does he intend to go so we won’t find him? He doesn’t intend to go to the Jewish people dispersed among the Greeks and teach the Greeks, does he?
john:7:35.nrsv: The Jews said to one another, "Where does this man intend to go that we will not find him? Does he intend to go to the Dispersion among the Greeks and teach the Greeks?

john:7:36.niv: What did he mean when he said, 'You will look for me, but you will not find me,' and 'Where I am, you cannot come'?"
john:7:36.csb: What is this remark he made: ‘You will look for me, and you will not find me; and where I am, you cannot come’?”
john:7:36.nrsv: What does he mean by saying, 'You will search for me and you will not find me' and 'Where I am, you cannot come'?"

john:7:37.niv: On the last and greatest day of the Feast, Jesus stood and said in a loud voice, "If anyone is thirsty, let him come to me and drink.
john:7:37.csb: On the last and most important day of the festival, Jesus stood up and cried out, “If anyone is thirsty, let him come to me and drink.
john:7:37.nrsv: On the last day of the festival, the great day, while Jesus was standing there, he cried out, "Let anyone who is thirsty come to me,

john:7:38.niv: Whoever believes in me, as the Scripture has said, streams of living water will flow from within him."
john:7:38.csb: The one who believes in me, as the Scripture has said, will have streams of living water flow from deep within him.”
john:7:38.nrsv: and let the one who believes in me drink. As the scripture has said, 'Out of the believer's heart shall flow rivers of living water.'"

john:7:39.niv: By this he meant the Spirit, whom those who believed in him were later to receive. Up to that time the Spirit had not been given, since Jesus had not yet been glorified.
john:7:39.csb: He said this about the Spirit. Those who believed in Jesus were going to receive the Spirit, for the Spirit had not yet been given because Jesus had not yet been glorified.
john:7:39.nrsv: Now he said this about the Spirit, which believers in him were to receive; for as yet there was no Spirit, because Jesus was not yet glorified.

john:7:40.niv: On hearing his words, some of the people said, "Surely this man is the Prophet."
john:7:40.csb: When some from the crowd heard these words, they said, “This truly is the Prophet.”
john:7:40.nrsv: When they heard these words, some in the crowd said, "This is really the prophet."

john:7:41.csb: Others said, “This is the Messiah.” But some said, “Surely the Messiah doesn’t come from Galilee, does he?
john:7:41.nrsv: Others said, "This is the Messiah." But some asked, "Surely the Messiah does not come from Galilee, does he?

john:7:42.niv: Still others asked, "How can the Christ come from Galilee? Does not the Scripture say that the Christ will come from David's family and from Bethlehem, the town where David lived?"
john:7:42.csb: Doesn’t the Scripture say that the Messiah comes from David’s offspring and from the town of Bethlehem, where David lived?”
john:7:42.nrsv: Has not the scripture said that the Messiah is descended from David and comes from Bethlehem, the village where David lived?"

john:7:43.niv: Thus the people were divided because of Jesus.
john:7:43.csb: So the crowd was divided because of him.
john:7:43.nrsv: So there was a division in the crowd because of him.

john:7:44.niv: Some wanted to seize him, but no one laid a hand on him.
john:7:44.csb: Some of them wanted to seize him, but no one laid hands on him.
john:7:44.nrsv: Some of them wanted to arrest him, but no one laid hands on him.

john:7:45.niv: Finally the temple guards went back to the chief priests and Pharisees, who asked them, "Why didn't you bring him in?"
john:7:45.csb: Then the servants came to the chief priests and Pharisees, who asked them, “Why didn’t you bring him?”
john:7:45.nrsv: Then the temple police went back to the chief priests and Pharisees, who asked them, "Why did you not arrest him?"

john:7:46.niv: "No one ever spoke the way this man does," the guards declared.
john:7:46.csb: The servants answered, “No man ever spoke like this!”
john:7:46.nrsv: The police answered, "Never has anyone spoken like this!"

john:7:47.niv: "You mean he has deceived you also?" the Pharisees retorted.
john:7:47.csb: Then the Pharisees responded to them: “Are you fooled too?
john:7:47.nrsv: Then the Pharisees replied, "Surely you have not been deceived too, have you?

john:7:48.niv: "Has any of the rulers or of the Pharisees believed in him?
john:7:48.csb: Have any of the rulers or Pharisees believed in him?
john:7:48.nrsv: Has any one of the authorities or of the Pharisees believed in him?

john:7:49.niv: No! But this mob that knows nothing of the law--there is a curse on them."
john:7:49.csb: But this crowd, which doesn’t know the law, is accursed.”
john:7:49.nrsv: But this crowd, which does not know the law-- they are accursed."

john:7:50.niv: Nicodemus, who had gone to Jesus earlier and who was one of their own number, asked,
john:7:50.csb: Nicodemus — the one who came to him previously and who was one of them — said to them,
john:7:50.nrsv: Nicodemus, who had gone to Jesus before, and who was one of them, asked,

john:7:51.niv: "Does our law condemn anyone without first hearing him to find out what he is doing?"
john:7:51.csb: “Our law doesn’t judge a man before it hears from him and knows what he’s doing, does it?”
john:7:51.nrsv: "Our law does not judge people without first giving them a hearing to find out what they are doing, does it?"

john:7:52.niv: They replied, "Are you from Galilee, too? Look into it, and you will find that a prophet does not come out of Galilee."
john:7:52.csb: “You aren’t from Galilee too, are you?” they replied. “Investigate and you will see that no prophet arises from Galilee.” [The earliest mss do not include 7:53–8:11.]
john:7:52.nrsv: They replied, "Surely you are not also from Galilee, are you? Search and you will see that no prophet is to arise from Galilee."

john:7:53.niv: Then each went to his own home.
john:7:53.csb: Then each one went to his house.
john:7:53.nrsv: Then each of them went home,

john:8:1.niv: But Jesus went to the Mount of Olives.
john:8:1.csb: But Jesus went to the Mount of Olives.
john:8:1.nrsv: while Jesus went to the Mount of Olives.

john:8:2.niv: At dawn he appeared again in the temple courts, where all the people gathered around him, and he sat down to teach them.
john:8:2.csb: At dawn he went to the temple again, and all the people were coming to him. He sat down and began to teach them.
john:8:2.nrsv: Early in the morning he came again to the temple. All the people came to him and he sat down and began to teach them.

john:8:3.niv: The teachers of the law and the Pharisees brought in a woman caught in adultery. They made her stand before the group
john:8:3.csb: Then the scribes and the Pharisees brought a woman caught in adultery, making her stand in the center.
john:8:3.nrsv: The scribes and the Pharisees brought a woman who had been caught in adultery; and making her stand before all of them,

john:8:4.niv: and said to Jesus, "Teacher, this woman was caught in the act of adultery.
john:8:4.csb: “Teacher,” they said to him, “this woman was caught in the act of committing adultery.
john:8:4.nrsv: they said to him, "Teacher, this woman was caught in the very act of committing adultery.

john:8:5.niv: In the Law Moses commanded us to stone such women. Now what do you say?"
john:8:5.csb: In the law Moses commanded us to stone such women. So what do you say?”
john:8:5.nrsv: Now in the law Moses commanded us to stone such women. Now what do you say?"

john:8:6.niv: They were using this question as a trap, in order to have a basis for accusing him.
john:8:6.csb: They asked this to trap him, in order that they might have evidence to accuse him. Jesus stooped down and started writing on the ground with his finger.
john:8:6.nrsv: They said this to test him, so that they might have some charge to bring against him. Jesus bent down and wrote with his finger on the ground.

john:8:7.niv: But Jesus bent down and started to write on the ground with his finger. When they kept on questioning him, he straightened up and said to them, "If any one of you is without sin, let him be the first to throw a stone at her."
john:8:7.csb: When they persisted in questioning him, he stood up and said to them, “The one without sin among you should be the first to throw a stone at her.”
john:8:7.nrsv: When they kept on questioning him, he straightened up and said to them, "Let anyone among you who is without sin be the first to throw a stone at her."

john:8:8.niv: Again he stooped down and wrote on the ground.
john:8:8.csb: Then he stooped down again and continued writing on the ground.
john:8:8.nrsv: And once again he bent down and wrote on the ground.

john:8:9.niv: At this, those who heard began to go away one at a time, the older ones first, until only Jesus was left, with the woman still standing there.
john:8:9.csb: When they heard this, they left one by one, starting with the older men. Only he was left, with the woman in the center.
john:8:9.nrsv: When they heard it, they went away, one by one, beginning with the elders; and Jesus was left alone with the woman standing before him.

john:8:10.niv: Jesus straightened up and asked her, "Woman, where are they? Has no one condemned you?"
john:8:10.csb: When Jesus stood up, he said to her, “Woman, where are they? Has no one condemned you?”
john:8:10.nrsv: Jesus straightened up and said to her, "Woman, where are they? Has no one condemned you?"

john:8:11.niv: "No one, sir," she said. "Then neither do I condemn you," Jesus declared. "Go now and leave your life of sin."
john:8:11.csb: “No one, Lord,” she answered. “Neither do I condemn you,” said Jesus. “Go, and from now on do not sin anymore.”
john:8:11.nrsv: She said, "No one, sir." And Jesus said, "Neither do I condemn you. Go your way, and from now on do not sin again."

john:8:12.niv: When Jesus spoke again to the people, he said, "I am the light of the world. Whoever follows me will never walk in darkness, but will have the light of life."
john:8:12.csb: Jesus spoke to them again: “I am the light of the world. Anyone who follows me will never walk in the darkness but will have the light of life.”
john:8:12.nrsv: Again Jesus spoke to them, saying, "I am the light of the world. Whoever follows me will never walk in darkness but will have the light of life."

john:8:13.niv: The Pharisees challenged him, "Here you are, appearing as your own witness; your testimony is not valid."
john:8:13.csb: So the Pharisees said to him, “You are testifying about yourself. Your testimony is not valid.”
john:8:13.nrsv: Then the Pharisees said to him, "You are testifying on your own behalf; your testimony is not valid."

john:8:14.niv: Jesus answered, "Even if I testify on my own behalf, my testimony is valid, for I know where I came from and where I am going. But you have no idea where I come from or where I am going.
john:8:14.csb: “Even if I testify about myself,” Jesus replied, “My testimony is true, because I know where I came from and where I’m going. But you don’t know where I come from or where I’m going.
john:8:14.nrsv: Jesus answered, "Even if I testify on my own behalf, my testimony is valid because I know where I have come from and where I am going, but you do not know where I come from or where I am going.

john:8:15.niv: You judge by human standards; I pass judgment on no one.
john:8:15.csb: You judge by human standards. I judge no one.
john:8:15.nrsv: You judge by human standards; I judge no one.

john:8:16.niv: But if I do judge, my decisions are right, because I am not alone. I stand with the Father, who sent me.
john:8:16.csb: And if I do judge, my judgment is true, because it is not I alone who judge, but I and the Father who sent me.
john:8:16.nrsv: Yet even if I do judge, my judgment is valid; for it is not I alone who judge, but I and the Father who sent me.

john:8:17.niv: In your own Law it is written that the testimony of two men is valid.
john:8:17.csb: Even in your law it is written that the testimony of two witnesses is true.
john:8:17.nrsv: In your law it is written that the testimony of two witnesses is valid.

john:8:18.niv: I am one who testifies for myself; my other witness is the Father, who sent me."
john:8:18.csb: I am the one who testifies about myself, and the Father who sent me testifies about me.”
john:8:18.nrsv: I testify on my own behalf, and the Father who sent me testifies on my behalf."

john:8:19.niv: Then they asked him, "Where is your father?"
john:8:19.csb: Then they asked him, “Where is your Father?” “You know neither me nor my Father,” Jesus answered. “If you knew me, you would also know my Father.”
john:8:19.nrsv: Then they said to him, "Where is your Father?" Jesus answered, "You know neither me nor my Father. If you knew me, you would know my Father also."

john:8:20.niv: "You do not know me or my Father," Jesus replied. "If you knew me, you would know my Father also." He spoke these words while teaching in the temple area near the place where the offerings were put. Yet no one seized him, because his time had not yet come.
john:8:20.csb: He spoke these words by the treasury, while teaching in the temple. But no one seized him, because his hour had not yet come.
john:8:20.nrsv: He spoke these words while he was teaching in the treasury of the temple, but no one arrested him, because his hour had not yet come.

john:8:21.niv: Once more Jesus said to them, "I am going away, and you will look for me, and you will die in your sin. Where I go, you cannot come."
john:8:21.csb: Then he said to them again, “I’m going away; you will look for me, and you will die in your sin. Where I’m going, you cannot come.”
john:8:21.nrsv: Again he said to them, "I am going away, and you will search for me, but you will die in your sin. Where I am going, you cannot come."

john:8:22.niv: This made the Jews ask, "Will he kill himself? Is that why he says, 'Where I go, you cannot come'?"
john:8:22.csb: So the Jews said again, “He won’t kill himself, will he, since he says, ‘Where I’m going, you cannot come’?”
john:8:22.nrsv: Then the Jews said, "Is he going to kill himself? Is that what he means by saying, 'Where I am going, you cannot come'?"

john:8:23.niv: But he continued, "You are from below; I am from above. You are of this world; I am not of this world.
john:8:23.csb: “You are from below,” he told them, “I am from above. You are of this world; I am not of this world.
john:8:23.nrsv: He said to them, "You are from below, I am from above; you are of this world, I am not of this world.

john:8:24.niv: I told you that you would die in your sins; if you do not believe that I am the one I claim to be, you will indeed die in your sins."
john:8:24.csb: Therefore I told you that you will die in your sins. For if you do not believe that I am he, you will die in your sins.”
john:8:24.nrsv: I told you that you would die in your sins, for you will die in your sins unless you believe that I am he."

john:8:25.csb: “Who are you?” they questioned. “Exactly what I’ve been telling you from the very beginning,” Jesus told them.
john:8:25.nrsv: They said to him, "Who are you?" Jesus said to them, "Why do I speak to you at all?

john:8:26.niv: "Just what I have been claiming all along," Jesus replied. "I have much to say in judgment of you. But he who sent me is reliable, and what I have heard from him I tell the world."
john:8:26.csb: “I have many things to say and to judge about you, but the one who sent me is true, and what I have heard from him — these things I tell the world.”
john:8:26.nrsv: I have much to say about you and much to condemn; but the one who sent me is true, and I declare to the world what I have heard from him."

john:8:27.niv: They did not understand that he was telling them about his Father.
john:8:27.csb: They did not know he was speaking to them about the Father.
john:8:27.nrsv: They did not understand that he was speaking to them about the Father.

john:8:28.niv: So Jesus said, "When you have lifted up the Son of Man, then you will know that I am the one I claim to be and that I do nothing on my own but speak just what the Father has taught me.
john:8:28.csb: So Jesus said to them, “When you lift up the Son of Man, then you will know that I am he, and that I do nothing on my own. But just as the Father taught me, I say these things.
john:8:28.nrsv: So Jesus said, "When you have lifted up the Son of Man, then you will realize that I am he, and that I do nothing on my own, but I speak these things as the Father instructed me.

john:8:29.niv: The one who sent me is with me; he has not left me alone, for I always do what pleases him."
john:8:29.csb: The one who sent me is with me. He has not left me alone, because I always do what pleases him.”
john:8:29.nrsv: And the one who sent me is with me; he has not left me alone, for I always do what is pleasing to him."

john:8:30.niv: Even as he spoke, many put their faith in him.
john:8:30.csb: As he was saying these things, many believed in him.
john:8:30.nrsv: As he was saying these things, many believed in him.

john:8:31.niv: To the Jews who had believed him, Jesus said, "If you hold to my teaching, you are really my disciples.
john:8:31.csb: Then Jesus said to the Jews who had believed him, “If you continue in my word, you really are my disciples.
john:8:31.nrsv: Then Jesus said to the Jews who had believed in him, "If you continue in my word, you are truly my disciples;

john:8:32.niv: Then you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free."
john:8:32.csb: You will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.”
john:8:32.nrsv: and you will know the truth, and the truth will make you free."

john:8:33.niv: They answered him, "We are Abraham's descendants and have never been slaves of anyone. How can you say that we shall be set free?"
john:8:33.csb: “We are descendants of Abraham,” they answered him, “and we have never been enslaved to anyone. How can you say, ‘You will become free’?”
john:8:33.nrsv: They answered him, "We are descendants of Abraham and have never been slaves to anyone. What do you mean by saying, 'You will be made free'?"

john:8:34.niv: Jesus replied, "I tell you the truth, everyone who sins is a slave to sin.
john:8:34.csb: Jesus responded, “Truly I tell you, everyone who commits sin is a slave of sin.
john:8:34.nrsv: Jesus answered them, "Very truly, I tell you, everyone who commits sin is a slave to sin.

john:8:35.niv: Now a slave has no permanent place in the family, but a son belongs to it forever.
john:8:35.csb: A slave does not remain in the household forever, but a son does remain forever.
john:8:35.nrsv: The slave does not have a permanent place in the household; the son has a place there forever.

john:8:36.niv: So if the Son sets you free, you will be free indeed.
john:8:36.csb: So if the Son sets you free, you really will be free.
john:8:36.nrsv: So if the Son makes you free, you will be free indeed.

john:8:37.niv: I know you are Abraham's descendants. Yet you are ready to kill me, because you have no room for my word.
john:8:37.csb: I know you are descendants of Abraham, but you are trying to kill me because my word has no place among you.
john:8:37.nrsv: I know that you are descendants of Abraham; yet you look for an opportunity to kill me, because there is no place in you for my word.

john:8:38.niv: I am telling you what I have seen in the Father's presence, and you do what you have heard from your father. "
john:8:38.csb: I speak what I have seen in the presence of the Father; so then, you do what you have heard from your father.”
john:8:38.nrsv: I declare what I have seen in the Father's presence; as for you, you should do what you have heard from the Father."

john:8:39.niv: "Abraham is our father," they answered. "If you were Abraham's children," said Jesus, "then you would
john:8:39.csb: “Our father is Abraham,” they replied. “If you were Abraham’s children,” Jesus told them, “you would do what Abraham did.
john:8:39.nrsv: They answered him, "Abraham is our father." Jesus said to them, "If you were Abraham's children, you would be doing what Abraham did,

john:8:40.niv: do the things Abraham did. As it is, you are determined to kill me, a man who has told you the truth that I heard from God. Abraham did not do such things.
john:8:40.csb: But now you are trying to kill me, a man who has told you the truth that I heard from God. Abraham did not do this.
john:8:40.nrsv: but now you are trying to kill me, a man who has told you the truth that I heard from God. This is not what Abraham did.

john:8:41.niv: You are doing the things your own father does.We are not illegitimate children," they protested. "The only Father we have is God himself."
john:8:41.csb: You’re doing what your father does.” “We weren’t born of sexual immorality,” they said. “We have one Father — God.”
john:8:41.nrsv: You are indeed doing what your father does." They said to him, "We are not illegitimate children; we have one father, God himself."

john:8:42.niv: Jesus said to them, "If God were your Father, you would love me, for I came from God and now am here. I have not come on my own; but he sent me.
john:8:42.csb: Jesus said to them, “If God were your Father, you would love me, because I came from God and I am here. For I didn’t come on my own, but he sent me.
john:8:42.nrsv: Jesus said to them, "If God were your Father, you would love me, for I came from God and now I am here. I did not come on my own, but he sent me.

john:8:43.niv: Why is my language not clear to you? Because you are unable to hear what I say.
john:8:43.csb: Why don’t you understand what I say? Because you cannot listen to my word.
john:8:43.nrsv: Why do you not understand what I say? It is because you cannot accept my word.

john:8:44.niv: You belong to your father, the devil, and you want to carry out your father's desire. He was a murderer from the beginning, not holding to the truth, for there is no truth in him. When he lies, he speaks his native language, for he is a liar and the father of lies.
john:8:44.csb: You are of your father the devil, and you want to carry out your father’s desires. He was a murderer from the beginning and does not stand in the truth, because there is no truth in him. When he tells a lie, he speaks from his own nature, because he is a liar and the father of lies.
john:8:44.nrsv: You are from your father the devil, and you choose to do your father's desires. He was a murderer from the beginning and does not stand in the truth, because there is no truth in him. When he lies, he speaks according to his own nature, for he is a liar and the father of lies.

john:8:45.niv: Yet because I tell the truth, you do not believe me!
john:8:45.csb: Yet because I tell the truth, you do not believe me.
john:8:45.nrsv: But because I tell the truth, you do not believe me.

john:8:46.niv: Can any of you prove me guilty of sin? If I am telling the truth, why don't you believe me?
john:8:46.csb: Who among you can convict me of sin? If I am telling the truth, why don’t you believe me?
john:8:46.nrsv: Which of you convicts me of sin? If I tell the truth, why do you not believe me?

john:8:47.niv: He who belongs to God hears what God says. The reason you do not hear is that you do not belong to God."
john:8:47.csb: The one who is from God listens to God’s words. This is why you don’t listen, because you are not from God.”
john:8:47.nrsv: Whoever is from God hears the words of God. The reason you do not hear them is that you are not from God."

john:8:48.niv: The Jews answered him, "Aren't we right in saying that you are a Samaritan and demon-possessed?"
john:8:48.csb: The Jews responded to him, “Aren’t we right in saying that you’re a Samaritan and have a demon?”
john:8:48.nrsv: The Jews answered him, "Are we not right in saying that you are a Samaritan and have a demon?"

john:8:49.niv: "I am not possessed by a demon," said Jesus, "but I honor my Father and you dishonor me.
john:8:49.csb: “I do not have a demon,” Jesus answered. “On the contrary, I honor my Father and you dishonor me.
john:8:49.nrsv: Jesus answered, "I do not have a demon; but I honor my Father, and you dishonor me.

john:8:50.niv: I am not seeking glory for myself; but there is one who seeks it, and he is the judge.
john:8:50.csb: I do not seek my own glory; there is one who seeks it and judges.
john:8:50.nrsv: Yet I do not seek my own glory; there is one who seeks it and he is the judge.

john:8:51.niv: I tell you the truth, if anyone keeps my word, he will never see death."
john:8:51.csb: Truly I tell you, if anyone keeps my word, he will never see death.”
john:8:51.nrsv: Very truly, I tell you, whoever keeps my word will never see death."

john:8:52.niv: At this the Jews exclaimed, "Now we know that you are demon-possessed! Abraham died and so did the prophets, yet you say that if anyone keeps your word, he will never taste death.
john:8:52.csb: Then the Jews said, “Now we know you have a demon. Abraham died and so did the prophets. You say, ‘If anyone keeps my word, he will never taste death.’
john:8:52.nrsv: The Jews said to him, "Now we know that you have a demon. Abraham died, and so did the prophets; yet you say, 'Whoever keeps my word will never taste death.'

john:8:53.niv: Are you greater than our father Abraham? He died, and so did the prophets. Who do you think you are?"
john:8:53.csb: Are you greater than our father Abraham who died? And the prophets died. Who do you claim to be?”
john:8:53.nrsv: Are you greater than our father Abraham, who died? The prophets also died. Who do you claim to be?"

john:8:54.niv: Jesus replied, "If I glorify myself, my glory means nothing. My Father, whom you claim as your God, is the one who glorifies me.
john:8:54.csb: “If I glorify myself,” Jesus answered, “my glory is nothing. My Father — about whom you say, ‘He is our God’ — he is the one who glorifies me.
john:8:54.nrsv: Jesus answered, "If I glorify myself, my glory is nothing. It is my Father who glorifies me, he of whom you say, 'He is our God,'

john:8:55.niv: Though you do not know him, I know him. If I said I did not, I would be a liar like you, but I do know him and keep his word.
john:8:55.csb: You do not know him, but I know him. If I were to say I don’t know him, I would be a liar like you. But I do know him, and I keep his word.
john:8:55.nrsv: though you do not know him. But I know him; if I would say that I do not know him, I would be a liar like you. But I do know him and I keep his word.

john:8:56.niv: Your father Abraham rejoiced at the thought of seeing my day; he saw it and was glad."
john:8:56.csb: Your father Abraham rejoiced to see my day; he saw it and was glad.”
john:8:56.nrsv: Your ancestor Abraham rejoiced that he would see my day; he saw it and was glad."

john:8:57.niv: "You are not yet fifty years old," the Jews said to him, "and you have seen Abraham!"
john:8:57.csb: The Jews replied, “You aren’t fifty years old yet, and you’ve seen Abraham?”
john:8:57.nrsv: Then the Jews said to him, "You are not yet fifty years old, and have you seen Abraham?"

john:8:58.niv: "I tell you the truth," Jesus answered, "before Abraham was born, I am!"
john:8:58.csb: Jesus said to them, “Truly I tell you, before Abraham was, I am.”
john:8:58.nrsv: Jesus said to them, "Very truly, I tell you, before Abraham was, I am."

john:8:59.niv: At this, they picked up stones to stone him, but Jesus hid himself, slipping away from the temple grounds.
john:8:59.csb: So they picked up stones to throw at him. But Jesus was hidden and went out of the temple.
john:8:59.nrsv: So they picked up stones to throw at him, but Jesus hid himself and went out of the temple.

john:9:1.niv: As he went along, he saw a man blind from birth.
john:9:1.csb: As he was passing by, he saw a man blind from birth.
john:9:1.nrsv: As he walked along, he saw a man blind from birth.

john:9:2.niv: His disciples asked him, "Rabbi, who sinned, this man or his parents, that he was born blind?"
john:9:2.csb: His disciples asked him: “Rabbi, who sinned, this man or his parents, that he was born blind?”
john:9:2.nrsv: His disciples asked him, "Rabbi, who sinned, this man or his parents, that he was born blind?"

john:9:3.niv: "Neither this man nor his parents sinned," said Jesus, "but this happened so that the work of God might be displayed in his life.
john:9:3.csb: “Neither this man nor his parents sinned,” Jesus answered. “This came about so that God’s works might be displayed in him.
john:9:3.nrsv: Jesus answered, "Neither this man nor his parents sinned; he was born blind so that God's works might be revealed in him.

john:9:4.niv: As long as it is day, we must do the work of him who sent me. Night is coming, when no one can work.
john:9:4.csb: We must do the works of him who sent me while it is day. Night is coming when no one can work.
john:9:4.nrsv: We must work the works of him who sent me while it is day; night is coming when no one can work.

john:9:5.niv: While I am in the world, I am the light of the world."
john:9:5.csb: As long as I am in the world, I am the light of the world.”
john:9:5.nrsv: As long as I am in the world, I am the light of the world."

john:9:6.niv: Having said this, he spit on the ground, made some mud with the saliva, and put it on the man's eyes.
john:9:6.csb: After he said these things he spit on the ground, made some mud from the saliva, and spread the mud on his eyes.
john:9:6.nrsv: When he had said this, he spat on the ground and made mud with the saliva and spread the mud on the man's eyes,

john:9:7.niv: "Go," he told him, "wash in the Pool of Siloam" (this word means Sent). So the man went and washed, and came home seeing.
john:9:7.csb: “Go,” he told him, “wash in the pool of Siloam” (which means “Sent”). So he left, washed, and came back seeing.
john:9:7.nrsv: saying to him, "Go, wash in the pool of Siloam" (which means Sent). Then he went and washed and came back able to see.

john:9:8.niv: His neighbors and those who had formerly seen him begging asked, "Isn't this the same man who used to sit and beg?"
john:9:8.csb: His neighbors and those who had seen him before as a beggar said, “Isn’t this the one who used to sit begging?”
john:9:8.nrsv: The neighbors and those who had seen him before as a beggar began to ask, "Is this not the man who used to sit and beg?"

john:9:9.niv: Some claimed that he was. Others said, "No, he only looks like him." But he himself insisted, "I am the man."
john:9:9.csb: Some said, “He’s the one.” Others were saying, “No, but he looks like him.” He kept saying, “I’m the one.”
john:9:9.nrsv: Some were saying, "It is he." Others were saying, "No, but it is someone like him." He kept saying, "I am the man."

john:9:10.niv: "How then were your eyes opened?" they demanded.
john:9:10.csb: So they asked him, “Then how were your eyes opened?”
john:9:10.nrsv: But they kept asking him, "Then how were your eyes opened?"

john:9:11.niv: He replied, "The man they call Jesus made some mud and put it on my eyes. He told me to go to Siloam and wash. So I went and washed, and then I could see."
john:9:11.csb: He answered, “The man called Jesus made mud, spread it on my eyes, and told me, ‘Go to Siloam and wash.’ So when I went and washed I received my sight.”
john:9:11.nrsv: He answered, "The man called Jesus made mud, spread it on my eyes, and said to me, 'Go to Siloam and wash.' Then I went and washed and received my sight."

john:9:12.niv: "Where is this man?" they asked him. "I don't know," he said.
john:9:12.csb: “Where is he?” they asked. “I don’t know,” he said.
john:9:12.nrsv: They said to him, "Where is he?" He said, "I do not know."

john:9:13.niv: They brought to the Pharisees the man who had been blind.
john:9:13.csb: They brought the man who used to be blind to the Pharisees.
john:9:13.nrsv: They brought to the Pharisees the man who had formerly been blind.

john:9:14.niv: Now the day on which Jesus had made the mud and opened the man's eyes was a Sabbath.
john:9:14.csb: The day that Jesus made the mud and opened his eyes was a Sabbath.
john:9:14.nrsv: Now it was a sabbath day when Jesus made the mud and opened his eyes.

john:9:15.niv: Therefore the Pharisees also asked him how he had received his sight. "He put mud on my eyes," the man replied, "and I washed, and now I see."
john:9:15.csb: Then the Pharisees asked him again how he received his sight. “He put mud on my eyes,” he told them. “I washed and I can see.”
john:9:15.nrsv: Then the Pharisees also began to ask him how he had received his sight. He said to them, "He put mud on my eyes. Then I washed, and now I see."

john:9:16.niv: Some of the Pharisees said, "This man is not from God, for he does not keep the Sabbath." But others asked, "How can a sinner do such miraculous signs?" So they were divided.
john:9:16.csb: Some of the Pharisees said, “This man is not from God, because he doesn’t keep the Sabbath.” But others were saying, “How can a sinful man perform such signs?” And there was a division among them.
john:9:16.nrsv: Some of the Pharisees said, "This man is not from God, for he does not observe the sabbath." But others said, "How can a man who is a sinner perform such signs?" And they were divided.

john:9:17.niv: Finally they turned again to the blind man, "What have you to say about him? It was your eyes he opened." The man replied, "He is a prophet."
john:9:17.csb: Again they asked the blind man, “What do you say about him, since he opened your eyes?” “He’s a prophet,” he said.
john:9:17.nrsv: So they said again to the blind man, "What do you say about him? It was your eyes he opened." He said, "He is a prophet."

john:9:18.niv: The Jews still did not believe that he had been blind and had received his sight until they sent for the man's parents.
john:9:18.csb: The Jews did not believe this about him — that he was blind and received sight — until they summoned the parents of the one who had received his sight.
john:9:18.nrsv: The Jews did not believe that he had been blind and had received his sight until they called the parents of the man who had received his sight

john:9:19.niv: "Is this your son?" they asked. "Is this the one you say was born blind? How is it that now he can see?"
john:9:19.csb: They asked them, “Is this your son, the one you say was born blind? How then does he now see?”
john:9:19.nrsv: and asked them, "Is this your son, who you say was born blind? How then does he now see?"

john:9:20.niv: "We know he is our son," the parents answered, "and we know he was born blind.
john:9:20.csb: “We know this is our son and that he was born blind,” his parents answered.
john:9:20.nrsv: His parents answered, "We know that this is our son, and that he was born blind;

john:9:21.niv: But how he can see now, or who opened his eyes, we don't know. Ask him. He is of age; he will speak for himself."
john:9:21.csb: “But we don’t know how he now sees, and we don’t know who opened his eyes. Ask him; he’s of age. He will speak for himself.”
john:9:21.nrsv: but we do not know how it is that now he sees, nor do we know who opened his eyes. Ask him; he is of age. He will speak for himself."

john:9:22.niv: His parents said this because they were afraid of the Jews, for already the Jews had decided that anyone who acknowledged that Jesus was the Christ would be put out of the synagogue.
john:9:22.csb: His parents said these things because they were afraid of the Jews, since the Jews had already agreed that if anyone confessed him as the Messiah, he would be banned from the synagogue.
john:9:22.nrsv: His parents said this because they were afraid of the Jews; for the Jews had already agreed that anyone who confessed Jesus to be the Messiah would be put out of the synagogue.

john:9:23.niv: That was why his parents said, "He is of age; ask him."
john:9:23.csb: This is why his parents said, “He’s of age; ask him.”
john:9:23.nrsv: Therefore his parents said, "He is of age; ask him."

john:9:24.niv: A second time they summoned the man who had been blind. "Give glory to God, "they said. "We know this man is a sinner."
john:9:24.csb: So a second time they summoned the man who had been blind and told him, “Give glory to God. We know that this man is a sinner.”
john:9:24.nrsv: So for the second time they called the man who had been blind, and they said to him, "Give glory to God! We know that this man is a sinner."

john:9:25.niv: He replied, "Whether he is a sinner or not, I don't know. One thing I do know. I was blind but now I see!"
john:9:25.csb: He answered, “Whether or not he’s a sinner, I don’t know. One thing I do know: I was blind, and now I can see!”
john:9:25.nrsv: He answered, "I do not know whether he is a sinner. One thing I do know, that though I was blind, now I see."

john:9:26.niv: Then they asked him, "What did he do to you? How did he open your eyes?"
john:9:26.csb: Then they asked him, “What did he do to you? How did he open your eyes?”
john:9:26.nrsv: They said to him, "What did he do to you? How did he open your eyes?"

john:9:27.niv: He answered, "I have told you already and you did not listen. Why do you want to hear it again? Do you want to become his disciples, too?"
john:9:27.csb: “I already told you,” he said, “and you didn’t listen. Why do you want to hear it again? You don’t want to become his disciples too, do you?”
john:9:27.nrsv: He answered them, "I have told you already, and you would not listen. Why do you want to hear it again? Do you also want to become his disciples?"

john:9:28.niv: Then they hurled insults at him and said, "You are this fellow's disciple! We are disciples of Moses!
john:9:28.csb: They ridiculed him: “You’re that man’s disciple, but we’re Moses’s disciples.
john:9:28.nrsv: Then they reviled him, saying, "You are his disciple, but we are disciples of Moses.

john:9:29.niv: We know that God spoke to Moses, but as for this fellow, we don't even know where he comes from."
john:9:29.csb: We know that God has spoken to Moses. But this man — we don’t know where he’s from.”
john:9:29.nrsv: We know that God has spoken to Moses, but as for this man, we do not know where he comes from."

john:9:30.niv: The man answered, "Now that is remarkable! You don't know where he comes from, yet he opened my eyes.
john:9:30.csb: “This is an amazing thing!” the man told them. “You don’t know where he is from, and yet he opened my eyes.
john:9:30.nrsv: The man answered, "Here is an astonishing thing! You do not know where he comes from, and yet he opened my eyes.

john:9:31.niv: We know that God does not listen to sinners. He listens to the godly man who does his will.
john:9:31.csb: We know that God doesn’t listen to sinners, but if anyone is God-fearing and does his will, he listens to him.
john:9:31.nrsv: We know that God does not listen to sinners, but he does listen to one who worships him and obeys his will.

john:9:32.niv: Nobody has ever heard of opening the eyes of a man born blind.
john:9:32.csb: Throughout history no one has ever heard of someone opening the eyes of a person born blind.
john:9:32.nrsv: Never since the world began has it been heard that anyone opened the eyes of a person born blind.

john:9:33.niv: If this man were not from God, he could do nothing."
john:9:33.csb: If this man were not from God, he wouldn’t be able to do anything.”
john:9:33.nrsv: If this man were not from God, he could do nothing."

john:9:34.niv: To this they replied, "You were steeped in sin at birth; how dare you lecture us!" And they threw him out.
john:9:34.csb: “You were born entirely in sin,” they replied, “and are you trying to teach us?” Then they threw him out.
john:9:34.nrsv: They answered him, "You were born entirely in sins, and are you trying to teach us?" And they drove him out.

john:9:35.niv: Jesus heard that they had thrown him out, and when he found him, he said, "Do you believe in the Son of Man?"
john:9:35.csb: Jesus heard that they had thrown the man out, and when he found him, he asked, “Do you believe in the Son of Man?”
john:9:35.nrsv: Jesus heard that they had driven him out, and when he found him, he said, "Do you believe in the Son of Man?"

john:9:36.niv: "Who is he, sir?" the man asked. "Tell me so that I may believe in him."
john:9:36.csb: “Who is he, Sir, that I may believe in him?” he asked.
john:9:36.nrsv: He answered, "And who is he, sir? Tell me, so that I may believe in him."

john:9:37.niv: Jesus said, "You have now seen him; in fact, he is the one speaking with you."
john:9:37.csb: Jesus answered, “You have seen him; in fact, he is the one speaking with you.”
john:9:37.nrsv: Jesus said to him, "You have seen him, and the one speaking with you is he."

john:9:38.niv: Then the man said, "Lord, I believe," and he worshiped him.
john:9:38.csb: “I believe, Lord!” he said, and he worshiped him.
john:9:38.nrsv: He said, "Lord, I believe." And he worshiped him.

john:9:39.niv: Jesus said, "For judgment I have come into this world, so that the blind will see and those who see will become blind."
john:9:39.csb: Jesus said, “I came into this world for judgment, in order that those who do not see will see and those who do see will become blind.”
john:9:39.nrsv: Jesus said, "I came into this world for judgment so that those who do not see may see, and those who do see may become blind."

john:9:40.niv: Some Pharisees who were with him heard him say this and asked, "What? Are we blind too?"
john:9:40.csb: Some of the Pharisees who were with him heard these things and asked him, “We aren’t blind too, are we?”
john:9:40.nrsv: Some of the Pharisees near him heard this and said to him, "Surely we are not blind, are we?"

john:9:41.niv: Jesus said, "If you were blind, you would not be guilty of sin; but now that you claim you can see, your guilt remains.
john:9:41.csb: “If you were blind,” Jesus told them, “you wouldn’t have sin. But now that you say, ‘We see,’ your sin remains.
john:9:41.nrsv: Jesus said to them, "If you were blind, you would not have sin. But now that you say, 'We see,' your sin remains.

john:10:1.niv: "I tell you the truth, the man who does not enter the sheep pen by the gate, but climbs in by some other way, is a thief and a robber.
john:10:1.csb: “Truly I tell you, anyone who doesn’t enter the sheep pen by the gate but climbs in some other way is a thief and a robber.
john:10:1.nrsv: "Very truly, I tell you, anyone who does not enter the sheepfold by the gate but climbs in by another way is a thief and a bandit.

john:10:2.niv: The man who enters by the gate is the shepherd of his sheep.
john:10:2.csb: The one who enters by the gate is the shepherd of the sheep.
john:10:2.nrsv: The one who enters by the gate is the shepherd of the sheep.

john:10:3.niv: The watchman opens the gate for him, and the sheep listen to his voice. He calls his own sheep by name and leads them out.
john:10:3.csb: The gatekeeper opens it for him, and the sheep hear his voice. He calls his own sheep by name and leads them out.
john:10:3.nrsv: The gatekeeper opens the gate for him, and the sheep hear his voice. He calls his own sheep by name and leads them out.

john:10:4.niv: When he has brought out all his own, he goes on ahead of them, and his sheep follow him because they know his voice.
john:10:4.csb: When he has brought all his own outside, he goes ahead of them. The sheep follow him because they know his voice.
john:10:4.nrsv: When he has brought out all his own, he goes ahead of them, and the sheep follow him because they know his voice.

john:10:5.niv: But they will never follow a stranger; in fact, they will run away from him because they do not recognize a stranger's voice."
john:10:5.csb: They will never follow a stranger; instead they will run away from him, because they don’t know the voice of strangers.”
john:10:5.nrsv: They will not follow a stranger, but they will run from him because they do not know the voice of strangers."

john:10:6.niv: Jesus used this figure of speech, but they did not understand what he was telling them.
john:10:6.csb: Jesus gave them this figure of speech, but they did not understand what he was telling them.
john:10:6.nrsv: Jesus used this figure of speech with them, but they did not understand what he was saying to them.

john:10:7.niv: Therefore Jesus said again, "I tell you the truth, I am the gate for the sheep.
john:10:7.csb: Jesus said again, “Truly I tell you, I am the gate for the sheep.
john:10:7.nrsv: So again Jesus said to them, "Very truly, I tell you, I am the gate for the sheep.

john:10:8.niv: All who ever came before me were thieves and robbers, but the sheep did not listen to them.
john:10:8.csb: All who came before me are thieves and robbers, but the sheep didn’t listen to them.
john:10:8.nrsv: All who came before me are thieves and bandits; but the sheep did not listen to them.

john:10:9.niv: I am the gate; whoever enters through me will be saved. He will come in and go out, and find pasture.
john:10:9.csb: I am the gate. If anyone enters by me, he will be saved and will come in and go out and find pasture.
john:10:9.nrsv: I am the gate. Whoever enters by me will be saved, and will come in and go out and find pasture.

john:10:10.niv: The thief comes only to steal and kill and destroy; I have come that they may have life, and have it to the full.
john:10:10.csb: A thief comes only to steal and kill and destroy. I have come so that they may have life and have it in abundance.
john:10:10.nrsv: The thief comes only to steal and kill and destroy. I came that they may have life, and have it abundantly.

john:10:11.niv: "I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep.
john:10:11.csb: “I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep.
john:10:11.nrsv: "I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep.

john:10:12.niv: The hired hand is not the shepherd who owns the sheep. So when he sees the wolf coming, he abandons the sheep and runs away. Then the wolf attacks the flock and scatters it.
john:10:12.csb: The hired hand, since he is not the shepherd and doesn’t own the sheep, leaves them and runs away when he sees a wolf coming. The wolf then snatches and scatters them.
john:10:12.nrsv: The hired hand, who is not the shepherd and does not own the sheep, sees the wolf coming and leaves the sheep and runs away-- and the wolf snatches them and scatters them.

john:10:13.niv: The man runs away because he is a hired hand and cares nothing for the sheep.
john:10:13.csb: This happens because he is a hired hand and doesn’t care about the sheep.
john:10:13.nrsv: The hired hand runs away because a hired hand does not care for the sheep.

john:10:14.niv: "I am the good shepherd; I know my sheep and my sheep know me--
john:10:14.csb: “I am the good shepherd. I know my own, and my own know me,
john:10:14.nrsv: I am the good shepherd. I know my own and my own know me,

john:10:15.niv: just as the Father knows me and I know the Father--and I lay down my life for the sheep.
john:10:15.csb: just as the Father knows me, and I know the Father. I lay down my life for the sheep.
john:10:15.nrsv: just as the Father knows me and I know the Father. And I lay down my life for the sheep.

john:10:16.niv: I have other sheep that are not of this sheep pen. I must bring them also. They too will listen to my voice, and there shall be one flock and one shepherd.
john:10:16.csb: But I have other sheep that are not from this sheep pen; I must bring them also, and they will listen to my voice. Then there will be one flock, one shepherd.
john:10:16.nrsv: I have other sheep that do not belong to this fold. I must bring them also, and they will listen to my voice. So there will be one flock, one shepherd.

john:10:17.niv: The reason my Father loves me is that I lay down my life--only to take it up again.
john:10:17.csb: This is why the Father loves me, because I lay down my life so that I may take it up again.
john:10:17.nrsv: For this reason the Father loves me, because I lay down my life in order to take it up again.

john:10:18.niv: No one takes it from me, but I lay it down of my own accord. I have authority to lay it down and authority to take it up again. This command I received from my Father."
john:10:18.csb: No one takes it from me, but I lay it down on my own. I have the right to lay it down, and I have the right to take it up again. I have received this command from my Father.”
john:10:18.nrsv: No one takes it from me, but I lay it down of my own accord. I have power to lay it down, and I have power to take it up again. I have received this command from my Father."

john:10:19.niv: At these words the Jews were again divided.
john:10:19.csb: Again the Jews were divided because of these words.
john:10:19.nrsv: Again the Jews were divided because of these words.

john:10:20.niv: Many of them said, "He is demon-possessed and raving mad. Why listen to him?"
john:10:20.csb: Many of them were saying, “He has a demon and he’s crazy. Why do you listen to him?”
john:10:20.nrsv: Many of them were saying, "He has a demon and is out of his mind. Why listen to him?"

john:10:21.niv: But others said, "These are not the sayings of a man possessed by a demon. Can a demon open the eyes of the blind?"
john:10:21.csb: Others were saying, “These aren’t the words of someone who is demon-possessed. Can a demon open the eyes of the blind?”
john:10:21.nrsv: Others were saying, "These are not the words of one who has a demon. Can a demon open the eyes of the blind?"

john:10:22.niv: Then came the Feast of Dedication at Jerusalem. It was winter,
john:10:22.csb: Then the Festival of Dedication took place in Jerusalem, and it was winter.
john:10:22.nrsv: At that time the festival of the Dedication took place in Jerusalem. It was winter,

john:10:23.niv: and Jesus was in the temple area walking in Solomon's Colonnade.
john:10:23.csb: Jesus was walking in the temple in Solomon’s Colonnade.
john:10:23.nrsv: and Jesus was walking in the temple, in the portico of Solomon.

john:10:24.niv: The Jews gathered around him, saying, "How long will you keep us in suspense? If you are the Christ, tell us plainly."
john:10:24.csb: The Jews surrounded him and asked, “How long are you going to keep us in suspense? If you are the Messiah, tell us plainly.”
john:10:24.nrsv: So the Jews gathered around him and said to him, "How long will you keep us in suspense? If you are the Messiah, tell us plainly."

john:10:25.niv: Jesus answered, "I did tell you, but you do not believe. The miracles I do in my Father's name speak for me,
john:10:25.csb: “I did tell you and you don’t believe,” Jesus answered them. “The works that I do in my Father’s name testify about me.
john:10:25.nrsv: Jesus answered, "I have told you, and you do not believe. The works that I do in my Father's name testify to me;

john:10:26.niv: but you do not believe because you are not my sheep.
john:10:26.csb: But you don’t believe because you are not of my sheep.
john:10:26.nrsv: but you do not believe, because you do not belong to my sheep.

john:10:27.niv: My sheep listen to my voice; I know them, and they follow me.
john:10:27.csb: My sheep hear my voice, I know them, and they follow me.
john:10:27.nrsv: My sheep hear my voice. I know them, and they follow me.

john:10:28.niv: I give them eternal life, and they shall never perish; no one can snatch them out of my hand.
john:10:28.csb: I give them eternal life, and they will never perish. No one will snatch them out of my hand.
john:10:28.nrsv: I give them eternal life, and they will never perish. No one will snatch them out of my hand.

john:10:29.niv: My Father, who has given them to me, is greater than all; no one can snatch them out of my Father's hand.
john:10:29.csb: My Father, who has given them to me, is greater than all. No one is able to snatch them out of the Father’s hand.
john:10:29.nrsv: What my Father has given me is greater than all else, and no one can snatch it out of the Father's hand.

john:10:30.niv: I and the Father are one."
john:10:30.csb: I and the Father are one.”
john:10:30.nrsv: The Father and I are one."

john:10:31.niv: Again the Jews picked up stones to stone him,
john:10:31.csb: Again the Jews picked up rocks to stone him.
john:10:31.nrsv: The Jews took up stones again to stone him.

john:10:32.niv: but Jesus said to them, "I have shown you many great miracles from the Father. For which of these do you stone me?"
john:10:32.csb: Jesus replied, “I have shown you many good works from the Father. For which of these works are you stoning me?”
john:10:32.nrsv: Jesus replied, "I have shown you many good works from the Father. For which of these are you going to stone me?"

john:10:33.niv: "We are not stoning you for any of these," replied the Jews, "but for blasphemy, because you, a mere man, claim to be God."
john:10:33.csb: “We aren’t stoning you for a good work,” the Jews answered, “but for blasphemy, because you — being a man — make yourself God.”
john:10:33.nrsv: The Jews answered, "It is not for a good work that we are going to stone you, but for blasphemy, because you, though only a human being, are making yourself God."

john:10:34.niv: Jesus answered them, "Is it not written in your Law, 'I have said you are gods'?
john:10:34.csb: Jesus answered them, “Isn’t it written in your law, I said, you are gods?
john:10:34.nrsv: Jesus answered, "Is it not written in your law, 'I said, you are gods'?

john:10:35.niv: If he called them 'gods,' to whom the word of God came--and the Scripture cannot be broken--
john:10:35.csb: If he called those whom the word of God came to ‘gods’ — and the Scripture cannot be broken —
john:10:35.nrsv: If those to whom the word of God came were called 'gods'-- and the scripture cannot be annulled--

john:10:36.niv: what about the one whom the Father set apart as his very own and sent into the world? Why then do you accuse me of blasphemy because I said, 'I am God's Son'?
john:10:36.csb: do you say, ‘You are blaspheming’ to the one the Father set apart and sent into the world, because I said: I am the Son of God?
john:10:36.nrsv: can you say that the one whom the Father has sanctified and sent into the world is blaspheming because I said, 'I am God's Son'?

john:10:37.niv: Do not believe me unless I do what my Father does.
john:10:37.csb: If I am not doing my Father’s works, don’t believe me.
john:10:37.nrsv: If I am not doing the works of my Father, then do not believe me.

john:10:38.niv: But if I do it, even though you do not believe me, believe the miracles, that you may know and understand that the Father is in me, and I in the Father."
john:10:38.csb: But if I am doing them and you don’t believe me, believe the works. This way you will know and understand that the Father is in me and I in the Father.”
john:10:38.nrsv: But if I do them, even though you do not believe me, believe the works, so that you may know and understand that the Father is in me and I am in the Father."

john:10:39.niv: Again they tried to seize him, but he escaped their grasp.
john:10:39.csb: Then they were trying again to seize him, but he eluded their grasp.
john:10:39.nrsv: Then they tried to arrest him again, but he escaped from their hands.

john:10:40.niv: Then Jesus went back across the Jordan to the place where John had been baptizing in the early days. Here he stayed
john:10:40.csb: So he departed again across the Jordan to the place where John had been baptizing earlier, and he remained there.
john:10:40.nrsv: He went away again across the Jordan to the place where John had been baptizing earlier, and he remained there.

john:10:41.niv: and many people came to him. They said, "Though John never performed a miraculous sign, all that John said about this man was true."
john:10:41.csb: Many came to him and said, “John never did a sign, but everything John said about this man was true.”
john:10:41.nrsv: Many came to him, and they were saying, "John performed no sign, but everything that John said about this man was true."

john:10:42.niv: And in that place many believed in Jesus.
john:10:42.csb: And many believed in him there.
john:10:42.nrsv: And many believed in him there.

john:11:1.niv: Now a man named Lazarus was sick. He was from Bethany, the village of Mary and her sister Martha.
john:11:1.csb: Now a man was sick, Lazarus from Bethany, the village of Mary and her sister Martha.
john:11:1.nrsv: Now a certain man was ill, Lazarus of Bethany, the village of Mary and her sister Martha.

john:11:2.niv: This Mary, whose brother Lazarus now lay sick, was the same one who poured perfume on the Lord and wiped his feet with her hair.
john:11:2.csb: Mary was the one who anointed the Lord with perfume and wiped his feet with her hair, and it was her brother Lazarus who was sick.
john:11:2.nrsv: Mary was the one who anointed the Lord with perfume and wiped his feet with her hair; her brother Lazarus was ill.

john:11:3.niv: So the sisters sent word to Jesus, "Lord, the one you love is sick."
john:11:3.csb: So the sisters sent a message to him: “Lord, the one you love is sick.”
john:11:3.nrsv: So the sisters sent a message to Jesus, "Lord, he whom you love is ill."

john:11:4.niv: When he heard this, Jesus said, "This sickness will not end in death. No, it is for God's glory so that God's Son may be glorified through it."
john:11:4.csb: When Jesus heard it, he said, “This sickness will not end in death but is for the glory of God, so that the Son of God may be glorified through it.”
john:11:4.nrsv: But when Jesus heard it, he said, "This illness does not lead to death; rather it is for God's glory, so that the Son of God may be glorified through it."

john:11:5.niv: Jesus loved Martha and her sister and Lazarus.
john:11:5.csb: Now Jesus loved Martha, her sister, and Lazarus.
john:11:5.nrsv: Accordingly, though Jesus loved Martha and her sister and Lazarus,

john:11:6.niv: Yet when he heard that Lazarus was sick, he stayed where he was two more days.
john:11:6.csb: So when he heard that he was sick, he stayed two more days in the place where he was.
john:11:6.nrsv: after having heard that Lazarus was ill, he stayed two days longer in the place where he was.

john:11:7.niv: Then he said to his disciples, "Let us go back to Judea."
john:11:7.csb: Then after that, he said to the disciples, “Let’s go to Judea again.”
john:11:7.nrsv: Then after this he said to the disciples, "Let us go to Judea again."

john:11:8.niv: "But Rabbi," they said, "a short while ago the Jews tried to stone you, and yet you are going back there?"
john:11:8.csb: “Rabbi,” the disciples told him, “just now the Jews tried to stone you, and you’re going there again?”
john:11:8.nrsv: The disciples said to him, "Rabbi, the Jews were just now trying to stone you, and are you going there again?"

john:11:9.niv: Jesus answered, "Are there not twelve hours of daylight? A man who walks by day will not stumble, for he sees by this world's light.
john:11:9.csb: “Aren’t there twelve hours in a day?” Jesus answered. “If anyone walks during the day, he doesn’t stumble, because he sees the light of this world.
john:11:9.nrsv: Jesus answered, "Are there not twelve hours of daylight? Those who walk during the day do not stumble, because they see the light of this world.

john:11:10.niv: It is when he walks by night that he stumbles, for he has no light."
john:11:10.csb: But if anyone walks during the night, he does stumble, because the light is not in him.”
john:11:10.nrsv: But those who walk at night stumble, because the light is not in them."

john:11:11.niv: After he had said this, he went on to tell them, "Our friend Lazarus has fallen asleep; but I am going there to wake him up."
john:11:11.csb: He said this, and then he told them, “Our friend Lazarus has fallen asleep, but I’m on my way to wake him up.”
john:11:11.nrsv: After saying this, he told them, "Our friend Lazarus has fallen asleep, but I am going there to awaken him."

john:11:12.niv: His disciples replied, "Lord, if he sleeps, he will get better."
john:11:12.csb: Then the disciples said to him, “Lord, if he has fallen asleep, he will get well.”
john:11:12.nrsv: The disciples said to him, "Lord, if he has fallen asleep, he will be all right."

john:11:13.niv: Jesus had been speaking of his death, but his disciples thought he meant natural sleep.
john:11:13.csb: Jesus, however, was speaking about his death, but they thought he was speaking about natural sleep.
john:11:13.nrsv: Jesus, however, had been speaking about his death, but they thought that he was referring merely to sleep.

john:11:14.niv: So then he told them plainly, "Lazarus is dead,
john:11:14.csb: So Jesus then told them plainly, “Lazarus has died.
john:11:14.nrsv: Then Jesus told them plainly, "Lazarus is dead.

john:11:15.niv: and for your sake I am glad I was not there, so that you may believe. But let us go to him."
john:11:15.csb: I’m glad for you that I wasn’t there so that you may believe. But let’s go to him.”
john:11:15.nrsv: For your sake I am glad I was not there, so that you may believe. But let us go to him."

john:11:16.niv: Then Thomas (called Didymus) said to the rest of the disciples, "Let us also go, that we may die with him."
john:11:16.csb: Then Thomas (called “Twin” ) said to his fellow disciples, “Let’s go too so that we may die with him.”
john:11:16.nrsv: Thomas, who was called the Twin, said to his fellow disciples, "Let us also go, that we may die with him."

john:11:17.niv: On his arrival, Jesus found that Lazarus had already been in the tomb for four days.
john:11:17.csb: When Jesus arrived, he found that Lazarus had already been in the tomb four days.
john:11:17.nrsv: When Jesus arrived, he found that Lazarus had already been in the tomb four days.

john:11:18.niv: Bethany was less than two miles from Jerusalem,
john:11:18.csb: Bethany was near Jerusalem (less than two miles away).
john:11:18.nrsv: Now Bethany was near Jerusalem, some two miles away,

john:11:19.niv: and many Jews had come to Martha and Mary to comfort them in the loss of their brother.
john:11:19.csb: Many of the Jews had come to Martha and Mary to comfort them about their brother.
john:11:19.nrsv: and many of the Jews had come to Martha and Mary to console them about their brother.

john:11:20.niv: When Martha heard that Jesus was coming, she went out to meet him, but Mary stayed at home.
john:11:20.csb: As soon as Martha heard that Jesus was coming, she went to meet him, but Mary remained seated in the house.
john:11:20.nrsv: When Martha heard that Jesus was coming, she went and met him, while Mary stayed at home.

john:11:21.niv: "Lord," Martha said to Jesus, "if you had been here, my brother would not have died.
john:11:21.csb: Then Martha said to Jesus, “Lord, if you had been here, my brother wouldn’t have died.
john:11:21.nrsv: Martha said to Jesus, "Lord, if you had been here, my brother would not have died.

john:11:22.niv: But I know that even now God will give you whatever you ask."
john:11:22.csb: Yet even now I know that whatever you ask from God, God will give you.”
john:11:22.nrsv: But even now I know that God will give you whatever you ask of him."

john:11:23.niv: Jesus said to her, "Your brother will rise again."
john:11:23.csb: “Your brother will rise again,” Jesus told her.
john:11:23.nrsv: Jesus said to her, "Your brother will rise again."

john:11:24.niv: Martha answered, "I know he will rise again in the resurrection at the last day."
john:11:24.csb: Martha said to him, “I know that he will rise again in the resurrection at the last day.”
john:11:24.nrsv: Martha said to him, "I know that he will rise again in the resurrection on the last day."

john:11:25.niv: Jesus said to her, "I am the resurrection and the life. He who believes in me will live, even though he dies;
john:11:25.csb: Jesus said to her, “I am the resurrection and the life. The one who believes in me, even if he dies, will live.
john:11:25.nrsv: Jesus said to her, "I am the resurrection and the life. Those who believe in me, even though they die, will live,

john:11:26.niv: and whoever lives and believes in me will never die. Do you believe this?"
john:11:26.csb: Everyone who lives and believes in me will never die. Do you believe this?”
john:11:26.nrsv: and everyone who lives and believes in me will never die. Do you believe this?"

john:11:27.niv: "Yes, Lord," she told him, "I believe that you are the Christ, the Son of God, who was to come into the world."
john:11:27.csb: “Yes, Lord,” she told him, “I believe you are the Messiah, the Son of God, who comes into the world.”
john:11:27.nrsv: She said to him, "Yes, Lord, I believe that you are the Messiah, the Son of God, the one coming into the world."

john:11:28.niv: And after she had said this, she went back and called her sister Mary aside. "The Teacher is here," she said, "and is asking for you."
john:11:28.csb: Having said this, she went back and called her sister Mary, saying in private, “The Teacher is here and is calling for you.”
john:11:28.nrsv: When she had said this, she went back and called her sister Mary, and told her privately, "The Teacher is here and is calling for you."

john:11:29.niv: When Mary heard this, she got up quickly and went to him.
john:11:29.csb: As soon as Mary heard this, she got up quickly and went to him.
john:11:29.nrsv: And when she heard it, she got up quickly and went to him.

john:11:30.niv: Now Jesus had not yet entered the village, but was still at the place where Martha had met him.
john:11:30.csb: Jesus had not yet come into the village but was still in the place where Martha had met him.
john:11:30.nrsv: Now Jesus had not yet come to the village, but was still at the place where Martha had met him.

john:11:31.niv: When the Jews who had been with Mary in the house, comforting her, noticed how quickly she got up and went out, they followed her, supposing she was going to the tomb to mourn there.
john:11:31.csb: The Jews who were with her in the house consoling her saw that Mary got up quickly and went out. They followed her, supposing that she was going to the tomb to cry there.
john:11:31.nrsv: The Jews who were with her in the house, consoling her, saw Mary get up quickly and go out. They followed her because they thought that she was going to the tomb to weep there.

john:11:32.niv: When Mary reached the place where Jesus was and saw him, she fell at his feet and said, "Lord, if you had been here, my brother would not have died."
john:11:32.csb: As soon as Mary came to where Jesus was and saw him, she fell at his feet and told him, “Lord, if you had been here, my brother would not have died!”
john:11:32.nrsv: When Mary came where Jesus was and saw him, she knelt at his feet and said to him, "Lord, if you had been here, my brother would not have died."

john:11:33.niv: When Jesus saw her weeping, and the Jews who had come along with her also weeping, he was deeply moved in spirit and troubled.
john:11:33.csb: When Jesus saw her crying, and the Jews who had come with her crying, he was deeply moved in his spirit and troubled.
john:11:33.nrsv: When Jesus saw her weeping, and the Jews who came with her also weeping, he was greatly disturbed in spirit and deeply moved.

john:11:34.niv: "Where have you laid him?" he asked. "Come and see, Lord," they replied.
john:11:34.csb: “Where have you put him?” he asked. “Lord,” they told him, “come and see.”
john:11:34.nrsv: He said, "Where have you laid him?" They said to him, "Lord, come and see."

john:11:36.niv: Then the Jews said, "See how he loved him!"
john:11:36.csb: So the Jews said, “See how he loved him!”
john:11:36.nrsv: So the Jews said, "See how he loved him!"

john:11:37.niv: But some of them said, "Could not he who opened the eyes of the blind man have kept this man from dying?"
john:11:37.csb: But some of them said, “Couldn’t he who opened the blind man’s eyes also have kept this man from dying?”
john:11:37.nrsv: But some of them said, "Could not he who opened the eyes of the blind man have kept this man from dying?"

john:11:38.niv: Jesus, once more deeply moved, came to the tomb. It was a cave with a stone laid across the entrance.
john:11:38.csb: Then Jesus, deeply moved again, came to the tomb. It was a cave, and a stone was lying against it.
john:11:38.nrsv: Then Jesus, again greatly disturbed, came to the tomb. It was a cave, and a stone was lying against it.

john:11:39.niv: "Take away the stone," he said. "But, Lord," said Martha, the sister of the dead man, "by this time there is a bad odor, for he has been there four days."
john:11:39.csb: “Remove the stone,” Jesus said. Martha, the dead man’s sister, told him, “Lord, there is already a stench because he has been dead four days.”
