acts:2:13.niv: Some, however, made fun of them and said, "They have had too much wine. "
acts:2:13.csb: But some sneered and said, “They’re drunk on new wine.”
acts:2:13.nrsv: But others sneered and said, "They are filled with new wine."

acts:2:14.niv: Then Peter stood up with the Eleven, raised his voice and addressed the crowd: "Fellow Jews and all of you who live in Jerusalem, let me explain this to you; listen carefully to what I say.
acts:2:14.csb: Peter stood up with the Eleven, raised his voice, and proclaimed to them: “Fellow Jews and all you residents of Jerusalem, let me explain this to you and pay attention to my words.
acts:2:14.nrsv: But Peter, standing with the eleven, raised his voice and addressed them, "Men of Judea and all who live in Jerusalem, let this be known to you, and listen to what I say.

acts:2:15.niv: These men are not drunk, as you suppose. It's only nine in the morning!
acts:2:15.csb: For these people are not drunk, as you suppose, since it’s only nine in the morning.
acts:2:15.nrsv: Indeed, these are not drunk, as you suppose, for it is only nine o'clock in the morning.

acts:2:16.niv: No, this is what was spoken by the prophet Joel:
acts:2:16.csb: On the contrary, this is what was spoken through the prophet Joel:
acts:2:16.nrsv: No, this is what was spoken through the prophet Joel:

acts:2:17.niv: "'In the last days, God says, I will pour out my Spirit on all people. Your sons and daughters will prophesy, your young men will see visions, your old men will dream dreams.
acts:2:17.csb: And it will be in the last days, says God, that I will pour out my Spirit on all people; then your sons and your daughters will prophesy, your young men will see visions, and your old men will dream dreams.
acts:2:17.nrsv: 'In the last days it will be, God declares, that I will pour out my Spirit upon all flesh, and your sons and your daughters shall prophesy, and your young men shall see visions, and your old men shall dream dreams.

acts:2:18.niv: Even on my servants, both men and women, I will pour out my Spirit in those days, and they will prophesy.
acts:2:18.csb: I will even pour out my Spirit on my servants in those days, both men and women and they will prophesy.
acts:2:18.nrsv: Even upon my slaves, both men and women, in those days I will pour out my Spirit; and they shall prophesy.

acts:2:19.niv: I will show wonders in the heaven above and signs on the earth below, blood and fire and billows of smoke.
acts:2:19.csb: I will display wonders in the heaven above and signs on the earth below: blood and fire and a cloud of smoke.
acts:2:19.nrsv: And I will show portents in the heaven above and signs on the earth below, blood, and fire, and smoky mist.

acts:2:20.niv: The sun will be turned to darkness and the moon to blood before the coming of the great and glorious day of the Lord.
acts:2:20.csb: The sun will be turned to darkness and the moon to blood before the great and glorious day of the Lord comes.
acts:2:20.nrsv: The sun shall be turned to darkness and the moon to blood, before the coming of the Lord's great and glorious day.

acts:2:21.niv: And everyone who calls on the name of the Lord will be saved.'
acts:2:21.csb: Then everyone who calls on the name of the Lord will be saved.
acts:2:21.nrsv: Then everyone who calls on the name of the Lord shall be saved.'

acts:2:22.niv: "Men of Israel, listen to this: Jesus of Nazareth was a man accredited by God to you by miracles, wonders and signs, which God did among you through him, as you yourselves know.
acts:2:22.csb: “Fellow Israelites, listen to these words: This Jesus of Nazareth was a man attested to you by God with miracles, wonders, and signs that God did among you through him, just as you yourselves know.
acts:2:22.nrsv: "You that are Israelites, listen to what I have to say: Jesus of Nazareth, a man attested to you by God with deeds of power, wonders, and signs that God did through him among you, as you yourselves know--

acts:2:23.niv: This man was handed over to you by God's set purpose and foreknowledge; and you, with the help of wicked men, put him to death by nailing him to the cross.
acts:2:23.csb: Though he was delivered up according to God’s determined plan and foreknowledge, you used lawless people to nail him to a cross and kill him.
acts:2:23.nrsv: this man, handed over to you according to the definite plan and foreknowledge of God, you crucified and killed by the hands of those outside the law.

acts:2:24.niv: But God raised him from the dead, freeing him from the agony of death, because it was impossible for death to keep its hold on him.
acts:2:24.csb: God raised him up, ending the pains of death, because it was not possible for him to be held by death.
acts:2:24.nrsv: But God raised him up, having freed him from death, because it was impossible for him to be held in its power.

acts:2:25.niv: David said about him: "'I saw the Lord always before me. Because he is at my right hand, I will not be shaken.
acts:2:25.csb: For David says of him: I saw the Lord ever before me; because he is at my right hand, I will not be shaken.
acts:2:25.nrsv: For David says concerning him, 'I saw the Lord always before me, for he is at my right hand so that I will not be shaken;

acts:2:26.niv: Therefore my heart is glad and my tongue rejoices; my body also will live in hope,
acts:2:26.csb: Therefore my heart is glad and my tongue rejoices. Moreover, my flesh will rest in hope,
acts:2:26.nrsv: therefore my heart was glad, and my tongue rejoiced; moreover my flesh will live in hope.

acts:2:27.niv: because you will not abandon me to the grave, nor will you let your Holy One see decay.
acts:2:27.csb: because you will not abandon me in Hades or allow your holy one to see decay.
acts:2:27.nrsv: For you will not abandon my soul to Hades, or let your Holy One experience corruption.

acts:2:28.niv: You have made known to me the paths of life; you will fill me with joy in your presence.'
acts:2:28.csb: You have revealed the paths of life to me; you will fill me with gladness in your presence.
acts:2:28.nrsv: You have made known to me the ways of life; you will make me full of gladness with your presence.'

acts:2:29.niv: "Brothers, I can tell you confidently that the patriarch David died and was buried, and his tomb is here to this day.
acts:2:29.csb: “Brothers and sisters, I can confidently speak to you about the patriarch David: He is both dead and buried, and his tomb is with us to this day.
acts:2:29.nrsv: "Fellow Israelites, I may say to you confidently of our ancestor David that he both died and was buried, and his tomb is with us to this day.

acts:2:30.niv: But he was a prophet and knew that God had promised him on oath that he would place one of his descendants on his throne.
acts:2:30.csb: Since he was a prophet, he knew that God had sworn an oath to him to seat one of his descendants on his throne.
acts:2:30.nrsv: Since he was a prophet, he knew that God had sworn with an oath to him that he would put one of his descendants on his throne.

acts:2:31.niv: Seeing what was ahead, he spoke of the resurrection of the Christ, that he was not abandoned to the grave, nor did his body see decay.
acts:2:31.csb: Seeing what was to come, he spoke concerning the resurrection of the Messiah: He was not abandoned in Hades, and his flesh did not experience decay.
acts:2:31.nrsv: Foreseeing this, David spoke of the resurrection of the Messiah, saying, 'He was not abandoned to Hades, nor did his flesh experience corruption.'

acts:2:32.niv: God has raised this Jesus to life, and we are all witnesses of the fact.
acts:2:32.csb: “God has raised this Jesus; we are all witnesses of this.
acts:2:32.nrsv: This Jesus God raised up, and of that all of us are witnesses.

acts:2:33.niv: Exalted to the right hand of God, he has received from the Father the promised Holy Spirit and has poured out what you now see and hear.
acts:2:33.csb: Therefore, since he has been exalted to the right hand of God and has received from the Father the promised Holy Spirit, he has poured out what you both see and hear.
acts:2:33.nrsv: Being therefore exalted at the right hand of God, and having received from the Father the promise of the Holy Spirit, he has poured out this that you both see and hear.

acts:2:34.niv: For David did not ascend to heaven, and yet he said, "'The Lord said to my Lord: "Sit at my right hand
acts:2:34.csb: For it was not David who ascended into the heavens, but he himself says: The Lord declared to my Lord, ‘Sit at my right hand
acts:2:34.nrsv: For David did not ascend into the heavens, but he himself says, 'The Lord said to my Lord, "Sit at my right hand,

acts:2:35.niv: until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet."'
acts:2:35.csb: until I make your enemies your footstool.’
acts:2:35.nrsv: until I make your enemies your footstool."'

acts:2:36.niv: "Therefore let all Israel be assured of this: God has made this Jesus, whom you crucified, both Lord and Christ."
acts:2:36.csb: “Therefore let all the house of Israel know with certainty that God has made this Jesus, whom you crucified, both Lord and Messiah.”
acts:2:36.nrsv: Therefore let the entire house of Israel know with certainty that God has made him both Lord and Messiah, this Jesus whom you crucified."

acts:2:37.niv: When the people heard this, they were cut to the heart and said to Peter and the other apostles, "Brothers, what shall we do?"
acts:2:37.csb: When they heard this, they were pierced to the heart and said to Peter and the rest of the apostles: “Brothers, what should we do?”
acts:2:37.nrsv: Now when they heard this, they were cut to the heart and said to Peter and to the other apostles, "Brothers, what should we do?"

acts:2:38.niv: Peter replied, "Repent and be baptized, every one of you, in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins. And you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.
acts:2:38.csb: Peter replied, “Repent and be baptized, each of you, in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins, and you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.
acts:2:38.nrsv: Peter said to them, "Repent, and be baptized every one of you in the name of Jesus Christ so that your sins may be forgiven; and you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.

acts:2:39.niv: The promise is for you and your children and for all who are far off--for all whom the Lord our God will call."
acts:2:39.csb: For the promise is for you and for your children, and for all who are far off, as many as the Lord our God will call.”
acts:2:39.nrsv: For the promise is for you, for your children, and for all who are far away, everyone whom the Lord our God calls to him."

acts:2:40.niv: With many other words he warned them; and he pleaded with them, "Save yourselves from this corrupt generation."
acts:2:40.csb: With many other words he testified and strongly urged them, saying, “Be saved from this corrupt generation!”
acts:2:40.nrsv: And he testified with many other arguments and exhorted them, saying, "Save yourselves from this corrupt generation."

acts:2:41.niv: Those who accepted his message were baptized, and about three thousand were added to their number that day.
acts:2:41.csb: So those who accepted his message were baptized, and that day about three thousand people were added to them.
acts:2:41.nrsv: So those who welcomed his message were baptized, and that day about three thousand persons were added.

acts:2:42.niv: They devoted themselves to the apostles' teaching and to the fellowship, to the breaking of bread and to prayer.
acts:2:42.csb: They devoted themselves to the apostles’ teaching, to the fellowship, to the breaking of bread, and to prayer.
acts:2:42.nrsv: They devoted themselves to the apostles' teaching and fellowship, to the breaking of bread and the prayers.

acts:2:43.niv: Everyone was filled with awe, and many wonders and miraculous signs were done by the apostles.
acts:2:43.csb: Everyone was filled with awe, and many wonders and signs were being performed through the apostles.
acts:2:43.nrsv: Awe came upon everyone, because many wonders and signs were being done by the apostles.

acts:2:44.niv: All the believers were together and had everything in common.
acts:2:44.csb: Now all the believers were together and held all things in common.
acts:2:44.nrsv: All who believed were together and had all things in common;

acts:2:45.niv: Selling their possessions and goods, they gave to anyone as he had need.
acts:2:45.csb: They sold their possessions and property and distributed the proceeds to all, as any had need.
acts:2:45.nrsv: they would sell their possessions and goods and distribute the proceeds to all, as any had need.

acts:2:46.niv: Every day they continued to meet together in the temple courts. They broke bread in their homes and ate together with glad and sincere hearts,
acts:2:46.csb: Every day they devoted themselves to meeting together in the temple, and broke bread from house to house. They ate their food with joyful and sincere hearts,
acts:2:46.nrsv: Day by day, as they spent much time together in the temple, they broke bread at home and ate their food with glad and generous hearts,

acts:2:47.niv: praising God and enjoying the favor of all the people. And the Lord added to their number daily those who were being saved.
acts:2:47.csb: praising God and enjoying the favor of all the people. Every day the Lord added to their number those who were being saved.
acts:2:47.nrsv: praising God and having the goodwill of all the people. And day by day the Lord added to their number those who were being saved.

acts:3:1.niv: One day Peter and John were going up to the temple at the time of prayer--at three in the afternoon.
acts:3:1.csb: Now Peter and John were going up to the temple for the time of prayer at three in the afternoon.
acts:3:1.nrsv: One day Peter and John were going up to the temple at the hour of prayer, at three o'clock in the afternoon.

acts:3:2.niv: Now a man crippled from birth was being carried to the temple gate called Beautiful, where he was put every day to beg from those going into the temple courts.
acts:3:2.csb: A man who was lame from birth was being carried there. He was placed each day at the temple gate called Beautiful, so that he could beg from those entering the temple.
acts:3:2.nrsv: And a man lame from birth was being carried in. People would lay him daily at the gate of the temple called the Beautiful Gate so that he could ask for alms from those entering the temple.

acts:3:3.niv: When he saw Peter and John about to enter, he asked them for money.
acts:3:3.csb: When he saw Peter and John about to enter the temple, he asked for money.
acts:3:3.nrsv: When he saw Peter and John about to go into the temple, he asked them for alms.

acts:3:4.niv: Peter looked straight at him, as did John. Then Peter said, "Look at us!"
acts:3:4.csb: Peter, along with John, looked straight at him and said, “Look at us.”
acts:3:4.nrsv: Peter looked intently at him, as did John, and said, "Look at us."

acts:3:5.niv: So the man gave them his attention, expecting to get something from them.
acts:3:5.csb: So he turned to them, expecting to get something from them.
acts:3:5.nrsv: And he fixed his attention on them, expecting to receive something from them.

acts:3:6.niv: Then Peter said, "Silver or gold I do not have, but what I have I give you. In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, walk."
acts:3:6.csb: But Peter said, “I don’t have silver or gold, but what I do have, I give you: In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, get up and walk!”
acts:3:6.nrsv: But Peter said, "I have no silver or gold, but what I have I give you; in the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, stand up and walk."

acts:3:7.niv: Taking him by the right hand, he helped him up, and instantly the man's feet and ankles became strong.
acts:3:7.csb: Then, taking him by the right hand he raised him up, and at once his feet and ankles became strong.
acts:3:7.nrsv: And he took him by the right hand and raised him up; and immediately his feet and ankles were made strong.

acts:3:8.niv: He jumped to his feet and began to walk. Then he went with them into the temple courts, walking and jumping, and praising God.
acts:3:8.csb: So he jumped up and started to walk, and he entered the temple with them — walking, leaping, and praising God.
acts:3:8.nrsv: Jumping up, he stood and began to walk, and he entered the temple with them, walking and leaping and praising God.

acts:3:9.niv: When all the people saw him walking and praising God,
acts:3:9.csb: All the people saw him walking and praising God,
acts:3:9.nrsv: All the people saw him walking and praising God,

acts:3:10.niv: they recognized him as the same man who used to sit begging at the temple gate called Beautiful, and they were filled with wonder and amazement at what had happened to him.
acts:3:10.csb: and they recognized that he was the one who used to sit and beg at the Beautiful Gate of the temple. So they were filled with awe and astonishment at what had happened to him.
acts:3:10.nrsv: and they recognized him as the one who used to sit and ask for alms at the Beautiful Gate of the temple; and they were filled with wonder and amazement at what had happened to him.

acts:3:11.niv: While the beggar held on to Peter and John, all the people were astonished and came running to them in the place called Solomon's Colonnade.
acts:3:11.csb: While he was holding on to Peter and John, all the people, utterly astonished, ran toward them in what is called Solomon’s Colonnade.
acts:3:11.nrsv: While he clung to Peter and John, all the people ran together to them in the portico called Solomon's Portico, utterly astonished.

acts:3:12.niv: When Peter saw this, he said to them: "Men of Israel, why does this surprise you? Why do you stare at us as if by our own power or godliness we had made this man walk?
acts:3:12.csb: When Peter saw this, he addressed the people: “Fellow Israelites, why are you amazed at this? Why do you stare at us, as though we had made him walk by our own power or godliness?
acts:3:12.nrsv: When Peter saw it, he addressed the people, "You Israelites, why do you wonder at this, or why do you stare at us, as though by our own power or piety we had made him walk?

acts:3:13.niv: The God of Abraham, Isaac and Jacob, the God of our fathers, has glorified his servant Jesus. You handed him over to be killed, and you disowned him before Pilate, though he had decided to let him go.
acts:3:13.csb: The God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, the God of our ancestors, has glorified his servant Jesus, whom you handed over and denied before Pilate, though he had decided to release him.
acts:3:13.nrsv: The God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob, the God of our ancestors has glorified his servant Jesus, whom you handed over and rejected in the presence of Pilate, though he had decided to release him.

acts:3:14.niv: You disowned the Holy and Righteous One and asked that a murderer be released to you.
acts:3:14.csb: You denied the Holy and Righteous One and asked to have a murderer released to you.
acts:3:14.nrsv: But you rejected the Holy and Righteous One and asked to have a murderer given to you,

acts:3:15.niv: You killed the author of life, but God raised him from the dead. We are witnesses of this.
acts:3:15.csb: You killed the source of life, whom God raised from the dead; we are witnesses of this.
acts:3:15.nrsv: and you killed the Author of life, whom God raised from the dead. To this we are witnesses.

acts:3:16.niv: By faith in the name of Jesus, this man whom you see and know was made strong. It is Jesus' name and the faith that comes through him that has given this complete healing to him, as you can all see.
acts:3:16.csb: By faith in his name, his name has made this man strong, whom you see and know. So the faith that comes through Jesus has given him this perfect health in front of all of you.
acts:3:16.nrsv: And by faith in his name, his name itself has made this man strong, whom you see and know; and the faith that is through Jesus has given him this perfect health in the presence of all of you.

acts:3:17.niv: "Now, brothers, I know that you acted in ignorance, as did your leaders.
acts:3:17.csb: “And now, brothers and sisters, I know that you acted in ignorance, just as your leaders also did.
acts:3:17.nrsv: "And now, friends, I know that you acted in ignorance, as did also your rulers.

acts:3:18.niv: But this is how God fulfilled what he had foretold through all the prophets, saying that his Christ would suffer.
acts:3:18.csb: In this way God fulfilled what he had predicted through all the prophets — that his Messiah would suffer.
acts:3:18.nrsv: In this way God fulfilled what he had foretold through all the prophets, that his Messiah would suffer.

acts:3:19.niv: Repent, then, and turn to God, so that your sins may be wiped out, that times of refreshing may come from the Lord,
acts:3:19.csb: Therefore repent and turn back, so that your sins may be wiped out,
acts:3:19.nrsv: Repent therefore, and turn to God so that your sins may be wiped out,

acts:3:20.niv: and that he may send the Christ, who has been appointed for you--even Jesus.
acts:3:20.csb: that seasons of refreshing may come from the presence of the Lord, and that he may send Jesus, who has been appointed for you as the Messiah.
acts:3:20.nrsv: so that times of refreshing may come from the presence of the Lord, and that he may send the Messiah appointed for you, that is, Jesus,

acts:3:21.niv: He must remain in heaven until the time comes for God to restore everything, as he promised long ago through his holy prophets.
acts:3:21.csb: Heaven must receive him until the time of the restoration of all things, which God spoke about through his holy prophets from the beginning.
acts:3:21.nrsv: who must remain in heaven until the time of universal restoration that God announced long ago through his holy prophets.

acts:3:22.niv: For Moses said, 'The Lord your God will raise up for you a prophet like me from among your own people; you must listen to everything he tells you.
acts:3:22.csb: Moses said: The Lord your God will raise up for you a prophet like me from among your brothers and sisters. You must listen to everything he tells you.
acts:3:22.nrsv: Moses said, 'The Lord your God will raise up for you from your own people a prophet like me. You must listen to whatever he tells you.

acts:3:23.niv: Anyone who does not listen to him will be completely cut off from among his people.'
acts:3:23.csb: And everyone who does not listen to that prophet will be completely cut off from the people.
acts:3:23.nrsv: And it will be that everyone who does not listen to that prophet will be utterly rooted out of the people.'

acts:3:24.niv: "Indeed, all the prophets from Samuel on, as many as have spoken, have foretold these days.
acts:3:24.csb: “In addition, all the prophets who have spoken, from Samuel and those after him, have also foretold these days.
acts:3:24.nrsv: And all the prophets, as many as have spoken, from Samuel and those after him, also predicted these days.

acts:3:25.niv: And you are heirs of the prophets and of the covenant God made with your fathers. He said to Abraham, 'Through your offspring all peoples on earth will be blessed.'
acts:3:25.csb: You are the sons of the prophets and of the covenant that God made with your ancestors, saying to Abraham, And all the families of the earth will be blessed through your offspring.
acts:3:25.nrsv: You are the descendants of the prophets and of the covenant that God gave to your ancestors, saying to Abraham, 'And in your descendants all the families of the earth shall be blessed.'

acts:3:26.niv: When God raised up his servant, he sent him first to you to bless you by turning each of you from your wicked ways."
acts:3:26.csb: God raised up his servant and sent him first to you to bless you by turning each of you from your evil ways.”
acts:3:26.nrsv: When God raised up his servant, he sent him first to you, to bless you by turning each of you from your wicked ways."

acts:4:1.niv: The priests and the captain of the temple guard and the Sadducees came up to Peter and John while they were speaking to the people.
acts:4:1.csb: While they were speaking to the people, the priests, the captain of the temple police, and the Sadducees confronted them,
acts:4:1.nrsv: While Peter and John were speaking to the people, the priests, the captain of the temple, and the Sadducees came to them,

acts:4:2.niv: They were greatly disturbed because the apostles were teaching the people and proclaiming in Jesus the resurrection of the dead.
acts:4:2.csb: because they were annoyed that they were teaching the people and proclaiming in Jesus the resurrection of the dead.
acts:4:2.nrsv: much annoyed because they were teaching the people and proclaiming that in Jesus there is the resurrection of the dead.

acts:4:3.niv: They seized Peter and John, and because it was evening, they put them in jail until the next day.
acts:4:3.csb: So they seized them and took them into custody until the next day since it was already evening.
acts:4:3.nrsv: So they arrested them and put them in custody until the next day, for it was already evening.

acts:4:4.niv: But many who heard the message believed, and the number of men grew to about five thousand.
acts:4:4.csb: But many of those who heard the message believed, and the number of the men came to about five thousand.
acts:4:4.nrsv: But many of those who heard the word believed; and they numbered about five thousand.

acts:4:5.niv: The next day the rulers, elders and teachers of the law met in Jerusalem.
acts:4:5.csb: The next day, their rulers, elders, and scribes assembled in Jerusalem
acts:4:5.nrsv: The next day their rulers, elders, and scribes assembled in Jerusalem,

acts:4:6.niv: Annas the high priest was there, and so were Caiaphas, John, Alexander and the other men of the high priest's family.
acts:4:6.csb: with Annas the high priest, Caiaphas, John, Alexander, and all the members of the high-priestly family.
acts:4:6.nrsv: with Annas the high priest, Caiaphas, John, and Alexander, and all who were of the high-priestly family.

acts:4:7.niv: They had Peter and John brought before them and began to question them: "By what power or what name did you do this?"
acts:4:7.csb: After they had Peter and John stand before them, they began to question them: “By what power or in what name have you done this?”
acts:4:7.nrsv: When they had made the prisoners stand in their midst, they inquired, "By what power or by what name did you do this?"

acts:4:8.niv: Then Peter, filled with the Holy Spirit, said to them: "Rulers and elders of the people!
acts:4:8.csb: Then Peter was filled with the Holy Spirit and said to them, “Rulers of the people and elders:
acts:4:8.nrsv: Then Peter, filled with the Holy Spirit, said to them, "Rulers of the people and elders,

acts:4:9.niv: If we are being called to account today for an act of kindness shown to a cripple and are asked how he was healed,
acts:4:9.csb: If we are being examined today about a good deed done to a disabled man, by what means he was healed,
acts:4:9.nrsv: if we are questioned today because of a good deed done to someone who was sick and are asked how this man has been healed,

acts:4:10.niv: then know this, you and all the people of Israel: It is by the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, whom you crucified but whom God raised from the dead, that this man stands before you healed.
acts:4:10.csb: let it be known to all of you and to all the people of Israel, that by the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, whom you crucified and whom God raised from the dead — by him this man is standing here before you healthy.
acts:4:10.nrsv: let it be known to all of you, and to all the people of Israel, that this man is standing before you in good health by the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, whom you crucified, whom God raised from the dead.

acts:4:11.niv: He is "'the stone you builders rejected, which has become the capstone. '
acts:4:11.csb: This Jesus is the stone rejected by you builders, which has become the cornerstone.
acts:4:11.nrsv: This Jesus is 'the stone that was rejected by you, the builders; it has become the cornerstone.'

acts:4:12.niv: Salvation is found in no one else, for there is no other name under heaven given to men by which we must be saved."
acts:4:12.csb: There is salvation in no one else, for there is no other name under heaven given to people by which we must be saved.”
acts:4:12.nrsv: There is salvation in no one else, for there is no other name under heaven given among mortals by which we must be saved."

acts:4:13.niv: When they saw the courage of Peter and John and realized that they were unschooled, ordinary men, they were astonished and they took note that these men had been with Jesus.
acts:4:13.csb: When they observed the boldness of Peter and John and realized that they were uneducated and untrained men, they were amazed and recognized that they had been with Jesus.
acts:4:13.nrsv: Now when they saw the boldness of Peter and John and realized that they were uneducated and ordinary men, they were amazed and recognized them as companions of Jesus.

acts:4:14.niv: But since they could see the man who had been healed standing there with them, there was nothing they could say.
acts:4:14.csb: And since they saw the man who had been healed standing with them, they had nothing to say in opposition.
acts:4:14.nrsv: When they saw the man who had been cured standing beside them, they had nothing to say in opposition.

acts:4:15.niv: So they ordered them to withdraw from the Sanhedrin and then conferred together.
acts:4:15.csb: After they ordered them to leave the Sanhedrin, they conferred among themselves,
acts:4:15.nrsv: So they ordered them to leave the council while they discussed the matter with one another.

acts:4:16.niv: "What are we going to do with these men?" they asked. "Everybody living in Jerusalem knows they have done an outstanding miracle, and we cannot deny it.
acts:4:16.csb: saying, “What should we do with these men? For an obvious sign has been done through them, clear to everyone living in Jerusalem, and we cannot deny it.
acts:4:16.nrsv: They said, "What will we do with them? For it is obvious to all who live in Jerusalem that a notable sign has been done through them; we cannot deny it.

acts:4:17.niv: But to stop this thing from spreading any further among the people, we must warn these men to speak no longer to anyone in this name."
acts:4:17.csb: But so that this does not spread any further among the people, let’s threaten them against speaking to anyone in this name again.”
acts:4:17.nrsv: But to keep it from spreading further among the people, let us warn them to speak no more to anyone in this name."

acts:4:18.niv: Then they called them in again and commanded them not to speak or teach at all in the name of Jesus.
acts:4:18.csb: So they called for them and ordered them not to speak or teach at all in the name of Jesus.
acts:4:18.nrsv: So they called them and ordered them not to speak or teach at all in the name of Jesus.

acts:4:19.niv: But Peter and John replied, "Judge for yourselves whether it is right in God's sight to obey you rather than God.
acts:4:19.csb: Peter and John answered them, “Whether it’s right in the sight of God for us to listen to you rather than to God, you decide;
acts:4:19.nrsv: But Peter and John answered them, "Whether it is right in God's sight to listen to you rather than to God, you must judge;

acts:4:20.niv: For we cannot help speaking about what we have seen and heard."
acts:4:20.csb: for we are unable to stop speaking about what we have seen and heard.”
acts:4:20.nrsv: for we cannot keep from speaking about what we have seen and heard."

acts:4:21.niv: After further threats they let them go. They could not decide how to punish them, because all the people were praising God for what had happened.
acts:4:21.csb: After threatening them further, they released them. They found no way to punish them because the people were all giving glory to God over what had been done.
acts:4:21.nrsv: After threatening them again, they let them go, finding no way to punish them because of the people, for all of them praised God for what had happened.

acts:4:22.niv: For the man who was miraculously healed was over forty years old.
acts:4:22.csb: For this sign of healing had been performed on a man over forty years old.
acts:4:22.nrsv: For the man on whom this sign of healing had been performed was more than forty years old.

acts:4:23.niv: On their release, Peter and John went back to their own people and reported all that the chief priests and elders had said to them.
acts:4:23.csb: After they were released, they went to their own people and reported everything the chief priests and the elders had said to them.
acts:4:23.nrsv: After they were released, they went to their friends and reported what the chief priests and the elders had said to them.

acts:4:24.niv: When they heard this, they raised their voices together in prayer to God. "Sovereign Lord," they said, "you made the heaven and the earth and the sea, and everything in them.
acts:4:24.csb: When they heard this, they raised their voices together to God and said, “Master, you are the one who made the heaven, the earth, and the sea, and everything in them.
acts:4:24.nrsv: When they heard it, they raised their voices together to God and said, "Sovereign Lord, who made the heaven and the earth, the sea, and everything in them,

acts:4:25.niv: You spoke by the Holy Spirit through the mouth of your servant, our father David: "'Why do the nations rage and the peoples plot in vain?
acts:4:25.csb: You said through the Holy Spirit, by the mouth of our father David your servant: Why do the Gentiles rage and the peoples plot futile things?
acts:4:25.nrsv: it is you who said by the Holy Spirit through our ancestor David, your servant: 'Why did the Gentiles rage, and the peoples imagine vain things?

acts:4:26.niv: The kings of the earth take their stand and the rulers gather together against the Lord and against his Anointed One. '
acts:4:26.csb: The kings of the earth take their stand and the rulers assemble together against the Lord and against his Messiah.
acts:4:26.nrsv: The kings of the earth took their stand, and the rulers have gathered together against the Lord and against his Messiah.'

acts:4:27.niv: Indeed Herod and Pontius Pilate met together with the Gentiles and the people of Israel in this city to conspire against your holy servant Jesus, whom you anointed.
acts:4:27.csb: “For, in fact, in this city both Herod and Pontius Pilate, with the Gentiles and the people of Israel, assembled together against your holy servant Jesus, whom you anointed,
acts:4:27.nrsv: For in this city, in fact, both Herod and Pontius Pilate, with the Gentiles and the peoples of Israel, gathered together against your holy servant Jesus, whom you anointed,

acts:4:28.niv: They did what your power and will had decided beforehand should happen.
acts:4:28.csb: to do whatever your hand and your will had predestined to take place.
acts:4:28.nrsv: to do whatever your hand and your plan had predestined to take place.

acts:4:29.niv: Now, Lord, consider their threats and enable your servants to speak your word with great boldness.
acts:4:29.csb: And now, Lord, consider their threats, and grant that your servants may speak your word with all boldness,
acts:4:29.nrsv: And now, Lord, look at their threats, and grant to your servants to speak your word with all boldness,

acts:4:30.niv: Stretch out your hand to heal and perform miraculous signs and wonders through the name of your holy servant Jesus."
acts:4:30.csb: while you stretch out your hand for healing, and signs and wonders are performed through the name of your holy servant Jesus.”
acts:4:30.nrsv: while you stretch out your hand to heal, and signs and wonders are performed through the name of your holy servant Jesus."

acts:4:31.niv: After they prayed, the place where they were meeting was shaken. And they were all filled with the Holy Spirit and spoke the word of God boldly.
acts:4:31.csb: When they had prayed, the place where they were assembled was shaken, and they were all filled with the Holy Spirit and began to speak the word of God boldly.
acts:4:31.nrsv: When they had prayed, the place in which they were gathered together was shaken; and they were all filled with the Holy Spirit and spoke the word of God with boldness.

acts:4:32.niv: All the believers were one in heart and mind. No one claimed that any of his possessions was his own, but they shared everything they had.
acts:4:32.csb: Now the entire group of those who believed were of one heart and mind, and no one claimed that any of his possessions was his own, but instead they held everything in common.
acts:4:32.nrsv: Now the whole group of those who believed were of one heart and soul, and no one claimed private ownership of any possessions, but everything they owned was held in common.

acts:4:33.niv: With great power the apostles continued to testify to the resurrection of the Lord Jesus, and much grace was upon them all.
acts:4:33.csb: With great power the apostles were giving testimony to the resurrection of the Lord Jesus, and great grace was on all of them.
acts:4:33.nrsv: With great power the apostles gave their testimony to the resurrection of the Lord Jesus, and great grace was upon them all.

acts:4:34.niv: There were no needy persons among them. For from time to time those who owned lands or houses sold them, brought the money from the sales
acts:4:34.csb: For there was not a needy person among them because all those who owned lands or houses sold them, brought the proceeds of what was sold,
acts:4:34.nrsv: There was not a needy person among them, for as many as owned lands or houses sold them and brought the proceeds of what was sold.

acts:4:35.niv: and put it at the apostles' feet, and it was distributed to anyone as he had need.
acts:4:35.csb: and laid them at the apostles’ feet. This was then distributed to each person as any had need.
acts:4:35.nrsv: They laid it at the apostles' feet, and it was distributed to each as any had need.

acts:4:36.niv: Joseph, a Levite from Cyprus, whom the apostles called Barnabas (which means Son of Encouragement),
acts:4:36.csb: Joseph, a Levite from Cyprus by birth, the one the apostles called Barnabas (which is translated Son of Encouragement),
acts:4:36.nrsv: There was a Levite, a native of Cyprus, Joseph, to whom the apostles gave the name Barnabas (which means "son of encouragement").

acts:4:37.niv: sold a field he owned and brought the money and put it at the apostles' feet.
acts:4:37.csb: sold a field he owned, brought the money, and laid it at the apostles’ feet.
acts:4:37.nrsv: He sold a field that belonged to him, then brought the money, and laid it at the apostles' feet.

acts:5:1.niv: Now a man named Ananias, together with his wife Sapphira, also sold a piece of property.
acts:5:1.csb: But a man named Ananias, with his wife Sapphira, sold a piece of property.
acts:5:1.nrsv: But a man named Ananias, with the consent of his wife Sapphira, sold a piece of property;

acts:5:2.niv: With his wife's full knowledge he kept back part of the money for himself, but brought the rest and put it at the apostles' feet.
acts:5:2.csb: However, he kept back part of the proceeds with his wife’s knowledge, and brought a portion of it and laid it at the apostles’ feet.
acts:5:2.nrsv: with his wife's knowledge, he kept back some of the proceeds, and brought only a part and laid it at the apostles' feet.

acts:5:3.niv: Then Peter said, "Ananias, how is it that Satan has so filled your heart that you have lied to the Holy Spirit and have kept for yourself some of the money you received for the land?
acts:5:3.csb: “Ananias,” Peter asked, “why has Satan filled your heart to lie to the Holy Spirit and keep back part of the proceeds of the land?
acts:5:3.nrsv: "Ananias," Peter asked, "why has Satan filled your heart to lie to the Holy Spirit and to keep back part of the proceeds of the land?

acts:5:4.niv: Didn't it belong to you before it was sold? And after it was sold, wasn't the money at your disposal? What made you think of doing such a thing? You have not lied to men but to God."
acts:5:4.csb: Wasn’t it yours while you possessed it? And after it was sold, wasn’t it at your disposal? Why is it that you planned this thing in your heart? You have not lied to people but to God.”
acts:5:4.nrsv: While it remained unsold, did it not remain your own? And after it was sold, were not the proceeds at your disposal? How is it that you have contrived this deed in your heart? You did not lie to us but to God!"

acts:5:5.niv: When Ananias heard this, he fell down and died. And great fear seized all who heard what had happened.
acts:5:5.csb: When he heard these words, Ananias dropped dead, and a great fear came on all who heard.
acts:5:5.nrsv: Now when Ananias heard these words, he fell down and died. And great fear seized all who heard of it.

acts:5:6.niv: Then the young men came forward, wrapped up his body, and carried him out and buried him.
acts:5:6.csb: The young men got up, wrapped his body, carried him out, and buried him.
acts:5:6.nrsv: The young men came and wrapped up his body, then carried him out and buried him.

acts:5:7.niv: About three hours later his wife came in, not knowing what had happened.
acts:5:7.csb: About three hours later, his wife came in, not knowing what had happened.
acts:5:7.nrsv: After an interval of about three hours his wife came in, not knowing what had happened.

acts:5:8.niv: Peter asked her, "Tell me, is this the price you and Ananias got for the land?Yes," she said, "that is the price."
acts:5:8.csb: “Tell me,” Peter asked her, “did you sell the land for this price?” “Yes,” she said, “for that price.”
acts:5:8.nrsv: Peter said to her, "Tell me whether you and your husband sold the land for such and such a price." And she said, "Yes, that was the price."

acts:5:9.niv: Peter said to her, "How could you agree to test the Spirit of the Lord? Look! The feet of the men who buried your husband are at the door, and they will carry you out also."
acts:5:9.csb: Then Peter said to her, “Why did you agree to test the Spirit of the Lord? Look, the feet of those who have buried your husband are at the door, and they will carry you out.”
acts:5:9.nrsv: Then Peter said to her, "How is it that you have agreed together to put the Spirit of the Lord to the test? Look, the feet of those who have buried your husband are at the door, and they will carry you out."

acts:5:10.niv: At that moment she fell down at his feet and died. Then the young men came in and, finding her dead, carried her out and buried her beside her husband.
acts:5:10.csb: Instantly she dropped dead at his feet. When the young men came in, they found her dead, carried her out, and buried her beside her husband.
acts:5:10.nrsv: Immediately she fell down at his feet and died. When the young men came in they found her dead, so they carried her out and buried her beside her husband.

acts:5:11.niv: Great fear seized the whole church and all who heard about these events.
acts:5:11.csb: Then great fear came on the whole church and on all who heard these things.
acts:5:11.nrsv: And great fear seized the whole church and all who heard of these things.

acts:5:12.niv: The apostles performed many miraculous signs and wonders among the people. And all the believers used to meet together in Solomon's Colonnade.
acts:5:12.csb: Many signs and wonders were being done among the people through the hands of the apostles. They were all together in Solomon’s Colonnade.
acts:5:12.nrsv: Now many signs and wonders were done among the people through the apostles. And they were all together in Solomon's Portico.

acts:5:13.niv: No one else dared join them, even though they were highly regarded by the people.
acts:5:13.csb: No one else dared to join them, but the people spoke well of them.
acts:5:13.nrsv: None of the rest dared to join them, but the people held them in high esteem.

acts:5:14.niv: Nevertheless, more and more men and women believed in the Lord and were added to their number.
acts:5:14.csb: Believers were added to the Lord in increasing numbers — multitudes of both men and women.
acts:5:14.nrsv: Yet more than ever believers were added to the Lord, great numbers of both men and women,

acts:5:15.niv: As a result, people brought the sick into the streets and laid them on beds and mats so that at least Peter's shadow might fall on some of them as he passed by.
acts:5:15.csb: As a result, they would carry the sick out into the streets and lay them on cots and mats so that when Peter came by, at least his shadow might fall on some of them.
acts:5:15.nrsv: so that they even carried out the sick into the streets, and laid them on cots and mats, in order that Peter's shadow might fall on some of them as he came by.

acts:5:16.niv: Crowds gathered also from the towns around Jerusalem, bringing their sick and those tormented by evil spirits, and all of them were healed.
acts:5:16.csb: In addition, a multitude came together from the towns surrounding Jerusalem, bringing the sick and those who were tormented by unclean spirits, and they were all healed.
acts:5:16.nrsv: A great number of people would also gather from the towns around Jerusalem, bringing the sick and those tormented by unclean spirits, and they were all cured.

acts:5:17.niv: Then the high priest and all his associates, who were members of the party of the Sadducees, were filled with jealousy.
acts:5:17.csb: Then the high priest rose up. He and all who were with him, who belonged to the party of the Sadducees, were filled with jealousy.
acts:5:17.nrsv: Then the high priest took action; he and all who were with him (that is, the sect of the Sadducees), being filled with jealousy,

acts:5:18.niv: They arrested the apostles and put them in the public jail.
acts:5:18.csb: So they arrested the apostles and put them in the public jail.
acts:5:18.nrsv: arrested the apostles and put them in the public prison.

acts:5:19.niv: But during the night an angel of the Lord opened the doors of the jail and brought them out.
acts:5:19.csb: But an angel of the Lord opened the doors of the jail during the night, brought them out, and said,
acts:5:19.nrsv: But during the night an angel of the Lord opened the prison doors, brought them out, and said,

acts:5:20.niv: "Go, stand in the temple courts," he said, "and tell the people the full message of this new life."
acts:5:20.csb: “Go and stand in the temple, and tell the people all about this life.”
acts:5:20.nrsv: "Go, stand in the temple and tell the people the whole message about this life."

acts:5:21.niv: At daybreak they entered the temple courts, as they had been told, and began to teach the people.
acts:5:21.csb: Hearing this, they entered the temple at daybreak and began to teach. When the high priest and those who were with him arrived, they convened the Sanhedrin — the full council of the Israelites — and sent orders to the jail to have them brought.
acts:5:21.nrsv: When they heard this, they entered the temple at daybreak and went on with their teaching. When the high priest and those with him arrived, they called together the council and the whole body of the elders of Israel, and sent to the prison to have them brought.

acts:5:22.niv: When the high priest and his associates arrived, they called together the Sanhedrin--the full assembly of the elders of Israel--and sent to the jail for the apostles. But on arriving at the jail, the officers did not find them there. So they went back and reported,
acts:5:22.csb: But when the servants got there, they did not find them in the jail, so they returned and reported,
acts:5:22.nrsv: But when the temple police went there, they did not find them in the prison; so they returned and reported,

acts:5:23.niv: "We found the jail securely locked, with the guards standing at the doors; but when we opened them, we found no one inside."
acts:5:23.csb: “We found the jail securely locked, with the guards standing in front of the doors, but when we opened them, we found no one inside.”
acts:5:23.nrsv: "We found the prison securely locked and the guards standing at the doors, but when we opened them, we found no one inside."

acts:5:24.niv: On hearing this report, the captain of the temple guard and the chief priests were puzzled, wondering what would come of this.
acts:5:24.csb: As the captain of the temple police and the chief priests heard these things, they were baffled about them, wondering what would come of this.
acts:5:24.nrsv: Now when the captain of the temple and the chief priests heard these words, they were perplexed about them, wondering what might be going on.

acts:5:25.niv: Then someone came and said, "Look! The men you put in jail are standing in the temple courts teaching the people."
acts:5:25.csb: Someone came and reported to them, “Look! The men you put in jail are standing in the temple and teaching the people.”
acts:5:25.nrsv: Then someone arrived and announced, "Look, the men whom you put in prison are standing in the temple and teaching the people!"

acts:5:26.niv: At that, the captain went with his officers and brought the apostles. They did not use force, because they feared that the people would stone them.
acts:5:26.csb: Then the commander went with the servants and brought them in without force, because they were afraid the people might stone them.
acts:5:26.nrsv: Then the captain went with the temple police and brought them, but without violence, for they were afraid of being stoned by the people.

acts:5:27.niv: Having brought the apostles, they made them appear before the Sanhedrin to be questioned by the high priest.
acts:5:27.csb: After they brought them in, they had them stand before the Sanhedrin, and the high priest asked,
acts:5:27.nrsv: When they had brought them, they had them stand before the council. The high priest questioned them,

acts:5:28.niv: "We gave you strict orders not to teach in this name," he said. "Yet you have filled Jerusalem with your teaching and are determined to make us guilty of this man's blood."
acts:5:28.csb: “Didn’t we strictly order you not to teach in this name? Look, you have filled Jerusalem with your teaching and are determined to make us guilty of this man’s blood.”
acts:5:28.nrsv: saying, "We gave you strict orders not to teach in this name, yet here you have filled Jerusalem with your teaching and you are determined to bring this man's blood on us."

acts:5:29.niv: Peter and the other apostles replied: "We must obey God rather than men!
acts:5:29.csb: Peter and the apostles replied, “We must obey God rather than people.
acts:5:29.nrsv: But Peter and the apostles answered, "We must obey God rather than any human authority.

acts:5:30.niv: The God of our fathers raised Jesus from the dead--whom you had killed by hanging him on a tree.
acts:5:30.csb: The God of our ancestors raised up Jesus, whom you had murdered by hanging him on a tree.
acts:5:30.nrsv: The God of our ancestors raised up Jesus, whom you had killed by hanging him on a tree.

acts:5:31.niv: God exalted him to his own right hand as Prince and Savior that he might give repentance and forgiveness of sins to Israel.
acts:5:31.csb: God exalted this man to his right hand as ruler and Savior, to give repentance to Israel and forgiveness of sins.
acts:5:31.nrsv: God exalted him at his right hand as Leader and Savior that he might give repentance to Israel and forgiveness of sins.

acts:5:32.niv: We are witnesses of these things, and so is the Holy Spirit, whom God has given to those who obey him."
acts:5:32.csb: We are witnesses of these things, and so is the Holy Spirit whom God has given to those who obey him.”
acts:5:32.nrsv: And we are witnesses to these things, and so is the Holy Spirit whom God has given to those who obey him."

acts:5:33.niv: When they heard this, they were furious and wanted to put them to death.
acts:5:33.csb: When they heard this, they were enraged and wanted to kill them.
acts:5:33.nrsv: When they heard this, they were enraged and wanted to kill them.

acts:5:34.niv: But a Pharisee named Gamaliel, a teacher of the law, who was honored by all the people, stood up in the Sanhedrin and ordered that the men be put outside for a little while.
acts:5:34.csb: But a Pharisee named Gamaliel, a teacher of the law who was respected by all the people, stood up in the Sanhedrin and ordered the men to be taken outside for a little while.
acts:5:34.nrsv: But a Pharisee in the council named Gamaliel, a teacher of the law, respected by all the people, stood up and ordered the men to be put outside for a short time.

acts:5:35.niv: Then he addressed them: "Men of Israel, consider carefully what you intend to do to these men.
acts:5:35.csb: He said to them, “Men of Israel, be careful about what you’re about to do to these men.
acts:5:35.nrsv: Then he said to them, "Fellow Israelites, consider carefully what you propose to do to these men.

acts:5:36.niv: Some time ago Theudas appeared, claiming to be somebody, and about four hundred men rallied to him. He was killed, all his followers were dispersed, and it all came to nothing.
acts:5:36.csb: Some time ago Theudas rose up, claiming to be somebody, and a group of about four hundred men rallied to him. He was killed, and all his followers were dispersed and came to nothing.
acts:5:36.nrsv: For some time ago Theudas rose up, claiming to be somebody, and a number of men, about four hundred, joined him; but he was killed, and all who followed him were dispersed and disappeared.

acts:5:37.niv: After him, Judas the Galilean appeared in the days of the census and led a band of people in revolt. He too was killed, and all his followers were scattered.
acts:5:37.csb: After this man, Judas the Galilean rose up in the days of the census and attracted a following. He also perished, and all his followers were scattered.
acts:5:37.nrsv: After him Judas the Galilean rose up at the time of the census and got people to follow him; he also perished, and all who followed him were scattered.

acts:5:38.niv: Therefore, in the present case I advise you: Leave these men alone! Let them go! For if their purpose or activity is of human origin, it will fail.
acts:5:38.csb: So in the present case, I tell you, stay away from these men and leave them alone. For if this plan or this work is of human origin, it will fail;
acts:5:38.nrsv: So in the present case, I tell you, keep away from these men and let them alone; because if this plan or this undertaking is of human origin, it will fail;

acts:5:39.niv: But if it is from God, you will not be able to stop these men; you will only find yourselves fighting against God."
acts:5:39.csb: but if it is of God, you will not be able to overthrow them. You may even be found fighting against God.” They were persuaded by him.
acts:5:39.nrsv: but if it is of God, you will not be able to overthrow them-- in that case you may even be found fighting against God!" They were convinced by him,

acts:5:40.niv: His speech persuaded them. They called the apostles in and had them flogged. Then they ordered them not to speak in the name of Jesus, and let them go.
acts:5:40.csb: After they called in the apostles and had them flogged, they ordered them not to speak in the name of Jesus and released them.
acts:5:40.nrsv: and when they had called in the apostles, they had them flogged. Then they ordered them not to speak in the name of Jesus, and let them go.

acts:5:41.niv: The apostles left the Sanhedrin, rejoicing because they had been counted worthy of suffering disgrace for the Name.
acts:5:41.csb: Then they went out from the presence of the Sanhedrin, rejoicing that they were counted worthy to be treated shamefully on behalf of the Name.
acts:5:41.nrsv: As they left the council, they rejoiced that they were considered worthy to suffer dishonor for the sake of the name.

acts:5:42.niv: Day after day, in the temple courts and from house to house, they never stopped teaching and proclaiming the good news that Jesus is the Christ.
acts:5:42.csb: Every day in the temple, and in various homes, they continued teaching and proclaiming the good news that Jesus is the Messiah.
acts:5:42.nrsv: And every day in the temple and at home they did not cease to teach and proclaim Jesus as the Messiah.

acts:6:1.niv: In those days when the number of disciples was increasing, the Grecian Jews among them complained against the Hebraic Jews because their widows were being overlooked in the daily distribution of food.
acts:6:1.csb: In those days, as the disciples were increasing in number, there arose a complaint by the Hellenistic Jews against the Hebraic Jews that their widows were being overlooked in the daily distribution.
acts:6:1.nrsv: Now during those days, when the disciples were increasing in number, the Hellenists complained against the Hebrews because their widows were being neglected in the daily distribution of food.

acts:6:2.niv: So the Twelve gathered all the disciples together and said, "It would not be right for us to neglect the ministry of the word of God in order to wait on tables.
acts:6:2.csb: The Twelve summoned the whole company of the disciples and said, “It would not be right for us to give up preaching the word of God to wait on tables.
acts:6:2.nrsv: And the twelve called together the whole community of the disciples and said, "It is not right that we should neglect the word of God in order to wait on tables.

acts:6:3.niv: Brothers, choose seven men from among you who are known to be full of the Spirit and wisdom. We will turn this responsibility over to them
acts:6:3.csb: Brothers and sisters, select from among you seven men of good reputation, full of the Spirit and wisdom, whom we can appoint to this duty.
acts:6:3.nrsv: Therefore, friends, select from among yourselves seven men of good standing, full of the Spirit and of wisdom, whom we may appoint to this task,

acts:6:4.niv: and will give our attention to prayer and the ministry of the word."
acts:6:4.csb: But we will devote ourselves to prayer and to the ministry of the word.”
acts:6:4.nrsv: while we, for our part, will devote ourselves to prayer and to serving the word."

acts:6:5.niv: This proposal pleased the whole group. They chose Stephen, a man full of faith and of the Holy Spirit; also Philip, Procorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and Nicolas from Antioch, a convert to Judaism.
acts:6:5.csb: This proposal pleased the whole company. So they chose Stephen, a man full of faith and the Holy Spirit, and Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and Nicolaus, a convert from Antioch.
acts:6:5.nrsv: What they said pleased the whole community, and they chose Stephen, a man full of faith and the Holy Spirit, together with Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and Nicolaus, a proselyte of Antioch.

acts:6:6.niv: They presented these men to the apostles, who prayed and laid their hands on them.
acts:6:6.csb: They had them stand before the apostles, who prayed and laid their hands on them.
acts:6:6.nrsv: They had these men stand before the apostles, who prayed and laid their hands on them.

acts:6:7.niv: So the word of God spread. The number of disciples in Jerusalem increased rapidly, and a large number of priests became obedient to the faith.
acts:6:7.csb: So the word of God spread, the disciples in Jerusalem increased greatly in number, and a large group of priests became obedient to the faith.
acts:6:7.nrsv: The word of God continued to spread; the number of the disciples increased greatly in Jerusalem, and a great many of the priests became obedient to the faith.

acts:6:8.niv: Now Stephen, a man full of God's grace and power, did great wonders and miraculous signs among the people.
acts:6:8.csb: Now Stephen, full of grace and power, was performing great wonders and signs among the people.
acts:6:8.nrsv: Stephen, full of grace and power, did great wonders and signs among the people.

acts:6:9.niv: Opposition arose, however, from members of the Synagogue of the Freedmen (as it was called)--Jews of Cyrene and Alexandria as well as the provinces of Cilicia and Asia. These men began to argue with Stephen,
acts:6:9.csb: Opposition arose, however, from some members of the Freedmen’s Synagogue, composed of both Cyrenians and Alexandrians, and some from Cilicia and Asia, and they began to argue with Stephen.
acts:6:9.nrsv: Then some of those who belonged to the synagogue of the Freedmen (as it was called), Cyrenians, Alexandrians, and others of those from Cilicia and Asia, stood up and argued with Stephen.

acts:6:10.niv: but they could not stand up against his wisdom or the Spirit by whom he spoke.
acts:6:10.csb: But they were unable to stand up against his wisdom and the Spirit by whom he was speaking.
acts:6:10.nrsv: But they could not withstand the wisdom and the Spirit with which he spoke.

acts:6:11.niv: Then they secretly persuaded some men to say, "We have heard Stephen speak words of blasphemy against Moses and against God."
acts:6:11.csb: Then they secretly persuaded some men to say, “We heard him speaking blasphemous words against Moses and God.”
acts:6:11.nrsv: Then they secretly instigated some men to say, "We have heard him speak blasphemous words against Moses and God."

acts:6:12.niv: So they stirred up the people and the elders and the teachers of the law. They seized Stephen and brought him before the Sanhedrin.
acts:6:12.csb: They stirred up the people, the elders, and the scribes; so they came, seized him, and took him to the Sanhedrin.
acts:6:12.nrsv: They stirred up the people as well as the elders and the scribes; then they suddenly confronted him, seized him, and brought him before the council.

acts:6:13.niv: They produced false witnesses, who testified, "This fellow never stops speaking against this holy place and against the law.
acts:6:13.csb: They also presented false witnesses who said, “This man never stops speaking against this holy place and the law.
acts:6:13.nrsv: They set up false witnesses who said, "This man never stops saying things against this holy place and the law;

acts:6:14.niv: For we have heard him say that this Jesus of Nazareth will destroy this place and change the customs Moses handed down to us."
acts:6:14.csb: For we heard him say that this Jesus of Nazareth will destroy this place and change the customs that Moses handed down to us.”
acts:6:14.nrsv: for we have heard him say that this Jesus of Nazareth will destroy this place and will change the customs that Moses handed on to us."

acts:6:15.niv: All who were sitting in the Sanhedrin looked intently at Stephen, and they saw that his face was like the face of an angel.
acts:6:15.csb: And all who were sitting in the Sanhedrin looked intently at him and saw that his face was like the face of an angel.
acts:6:15.nrsv: And all who sat in the council looked intently at him, and they saw that his face was like the face of an angel.

acts:7:1.niv: Then the high priest asked him, "Are these charges true?"
acts:7:1.csb: “Are these things true?” the high priest asked.
acts:7:1.nrsv: Then the high priest asked him, "Are these things so?"

acts:7:2.niv: To this he replied: "Brothers and fathers, listen to me! The God of glory appeared to our father Abraham while he was still in Mesopotamia, before he lived in Haran.
acts:7:2.csb: “Brothers and fathers,” he replied, “listen: The God of glory appeared to our father Abraham when he was in Mesopotamia, before he settled in Haran,
acts:7:2.nrsv: And Stephen replied: "Brothers and fathers, listen to me. The God of glory appeared to our ancestor Abraham when he was in Mesopotamia, before he lived in Haran,

acts:7:3.niv: 'Leave your country and your people,' God said, 'and go to the land I will show you.'
acts:7:3.csb: and said to him: Leave your country and relatives, and come to the land that I will show you.
acts:7:3.nrsv: and said to him, 'Leave your country and your relatives and go to the land that I will show you.'

acts:7:4.niv: "So he left the land of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran. After the death of his father, God sent him to this land where you are now living.
acts:7:4.csb: “Then he left the land of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran. From there, after his father died, God had him move to this land in which you are now living.
acts:7:4.nrsv: Then he left the country of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran. After his father died, God had him move from there to this country in which you are now living.

acts:7:5.niv: He gave him no inheritance here, not even a foot of ground. But God promised him that he and his descendants after him would possess the land, even though at that time Abraham had no child.
acts:7:5.csb: He didn’t give him an inheritance in it — not even a foot of ground — but he promised to give it to him as a possession, and to his descendants after him, even though he was childless.
acts:7:5.nrsv: He did not give him any of it as a heritage, not even a foot's length, but promised to give it to him as his possession and to his descendants after him, even though he had no child.

acts:7:6.niv: God spoke to him in this way: 'Your descendants will be strangers in a country not their own, and they will be enslaved and mistreated four hundred years.
acts:7:6.csb: God spoke in this way: His descendants would be strangers in a foreign country, and they would enslave and oppress them for four hundred years.
acts:7:6.nrsv: And God spoke in these terms, that his descendants would be resident aliens in a country belonging to others, who would enslave them and mistreat them during four hundred years.

acts:7:7.niv: But I will punish the nation they serve as slaves,' God said, 'and afterward they will come out of that country and worship me in this place.'
acts:7:7.csb: I will judge the nation that they will serve as slaves, God said. After this, they will come out and worship me in this place.
acts:7:7.nrsv: 'But I will judge the nation that they serve,' said God, 'and after that they shall come out and worship me in this place.'

acts:7:8.niv: Then he gave Abraham the covenant of circumcision. And Abraham became the father of Isaac and circumcised him eight days after his birth. Later Isaac became the father of Jacob, and Jacob became the father of the twelve patriarchs.
acts:7:8.csb: And so he gave Abraham the covenant of circumcision. After this, he fathered Isaac and circumcised him on the eighth day. Isaac became the father of Jacob, and Jacob became the father of the twelve patriarchs.
acts:7:8.nrsv: Then he gave him the covenant of circumcision. And so Abraham became the father of Isaac and circumcised him on the eighth day; and Isaac became the father of Jacob, and Jacob of the twelve patriarchs.

acts:7:9.niv: "Because the patriarchs were jealous of Joseph, they sold him as a slave into Egypt. But God was with him
acts:7:9.csb: “The patriarchs became jealous of Joseph and sold him into Egypt, but God was with him
acts:7:9.nrsv: "The patriarchs, jealous of Joseph, sold him into Egypt; but God was with him,

acts:7:10.niv: and rescued him from all his troubles. He gave Joseph wisdom and enabled him to gain the goodwill of Pharaoh king of Egypt; so he made him ruler over Egypt and all his palace.
acts:7:10.csb: and rescued him out of all his troubles. He gave him favor and wisdom in the sight of Pharaoh, king of Egypt, who appointed him ruler over Egypt and over his whole household.
acts:7:10.nrsv: and rescued him from all his afflictions, and enabled him to win favor and to show wisdom when he stood before Pharaoh, king of Egypt, who appointed him ruler over Egypt and over all his household.

acts:7:11.niv: "Then a famine struck all Egypt and Canaan, bringing great suffering, and our fathers could not find food.
acts:7:11.csb: Now a famine and great suffering came over all of Egypt and Canaan, and our ancestors could find no food.
acts:7:11.nrsv: Now there came a famine throughout Egypt and Canaan, and great suffering, and our ancestors could find no food.

acts:7:12.niv: When Jacob heard that there was grain in Egypt, he sent our fathers on their first visit.
acts:7:12.csb: When Jacob heard there was grain in Egypt, he sent our ancestors there the first time.
acts:7:12.nrsv: But when Jacob heard that there was grain in Egypt, he sent our ancestors there on their first visit.

acts:7:13.niv: On their second visit, Joseph told his brothers who he was, and Pharaoh learned about Joseph's family.
acts:7:13.csb: The second time, Joseph revealed himself to his brothers, and Joseph’s family became known to Pharaoh.
acts:7:13.nrsv: On the second visit Joseph made himself known to his brothers, and Joseph's family became known to Pharaoh.

acts:7:14.niv: After this, Joseph sent for his father Jacob and his whole family, seventy-five in all.
acts:7:14.csb: Joseph invited his father Jacob and all his relatives, seventy-five people in all,
acts:7:14.nrsv: Then Joseph sent and invited his father Jacob and all his relatives to come to him, seventy-five in all;

acts:7:15.niv: Then Jacob went down to Egypt, where he and our fathers died.
acts:7:15.csb: and Jacob went down to Egypt. He and our ancestors died there,
acts:7:15.nrsv: so Jacob went down to Egypt. He himself died there as well as our ancestors,

acts:7:16.niv: Their bodies were brought back to Shechem and placed in the tomb that Abraham had bought from the sons of Hamor at Shechem for a certain sum of money.
acts:7:16.csb: were carried back to Shechem, and were placed in the tomb that Abraham had bought for a sum of silver from the sons of Hamor in Shechem.
acts:7:16.nrsv: and their bodies were brought back to Shechem and laid in the tomb that Abraham had bought for a sum of silver from the sons of Hamor in Shechem.

acts:7:17.niv: "As the time drew near for God to fulfill his promise to Abraham, the number of our people in Egypt greatly increased.
acts:7:17.csb: “As the time was approaching to fulfill the promise that God had made to Abraham, the people flourished and multiplied in Egypt
acts:7:17.nrsv: "But as the time drew near for the fulfillment of the promise that God had made to Abraham, our people in Egypt increased and multiplied

acts:7:18.niv: Then another king, who knew nothing about Joseph, became ruler of Egypt.
acts:7:18.csb: until a different king who did not know Joseph ruled over Egypt.
acts:7:18.nrsv: until another king who had not known Joseph ruled over Egypt.

acts:7:19.niv: He dealt treacherously with our people and oppressed our forefathers by forcing them to throw out their newborn babies so that they would die.
acts:7:19.csb: He dealt deceitfully with our race and oppressed our ancestors by making them abandon their infants outside so that they wouldn’t survive.
acts:7:19.nrsv: He dealt craftily with our race and forced our ancestors to abandon their infants so that they would die.

acts:7:20.niv: "At that time Moses was born, and he was no ordinary child. For three months he was cared for in his father's house.
acts:7:20.csb: At this time Moses was born, and he was beautiful in God’s sight. He was cared for in his father’s home for three months.
acts:7:20.nrsv: At this time Moses was born, and he was beautiful before God. For three months he was brought up in his father's house;

acts:7:21.niv: When he was placed outside, Pharaoh's daughter took him and brought him up as her own son.
acts:7:21.csb: When he was put outside, Pharaoh’s daughter adopted and raised him as her own son.
acts:7:21.nrsv: and when he was abandoned, Pharaoh's daughter adopted him and brought him up as her own son.

acts:7:22.niv: Moses was educated in all the wisdom of the Egyptians and was powerful in speech and action.
acts:7:22.csb: So Moses was educated in all the wisdom of the Egyptians and was powerful in his speech and actions.
acts:7:22.nrsv: So Moses was instructed in all the wisdom of the Egyptians and was powerful in his words and deeds.

acts:7:23.niv: "When Moses was forty years old, he decided to visit his fellow Israelites.
acts:7:23.csb: “When he was forty years old, he decided to visit his own people, the Israelites.
acts:7:23.nrsv: "When he was forty years old, it came into his heart to visit his relatives, the Israelites.

acts:7:24.niv: He saw one of them being mistreated by an Egyptian, so he went to his defense and avenged him by killing the Egyptian.
acts:7:24.csb: When he saw one of them being mistreated, he came to his rescue and avenged the oppressed man by striking down the Egyptian.
acts:7:24.nrsv: When he saw one of them being wronged, he defended the oppressed man and avenged him by striking down the Egyptian.

acts:7:25.niv: Moses thought that his own people would realize that God was using him to rescue them, but they did not.
acts:7:25.csb: He assumed his people would understand that God would give them deliverance through him, but they did not understand.
acts:7:25.nrsv: He supposed that his kinsfolk would understand that God through him was rescuing them, but they did not understand.

acts:7:26.niv: The next day Moses came upon two Israelites who were fighting. He tried to reconcile them by saying, 'Men, you are brothers; why do you want to hurt each other?'
acts:7:26.csb: The next day he showed up while they were fighting and tried to reconcile them peacefully, saying, ‘Men, you are brothers. Why are you mistreating each other? ’
acts:7:26.nrsv: The next day he came to some of them as they were quarreling and tried to reconcile them, saying, 'Men, you are brothers; why do you wrong each other?'

acts:7:27.niv: "But the man who was mistreating the other pushed Moses aside and said, 'Who made you ruler and judge over us?
acts:7:27.csb: “But the one who was mistreating his neighbor pushed Moses aside, saying: Who appointed you a ruler and a judge over us?
acts:7:27.nrsv: But the man who was wronging his neighbor pushed Moses aside, saying, 'Who made you a ruler and a judge over us?

acts:7:28.niv: Do you want to kill me as you killed the Egyptian yesterday?'
acts:7:28.csb: Do you want to kill me, the same way you killed the Egyptian yesterday?
acts:7:28.nrsv: Do you want to kill me as you killed the Egyptian yesterday?'

acts:7:29.niv: When Moses heard this, he fled to Midian, where he settled as a foreigner and had two sons.
acts:7:29.csb: “When he heard this, Moses fled and became an exile in the land of Midian, where he became the father of two sons.
acts:7:29.nrsv: When he heard this, Moses fled and became a resident alien in the land of Midian. There he became the father of two sons.

acts:7:30.niv: "After forty years had passed, an angel appeared to Moses in the flames of a burning bush in the desert near Mount Sinai.
acts:7:30.csb: After forty years had passed, an angel appeared to him in the wilderness of Mount Sinai, in the flame of a burning bush.
acts:7:30.nrsv: "Now when forty years had passed, an angel appeared to him in the wilderness of Mount Sinai, in the flame of a burning bush.

acts:7:31.niv: When he saw this, he was amazed at the sight. As he went over to look more closely, he heard the Lord's voice:
acts:7:31.csb: When Moses saw it, he was amazed at the sight. As he was approaching to look at it, the voice of the Lord came:
acts:7:31.nrsv: When Moses saw it, he was amazed at the sight; and as he approached to look, there came the voice of the Lord:

acts:7:32.niv: 'I am the God of your fathers, the God of Abraham, Isaac and Jacob.' Moses trembled with fear and did not dare to look.
acts:7:32.csb: I am the God of your ancestors — the God of Abraham, of Isaac, and of Jacob. Moses began to tremble and did not dare to look.
acts:7:32.nrsv: 'I am the God of your ancestors, the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.' Moses began to tremble and did not dare to look.

acts:7:33.niv: "Then the Lord said to him, 'Take off your sandals; the place where you are standing is holy ground.
acts:7:33.csb: “The Lord said to him: Take off the sandals from your feet, because the place where you are standing is holy ground.
acts:7:33.nrsv: Then the Lord said to him, 'Take off the sandals from your feet, for the place where you are standing is holy ground.

acts:7:34.niv: I have indeed seen the oppression of my people in Egypt. I have heard their groaning and have come down to set them free. Now come, I will send you back to Egypt.'
acts:7:34.csb: I have certainly seen the oppression of my people in Egypt; I have heard their groaning and have come down to set them free. And now, come, I will send you to Egypt.
acts:7:34.nrsv: I have surely seen the mistreatment of my people who are in Egypt and have heard their groaning, and I have come down to rescue them. Come now, I will send you to Egypt.'

acts:7:35.niv: "This is the same Moses whom they had rejected with the words, 'Who made you ruler and judge?' He was sent to be their ruler and deliverer by God himself, through the angel who appeared to him in the bush.
acts:7:35.csb: “This Moses, whom they rejected when they said, Who appointed you a ruler and a judge? — this one God sent as a ruler and a deliverer through the angel who appeared to him in the bush.
acts:7:35.nrsv: "It was this Moses whom they rejected when they said, 'Who made you a ruler and a judge?' and whom God now sent as both ruler and liberator through the angel who appeared to him in the bush.

acts:7:36.niv: He led them out of Egypt and did wonders and miraculous signs in Egypt, at the Red Sea and for forty years in the desert.
acts:7:36.csb: This man led them out and performed wonders and signs in the land of Egypt, at the Red Sea, and in the wilderness for forty years.
acts:7:36.nrsv: He led them out, having performed wonders and signs in Egypt, at the Red Sea, and in the wilderness for forty years.

acts:7:37.niv: "This is that Moses who told the Israelites, 'God will send you a prophet like me from your own people.'
acts:7:37.csb: “This is the Moses who said to the Israelites: God will raise up for you a prophet like me from among your brothers and sisters.
acts:7:37.nrsv: This is the Moses who said to the Israelites, 'God will raise up a prophet for you from your own people as he raised me up.'

acts:7:38.niv: He was in the assembly in the desert, with the angel who spoke to him on Mount Sinai, and with our fathers; and he received living words to pass on to us.
acts:7:38.csb: He is the one who was in the assembly in the wilderness, with the angel who spoke to him on Mount Sinai, and with our ancestors. He received living oracles to give to us.
acts:7:38.nrsv: He is the one who was in the congregation in the wilderness with the angel who spoke to him at Mount Sinai, and with our ancestors; and he received living oracles to give to us.

acts:7:39.niv: "But our fathers refused to obey him. Instead, they rejected him and in their hearts turned back to Egypt.
acts:7:39.csb: Our ancestors were unwilling to obey him. Instead, they pushed him aside, and in their hearts turned back to Egypt.
acts:7:39.nrsv: Our ancestors were unwilling to obey him; instead, they pushed him aside, and in their hearts they turned back to Egypt,

acts:7:40.niv: They told Aaron, 'Make us gods who will go before us. As for this fellow Moses who led us out of Egypt--we don't know what has happened to him!'
acts:7:40.csb: They told Aaron: Make us gods who will go before us. As for this Moses who brought us out of the land of Egypt, we don’t know what’s happened to him.
acts:7:40.nrsv: saying to Aaron, 'Make gods for us who will lead the way for us; as for this Moses who led us out from the land of Egypt, we do not know what has happened to him.'
